 `/Users/sean/.claude/projects/-Users-sean-Source-SpecStory-getspecstory-specstory-cli/28a0b619-147f-483f-bfcc-a4d48d212fd0/tool-results/bgmi04u22.txt`

```text
1	diff --git a/specstory-cli/pkg/sessionindex/store.go b/specstory-cli/pkg/sessionindex/store.go
2	new file mode 100644
3	index 0000000..507ba2d
4	--- /dev/null
5	+++ b/specstory-cli/pkg/sessionindex/store.go
6	@@ -0,0 +1,633 @@
7	+// Package sessionindex implements sessions.db — the machine-level index of every coding-agent
8	+// session SpecStory knows about, across all projects and providers. It backs the
9	+// `specstory resume` selection UX and is (re)built by `specstory reindex`.
10	+//
11	+// sessions.db is a DERIVED CACHE over the native session stores: it can be deleted and
12	+// fully rebuilt at any time. See docs/SESSIONS-DB.md for the design and schema.
13	+package sessionindex
14	+
15	+import (
16	+	"context"
17	+	"database/sql"
18	+	"errors"
19	+	"fmt"
20	+	"os"
21	+	"path/filepath"
22	+	"sort"
23	+	"strings"
24	+
25	+	_ "modernc.org/sqlite" // SQLite driver (pure Go), same as pkg/provenance
26	+)
27	+
28	+// Connection-pool sizing. Writes (reindex) must serialize on a single connection to
29	+// avoid SQLITE_BUSY/deadlocks. The interactive browse path (resume/search) only reads,
30	+// so it opens several connections — WAL permits many concurrent readers, which keeps a
31	+// single slow full-text query (a broad prefix can take seconds) from starving the UI.
32	+const (
33	+	writerConns = 1
34	+	readerConns = 4
35	+)
36	+
37	+// Session is one row of the restore index (the `sessions` table), plus Body, which is
38	+// indexed into the FTS table rather than stored on the row. See docs/SESSIONS-DB.md.
39	+type Session struct {
40	+	ProjectID    string // resolved git_id (else workspace_id)
41	+	ProjectName  string
42	+	Agent        string // provider id: claude, codex, gemini, droid, deepseek, cursor
43	+	SessionID    string // native session id (uuid)
44	+	CreatedAt    string // ISO 8601, first turn
45	+	UpdatedAt    string // ISO 8601, last activity (last turn, else file mtime)
46	+	UserTurns    int    // count of user prompts
47	+	TotalTurns   int    // count of all messages
48	+	Slug         string
49	+	Name         string
50	+	NativePath   string // absolute path the provider opens to read this session
51	+	OriginCwd    string // working directory the session was launched from
52	+	Size         int64  // native file size, bytes — part of the freshness fingerprint
53	+	Mtime        int64  // native file mtime, epoch ms — part of the freshness fingerprint
54	+	IndexVersion int    // reindex logic version that wrote this row — part of the fingerprint
55	+	IndexedAt    string // ISO 8601, when reindex last wrote this row
56	+
57	+	// Body is the full conversation text, indexed into sessions_fts (not persisted on
58	+	// the sessions row). Left empty on rows read back out of the index.
59	+	Body string
60	+
61	+	// IsNew is a write-time hint, not a persisted column: when true, the caller has
62	+	// determined this (agent, session_id) has no existing index row, so upsertOne can skip
63	+	// looking up and deleting a prior FTS row before insert. Leave false when unsure — the
64	+	// lookup-and-delete is then kept, which is always correct (a missing prior row is a no-op).
65	+	IsNew bool
66	+}
67	+
68	+// Fingerprint identifies an indexed session's freshness: the native file's size and
69	+// mtime, plus the reindex logic version that produced the row. reindex skips a session
70	+// whose fingerprint is unchanged. See docs/SESSIONS-DB.md.
71	+type Fingerprint struct {
72	+	Size    int64
73	+	Mtime   int64
74	+	Version int
75	+}
76	+
77	+// Store is a handle to sessions.db.
78	+type Store struct {
79	+	db *sql.DB
80	+}
81	+
82	+// DefaultPath returns ~/.specstory/sessions.db.
83	+func DefaultPath() (string, error) {
84	+	home, err := os.UserHomeDir()
85	+	if err != nil {
86	+		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to resolve home directory: %w", err)
87	+	}
88	+	return filepath.Join(home, ".specstory", "sessions.db"), nil
89	+}
90	+
91	+// Open opens (or creates) sessions.db for writing — a single serialized connection,
92	+// applying WAL + performance pragmas (matching pkg/provenance) and ensuring the schema
93	+// exists. Use OpenReader for the read-only interactive browse path.
94	+func Open(path string) (*Store, error) {
95	+	return openWith(path, writerConns)
96	+}
97	+
98	+// OpenReader opens sessions.db for the interactive browse path (resume/search), which
99	+// only reads. It allows several concurrent connections so one slow full-text query can't
100	+// starve the UI (WAL permits many simultaneous readers). Never write through this handle.
101	+func OpenReader(path string) (*Store, error) {
102	+	return openWith(path, readerConns)
103	+}
104	+
105	+func openWith(path string, maxConns int) (*Store, error) {
106	+	if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(path), 0o755); err != nil {
107	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("creating database directory: %w", err)
108	+	}
109	+
110	+	dsn := fmt.Sprintf("file:%s?_pragma=busy_timeout=15000&_pragma=journal_mode(WAL)", filepath.ToSlash(path))
111	+	db, err := sql.Open("sqlite", dsn)
112	+	if err != nil {
113	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("opening sessions.db: %w", err)
114	+	}
115	+
116	+	db.SetMaxOpenConns(maxConns)
117	+	db.SetMaxIdleConns(maxConns)
118	+
119	+	for _, p := range []string{
120	+		"PRAGMA synchronous=NORMAL",
121	+		"PRAGMA cache_size = -64000",
122	+		"PRAGMA temp_store = MEMORY",
123	+		"PRAGMA mmap_size = 268435456",
124	+		"PRAGMA page_size = 8192",
125	+	} {
126	+		if _, err := db.Exec(p); err != nil {
127	+			_ = db.Close()
128	+			return nil, fmt.Errorf("setting pragma %q: %w", p, err)
129	+		}
130	+	}
131	+
132	+	s := &Store{db: db}
133	+	if err := s.ensureSchema(); err != nil {
134	+		_ = db.Close()
135	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("ensuring schema: %w", err)
136	+	}
137	+	return s, nil
138	+}
139	+
140	+// Close closes the database connection.
141	+func (s *Store) Close() error {
142	+	return s.db.Close()
143	+}
144	+
145	+// sessionColumns is the canonical sessions column list, shared by every SELECT so the
146	+// scan order in scanSession stays in lockstep with it.
147	+const sessionColumns = `project_id, project_name, agent, session_id, created_at, updated_at,
148	+	user_turns, total_turns, slug, name, native_path, origin_cwd, size, mtime, index_version, indexed_at`
149	+
150	+// sessionInsertColumns is sessionColumns plus fts_rowid, the internal link to the session's
151	+// FTS row. fts_rowid is write-only — set on insert, read only by SessionBody's join — so it is
152	+// deliberately kept out of sessionColumns and the Session struct rather than threaded through
153	+// every read path.
154	+const sessionInsertColumns = sessionColumns + `, fts_rowid`
155	+
156	+func (s *Store) ensureSchema() error {
157	+	schema := `
158	+	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sessions (
159	+		project_id   TEXT NOT NULL,
160	+		project_name TEXT,
161	+		agent        TEXT NOT NULL,
162	+		session_id   TEXT NOT NULL,
163	+		created_at   TEXT,
164	+		updated_at   TEXT,
165	+		user_turns   INTEGER,
166	+		total_turns  INTEGER,
167	+		slug         TEXT,
168	+		name         TEXT,
169	+		native_path  TEXT,
170	+		origin_cwd   TEXT,
171	+		size          INTEGER,
172	+		mtime         INTEGER,
173	+		index_version INTEGER,
174	+		indexed_at    TEXT,
175	+		fts_rowid     INTEGER,
176	+		PRIMARY KEY (agent, session_id)
177	+	);
178	+	-- Composite over the project filter PLUS the picker's sort order. ListByProject
179	+	-- (the resume picker's hot path) filters by project_id and orders by
180	+	-- updated_at DESC, created_at DESC; this index serves both, so SQLite walks it
181	+	-- backward instead of filtering then sorting in a temp b-tree. project_id is the
182	+	-- left prefix, so ProjectCount/UnattributedCount still use it. Replaces the old
183	+	-- single-column idx_sessions_project (dropped in the migration below).
184	+	CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_sessions_project_recent ON sessions(project_id, updated_at, created_at);
185	+
186	+	-- Standalone FTS5 index over the conversation body + name. session_id/agent ride
187	+	-- along UNINDEXED as join keys back to the sessions row. See docs/SESSIONS-DB.md.
188	+	CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sessions_fts USING fts5(
189	+		session_id UNINDEXED,
190	+		agent UNINDEXED,
191	+		name,
192	+		body
193	+	);
194	+	`
195	+	if _, err := s.db.Exec(schema); err != nil {
196	+		return err
197	+	}
198	+	// Migration for indexes created before index_version existed (the column is part of
199	+	// the freshness fingerprint). Ignore the error when it already exists.
200	+	_, _ = s.db.Exec(`ALTER TABLE sessions ADD COLUMN index_version INTEGER DEFAULT 0`)
201	+	// fts_rowid links a session row to its sessions_fts row, so the body read and the
202	+	// delete-before-insert are O(1) rowid lookups instead of whole-FTS scans (session_id/agent
203	+	// are UNINDEXED). NULL on rows written before this column existed; a reindex (reindexVersion
204	+	// bump) repopulates it. Ignore the error when the column already exists.
205	+	_, _ = s.db.Exec(`ALTER TABLE sessions ADD COLUMN fts_rowid INTEGER`)
206	+	// Drop the old single-column project index now superseded by the composite
207	+	// idx_sessions_project_recent (project_id is its left prefix). Idempotent; a no-op
208	+	// on fresh databases that never had it.
209	+	_, _ = s.db.Exec(`DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_sessions_project`)
210	+	return nil
211	+}
212	+
213	+// Fingerprints returns the freshness fingerprint of every indexed session, keyed by
214	+// fingerprintKey(agent, session_id). reindex loads this once and skips any session
215	+// whose native file is unchanged (same size + mtime) and was indexed by the current
216	+// logic version.
217	+func (s *Store) Fingerprints() (map[string]Fingerprint, error) {
218	+	rows, err := s.db.Query(`SELECT agent, session_id, size, mtime, index_version FROM sessions`)
219	+	if err != nil {
220	+		return nil, err
221	+	}
222	+	defer func() { _ = rows.Close() }()
223	+
224	+	out := make(map[string]Fingerprint)
225	+	for rows.Next() {
226	+		var agent, sessionID string
227	+		var fp Fingerprint
228	+		if err := rows.Scan(&agent, &sessionID, &fp.Size, &fp.Mtime, &fp.Version); err != nil {
229	+			return nil, err
230	+		}
231	+		out[FingerprintKey(agent, sessionID)] = fp
232	+	}
233	+	return out, rows.Err()
234	+}
235	+
236	+// FingerprintKey is the map key for a session's fingerprint: agent + NUL + session_id.
237	+func FingerprintKey(agent, sessionID string) string {
238	+	return agent + "\x00" + sessionID
239	+}
240	+
241	+// sessionUpsertStmts are the per-row statements UpsertBatch prepares once and reuses across
242	+// every row in a transaction, so the SQL is parsed once per batch instead of once per row.
243	+type sessionUpsertStmts struct {
244	+	insSession    *sql.Stmt
245	+	selOldRowid   *sql.Stmt
246	+	delFTSByRowid *sql.Stmt
247	+	delFTSByKey   *sql.Stmt
248	+	insFTS        *sql.Stmt
249	+}
250	+
251	+// upsertOne writes one session's row and full-text row using the batch's prepared statements.
252	+// FTS5 standalone tables are not auto-synced, so the FTS row is maintained by hand. The new FTS
253	+// row is inserted first so its rowid can be stored on the sessions row (fts_rowid) — that link
254	+// is what makes later body reads and replace-deletes O(1) rowid lookups instead of whole-FTS
255	+// scans (session_id/agent are UNINDEXED). For an existing session the prior FTS row is removed
256	+// first; brand-new sessions (sess.IsNew) skip that lookup-and-delete entirely.
257	+func upsertOne(st sessionUpsertStmts, sess Session) error {
258	+	if !sess.IsNew {
259	+		if err := deleteOldFTSRow(st, sess); err != nil {
260	+			return err
261	+		}
262	+	}
263	+	res, err := st.insFTS.Exec(sess.SessionID, sess.Agent, sess.Name, sess.Body)
264	+	if err != nil {
265	+		return fmt.Errorf("insert fts row: %w", err)
266	+	}
267	+	ftsRowid, err := res.LastInsertId()
268	+	if err != nil {
269	+		return fmt.Errorf("read fts rowid: %w", err)
270	+	}
271	+	if _, err := st.insSession.Exec(
272	+		sess.ProjectID, sess.ProjectName, sess.Agent, sess.SessionID, sess.CreatedAt, sess.UpdatedAt,
273	+		sess.UserTurns, sess.TotalTurns, sess.Slug, sess.Name, sess.NativePath, sess.OriginCwd,
274	+		sess.Size, sess.Mtime, sess.IndexVersion, sess.IndexedAt, ftsRowid); err != nil {
275	+		return fmt.Errorf("upsert session row: %w", err)
276	+	}
277	+	return nil
278	+}
279	+
280	+// deleteOldFTSRow removes an existing session's current FTS row before the sessions row is
281	+// replaced (INSERT OR REPLACE would otherwise drop the fts_rowid link and orphan the FTS row).
282	+// It resolves the row by its stored fts_rowid (O(1)); for rows written before fts_rowid existed
283	+// (NULL) it falls back to the by-key delete — a whole-FTS scan that a reindex retires by
284	+// repopulating fts_rowid.
285	+func deleteOldFTSRow(st sessionUpsertStmts, sess Session) error {
286	+	var oldRowid sql.NullInt64
287	+	switch err := st.selOldRowid.QueryRow(sess.Agent, sess.SessionID).Scan(&oldRowid); {
288	+	case errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows):
289	+		return nil // no prior row to remove
290	+	case err != nil:
291	+		return fmt.Errorf("look up old fts rowid: %w", err)
292	+	}
293	+	if oldRowid.Valid {
294	+		if _, err := st.delFTSByRowid.Exec(oldRowid.Int64); err != nil {
295	+			return fmt.Errorf("clear fts row by rowid: %w", err)
296	+		}
297	+		return nil
298	+	}
299	+	if _, err := st.delFTSByKey.Exec(sess.Agent, sess.SessionID); err != nil {
300	+		return fmt.Errorf("clear legacy fts row: %w", err)
301	+	}
302	+	return nil
303	+}
304	+
305	+// prepareUpsert prepares the per-row statements on tx and returns them with a closer.
306	+func prepareUpsert(tx *sql.Tx) (sessionUpsertStmts, func(), error) {
307	+	var prepared []*sql.Stmt
308	+	closer := func() {
309	+		for _, s := range prepared {
310	+			_ = s.Close()
311	+		}
312	+	}
313	+	prep := func(what, query string) (*sql.Stmt, error) {
314	+		s, err := tx.Prepare(query)
315	+		if err != nil {
316	+			closer()
317	+			return nil, fmt.Errorf("prepare %s: %w", what, err)
318	+		}
319	+		prepared = append(prepared, s)
320	+		return s, nil
321	+	}
322	+
323	+	insSession, err := prep("session upsert", `INSERT OR REPLACE INTO sessions (`+sessionInsertColumns+`)
324	+		VALUES (?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?)`)
325	+	if err != nil {
326	+		return sessionUpsertStmts{}, func() {}, err
327	+	}
328	+	selOldRowid, err := prep("fts rowid lookup", `SELECT fts_rowid FROM sessions WHERE agent = ? AND session_id = ?`)
329	+	if err != nil {
330	+		return sessionUpsertStmts{}, func() {}, err
331	+	}
332	+	delFTSByRowid, err := prep("fts delete by rowid", `DELETE FROM sessions_fts WHERE rowid = ?`)
333	+	if err != nil {
334	+		return sessionUpsertStmts{}, func() {}, err
335	+	}
336	+	delFTSByKey, err := prep("fts delete by key", `DELETE FROM sessions_fts WHERE agent = ? AND session_id = ?`)
337	+	if err != nil {
338	+		return sessionUpsertStmts{}, func() {}, err
339	+	}
340	+	insFTS, err := prep("fts insert", `INSERT INTO sessions_fts (session_id, agent, name, body) VALUES (?,?,?,?)`)
341	+	if err != nil {
342	+		return sessionUpsertStmts{}, func() {}, err
343	+	}
344	+	return sessionUpsertStmts{
345	+		insSession:    insSession,
346	+		selOldRowid:   selOldRowid,
347	+		delFTSByRowid: delFTSByRowid,
348	+		delFTSByKey:   delFTSByKey,
349	+		insFTS:        insFTS,
350	+	}, closer, nil
351	+}
352	+
353	+// Upsert inserts or replaces a single session row and its full-text row, atomically. A
354	+// re-index of the same session (same agent + session_id) replaces both in lockstep.
355	+func (s *Store) Upsert(sess Session) error {
356	+	return s.UpsertBatch([]Session{sess})
357	+}
358	+
359	+// UpsertBatch writes many sessions in one transaction — the write path for `reindex`,
360	+// which batches to avoid thousands of tiny WAL commits.
361	+func (s *Store) UpsertBatch(sessions []Session) error {
362	+	if len(sessions) == 0 {
363	+		return nil
364	+	}
365	+	tx, err := s.db.Begin()
366	+	if err != nil {
367	+		return fmt.Errorf("begin upsert: %w", err)
368	+	}
369	+	committed := false
370	+	defer func() {
371	+		if !committed {
372	+			_ = tx.Rollback()
373	+		}
374	+	}()
375	+
376	+	st, closeStmts, err := prepareUpsert(tx)
377	+	if err != nil {
378	+		return err
379	+	}
380	+	defer closeStmts()
381	+
382	+	for _, sess := range sessions {
383	+		if err := upsertOne(st, sess); err != nil {
384	+			return err
385	+		}
386	+	}
387	+
388	+	if err := tx.Commit(); err != nil {
389	+		return fmt.Errorf("commit upsert: %w", err)
390	+	}
391	+	committed = true
392	+	return nil
393	+}
394	+
395	+// Exists reports whether a session row is already indexed, looked up by the primary key
396	+// (agent, session_id) so it stays O(log n) instead of scanning. Because a session's sessions
397	+// row and its sessions_fts row are always written together (upsertOne, one transaction), a
398	+// missing sessions row guarantees a missing FTS row — which lets the live writer set
399	+// Session.IsNew and skip the whole-table FTS delete for genuinely new sessions.
400	+func (s *Store) Exists(agent, sessionID string) (bool, error) {
401	+	var one int
402	+	err := s.db.QueryRow(`SELECT 1 FROM sessions WHERE agent = ? AND session_id = ?`, agent, sessionID).Scan(&one)
403	+	if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
404	+		return false, nil
405	+	}
406	+	if err != nil {
407	+		return false, err
408	+	}
409	+	return true, nil
410	+}
411	+
412	+// Count returns the number of indexed sessions.
413	+func (s *Store) Count() (int, error) {
414	+	var n int
415	+	if err := s.db.QueryRow(`SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sessions`).Scan(&n); err != nil {
416	+		return 0, err
417	+	}
418	+	return n, nil
419	+}
420	+
421	+// ProjectCount returns the number of distinct attributed projects in the index
422	+// (excluding the unknownID bucket).
423	+func (s *Store) ProjectCount(unknownID string) (int, error) {
424	+	var n int
425	+	err := s.db.QueryRow(`SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT project_id) FROM sessions WHERE project_id != ?`, unknownID).Scan(&n)
426	+	return n, err
427	+}
428	+
429	+// UnattributedCount returns the number of sessions in the unknownID bucket.
430	+func (s *Store) UnattributedCount(unknownID string) (int, error) {
431	+	var n int
432	+	err := s.db.QueryRow(`SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sessions WHERE project_id = ?`, unknownID).Scan(&n)
433	+	return n, err
434	+}
435	+
436	+// ListByProject returns a project's sessions, newest activity first. Body is not
437	+// populated (it lives only in the FTS index). Used by the `specstory resume` picker.
438	+func (s *Store) ListByProject(projectID string) ([]Session, error) {
439	+	rows, err := s.db.Query(`SELECT `+sessionColumns+`
440	+		FROM sessions WHERE project_id = ? ORDER BY updated_at DESC, created_at DESC`, projectID)
441	+	if err != nil {
442	+		return nil, err
443	+	}
444	+	defer func() { _ = rows.Close() }()
445	+	return scanSessions(rows)
446	+}
447	+
448	+// ProjectSummary is a rolled-up view of one project for the all-projects picker.
449	+type ProjectSummary struct {
450	+	ProjectID    string
451	+	ProjectName  string
452	+	Sessions     int            // total sessions in the project
453	+	LastActivity string         // most recent updated_at across the project
454	+	AgentCounts  map[string]int // sessions per agent (claude, codex, …)
455	+}
456	+
457	+// ListProjects returns one rolled-up summary per project, most recently active first.
458	+// Used by the all-projects view (date-bucketed). The unknown-project bucket is included;
459	+// the caller decides how to present it.
460	+func (s *Store) ListProjects() ([]ProjectSummary, error) {
461	+	rows, err := s.db.Query(`SELECT project_id, project_name, agent, COUNT(*), MAX(updated_at)
462	+		FROM sessions GROUP BY project_id, agent`)
463	+	if err != nil {
464	+		return nil, err
465	+	}
466	+	defer func() { _ = rows.Close() }()
467	+
468	+	byID := map[string]*ProjectSummary{}
469	+	for rows.Next() {
470	+		var pid, pname, agent, last string
471	+		var n int
472	+		if err := rows.Scan(&pid, &pname, &agent, &n, &last); err != nil {
473	+			return nil, err
474	+		}
475	+		ps, ok := byID[pid]
476	+		if !ok {
477	+			ps = &ProjectSummary{ProjectID: pid, ProjectName: pname, AgentCounts: map[string]int{}}
478	+			byID[pid] = ps
479	+		}
480	+		ps.Sessions += n
481	+		ps.AgentCounts[agent] += n
482	+		if ps.ProjectName == "" {
483	+			ps.ProjectName = pname
484	+		}
485	+		if last > ps.LastActivity {
486	+			ps.LastActivity = last
487	+		}
488	+	}
489	+	if err := rows.Err(); err != nil {
490	+		return nil, err
491	+	}
492	+
493	+	out := make([]ProjectSummary, 0, len(byID))
494	+	for _, ps := range byID {
495	+		out = append(out, *ps)
496	+	}
497	+	sort.Slice(out, func(i, j int) bool { return out[i].LastActivity > out[j].LastActivity })
498	+	return out, nil
499	+}
500	+
501	+// SessionBody returns the full-text conversation body for a session (for the preview
502	+// pane), or "" if the session has no indexed body (e.g. Cursor, metadata-only).
503	+func (s *Store) SessionBody(agent, sessionID string) (string, error) {
504	+	var body string
505	+	// Fast path: resolve the FTS row by the rowid stored on the sessions row (O(1)), instead of
506	+	// scanning the whole FTS (session_id/agent are UNINDEXED).
507	+	err := s.db.QueryRow(`SELECT f.body FROM sessions s
508	+		JOIN sessions_fts f ON f.rowid = s.fts_rowid
509	+		WHERE s.agent = ? AND s.session_id = ?`, agent, sessionID).Scan(&body)
510	+	if err == nil {
511	+		return body, nil
512	+	}
513	+	if !errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
514	+		return "", err
515	+	}
516	+	// No fts_rowid link (a row written before the column existed). Fall back to the by-key scan;
517	+	// a reindex repopulates fts_rowid and retires this path.
518	+	err = s.db.QueryRow(`SELECT body FROM sessions_fts WHERE agent = ? AND session_id = ?`,
519	+		agent, sessionID).Scan(&body)
520	+	if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
521	+		return "", nil
522	+	}
523	+	return body, err
524	+}
525	+
526	+// Search runs a full-text query and returns matching sessions, most recent first.
527	+// projectID scopes the search to one project; "" searches across all projects.
528	+func (s *Store) Search(query, projectID string) ([]Session, error) {
529	+	return s.SearchContext(context.Background(), query, projectID)
530	+}
531	+
532	+// SearchContext is Search bound to a context. The interactive search cancels the context
533	+// when a newer keystroke arrives, which aborts a slow in-flight query and frees its
534	+// connection. Snippets are deliberately fetched separately for only the visible rows:
535	+// FTS5 snippet generation over hundreds of full transcripts dominates broad searches.
536	+func (s *Store) SearchContext(ctx context.Context, query, projectID string) ([]Session, error) {
537	+	q := `SELECT ` + prefixed("s", sessionColumns) + `
538	+		FROM sessions_fts
539	+		JOIN sessions s ON s.agent = sessions_fts.agent AND s.session_id = sessions_fts.session_id
540	+		WHERE sessions_fts MATCH ?`
541	+	args := []any{query}
542	+	if projectID != "" {
543	+		q += ` AND s.project_id = ?`
544	+		args = append(args, projectID)
545	+	}
546	+	// Order by recency, not FTS5's BM25 rank. For full-conversation transcripts BM25 is
547	+	// noisy (length-normalization dominates) and a single-term query gets no IDF signal,
548	+	// so relevance order looks arbitrary; newest-first is what's useful when browsing your
549	+	// own history. It also makes LIMIT keep the 500 most RECENT matches (vs the 500 densest)
550	+	// and skips BM25 scoring entirely. updated_at is ISO 8601, so TEXT sort = chronological.
551	+	q += ` ORDER BY s.updated_at DESC LIMIT 500`
552	+
553	+	rows, err := s.db.QueryContext(ctx, q, args...)
554	+	if err != nil {
555	+		return nil, err
556	+	}
557	+	defer func() { _ = rows.Close() }()
558	+
559	+	return scanSessions(rows)
560	+}
561	+
562	+// Snippets returns highlighted match snippets for the provided sessions. The result is
563	+// keyed by FingerprintKey(agent, session_id). Matched terms are wrapped in \x02 and \x03
564	+// (STX/ETX) so callers can highlight without colliding with conversation text.
565	+func (s *Store) Snippets(query string, sessions []Session) (map[string]string, error) {
566	+	return s.SnippetsContext(context.Background(), query, sessions)
567	+}
568	+
569	+// SnippetsContext is Snippets bound to a context. Callers should pass only the currently
570	+// visible rows; snippet() is intentionally lazy because it is the expensive part of FTS
571	+// search on long transcripts.
572	+func (s *Store) SnippetsContext(ctx context.Context, query string, sessions []Session) (map[string]string, error) {
573	+	out := map[string]string{}
574	+	if query == "" || len(sessions) == 0 {
575	+		return out, nil
576	+	}
577	+
578	+	clauses := make([]string, 0, len(sessions))
579	+	args := []any{query}
580	+	seen := map[string]bool{}
581	+	for _, sess := range sessions {
582	+		key := FingerprintKey(sess.Agent, sess.SessionID)
583	+		if seen[key] {
584	+			continue
585	+		}
586	+		seen[key] = true
587	+		clauses = append(clauses, `(agent = ? AND session_id = ?)`)
588	+		args = append(args, sess.Agent, sess.SessionID)
589	+	}
590	+	if len(clauses) == 0 {
591	+		return out, nil
592	+	}
593	+
594	+	q := `SELECT agent, session_id, snippet(sessions_fts, 3, char(2), char(3), '…', 12)
595	+		FROM sessions_fts
596	+		WHERE sessions_fts MATCH ? AND (` + strings.Join(clauses, ` OR `) + `)`
597	+	rows, err := s.db.QueryContext(ctx, q, args...)
598	+	if err != nil {
599	+		return nil, err
600	+	}
601	+	defer func() { _ = rows.Close() }()
602	+
603	+	for rows.Next() {
604	+		var agent, sessionID, snippet string
605	+		if err := rows.Scan(&agent, &sessionID, &snippet); err != nil {
606	+			return nil, err
607	+		}
608	+		out[FingerprintKey(agent, sessionID)] = snippet
609	+	}
610	+	return out, rows.Err()
611	+}
612	+
613	+// prefixed qualifies each comma-separated column in cols with the given table alias,
614	+// e.g. prefixed("s", "a, b") -> "s.a, s.b". Used to disambiguate the sessions columns
615	+// in the FTS join (where session_id/agent also exist on the FTS table).
616	+func prefixed(alias, cols string) string {
617	+	parts := strings.Split(cols, ",")
618	+	for i, p := range parts {
619	+		parts[i] = alias + "." + strings.TrimSpace(p)
620	+	}
621	+	return strings.Join(parts, ", ")
622	+}
623	+
624	+// scanSessions scans rows selected with sessionColumns (in order) into Sessions.
625	+func scanSessions(rows *sql.Rows) ([]Session, error) {
626	+	var out []Session
627	+	for rows.Next() {
628	+		var s Session
629	+		if err := rows.Scan(
630	+			&s.ProjectID, &s.ProjectName, &s.Agent, &s.SessionID, &s.CreatedAt, &s.UpdatedAt,
631	+			&s.UserTurns, &s.TotalTurns, &s.Slug, &s.Name, &s.NativePath, &s.OriginCwd,
632	+			&s.Size, &s.Mtime, &s.IndexVersion, &s.IndexedAt,
633	+		); err != nil {
634	+			return nil, err
635	+		}
636	+		out = append(out, s)
637	+	}
638	+	return out, rows.Err()
639	+}
640	diff --git a/specstory-cli/pkg/sessionindex/store_test.go b/specstory-cli/pkg/sessionindex/store_test.go
641	new file mode 100644
642	index 0000000..9182a18
643	--- /dev/null
644	+++ b/specstory-cli/pkg/sessionindex/store_test.go
645	@@ -0,0 +1,281 @@
646	+package sessionindex
647	+
648	+import (
649	+	"database/sql"
650	+	"path/filepath"
651	+	"strings"
652	+	"testing"
653	+)
654	+
655	+func newSession(agent, id, projectID, name, body string) Session {
656	+	return Session{
657	+		ProjectID:   projectID,
658	+		ProjectName: "widgets",
659	+		Agent:       agent,
660	+		SessionID:   id,
661	+		CreatedAt:   "2026-06-18T10:00:00Z",
662	+		UpdatedAt:   "2026-06-18T11:00:00Z",
663	+		UserTurns:   3,
664	+		TotalTurns:  9,
665	+		Slug:        "do-the-thing",
666	+		Name:        name,
667	+		NativePath:  "/store/" + id + ".jsonl",
668	+		OriginCwd:   "/repo",
669	+		Size:        1234,
670	+		Mtime:       1700000000000,
671	+		IndexedAt:   "2026-06-18T12:00:00Z",
672	+		Body:        body,
673	+	}
674	+}
675	+
676	+func openTemp(t *testing.T) *Store {
677	+	t.Helper()
678	+	s, err := Open(filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "sessions.db"))
679	+	if err != nil {
680	+		t.Fatalf("Open: %v", err)
681	+	}
682	+	t.Cleanup(func() { _ = s.Close() })
683	+	return s
684	+}
685	+
686	+func TestUpsertAndListByProject(t *testing.T) {
687	+	s := openTemp(t)
688	+
689	+	if err := s.Upsert(newSession("claude", "c1", "proj-a", "Auth refactor", "rewrote the login flow")); err != nil {
690	+		t.Fatal(err)
691	+	}
692	+	if err := s.Upsert(newSession("codex", "x1", "proj-a", "Billing migration", "moved billing to stripe")); err != nil {
693	+		t.Fatal(err)
694	+	}
695	+	if err := s.Upsert(newSession("claude", "c2", "proj-b", "Other project", "unrelated work")); err != nil {
696	+		t.Fatal(err)
697	+	}
698	+
699	+	if n, err := s.Count(); err != nil || n != 3 {
700	+		t.Fatalf("Count = %d, %v; want 3", n, err)
701	+	}
702	+
703	+	got, err := s.ListByProject("proj-a")
704	+	if err != nil {
705	+		t.Fatal(err)
706	+	}
707	+	if len(got) != 2 {
708	+		t.Fatalf("ListByProject(proj-a) = %d rows; want 2", len(got))
709	+	}
710	+	for _, sess := range got {
711	+		if sess.ProjectID != "proj-a" {
712	+			t.Errorf("got project_id %q; want proj-a", sess.ProjectID)
713	+		}
714	+		if sess.UserTurns != 3 || sess.TotalTurns != 9 {
715	+			t.Errorf("turn counts not round-tripped: user=%d total=%d", sess.UserTurns, sess.TotalTurns)
716	+		}
717	+	}
718	+}
719	+
720	+func TestSearchFTS(t *testing.T) {
721	+	s := openTemp(t)
722	+	mustUpsert(t, s, newSession("claude", "c1", "proj-a", "Auth refactor", "rewrote the login flow with oauth"))
723	+	mustUpsert(t, s, newSession("codex", "x1", "proj-a", "Billing migration", "moved billing to stripe webhooks"))
724	+
725	+	// Body match
726	+	hits, err := s.Search("oauth", "")
727	+	if err != nil {
728	+		t.Fatal(err)
729	+	}
730	+	if len(hits) != 1 || hits[0].SessionID != "c1" {
731	+		t.Fatalf("Search(oauth) = %+v; want only c1", hits)
732	+	}
733	+
734	+	// Name match
735	+	hits, err = s.Search("billing", "")
736	+	if err != nil {
737	+		t.Fatal(err)
738	+	}
739	+	if len(hits) != 1 || hits[0].SessionID != "x1" {
740	+		t.Fatalf("Search(billing) = %+v; want only x1", hits)
741	+	}
742	+}
743	+
744	+func TestUpsertIsIdempotent(t *testing.T) {
745	+	s := openTemp(t)
746	+	mustUpsert(t, s, newSession("claude", "c1", "proj-a", "First name", "first body text"))
747	+	// Re-index the same (agent, session_id) with changed content.
748	+	mustUpsert(t, s, newSession("claude", "c1", "proj-a", "Renamed", "second body text"))
749	+
750	+	if n, _ := s.Count(); n != 1 {
751	+		t.Fatalf("Count = %d after re-upsert; want 1 (replaced, not duplicated)", n)
752	+	}
753	+	// Old FTS content must be gone; new content searchable.
754	+	if hits, _ := s.Search("first", ""); len(hits) != 0 {
755	+		t.Errorf("stale FTS row survived re-upsert: %+v", hits)
756	+	}
757	+	if hits, _ := s.Search("second", ""); len(hits) != 1 {
758	+		t.Errorf("new FTS content not searchable after re-upsert")
759	+	}
760	+}
761	+
762	+// TestUpsertHandlesLegacyNullRowid covers the migration window: rows written before fts_rowid
763	+// existed have it NULL. Both the body read and the replace-before-insert must fall back to the
764	+// by-key path for such a row, and a re-index must repopulate fts_rowid without duplicating or
765	+// orphaning the FTS row.
766	+func TestUpsertHandlesLegacyNullRowid(t *testing.T) {
767	+	s := openTemp(t)
768	+	mustUpsert(t, s, newSession("claude", "c1", "proj-a", "Legacy", "old body text"))
769	+
770	+	// Simulate a pre-migration row: clear the fts_rowid link the writer just set.
771	+	if _, err := s.db.Exec(`UPDATE sessions SET fts_rowid = NULL WHERE agent = 'claude' AND session_id = 'c1'`); err != nil {
772	+		t.Fatalf("clear fts_rowid: %v", err)
773	+	}
774	+
775	+	// Body read must fall back to the by-key lookup and still find the body.
776	+	if body, err := s.SessionBody("claude", "c1"); err != nil || body != "old body text" {
777	+		t.Errorf("SessionBody (NULL fts_rowid) = %q, %v; want fallback to old body", body, err)
778	+	}
779	+
780	+	// Re-index the same session: with no fts_rowid to seek, the delete must fall back to the
781	+	// by-key path so the stale FTS row is removed rather than duplicated.
782	+	mustUpsert(t, s, newSession("claude", "c1", "proj-a", "Repopulated", "new body text"))
783	+
784	+	if n, _ := s.Count(); n != 1 {
785	+		t.Fatalf("Count = %d after re-upsert of legacy row; want 1", n)
786	+	}
787	+	if hits, _ := s.Search("old", ""); len(hits) != 0 {
788	+		t.Errorf("stale FTS row survived re-upsert of legacy row: %+v", hits)
789	+	}
790	+	if hits, _ := s.Search("new", ""); len(hits) != 1 {
791	+		t.Errorf("new FTS content not searchable after re-upsert of legacy row")
792	+	}
793	+	// fts_rowid is now repopulated, so the body read takes the fast path again.
794	+	var rowid sql.NullInt64
795	+	if err := s.db.QueryRow(`SELECT fts_rowid FROM sessions WHERE agent = 'claude' AND session_id = 'c1'`).Scan(&rowid); err != nil {
796	+		t.Fatal(err)
797	+	}
798	+	if !rowid.Valid {
799	+		t.Error("fts_rowid still NULL after re-upsert; want repopulated")
800	+	}
801	+	if body, err := s.SessionBody("claude", "c1"); err != nil || body != "new body text" {
802	+		t.Errorf("SessionBody after repopulate = %q, %v; want new body via fast path", body, err)
803	+	}
804	+}
805	+
806	+func mustUpsert(t *testing.T, s *Store, sess Session) {
807	+	t.Helper()
808	+	if err := s.Upsert(sess); err != nil {
809	+		t.Fatalf("Upsert(%s): %v", sess.SessionID, err)
810	+	}
811	+}
812	+
813	+func TestFingerprintsRoundTrip(t *testing.T) {
814	+	s := openTemp(t)
815	+	a := newSession("claude", "c1", "proj-a", "Auth", "body")
816	+	a.Size, a.Mtime, a.IndexVersion = 111, 222, 3
817	+	mustUpsert(t, s, a)
818	+	mustUpsert(t, s, newSession("codex", "x1", "proj-a", "Bill", "body"))
819	+
820	+	fps, err := s.Fingerprints()
821	+	if err != nil {
822	+		t.Fatal(err)
823	+	}
824	+	if len(fps) != 2 {
825	+		t.Fatalf("got %d fingerprints; want 2", len(fps))
826	+	}
827	+	got, ok := fps[FingerprintKey("claude", "c1")]
828	+	if !ok {
829	+		t.Fatal("missing fingerprint for claude/c1")
830	+	}
831	+	if got.Size != 111 || got.Mtime != 222 || got.Version != 3 {
832	+		t.Errorf("fingerprint = %+v; want {111 222 3}", got)
833	+	}
834	+}
835	+
836	+func TestProjectAndUnattributedCounts(t *testing.T) {
837	+	s := openTemp(t)
838	+	mustUpsert(t, s, newSession("claude", "c1", "proj-a", "a", "b"))
839	+	mustUpsert(t, s, newSession("codex", "x1", "proj-a", "a", "b")) // same project
840	+	mustUpsert(t, s, newSession("claude", "c2", "proj-b", "a", "b"))
841	+	mustUpsert(t, s, newSession("cursor", "z1", "unknown", "a", "b"))
842	+	mustUpsert(t, s, newSession("cursor", "z2", "unknown", "a", "b"))
843	+
844	+	if n, err := s.ProjectCount("unknown"); err != nil || n != 2 {
845	+		t.Errorf("ProjectCount = %d, %v; want 2 (proj-a, proj-b)", n, err)
846	+	}
847	+	if n, err := s.UnattributedCount("unknown"); err != nil || n != 2 {
848	+		t.Errorf("UnattributedCount = %d, %v; want 2", n, err)
849	+	}
850	+}
851	+
852	+func TestListProjectsRollup(t *testing.T) {
853	+	s := openTemp(t)
854	+	a := newSession("claude", "c1", "proj-a", "n", "b")
855	+	a.UpdatedAt = "2026-06-18T10:00:00Z"
856	+	mustUpsert(t, s, a)
857	+	b := newSession("codex", "x1", "proj-a", "n", "b")
858	+	b.UpdatedAt = "2026-06-18T12:00:00Z"
859	+	mustUpsert(t, s, b)
860	+	c := newSession("claude", "c2", "proj-b", "n", "b")
861	+	c.UpdatedAt = "2026-06-17T09:00:00Z"
862	+	mustUpsert(t, s, c)
863	+
864	+	projs, err := s.ListProjects()
865	+	if err != nil {
866	+		t.Fatal(err)
867	+	}
868	+	if len(projs) != 2 {
869	+		t.Fatalf("got %d projects; want 2", len(projs))
870	+	}
871	+	// proj-a is most recently active (codex at 12:00) → first.
872	+	if projs[0].ProjectID != "proj-a" {
873	+		t.Errorf("expected proj-a first (most recent), got %s", projs[0].ProjectID)
874	+	}
875	+	if projs[0].Sessions != 2 || projs[0].AgentCounts["claude"] != 1 || projs[0].AgentCounts["codex"] != 1 {
876	+		t.Errorf("proj-a rollup wrong: sessions=%d agents=%v", projs[0].Sessions, projs[0].AgentCounts)
877	+	}
878	+	if projs[0].LastActivity != "2026-06-18T12:00:00Z" {
879	+		t.Errorf("proj-a last activity = %q; want the codex timestamp", projs[0].LastActivity)
880	+	}
881	+}
882	+
883	+func TestSessionBody(t *testing.T) {
884	+	s := openTemp(t)
885	+	mustUpsert(t, s, newSession("claude", "c1", "proj-a", "name", "the full conversation text"))
886	+	body, err := s.SessionBody("claude", "c1")
887	+	if err != nil || body != "the full conversation text" {

889	+	}

892	+		t.Errorf("SessionBody(missing) = %q, %v; want empty", body, err)
893	+	}
894	+}
```

```text
1	// Package sessionindex implements sessions.db — the machine-level index of every coding-agent
2	// session SpecStory knows about, across all projects and providers. It backs the
3	// `specstory resume` selection UX and is (re)built by `specstory reindex`.
4	//
5	// sessions.db is a DERIVED CACHE over the native session stores: it can be deleted and
6	// fully rebuilt at any time. See docs/SESSIONS-DB.md for the design and schema.
7	package sessionindex
8	
9	import (
10		"context"
11		"database/sql"
12		"errors"
13		"fmt"
14		"os"
15		"path/filepath"
16		"sort"
17		"strings"
18	
19		_ "modernc.org/sqlite" // SQLite driver (pure Go), same as pkg/provenance
20	)
21	
22	// Connection-pool sizing. Writes (reindex) must serialize on a single connection to
23	// avoid SQLITE_BUSY/deadlocks. The interactive browse path (resume/search) only reads,
24	// so it opens several connections — WAL permits many concurrent readers, which keeps a
25	// single slow full-text query (a broad prefix can take seconds) from starving the UI.
26	const (
27		writerConns = 1
28		readerConns = 4
29	)
30	
31	// Session is one row of the restore index (the `sessions` table), plus Body, which is
32	// indexed into the FTS table rather than stored on the row. See docs/SESSIONS-DB.md.
33	type Session struct {
34		ProjectID    string // resolved git_id (else workspace_id)
35		ProjectName  string
36		Agent        string // provider id: claude, codex, gemini, droid, deepseek, cursor
37		SessionID    string // native session id (uuid)
38		CreatedAt    string // ISO 8601, first turn
39		UpdatedAt    string // ISO 8601, last activity (last turn, else file mtime)
40		UserTurns    int    // count of user prompts
41		TotalTurns   int    // count of all messages
42		Slug         string
43		Name         string
44		NativePath   string // absolute path the provider opens to read this session
45		OriginCwd    string // working directory the session was launched from
46		Size         int64  // native file size, bytes — part of the freshness fingerprint
47		Mtime        int64  // native file mtime, epoch ms — part of the freshness fingerprint
48		IndexVersion int    // reindex logic version that wrote this row — part of the fingerprint
49		IndexedAt    string // ISO 8601, when reindex last wrote this row
50	
51		// Body is the full conversation text, indexed into sessions_fts (not persisted on
52		// the sessions row). Left empty on rows read back out of the index.
53		Body string
54	
55		// IsNew is a write-time hint, not a persisted column: when true, the caller has
56		// determined this (agent, session_id) has no existing index row, so upsertOne can skip
57		// looking up and deleting a prior FTS row before insert. Leave false when unsure — the
58		// lookup-and-delete is then kept, which is always correct (a missing prior row is a no-op).
59		IsNew bool
60	}
61	
62	// Fingerprint identifies an indexed session's freshness: the native file's size and
63	// mtime, plus the reindex logic version that produced the row. reindex skips a session
64	// whose fingerprint is unchanged. See docs/SESSIONS-DB.md.
65	type Fingerprint struct {
66		Size    int64
67		Mtime   int64
68		Version int
69	}
70	
71	// Store is a handle to sessions.db.
72	type Store struct {
73		db *sql.DB
74	}
75	
76	// DefaultPath returns ~/.specstory/sessions.db.
77	func DefaultPath() (string, error) {
78		home, err := os.UserHomeDir()
79		if err != nil {
80			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to resolve home directory: %w", err)
81		}
82		return filepath.Join(home, ".specstory", "sessions.db"), nil
83	}
84	
85	// Open opens (or creates) sessions.db for writing — a single serialized connection,
86	// applying WAL + performance pragmas (matching pkg/provenance) and ensuring the schema
87	// exists. Use OpenReader for the read-only interactive browse path.
88	func Open(path string) (*Store, error) {
89		return openWith(path, writerConns)
90	}
91	
92	// OpenReader opens sessions.db for the interactive browse path (resume/search), which
93	// only reads. It allows several concurrent connections so one slow full-text query can't
94	// starve the UI (WAL permits many simultaneous readers). Never write through this handle.
95	func OpenReader(path string) (*Store, error) {
96		return openWith(path, readerConns)
97	}
98	
99	func openWith(path string, maxConns int) (*Store, error) {
100		if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(path), 0o755); err != nil {
101			return nil, fmt.Errorf("creating database directory: %w", err)
102		}
103	
104		dsn := fmt.Sprintf("file:%s?_pragma=busy_timeout=15000&_pragma=journal_mode(WAL)", filepath.ToSlash(path))
105		db, err := sql.Open("sqlite", dsn)
106		if err != nil {
107			return nil, fmt.Errorf("opening sessions.db: %w", err)
108		}
109	
110		db.SetMaxOpenConns(maxConns)
111		db.SetMaxIdleConns(maxConns)
112	
113		for _, p := range []string{
114			"PRAGMA synchronous=NORMAL",
115			"PRAGMA cache_size = -64000",
116			"PRAGMA temp_store = MEMORY",
117			"PRAGMA mmap_size = 268435456",
118			"PRAGMA page_size = 8192",
119		} {
120			if _, err := db.Exec(p); err != nil {
121				_ = db.Close()
122				return nil, fmt.Errorf("setting pragma %q: %w", p, err)
123			}
124		}
125	
126		s := &Store{db: db}
127		if err := s.ensureSchema(); err != nil {
128			_ = db.Close()
129			return nil, fmt.Errorf("ensuring schema: %w", err)
130		}
131		return s, nil
132	}
133	
134	// Close closes the database connection.
135	func (s *Store) Close() error {
136		return s.db.Close()
137	}
138	
139	// sessionColumns is the canonical sessions column list, shared by every SELECT so the
140	// scan order in scanSession stays in lockstep with it.
141	const sessionColumns = `project_id, project_name, agent, session_id, created_at, updated_at,
142		user_turns, total_turns, slug, name, native_path, origin_cwd, size, mtime, index_version, indexed_at`
143	
144	// sessionInsertColumns is sessionColumns plus fts_rowid, the internal link to the session's
145	// FTS row. fts_rowid is write-only — set on insert, read only by SessionBody's join — so it is
146	// deliberately kept out of sessionColumns and the Session struct rather than threaded through
147	// every read path.
148	const sessionInsertColumns = sessionColumns + `, fts_rowid`
149	
150	func (s *Store) ensureSchema() error {
151		schema := `
152		CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sessions (
153			project_id   TEXT NOT NULL,
154			project_name TEXT,
155			agent        TEXT NOT NULL,
156			session_id   TEXT NOT NULL,
157			created_at   TEXT,
158			updated_at   TEXT,
159			user_turns   INTEGER,
160			total_turns  INTEGER,
161			slug         TEXT,
162			name         TEXT,
163			native_path  TEXT,
164			origin_cwd   TEXT,
165			size          INTEGER,
166			mtime         INTEGER,
167			index_version INTEGER,
168			indexed_at    TEXT,
169			fts_rowid     INTEGER,
170			PRIMARY KEY (agent, session_id)
171		);
172		-- Composite over the project filter PLUS the picker's sort order. ListByProject
173		-- (the resume picker's hot path) filters by project_id and orders by
174		-- updated_at DESC, created_at DESC; this index serves both, so SQLite walks it
175		-- backward instead of filtering then sorting in a temp b-tree. project_id is the
176		-- left prefix, so ProjectCount/UnattributedCount still use it. Replaces the old
177		-- single-column idx_sessions_project (dropped in the migration below).
178		CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_sessions_project_recent ON sessions(project_id, updated_at, created_at);
179	
180		-- Standalone FTS5 index over the conversation body + name. session_id/agent ride
181		-- along UNINDEXED as join keys back to the sessions row. See docs/SESSIONS-DB.md.
182		CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sessions_fts USING fts5(
183			session_id UNINDEXED,
184			agent UNINDEXED,
185			name,
186			body
187		);
188		`
189		if _, err := s.db.Exec(schema); err != nil {
190			return err
191		}
192		// Migration for indexes created before index_version existed (the column is part of
193		// the freshness fingerprint). Ignore the error when it already exists.
194		_, _ = s.db.Exec(`ALTER TABLE sessions ADD COLUMN index_version INTEGER DEFAULT 0`)
195		// fts_rowid links a session row to its sessions_fts row, so the body read and the
196		// delete-before-insert are O(1) rowid lookups instead of whole-FTS scans (session_id/agent
197		// are UNINDEXED). NULL on rows written before this column existed; a reindex (reindexVersion
198		// bump) repopulates it. Ignore the error when the column already exists.
199		_, _ = s.db.Exec(`ALTER TABLE sessions ADD COLUMN fts_rowid INTEGER`)
200		// Drop the old single-column project index now superseded by the composite
201		// idx_sessions_project_recent (project_id is its left prefix). Idempotent; a no-op
202		// on fresh databases that never had it.
203		_, _ = s.db.Exec(`DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_sessions_project`)
204		return nil
205	}
206	
207	// Fingerprints returns the freshness fingerprint of every indexed session, keyed by
208	// fingerprintKey(agent, session_id). reindex loads this once and skips any session
209	// whose native file is unchanged (same size + mtime) and was indexed by the current
210	// logic version.
211	func (s *Store) Fingerprints() (map[string]Fingerprint, error) {
212		rows, err := s.db.Query(`SELECT agent, session_id, size, mtime, index_version FROM sessions`)
213		if err != nil {
214			return nil, err
215		}
216		defer func() { _ = rows.Close() }()
217	
218		out := make(map[string]Fingerprint)
219		for rows.Next() {
220			var agent, sessionID string
221			var fp Fingerprint
222			if err := rows.Scan(&agent, &sessionID, &fp.Size, &fp.Mtime, &fp.Version); err != nil {
223				return nil, err
224			}
225			out[FingerprintKey(agent, sessionID)] = fp
226		}
227		return out, rows.Err()
228	}
229	
230	// FingerprintKey is the map key for a session's fingerprint: agent + NUL + session_id.
231	func FingerprintKey(agent, sessionID string) string {
232		return agent + "\x00" + sessionID
233	}
234	
235	// sessionUpsertStmts are the per-row statements UpsertBatch prepares once and reuses across
236	// every row in a transaction, so the SQL is parsed once per batch instead of once per row.
237	type sessionUpsertStmts struct {
238		insSession    *sql.Stmt
239		selOldRowid   *sql.Stmt
240		delFTSByRowid *sql.Stmt
241		delFTSByKey   *sql.Stmt
242		insFTS        *sql.Stmt
243	}
244	
245	// upsertOne writes one session's row and full-text row using the batch's prepared statements.
246	// FTS5 standalone tables are not auto-synced, so the FTS row is maintained by hand. The new FTS
247	// row is inserted first so its rowid can be stored on the sessions row (fts_rowid) — that link
248	// is what makes later body reads and replace-deletes O(1) rowid lookups instead of whole-FTS
249	// scans (session_id/agent are UNINDEXED). For an existing session the prior FTS row is removed
250	// first; brand-new sessions (sess.IsNew) skip that lookup-and-delete entirely.
251	func upsertOne(st sessionUpsertStmts, sess Session) error {
252		if !sess.IsNew {
253			if err := deleteOldFTSRow(st, sess); err != nil {
254				return err
255			}
256		}
257		res, err := st.insFTS.Exec(sess.SessionID, sess.Agent, sess.Name, sess.Body)
258		if err != nil {
259			return fmt.Errorf("insert fts row: %w", err)
260		}
261		ftsRowid, err := res.LastInsertId()
262		if err != nil {
263			return fmt.Errorf("read fts rowid: %w", err)
264		}
265		if _, err := st.insSession.Exec(
266			sess.ProjectID, sess.ProjectName, sess.Agent, sess.SessionID, sess.CreatedAt, sess.UpdatedAt,
267			sess.UserTurns, sess.TotalTurns, sess.Slug, sess.Name, sess.NativePath, sess.OriginCwd,
268			sess.Size, sess.Mtime, sess.IndexVersion, sess.IndexedAt, ftsRowid); err != nil {
269			return fmt.Errorf("upsert session row: %w", err)
270		}
271		return nil
272	}
273	
274	// deleteOldFTSRow removes an existing session's current FTS row before the sessions row is
275	// replaced (INSERT OR REPLACE would otherwise drop the fts_rowid link and orphan the FTS row).
276	// It resolves the row by its stored fts_rowid (O(1)); for rows written before fts_rowid existed
277	// (NULL) it falls back to the by-key delete — a whole-FTS scan that a reindex retires by
278	// repopulating fts_rowid.
279	func deleteOldFTSRow(st sessionUpsertStmts, sess Session) error {
280		var oldRowid sql.NullInt64
281		switch err := st.selOldRowid.QueryRow(sess.Agent, sess.SessionID).Scan(&oldRowid); {
282		case errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows):
283			return nil // no prior row to remove
284		case err != nil:
285			return fmt.Errorf("look up old fts rowid: %w", err)
286		}
287		if oldRowid.Valid {
288			if _, err := st.delFTSByRowid.Exec(oldRowid.Int64); err != nil {
289				return fmt.Errorf("clear fts row by rowid: %w", err)
290			}
291			return nil
292		}
293		if _, err := st.delFTSByKey.Exec(sess.Agent, sess.SessionID); err != nil {
294			return fmt.Errorf("clear legacy fts row: %w", err)
295		}
296		return nil
297	}
298	
299	// prepareUpsert prepares the per-row statements on tx and returns them with a closer.
300	func prepareUpsert(tx *sql.Tx) (sessionUpsertStmts, func(), error) {
301		var prepared []*sql.Stmt
302		closer := func() {
303			for _, s := range prepared {
304				_ = s.Close()
305			}
306		}
307		prep := func(what, query string) (*sql.Stmt, error) {
308			s, err := tx.Prepare(query)
309			if err != nil {
310				closer()
311				return nil, fmt.Errorf("prepare %s: %w", what, err)
312			}
313			prepared = append(prepared, s)
314			return s, nil
315		}
316	
317		insSession, err := prep("session upsert", `INSERT OR REPLACE INTO sessions (`+sessionInsertColumns+`)
318			VALUES (?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?)`)
319		if err != nil {
320			return sessionUpsertStmts{}, func() {}, err
321		}
322		selOldRowid, err := prep("fts rowid lookup", `SELECT fts_rowid FROM sessions WHERE agent = ? AND session_id = ?`)
323		if err != nil {
324			return sessionUpsertStmts{}, func() {}, err
325		}
326		delFTSByRowid, err := prep("fts delete by rowid", `DELETE FROM sessions_fts WHERE rowid = ?`)
327		if err != nil {
328			return sessionUpsertStmts{}, func() {}, err
329		}
330		delFTSByKey, err := prep("fts delete by key", `DELETE FROM sessions_fts WHERE agent = ? AND session_id = ?`)
331		if err != nil {
332			return sessionUpsertStmts{}, func() {}, err
333		}
334		insFTS, err := prep("fts insert", `INSERT INTO sessions_fts (session_id, agent, name, body) VALUES (?,?,?,?)`)
335		if err != nil {
336			return sessionUpsertStmts{}, func() {}, err
337		}
338		return sessionUpsertStmts{
339			insSession:    insSession,
340			selOldRowid:   selOldRowid,
341			delFTSByRowid: delFTSByRowid,
342			delFTSByKey:   delFTSByKey,
343			insFTS:        insFTS,
344		}, closer, nil
345	}
346	
347	// Upsert inserts or replaces a single session row and its full-text row, atomically. A
348	// re-index of the same session (same agent + session_id) replaces both in lockstep.
349	func (s *Store) Upsert(sess Session) error {
350		return s.UpsertBatch([]Session{sess})
351	}
352	
353	// UpsertBatch writes many sessions in one transaction — the write path for `reindex`,
354	// which batches to avoid thousands of tiny WAL commits.
355	func (s *Store) UpsertBatch(sessions []Session) error {
356		if len(sessions) == 0 {
357			return nil
358		}
359		tx, err := s.db.Begin()
360		if err != nil {
361			return fmt.Errorf("begin upsert: %w", err)
362		}
363		committed := false
364		defer func() {
365			if !committed {
366				_ = tx.Rollback()
367			}
368		}()
369	
370		st, closeStmts, err := prepareUpsert(tx)
371		if err != nil {
372			return err
373		}
374		defer closeStmts()
375	
376		for _, sess := range sessions {
377			if err := upsertOne(st, sess); err != nil {
378				return err
379			}
380		}
381	
382		if err := tx.Commit(); err != nil {
383			return fmt.Errorf("commit upsert: %w", err)
384		}
385		committed = true
386		return nil
387	}
388	
389	// Exists reports whether a session row is already indexed, looked up by the primary key
390	// (agent, session_id) so it stays O(log n) instead of scanning. Because a session's sessions
391	// row and its sessions_fts row are always written together (upsertOne, one transaction), a
392	// missing sessions row guarantees a missing FTS row — which lets the live writer set
393	// Session.IsNew and skip the whole-table FTS delete for genuinely new sessions.
394	func (s *Store) Exists(agent, sessionID string) (bool, error) {
395		var one int
396		err := s.db.QueryRow(`SELECT 1 FROM sessions WHERE agent = ? AND session_id = ?`, agent, sessionID).Scan(&one)
397		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
398			return false, nil
399		}
400		if err != nil {
401			return false, err
402		}
403		return true, nil
404	}
405	
406	// Count returns the number of indexed sessions.
407	func (s *Store) Count() (int, error) {
408		var n int
409		if err := s.db.QueryRow(`SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sessions`).Scan(&n); err != nil {
410			return 0, err
411		}
412		return n, nil
413	}
414	
415	// ProjectCount returns the number of distinct attributed projects in the index
416	// (excluding the unknownID bucket).
417	func (s *Store) ProjectCount(unknownID string) (int, error) {
418		var n int
419		err := s.db.QueryRow(`SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT project_id) FROM sessions WHERE project_id != ?`, unknownID).Scan(&n)
420		return n, err
421	}
422	
423	// UnattributedCount returns the number of sessions in the unknownID bucket.
424	func (s *Store) UnattributedCount(unknownID string) (int, error) {
425		var n int
426		err := s.db.QueryRow(`SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sessions WHERE project_id = ?`, unknownID).Scan(&n)
427		return n, err
428	}
429	
430	// ListByProject returns a project's sessions, newest activity first. Body is not
431	// populated (it lives only in the FTS index). Used by the `specstory resume` picker.
432	func (s *Store) ListByProject(projectID string) ([]Session, error) {
433		rows, err := s.db.Query(`SELECT `+sessionColumns+`
434			FROM sessions WHERE project_id = ? ORDER BY updated_at DESC, created_at DESC`, projectID)
435		if err != nil {
436			return nil, err
437		}
438		defer func() { _ = rows.Close() }()
439		return scanSessions(rows)
440	}
441	
442	// ProjectSummary is a rolled-up view of one project for the all-projects picker.
443	type ProjectSummary struct {
444		ProjectID    string
445		ProjectName  string
446		Sessions     int            // total sessions in the project
447		LastActivity string         // most recent updated_at across the project
448		AgentCounts  map[string]int // sessions per agent (claude, codex, …)
449	}
450	
451	// ListProjects returns one rolled-up summary per project, most recently active first.
452	// Used by the all-projects view (date-bucketed). The unknown-project bucket is included;
453	// the caller decides how to present it.
454	func (s *Store) ListProjects() ([]ProjectSummary, error) {
455		rows, err := s.db.Query(`SELECT project_id, project_name, agent, COUNT(*), MAX(updated_at)
456			FROM sessions GROUP BY project_id, agent`)
457		if err != nil {
458			return nil, err
459		}
460		defer func() { _ = rows.Close() }()
461	
462		byID := map[string]*ProjectSummary{}
463		for rows.Next() {
464			var pid, pname, agent, last string
465			var n int
466			if err := rows.Scan(&pid, &pname, &agent, &n, &last); err != nil {
467				return nil, err
468			}
469			ps, ok := byID[pid]
470			if !ok {
471				ps = &ProjectSummary{ProjectID: pid, ProjectName: pname, AgentCounts: map[string]int{}}
472				byID[pid] = ps
473			}
474			ps.Sessions += n
475			ps.AgentCounts[agent] += n
476			if ps.ProjectName == "" {
477				ps.ProjectName = pname
478			}
479			if last > ps.LastActivity {
480				ps.LastActivity = last
481			}
482		}
483		if err := rows.Err(); err != nil {
484			return nil, err
485		}
486	
487		out := make([]ProjectSummary, 0, len(byID))
488		for _, ps := range byID {
489			out = append(out, *ps)
490		}
491		sort.Slice(out, func(i, j int) bool { return out[i].LastActivity > out[j].LastActivity })
492		return out, nil
493	}
494	
495	// SessionBody returns the full-text conversation body for a session (for the preview
496	// pane), or "" if the session has no indexed body (e.g. Cursor, metadata-only).
497	func (s *Store) SessionBody(agent, sessionID string) (string, error) {
498		var body string
499		// Fast path: resolve the FTS row by the rowid stored on the sessions row (O(1)), instead of
500		// scanning the whole FTS (session_id/agent are UNINDEXED).
501		err := s.db.QueryRow(`SELECT f.body FROM sessions s
502			JOIN sessions_fts f ON f.rowid = s.fts_rowid
503			WHERE s.agent = ? AND s.session_id = ?`, agent, sessionID).Scan(&body)
504		if err == nil {
505			return body, nil
506		}
507		if !errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
508			return "", err
509		}
510		// No fts_rowid link (a row written before the column existed). Fall back to the by-key scan;
511		// a reindex repopulates fts_rowid and retires this path.
512		err = s.db.QueryRow(`SELECT body FROM sessions_fts WHERE agent = ? AND session_id = ?`,
513			agent, sessionID).Scan(&body)
514		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
515			return "", nil
516		}
517		return body, err
518	}
519	
520	// Search runs a full-text query and returns matching sessions, most recent first.
521	// projectID scopes the search to one project; "" searches across all projects.
522	func (s *Store) Search(query, projectID string) ([]Session, error) {
523		return s.SearchContext(context.Background(), query, projectID)
524	}
525	
526	// SearchContext is Search bound to a context. The interactive search cancels the context
527	// when a newer keystroke arrives, which aborts a slow in-flight query and frees its
528	// connection. Snippets are deliberately fetched separately for only the visible rows:
529	// FTS5 snippet generation over hundreds of full transcripts dominates broad searches.
530	func (s *Store) SearchContext(ctx context.Context, query, projectID string) ([]Session, error) {
531		q := `SELECT ` + prefixed("s", sessionColumns) + `
532			FROM sessions_fts
533			JOIN sessions s ON s.agent = sessions_fts.agent AND s.session_id = sessions_fts.session_id
534			WHERE sessions_fts MATCH ?`
535		args := []any{query}
536		if projectID != "" {
537			q += ` AND s.project_id = ?`
538			args = append(args, projectID)
539		}
540		// Order by recency, not FTS5's BM25 rank. For full-conversation transcripts BM25 is
541		// noisy (length-normalization dominates) and a single-term query gets no IDF signal,
542		// so relevance order looks arbitrary; newest-first is what's useful when browsing your
543		// own history. It also makes LIMIT keep the 500 most RECENT matches (vs the 500 densest)
544		// and skips BM25 scoring entirely. updated_at is ISO 8601, so TEXT sort = chronological.
545		q += ` ORDER BY s.updated_at DESC LIMIT 500`
546	
547		rows, err := s.db.QueryContext(ctx, q, args...)
548		if err != nil {
549			return nil, err
550		}
551		defer func() { _ = rows.Close() }()
552	
553		return scanSessions(rows)
554	}
555	
556	// Snippets returns highlighted match snippets for the provided sessions. The result is
557	// keyed by FingerprintKey(agent, session_id). Matched terms are wrapped in \x02 and \x03
558	// (STX/ETX) so callers can highlight without colliding with conversation text.
559	func (s *Store) Snippets(query string, sessions []Session) (map[string]string, error) {
560		return s.SnippetsContext(context.Background(), query, sessions)
561	}
562	
563	// SnippetsContext is Snippets bound to a context. Callers should pass only the currently
564	// visible rows; snippet() is intentionally lazy because it is the expensive part of FTS
565	// search on long transcripts.
566	func (s *Store) SnippetsContext(ctx context.Context, query string, sessions []Session) (map[string]string, error) {
567		out := map[string]string{}
568		if query == "" || len(sessions) == 0 {
569			return out, nil
570		}
571	
572		clauses := make([]string, 0, len(sessions))
573		args := []any{query}
574		seen := map[string]bool{}
575		for _, sess := range sessions {
576			key := FingerprintKey(sess.Agent, sess.SessionID)
577			if seen[key] {
578				continue
579			}
580			seen[key] = true
581			clauses = append(clauses, `(agent = ? AND session_id = ?)`)
582			args = append(args, sess.Agent, sess.SessionID)
583		}
584		if len(clauses) == 0 {
585			return out, nil
586		}
587	
588		q := `SELECT agent, session_id, snippet(sessions_fts, 3, char(2), char(3), '…', 12)
589			FROM sessions_fts
590			WHERE sessions_fts MATCH ? AND (` + strings.Join(clauses, ` OR `) + `)`
591		rows, err := s.db.QueryContext(ctx, q, args...)
592		if err != nil {
593			return nil, err
594		}
595		defer func() { _ = rows.Close() }()
596	
597		for rows.Next() {
598			var agent, sessionID, snippet string
599			if err := rows.Scan(&agent, &sessionID, &snippet); err != nil {
600				return nil, err
601			}
602			out[FingerprintKey(agent, sessionID)] = snippet
603		}
604		return out, rows.Err()
605	}
606	
607	// prefixed qualifies each comma-separated column in cols with the given table alias,
608	// e.g. prefixed("s", "a, b") -> "s.a, s.b". Used to disambiguate the sessions columns
609	// in the FTS join (where session_id/agent also exist on the FTS table).
610	func prefixed(alias, cols string) string {
611		parts := strings.Split(cols, ",")
612		for i, p := range parts {
613			parts[i] = alias + "." + strings.TrimSpace(p)
614		}
615		return strings.Join(parts, ", ")
616	}
617	
618	// scanSessions scans rows selected with sessionColumns (in order) into Sessions.
619	func scanSessions(rows *sql.Rows) ([]Session, error) {
620		var out []Session
621		for rows.Next() {
622			var s Session
623			if err := rows.Scan(
624				&s.ProjectID, &s.ProjectName, &s.Agent, &s.SessionID, &s.CreatedAt, &s.UpdatedAt,
625				&s.UserTurns, &s.TotalTurns, &s.Slug, &s.Name, &s.NativePath, &s.OriginCwd,
626				&s.Size, &s.Mtime, &s.IndexVersion, &s.IndexedAt,
627			); err != nil {
628				return nil, err
629			}
630			out = append(out, s)
631		}
632		return out, rows.Err()
633	}
634
```

```text
10	func newSession(agent, id, projectID, name, body string) Session {
11		return Session{
12			ProjectID:   projectID,
13			ProjectName: "widgets",
14			Agent:       agent,
15			SessionID:   id,
16			CreatedAt:   "2026-06-18T10:00:00Z",
17			UpdatedAt:   "2026-06-18T11:00:00Z",
18			UserTurns:   3,
19			TotalTurns:  9,

21			Name:        name,
22			NativePath:  "/store/" + id + ".jsonl",
23			OriginCwd:   "/repo",
24			Size:        1234,
25			Mtime:       1700000000000,
26			IndexedAt:   "2026-06-18T12:00:00Z",
27			Body:        body,
28		}

31	func openTemp(t *testing.T) *Store {
32		t.Helper()
33		s, err := Open(filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "sessions.db"))
34		if err != nil {
35			t.Fatalf("Open: %v", err)
36		}
37		t.Cleanup(func() { _ = s.Close() })
38		return s
39	}
40	
41	func TestUpsertAndListByProject(t *testing.T) {
42		s := openTemp(t)
43	
44		if err := s.Upsert(newSession("claude", "c1", "proj-a", "Auth refactor", "rewrote the login flow")); err != nil {
45			t.Fatal(err)
46		}
47		if err := s.Upsert(newSession("codex", "x1", "proj-a", "Billing migration", "moved billing to stripe")); err != nil {
48			t.Fatal(err)
49		}
50		if err := s.Upsert(newSession("claude", "c2", "proj-b", "Other project", "unrelated work")); err != nil {
51			t.Fatal(err)
52		}
53	
54		if n, err := s.Count(); err != nil || n != 3 {
55			t.Fatalf("Count = %d, %v; want 3", n, err)
56		}
57	
58		got, err := s.ListByProject("proj-a")
59		if err != nil {
60			t.Fatal(err)
61		}
62		if len(got) != 2 {
63			t.Fatalf("ListByProject(proj-a) = %d rows; want 2", len(got))
64		}
65		for _, sess := range got {
66			if sess.ProjectID != "proj-a" {
67				t.Errorf("got project_id %q; want proj-a", sess.ProjectID)
68			}
69			if sess.UserTurns != 3 || sess.TotalTurns != 9 {
70				t.Errorf("turn counts not round-tripped: user=%d total=%d", sess.UserTurns, sess.TotalTurns)
71			}
72		}
73	}
74	
75	func TestSearchFTS(t *testing.T) {
76		s := openTemp(t)
77		mustUpsert(t, s, newSession("claude", "c1", "proj-a", "Auth refactor", "rewrote the login flow with oauth"))
78		mustUpsert(t, s, newSession("codex", "x1", "proj-a", "Billing migration", "moved billing to stripe webhooks"))
79	
80		// Body match
81		hits, err := s.Search("oauth", "")
82		if err != nil {
83			t.Fatal(err)
84		}
85		if len(hits) != 1 || hits[0].SessionID != "c1" {
86			t.Fatalf("Search(oauth) = %+v; want only c1", hits)
87		}
88	
89		// Name match
90		hits, err = s.Search("billing", "")
91		if err != nil {
92			t.Fatal(err)
93		}
94		if len(hits) != 1 || hits[0].SessionID != "x1" {
95			t.Fatalf("Search(billing) = %+v; want only x1", hits)
96		}
97	}
98	
99	func TestUpsertIsIdempotent(t *testing.T) {
100		s := openTemp(t)
101		mustUpsert(t, s, newSession("claude", "c1", "proj-a", "First name", "first body text"))
102		// Re-index the same (agent, session_id) with changed content.
103		mustUpsert(t, s, newSession("claude", "c1", "proj-a", "Renamed", "second body text"))
104	
105		if n, _ := s.Count(); n != 1 {
106			t.Fatalf("Count = %d after re-upsert; want 1 (replaced, not duplicated)", n)
107		}
108		// Old FTS content must be gone; new content searchable.
109		if hits, _ := s.Search("first", ""); len(hits) != 0 {
110			t.Errorf("stale FTS row survived re-upsert: %+v", hits)
111		}
112		if hits, _ := s.Search("second", ""); len(hits) != 1 {
113			t.Errorf("new FTS content not searchable after re-upsert")
114		}
115	}
116	
117	// TestUpsertHandlesLegacyNullRowid covers the migration window: rows written before fts_rowid
118	// existed have it NULL. Both the body read and the replace-before-insert must fall back to the
119	// by-key path for such a row, and a re-index must repopulate fts_rowid without duplicating or
120	// orphaning the FTS row.
121	func TestUpsertHandlesLegacyNullRowid(t *testing.T) {
122		s := openTemp(t)
123		mustUpsert(t, s, newSession("claude", "c1", "proj-a", "Legacy", "old body text"))
124	
125		// Simulate a pre-migration row: clear the fts_rowid link the writer just set.
126		if _, err := s.db.Exec(`UPDATE sessions SET fts_rowid = NULL WHERE agent = 'claude' AND session_id = 'c1'`); err != nil {
127			t.Fatalf("clear fts_rowid: %v", err)
128		}
129	
130		// Body read must fall back to the by-key lookup and still find the body.
131		if body, err := s.SessionBody("claude", "c1"); err != nil || body != "old body text" {
132			t.Errorf("SessionBody (NULL fts_rowid) = %q, %v; want fallback to old body", body, err)
133		}
134	
135		// Re-index the same session: with no fts_rowid to seek, the delete must fall back to the
136		// by-key path so the stale FTS row is removed rather than duplicated.
137		mustUpsert(t, s, newSession("claude", "c1", "proj-a", "Repopulated", "new body text"))
138	
139		if n, _ := s.Count(); n != 1 {
140			t.Fatalf("Count = %d after re-upsert of legacy row; want 1", n)
141		}
142		if hits, _ := s.Search("old", ""); len(hits) != 0 {
143			t.Errorf("stale FTS row survived re-upsert of legacy row: %+v", hits)
144		}
145		if hits, _ := s.Search("new", ""); len(hits) != 1 {
146			t.Errorf("new FTS content not searchable after re-upsert of legacy row")
147		}
148		// fts_rowid is now repopulated, so the body read takes the fast path again.
149		var rowid sql.NullInt64
150		if err := s.db.QueryRow(`SELECT fts_rowid FROM sessions WHERE agent = 'claude' AND session_id = 'c1'`).Scan(&rowid); err != nil {
151			t.Fatal(err)
152		}
153		if !rowid.Valid {
154			t.Error("fts_rowid still NULL after re-upsert; want repopulated")
155		}
156		if body, err := s.SessionBody("claude", "c1"); err != nil || body != "new body text" {
157			t.Errorf("SessionBody after repopulate = %q, %v; want new body via fast path", body, err)
158		}
159	}
160	
161	func mustUpsert(t *testing.T, s *Store, sess Session) {
162		t.Helper()
163		if err := s.Upsert(sess); err != nil {
164			t.Fatalf("Upsert(%s): %v", sess.SessionID, err)
165		}
166	}
167

171		a.Size, a.Mtime, a.IndexVersion = 111, 222, 3
172		mustUpsert(t, s, a)

181		}

183		if !ok {
184			t.Fatal("missing fingerprint for claude/c1")
185		}
186		if got.Size != 111 || got.Mtime != 222 || got.Version != 3 {
187			t.Errorf("fingerprint = %+v; want {111 222 3}", got)
188		}
189	}

191	func TestProjectAndUnattributedCounts(t *testing.T) {
192		s := openTemp(t)
193		mustUpsert(t, s, newSession("claude", "c1", "proj-a", "a", "b"))
194		mustUpsert(t, s, newSession("codex", "x1", "proj-a", "a", "b")) // same project
195		mustUpsert(t, s, newSession("claude", "c2", "proj-b", "a", "b"))
196		mustUpsert(t, s, newSession("cursor", "z1", "unknown", "a", "b"))
197		mustUpsert(t, s, newSession("cursor", "z2", "unknown", "a", "b"))
198	
199		if n, err := s.ProjectCount("unknown"); err != nil || n != 2 {
200			t.Errorf("ProjectCount = %d, %v; want 2 (proj-a, proj-b)", n, err)
201		}
202		if n, err := s.UnattributedCount("unknown"); err != nil || n != 2 {
203			t.Errorf("UnattributedCount = %d, %v; want 2", n, err)
204		}
205	}
206	
207	func TestListProjectsRollup(t *testing.T) {
208		s := openTemp(t)
209		a := newSession("claude", "c1", "proj-a", "n", "b")
210		a.UpdatedAt = "2026-06-18T10:00:00Z"
211		mustUpsert(t, s, a)
212		b := newSession("codex", "x1", "proj-a", "n", "b")
213		b.UpdatedAt = "2026-06-18T12:00:00Z"
214		mustUpsert(t, s, b)
215		c := newSession("claude", "c2", "proj-b", "n", "b")
216		c.UpdatedAt = "2026-06-17T09:00:00Z"
217		mustUpsert(t, s, c)
218	
219		projs, err := s.ListProjects()
220		if err != nil {
221			t.Fatal(err)
222		}
223		if len(projs) != 2 {
224			t.Fatalf("got %d projects; want 2", len(projs))
225		}
226		// proj-a is most recently active (codex at 12:00) → first.
227		if projs[0].ProjectID != "proj-a" {
228			t.Errorf("expected proj-a first (most recent), got %s", projs[0].ProjectID)
229		}
230		if projs[0].Sessions != 2 || projs[0].AgentCounts["claude"] != 1 || projs[0].AgentCounts["codex"] != 1 {
231			t.Errorf("proj-a rollup wrong: sessions=%d agents=%v", projs[0].Sessions, projs[0].AgentCounts)
232		}
233		if projs[0].LastActivity != "2026-06-18T12:00:00Z" {
234			t.Errorf("proj-a last activity = %q; want the codex timestamp", projs[0].LastActivity)
235		}
236	}
237	
238	func TestSessionBody(t *testing.T) {
239		s := openTemp(t)
240		mustUpsert(t, s, newSession("claude", "c1", "proj-a", "name", "the full conversation text"))
241		body, err := s.SessionBody("claude", "c1")
242		if err != nil || body != "the full conversation text" {
243			t.Errorf("SessionBody = %q, %v; want the body", body, err)
244		}
245		// Missing session → empty, no error.
246		if body, err := s.SessionBody("claude", "nope"); err != nil || body != "" {
247			t.Errorf("SessionBody(missing) = %q, %v; want empty", body, err)
248		}
249	}
250	
251	func TestSearchAndSnippets(t *testing.T) {
252		s := openTemp(t)
253		mustUpsert(t, s, newSession("claude", "c1", "proj-a", "Auth", "we rewrote the login flow with oauth tokens today"))
254		mustUpsert(t, s, newSession("codex", "x1", "proj-b", "Bill", "billing moved to stripe"))
255	
256		// Scoped to proj-a finds c1; snippets are fetched lazily for the visible rows.
257		hits, err := s.Search("oauth*", "proj-a")
258		if err != nil {
259			t.Fatal(err)
260		}
261		if len(hits) != 1 || hits[0].SessionID != "c1" {
262			t.Fatalf("scoped search = %+v; want only c1", hits)
263		}
264		snips, err := s.Snippets("oauth*", hits)
265		if err != nil {
266			t.Fatal(err)
267		}
268		snip := snips[FingerprintKey("claude", "c1")]
269		if !strings.Contains(snip, "\x02oauth\x03") {
270			t.Errorf("snippet missing marked match: %q", snip)
271		}
272	
273		// Scoping excludes other projects.
274		if hits, _ := s.Search("oauth*", "proj-b"); len(hits) != 0 {
275			t.Errorf("proj-b should not match oauth: %+v", hits)
276		}
277		// Empty projectID searches everything.
278		if hits, _ := s.Search("stripe*", ""); len(hits) != 1 || hits[0].SessionID != "x1" {
279			t.Errorf("global search for stripe = %+v; want x1", hits)
280		}
281	}
282
```

```text
+// Best-effort UI writers. The destination is the user's terminal, so write
+// errors are not actionable and are intentionally ignored.
+func fprintf(w io.Writer, format string, a ...any) { _, _ = fmt.Fprintf(w, format, a...) }
+func fprintln(w io.Writer, a ...any)               { _, _ = fmt.Fprintln(w, a...) }
+func fprint(w io.Writer, a ...any)                 { _, _ = fmt.Fprint(w, a...) }
+
+// resumePlan captures the user's choices from the interactive selection.
+type resumePlan struct {
+	from      spi.Provider
+	fromID    string
+	sessionID string
+	// fromCwd is the directory the source session was originally launched from
+	// (the index's origin_cwd). It can differ from the user's current cwd when a
+	// session is picked from another project via the all-projects browser. The
+	// source data load must use this, because some providers (e.g. Codex) locate a
+	// session by matching its recorded cwd — looking under the current cwd would
+	// find nothing. Empty for older index rows; callers fall back to the current cwd.
+	fromCwd string
```
