```text
1	package cmd
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"
6		"log/slog"
7		"os"
8		"os/signal"
9		"runtime"
10		"sort"
11		"strings"
12		"sync"
13		"sync/atomic"
14		"syscall"
15		"time"
16	
17		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
18	
19		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/analytics"
20		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/sessionindex"
21		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
22		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/factory"
23		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
24		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/utils"
25	)
26	
27	const (
28		// unknownProjectID buckets sessions whose originating cwd could not be resolved
29		// (a few Claude warmups; all Cursor sessions until the deferred cwd recovery lands).
30		unknownProjectID = "unknown"
31	
32		// reindexWriteBatch is how many sessions the writer goroutine commits per transaction.
33		reindexWriteBatch = 200
34	
35		// reindexVersion is the logic version stamped on every indexed row. Bump it whenever
36		// the parse/flatten/derivation logic changes so the next reindex re-parses everything
37		// even when files are unchanged. It is part of the freshness fingerprint.
38		//   2: skip slash-command noise when choosing a Claude session title (fixes UUID titles)
39		//   3: also skip "[Request interrupted by user…]" markers
40		//   4: canonicalize (case-fold) the workspace_id path hash + prefer a persisted
41		//      .project.json workspace_id, so a project's id no longer varies with path casing
42		//   5: parse Codex bodies from the enumerated NativePath (was a by-id tree search), so a
43		//      session's body and its freshness fingerprint now derive from the same file
44		//   6: populate sessions.fts_rowid (the O(1) link to each session's FTS row) — existing
45		//      rows have it NULL until re-parsed, so bump to repopulate the whole index once
46		reindexVersion = 6
47	)
48	
49	// CreateReindexCommand builds the `specstory reindex` command: a full, from-scratch
50	// rebuild of the restore index (~/.specstory/sessions.db) of every coding-agent session
51	// SpecStory can find, across all projects and providers. See docs/SESSIONS-DB.md.
52	func CreateReindexCommand() *cobra.Command {
53		cmd := &cobra.Command{
54			Use:   "reindex",
55			Short: "Rebuild the restore index of all known agent sessions",
56			Long: `Rebuild the restore index used by 'specstory resume'.
57	
58	'reindex' enumerates every session across all installed agents and projects and writes a
59	searchable index to ~/.specstory/sessions.db. It is incremental: a session whose native
60	file is unchanged since it was last indexed is skipped, so re-runs are fast. Use --force to
61	re-index everything regardless. The index is a derived cache: it is safe to delete.`,
62			Args: cobra.NoArgs,
63			RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
64				force, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("force")
65				return runReindex(force)
66			},
67		}
68		cmd.Flags().Bool("force", false, "re-index every session even if unchanged")
69		return cmd
70	}
71	
72	// reindexItem is one unit of work: a session to parse and index. size/mtime are the
73	// native file's stat, captured during the freshness check and reused (no re-stat).
74	type reindexItem struct {
75		agent string
76		prov  spi.Provider
77		ref   spi.GlobalSessionRef
78		size  int64
79		mtime int64
80		isNew bool // no existing index row → writer can skip the standalone-FTS delete
81	}
82	
83	func runReindex(force bool) error {
84		start := time.Now()
85	
86		// Graceful Ctrl+C: stop feeding work, flush what's written, exit cleanly.
87		ctx, cancel := signal.NotifyContext(context.Background(), os.Interrupt, syscall.SIGTERM)
88		defer cancel()
89	
90		dbPath, err := sessionindex.DefaultPath()
91		if err != nil {
92			return err
93		}
94		store, err := sessionindex.Open(dbPath)
95		if err != nil {
96			return fmt.Errorf("opening restore index: %w", err)
97		}
98		defer func() { _ = store.Close() }()
99	
100		registry := factory.GetRegistry()
101	
102		// ---- Phase 1: enumerate every provider concurrently, then dedup ----
103		ids, provs, perProvider := enumerateAll(registry, true)
104	
105		// Existing fingerprints, so unchanged sessions can be skipped (the incremental path).
106		fingerprints := map[string]sessionindex.Fingerprint{}
107		if !force {
108			if fps, err := store.Fingerprints(); err != nil {
109				slog.Warn("reindex: could not load fingerprints, re-indexing all", "error", err)
110			} else {
111				fingerprints = fps
112			}
113		}
114	
115		best, order, foundPerAgent := dedupRefs(ids, provs, perProvider)
116		found := len(order)
117		cache := &projectIDCache{m: map[string]projectIDName{}}
118		work, totals, unchanged := selectWork(order, best, fingerprints, "", force, cache)
119	
120		if found == 0 {
121			fprintln(os.Stderr, "No agent sessions found to index.")
122			return nil
123		}
124		fprintf(os.Stderr, "✓   Found %d sessions  ·  %s\n", found, summarizeCounts(ids, foundPerAgent))
125		if len(work) == 0 {
126			fprintf(os.Stderr, "✓   All %d sessions already up to date.  (%.1fs)\n", found, time.Since(start).Seconds())
127			return nil
128		}
129		if unchanged > 0 {
130			fprintf(os.Stderr, "    %d unchanged · indexing %d…\n\n", unchanged, len(work))
131		} else {
132			fprintln(os.Stderr, "")
133		}
134	
135		// ---- Phase 2+3: parse (worker pool) → write (single goroutine), with live progress ----
136		prog := newReindexProgress(ids, totals)
137		prog.begin()
138		indexedAt := start.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)
139		writeErr := processWork(ctx, store, work, cache, indexedAt, min(runtime.NumCPU(), 12), prog)
140		prog.end()
141	
142		if writeErr != nil {
143			return fmt.Errorf("writing restore index: %w", writeErr)
144		}
145	
146		// ---- Summary (counts reflect the WHOLE index, not just this run's work) ----
147		elapsed := time.Since(start)
148		projects, _ := store.ProjectCount(unknownProjectID)
149		unattributed, _ := store.UnattributedCount(unknownProjectID)
150		printReindexSummary(ids, foundPerAgent, dbPath, elapsed, prog.totalDone(), unchanged, projects, unattributed)
151		analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventReindexCompleted, analytics.Properties{
152			"sessions":     len(work),
153			"unchanged":    unchanged,
154			"projects":     projects,
155			"unattributed": unattributed,
156			"duration_ms":  elapsed.Milliseconds(),
157		})
158		return nil
159	}
160	
161	// ---- reindex engine (shared by the foreground command and the background warm) ----
162	
163	// enumerateAll concurrently lists every installed provider's sessions. The returned slices
164	// are index-aligned: provs[i]/perProvider[i] correspond to ids[i] (a nil provs[i] marks a
165	// provider that failed to load). When visible, it renders a live "Scanning agents…" line
166	// (foreground reindex); the background warm passes visible=false and stays silent.
167	func enumerateAll(registry *factory.Registry, visible bool) (ids []string, provs []spi.Provider, perProvider [][]spi.GlobalSessionRef) {
168		ids = registry.ListIDs()
169		perProvider = make([][]spi.GlobalSessionRef, len(ids))
170		provs = make([]spi.Provider, len(ids))
171	
172		var scan *scanProgress
173		if visible {
174			scan = newScanProgress(ids)
175			scan.begin()
176			defer scan.end()
177		}
178	
179		var ewg sync.WaitGroup
180		for i, id := range ids {
181			prov, err := registry.Get(id)
182			if err != nil {
183				continue
184			}
185			provs[i] = prov
186			reporter := scan.reporterFor(id)
187			ewg.Add(1)
188			go func(i int, id string, prov spi.Provider, reporter *spi.ScanReporter) {
189				defer ewg.Done()
190				var refs []spi.GlobalSessionRef
191				var err error
192				// Providers that can report progress (Codex, the long pole) tick the live line; the
193				// rest snap to their final count via markDone when they finish.
194				if pe, ok := prov.(spi.ProgressEnumerator); ok {
195					refs, err = pe.ListAllAgentChatSessionsProgress(reporter)
196				} else {
197					refs, err = prov.ListAllAgentChatSessions()
198				}
199				if err != nil {
200					slog.Warn("reindex: enumeration failed", "provider", id, "error", err)
201				}
202				perProvider[i] = refs
203				scan.markDone(id, len(refs))
204			}(i, id, prov, reporter)
205		}
206		ewg.Wait()
207		return ids, provs, perProvider
208	}
209	
210	// dedupRefs collapses enumerated refs by (agent, session_id), keeping the freshest file. A
211	// resumed session can span MULTIPLE native files sharing one id; they all map to one
212	// (agent, session_id) row, so without dedup the duplicates ping-pong — each run, one file's
213	// stat never matches the fingerprint the other file just stored, re-indexing both forever.
214	// Keeping the freshest by mtime makes the fingerprint stable. Sessions with no native id are
215	// dropped (unresumable, and would collide on the PK). order is first-seen order (deterministic
216	// processing); foundPerAgent is the distinct-session count per agent (for the "Found" line).
217	func dedupRefs(ids []string, provs []spi.Provider, perProvider [][]spi.GlobalSessionRef) (best map[string]reindexItem, order []string, foundPerAgent map[string]int) {
218		best = map[string]reindexItem{}
219		foundPerAgent = map[string]int{}
220		for i, id := range ids {
221			if provs[i] == nil {
222				continue
223			}
224			for _, ref := range perProvider[i] {
225				if strings.TrimSpace(ref.SessionID) == "" {
226					slog.Debug("reindex: skipping session with no id", "agent", id, "path", ref.NativePath)
227					continue
228				}
229				size, mtime := statNative(ref.NativePath)
230				item := reindexItem{agent: id, prov: provs[i], ref: ref, size: size, mtime: mtime}
231				key := sessionindex.FingerprintKey(id, ref.SessionID)
232				if cur, ok := best[key]; ok {
233					if mtime > cur.mtime {
234						best[key] = item
235					}
236					continue
237				}
238				best[key] = item
239				order = append(order, key)
240			}
241		}
242		for _, key := range order {
243			foundPerAgent[best[key].agent]++
244		}
245		return best, order, foundPerAgent
246	}
247	
248	// selectWork applies the incremental fingerprint skip (and an optional project filter) to the
249	// deduped set. projectFilter "" means all projects; otherwise only sessions whose originating
250	// cwd resolves to that project id are kept (used by the background warm's current-project
251	// pass). totals is the per-agent to-do count (drives the foreground progress bars).
252	func selectWork(order []string, best map[string]reindexItem, fingerprints map[string]sessionindex.Fingerprint, projectFilter string, force bool, cache *projectIDCache) (work []reindexItem, totals map[string]int, unchanged int) {
253		totals = map[string]int{}
254		for _, key := range order {
255			item := best[key]
256			if projectFilter != "" {
257				if pid, _ := cache.resolve(item.ref.OriginCwd); pid != projectFilter {
258					continue
259				}
260			}
261			fp, ok := fingerprints[key]
262			if ok && fp.Size == item.size && fp.Mtime == item.mtime && fp.Version == reindexVersion {
263				unchanged++
264				continue // already indexed and unchanged
265			}
266			// A session absent from the fingerprint set has no sessions row, hence no sessions_fts
267			// row either (the pair is written atomically) — so the writer can skip the O(rows)
268			// standalone-FTS delete. Under --force the fingerprint map is deliberately empty, so
269			// absence proves nothing; keep the delete to avoid duplicate FTS rows.
270			item.isNew = !force && !ok
271			work = append(work, item)
272			totals[item.agent]++
273		}
274		return work, totals, unchanged
275	}
276	
277	// reindexReporter receives per-session progress from processWork. The foreground command
278	// passes *reindexProgress (live bars); the background warm passes nopReporter (silent).
279	type reindexReporter interface {
280		observe(projectID string)
281		inc(agent string)
282	}
283	
284	// nopReporter is the silent reporter used by the background warm.
285	type nopReporter struct{}
286	
287	func (nopReporter) observe(string) {}
288	func (nopReporter) inc(string)     {}
289	
290	// processWork parses each work item (worker pool) and writes the results to the store via a
291	// single writer goroutine, reporting progress through rep. It stops feeding work when ctx is
292	// cancelled; the writer flushes what it already received. Returns the writer's error, if any.
293	func processWork(ctx context.Context, store *sessionindex.Store, work []reindexItem, cache *projectIDCache, indexedAt string, workers int, rep reindexReporter) error {
294		if len(work) == 0 {
295			return nil
296		}
297		if workers < 1 {
298			workers = 1
299		}
300	
301		// Schedule the largest sessions first. A few huge Codex files (hundreds of MB) would
302		// otherwise be end-of-run stragglers that leave most worker cores idle; starting them
303		// first overlaps them with the long tail of small sessions. Order does not affect the
304		// resulting index.
305		sort.SliceStable(work, func(i, j int) bool { return work[i].size > work[j].size })
306	
307		sessionsCh := make(chan sessionindex.Session, 256)
308		var writeErr error
309		writerDone := make(chan struct{})
310		go func() {
311			defer close(writerDone)
312			writeErr = drainToStore(store, sessionsCh)
313		}()
314	
315		workCh := make(chan reindexItem)
316		var wwg sync.WaitGroup
317		for w := 0; w < workers; w++ {
318			wwg.Add(1)
319			go func() {
320				defer wwg.Done()
321				for item := range workCh {
322					sess := buildSession(item, cache, indexedAt)
323					rep.observe(sess.ProjectID)
324					sessionsCh <- sess
325					rep.inc(item.agent)
326				}
327			}()
328		}
329	
330		for _, item := range work {
331			select {
332			case <-ctx.Done():
333				goto closing
334			case workCh <- item:
335			}
336		}
337	closing:
338		close(workCh)
339		wwg.Wait()
340		close(sessionsCh)
341		<-writerDone
342		return writeErr
343	}
344	
345	// warmIndexInBackground keeps ~/.specstory/sessions.db warm as a side effect of resume/search.
346	// It runs two SILENT incremental passes over its own writer handle: the current project first
347	// (so its rows refresh quickly, and the open TUI re-queries via onProjectWarmed), then the full
348	// corpus. It enumerates once and reuses the result for both passes. It stops promptly when ctx
349	// is cancelled (the TUI exited); an abandoned write batch is safe under WAL. Errors are logged
350	// at debug and never surfaced — warmth is best-effort.
351	func warmIndexInBackground(ctx context.Context, dbPath, currentProjectID string, onProjectWarmed func()) {
352		store, err := sessionindex.Open(dbPath)
353		if err != nil {
354			slog.Debug("warm: could not open index", "error", err)
355			return
356		}
357		defer func() { _ = store.Close() }()
358	
359		registry := factory.GetRegistry()
360		ids, provs, perProvider := enumerateAll(registry, false)
361		if ctx.Err() != nil {
362			return
363		}
364		best, order, _ := dedupRefs(ids, provs, perProvider)
365		cache := &projectIDCache{m: map[string]projectIDName{}}
366		indexedAt := time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)
367	
368		// Gentle: fewer workers than a foreground reindex, since the user is interacting.
369		workers := min(runtime.NumCPU(), 4)
370	
371		// Pass 1 — the current project, so its rows are fresh almost immediately.
372		fps, _ := store.Fingerprints()
373		work, _, _ := selectWork(order, best, fps, currentProjectID, false, cache)
374		if err := processWork(ctx, store, work, cache, indexedAt, workers, nopReporter{}); err != nil {
375			slog.Debug("warm: current-project pass failed", "error", err)
376		}
377		if ctx.Err() != nil {
378			return
379		}
380		if onProjectWarmed != nil {
381			onProjectWarmed() // let the open TUI re-query the (now fresh) current project
382		}
383	
384		// Pass 2 — everything else. Reload fingerprints so pass-1's writes now register as
385		// unchanged and are skipped, leaving only the rest of the corpus to parse.
386		fps, _ = store.Fingerprints()
387		work, _, _ = selectWork(order, best, fps, "", false, cache)
388		if err := processWork(ctx, store, work, cache, indexedAt, workers, nopReporter{}); err != nil {
389			slog.Debug("warm: full pass failed", "error", err)
390		}
391	}
392	
393	// ---- real-time indexing (watch / run / resume launch) ----
394	
395	// LiveIndexer keeps ~/.specstory/sessions.db current in real time, alongside the markdown
396	// files that watch/run/resume already write as a session changes. It holds one writer handle
397	// for the command's lifetime and upserts a single row per detected update, reusing the same
398	// content derivation as reindex. The project is resolved once (a watch/run has a fixed cwd).
399	//
400	// Everything is best-effort: real-time indexing must never disrupt the agent or the markdown
401	// write, so construction failures yield a nil *LiveIndexer (whose methods are no-ops) and
402	// per-update errors are logged at debug. A live row carries no native-file fingerprint
403	// (Size/Mtime/NativePath are unknown without enumerating), so the next reindex re-verifies and
404	// rewrites it with a stable fingerprint — the live row is correct and queryable in the interim.
405	type LiveIndexer struct {
406		store       *sessionindex.Store
407		cwd         string
408		projectID   string
409		projectName string
410		indexedAt   string
411	
412		mu       sync.Mutex
413		lastSeen map[string]string // session id -> last indexed UpdatedAt, to skip no-op upserts
414	}
415	
416	// NewLiveIndexer opens the index for writing and resolves the project for cwd. It returns nil
417	// (not an error) when the index can't be opened, so the caller keeps running with real-time
418	// indexing simply disabled. The caller owns Close.
419	func NewLiveIndexer(cwd string) *LiveIndexer {
420		dbPath, err := sessionindex.DefaultPath()
421		if err != nil {
422			slog.Debug("live index: no db path; real-time indexing disabled", "error", err)
423			return nil
424		}
425		store, err := sessionindex.Open(dbPath)
426		if err != nil {
427			slog.Debug("live index: cannot open index; real-time indexing disabled", "error", err)
428			return nil
429		}
430		projectID, projectName, err := utils.ComputeProjectID(cwd)
431		if err != nil || projectID == "" {
432			projectID, projectName = unknownProjectID, ""
433		}
434		return &LiveIndexer{
435			store:       store,
436			cwd:         cwd,
437			projectID:   projectID,
438			projectName: projectName,
439			indexedAt:   time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
440			lastSeen:    map[string]string{},
441		}
442	}
443	
444	// Record upserts the live session into the index. agentID is the provider's registry id (e.g.
445	// "claude"). It skips the write when the session's last activity hasn't advanced since the
446	// previous record (the watcher can fire repeatedly without new content). Safe on a nil
447	// receiver, and serialized so concurrent provider watchers don't contend on the single writer.
448	func (li *LiveIndexer) Record(agentID string, sess *spi.AgentChatSession) {
449		if li == nil || sess == nil || sess.SessionData == nil {
450			return
451		}
452		data := sess.SessionData
453	
454		updatedAt := sess.CreatedAt
455		if ts := lastTimestamp(data); ts != "" {
456			updatedAt = ts
457		}
458	
459		li.mu.Lock()
460		defer li.mu.Unlock()
461	
462		// Nothing advanced since the last write → skip the (FTS-heavy) upsert.
463		if updatedAt != "" && li.lastSeen[sess.SessionID] == updatedAt {
464			return
465		}
466	
467		// Skip the whole-table FTS delete (session_id/agent are UNINDEXED, so it scans the entire
468		// FTS) when this session has no existing row to replace. Only the first write of a session
469		// in this process can be new, and even then only if it isn't already in the index from a
470		// prior run — so we check by primary key just once, before lastSeen records the write.
471		isNew := false
472		if _, written := li.lastSeen[sess.SessionID]; !written {
473			if exists, err := li.store.Exists(agentID, sess.SessionID); err == nil {
474				isNew = !exists
475			}
476		}
477	
478		userTurns, totalTurns := countTurns(data)
479		row := sessionindex.Session{
480			ProjectID:    li.projectID,
481			ProjectName:  li.projectName,
482			Agent:        agentID,
483			SessionID:    sess.SessionID,
484			CreatedAt:    sess.CreatedAt,
485			UpdatedAt:    updatedAt,
486			UserTurns:    userTurns,
487			TotalTurns:   totalTurns,
488			Slug:         sess.Slug,
489			OriginCwd:    li.cwd,
490			IndexVersion: reindexVersion,
491			IndexedAt:    li.indexedAt,
492			Body:         flattenBody(data),
493			IsNew:        isNew,
494		}
495		if err := li.store.Upsert(row); err != nil {
496			slog.Debug("live index: upsert failed", "sessionId", sess.SessionID, "error", err)
497			return
498		}
499		li.lastSeen[sess.SessionID] = updatedAt
500	}
501	
502	// Close releases the writer handle. Safe on a nil receiver.
503	func (li *LiveIndexer) Close() {
504		if li == nil {
505			return
506		}
507		if err := li.store.Close(); err != nil {
508			slog.Debug("live index: close failed", "error", err)
509		}
510	}
511	
512	// buildSession turns one enumeration ref into a fully-built index row. Every session
513	// yields a row from its metadata; the body + turn counts + last-activity time are
514	// ENRICHED via a full parse only when the originating cwd lets us re-locate the
515	// session (so Cursor, whose cwd is unknown for now, lands metadata-only). Nothing is
516	// dropped.
517	func buildSession(item reindexItem, cache *projectIDCache, indexedAt string) sessionindex.Session {
518		ref := item.ref
519		projectID, projectName := cache.resolve(ref.OriginCwd)
520	
521		sess := sessionindex.Session{
522			ProjectID:    projectID,
523			ProjectName:  projectName,
524			Agent:        item.agent,
525			SessionID:    ref.SessionID,
526			CreatedAt:    ref.CreatedAt,
527			UpdatedAt:    ref.CreatedAt, // default; overwritten by last-turn timestamp below
528			Slug:         ref.Slug,
529			Name:         ref.Name,
530			NativePath:   ref.NativePath,
531			OriginCwd:    ref.OriginCwd,
532			Size:         item.size, // captured during the freshness check (no re-stat)
533			Mtime:        item.mtime,
534			IndexVersion: reindexVersion,
535			IndexedAt:    indexedAt,
536			IsNew:        item.isNew,
537		}
538	
539		if ref.OriginCwd == "" {
540			return sess // cannot re-locate without a cwd → metadata-only
541		}
542		full, err := parseFullSession(item.prov, ref)
543		if err != nil || full == nil || full.SessionData == nil {
544			slog.Debug("reindex: full parse unavailable, indexing metadata only",
545				"agent", item.agent, "session", ref.SessionID, "error", err)
546			return sess
547		}
548	
549		data := full.SessionData
550		sess.Body = flattenBody(data)
551		sess.UserTurns, sess.TotalTurns = countTurns(data)
552		if ts := lastTimestamp(data); ts != "" {
553			sess.UpdatedAt = ts
554		}
555		return sess
556	}
557	
558	// parseFullSession reads a session's full data for indexing. It prefers a provider's
559	// path-keyed parse (spi.PathSessionReader) using the ref's already-known NativePath, which
560	// avoids the provider's by-id discovery search — for Codex that search walks the entire
561	// ~/.codex/sessions tree, so this is the difference between an O(N) and an O(N²) reindex.
562	// Providers without the capability (or refs lacking a NativePath) fall back to the by-id
563	// lookup. Parsing the known NativePath is also more consistent: the row's freshness
564	// fingerprint is stat'd from NativePath, so the body now comes from that same file.
565	func parseFullSession(prov spi.Provider, ref spi.GlobalSessionRef) (*spi.AgentChatSession, error) {
566		if pr, ok := prov.(spi.PathSessionReader); ok && ref.NativePath != "" {
567			return pr.GetAgentChatSessionByPath(ref.NativePath, ref.OriginCwd, false)
568		}
569		return prov.GetAgentChatSession(ref.OriginCwd, ref.SessionID, false)
570	}
571	
572	// drainToStore writes parsed sessions to the index in batched transactions.
573	func drainToStore(store *sessionindex.Store, ch <-chan sessionindex.Session) error {
574		batch := make([]sessionindex.Session, 0, reindexWriteBatch)
575		flush := func() error {
576			if err := store.UpsertBatch(batch); err != nil {
577				return err
578			}
579			batch = batch[:0]
580			return nil
581		}
582		for sess := range ch {
583			batch = append(batch, sess)
584			if len(batch) >= reindexWriteBatch {
585				if err := flush(); err != nil {
586					return err
587				}
588			}
589		}
590		return flush()
591	}
592	
593	// ---- session field derivation ----
594	
595	// flattenBody renders SessionData to plain user/agent text for full-text indexing,
596	// reusing the reconstruction flattener (synthetic noise already stripped). No
597	// migration note is prepended — this is index content, not a resumed transcript.
598	func flattenBody(data *schema.SessionData) string {
599		turns := spi.FlattenSessionData(data, "")
600		var b strings.Builder
601		for _, t := range turns {
602			if t.Text == "" {
603				continue
604			}
605			if b.Len() > 0 {
606				b.WriteString("\n\n")
607			}
608			b.WriteString(t.Text)
609		}
610		return b.String()
611	}
612	
613	// countTurns returns (user prompts, all messages) across the session's exchanges.
614	func countTurns(data *schema.SessionData) (userTurns, totalTurns int) {
615		for _, ex := range data.Exchanges {
616			for _, m := range ex.Messages {
617				totalTurns++
618				if m.Role == schema.RoleUser {
619					userTurns++
620				}
621			}
622		}
623		return userTurns, totalTurns
624	}
625	
626	// lastTimestamp returns the last non-empty message timestamp in the session, or "".
627	func lastTimestamp(data *schema.SessionData) string {
628		last := ""
629		for _, ex := range data.Exchanges {
630			for _, m := range ex.Messages {
631				if m.Timestamp != "" {
632					last = m.Timestamp
633				}
634			}
635		}
636		return last
637	}
638	
639	// statNative returns the native file's size (bytes) and mtime (epoch ms), or zeros.
640	func statNative(path string) (size, mtimeMs int64) {
641		info, err := os.Stat(path)
642		if err != nil {
643			return 0, 0
644		}
645		return info.Size(), info.ModTime().UnixMilli()
646	}
647	
648	// ---- project identity cache (cwd -> project id/name) ----
649	
650	type projectIDName struct{ id, name string }
651	
652	// projectIDCache memoizes ComputeProjectID by cwd, since many sessions share a cwd and
653	// each resolution walks the filesystem to the git root.
654	type projectIDCache struct {
655		mu sync.Mutex
656		m  map[string]projectIDName
657	}
658	
659	func (c *projectIDCache) resolve(cwd string) (string, string) {
660		if strings.TrimSpace(cwd) == "" {
661			return unknownProjectID, ""
662		}
663		c.mu.Lock()
664		defer c.mu.Unlock()
665		if v, ok := c.m[cwd]; ok {
666			return v.id, v.name
667		}
668		id, name, err := utils.ComputeProjectID(cwd)
669		if err != nil || id == "" {
670			id, name = unknownProjectID, ""
671		}
672		c.m[cwd] = projectIDName{id, name}
673		return id, name
674	}
675	
676	// ---- helpers ----
677	
678	// summarizeCounts renders "claude 683 · codex 70 · …" in registry order, omitting zeros.
679	func summarizeCounts(ids []string, totals map[string]int) string {
680		var parts []string
681		for _, id := range ids {
682			if totals[id] > 0 {
683				parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("%s %d", id, totals[id]))
684			}
685		}
686		return strings.Join(parts, " · ")
687	}
688	
689	// ---- live progress (per-agent bars) ----
690	
691	// reindexProgress renders live per-agent progress. Workers bump atomic per-agent
692	// counters and observe project ids; a single render goroutine redraws the block in
693	// place on a TTY (ANSI cursor-up), or prints periodic plain lines otherwise. No worker
694	// draws directly. See docs/SESSIONS-DB.md "Reindex Progress UX".
695	type reindexProgress struct {
696		agents []string          // agents with >0 sessions, in registry order
697		totals map[string]int    // per-agent session totals
698		done   map[string]*int64 // per-agent completed counters (atomic)
699		tty    bool
700	
701		mu       sync.Mutex
702		projects map[string]struct{} // distinct non-unknown project ids seen
703	
704		stopCh     chan struct{}
705		doneCh     chan struct{}
706		linesDrawn int
707	}
708	
709	func newReindexProgress(ids []string, totals map[string]int) *reindexProgress {
710		done := make(map[string]*int64)
711		var agents []string
712		for _, id := range ids {
713			if totals[id] > 0 {
714				agents = append(agents, id)
715				done[id] = new(int64)
716			}
717		}
718		return &reindexProgress{
719			agents:   agents,
720			totals:   totals,
721			done:     done,
722			tty:      isTerminal(os.Stderr),
723			projects: map[string]struct{}{},
724			stopCh:   make(chan struct{}),
725			doneCh:   make(chan struct{}),
726		}
727	}
728	
729	func (p *reindexProgress) begin() {
730		go p.loop()
731	}
732	
733	func (p *reindexProgress) end() {
734		close(p.stopCh)
735		<-p.doneCh
736		p.draw(true) // final frame at 100%
737	}
738	
739	func (p *reindexProgress) inc(agent string) {
740		if c := p.done[agent]; c != nil {
741			atomic.AddInt64(c, 1)
742		}
743	}
744	
745	func (p *reindexProgress) observe(projectID string) {
746		// Unknown-project sessions are not counted toward the live "projects" total (the
747		// final unattributed count comes from the DB at summary time).
748		if projectID == unknownProjectID {
749			return
750		}
751		p.mu.Lock()
752		p.projects[projectID] = struct{}{}
753		p.mu.Unlock()
754	}
755	
756	func (p *reindexProgress) projectCount() int {
757		p.mu.Lock()
758		defer p.mu.Unlock()
759		return len(p.projects)
760	}
761	
762	func (p *reindexProgress) totalDone() int {
763		var n int64
764		for _, c := range p.done {
765			n += atomic.LoadInt64(c)
766		}
767		return int(n)
768	}
769	
770	func (p *reindexProgress) grandTotal() int {
771		n := 0
772		for _, a := range p.agents {
773			n += p.totals[a]
774		}
775		return n
776	}
777	
778	func (p *reindexProgress) loop() {
779		defer close(p.doneCh)
780		interval := 100 * time.Millisecond
781		if !p.tty {
782			interval = 1500 * time.Millisecond
783		}
784		ticker := time.NewTicker(interval)
785		defer ticker.Stop()
786		for {
787			select {
788			case <-p.stopCh:
789				return
790			case <-ticker.C:
791				p.draw(false)
792			}
793		}
794	}
795	
796	func (p *reindexProgress) draw(final bool) {
797		if !p.tty {
798			if !final { // the final state is covered by the summary line
799				fprintf(os.Stderr, "indexed %d/%d…\n", p.totalDone(), p.grandTotal())
800			}
801			return
802		}
803	
804		lines := p.frameLines()
805		var b strings.Builder
806		if p.linesDrawn > 0 {
807			fmt.Fprintf(&b, "\033[%dA", p.linesDrawn) // move cursor up to the block's top
808		}
809		for _, ln := range lines {
810			b.WriteString("\033[2K") // clear the line, then redraw it
811			b.WriteString(ln)
812			b.WriteString("\n")
813		}
814		fprint(os.Stderr, b.String())
815		p.linesDrawn = len(lines)
816	}
817	
818	func (p *reindexProgress) frameLines() []string {
819		doneN, totalN := p.totalDone(), p.grandTotal()
820		lines := []string{
821			fmt.Sprintf("Indexing  %d/%d · %d projects", doneN, totalN, p.projectCount()),
822			"",
823		}
824	
825		nameW := 0
826		for _, a := range p.agents {
827			if len(a) > nameW {
828				nameW = len(a)
829			}
830		}
831		for _, a := range p.agents {
832			d := atomic.LoadInt64(p.done[a])
833			t := int64(p.totals[a])
834			check := ""
835			if d >= t {
836				check = " ✓"
837			}
838			lines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf("  %-*s %s %d/%d%s", nameW, a, progressBar(d, t), d, t, check))
839		}
840		return lines
841	}
842	
843	// progressBar renders a fixed-width unicode bar, e.g. ▕███████░░░░░░░▏.
844	func progressBar(done, total int64) string {
845		const w = 14
846		filled := 0
847		if total > 0 {
848			filled = int(done * int64(w) / total)
849		}
850		if filled > w {
851			filled = w
852		}
853		return "▕" + strings.Repeat("█", filled) + strings.Repeat("░", w-filled) + "▏"
854	}
855	
856	// isTerminal reports whether f is a character device (an interactive terminal),
857	// using only the standard library (no isatty dependency).
858	func isTerminal(f *os.File) bool {
859		info, err := f.Stat()
860		if err != nil {
861			return false
862		}
863		return info.Mode()&os.ModeCharDevice != 0
864	}
865	
866	// ---- scan progress (live "Scanning agents…" line) ----
867	
868	// scanSpinner is the braille spinner cycled while enumerating.
869	var scanSpinner = []string{"⠋", "⠙", "⠹", "⠸", "⠼", "⠴", "⠦", "⠧", "⠇", "⠏"}
870	
871	// scanAgent holds one provider's live enumeration counter.
872	type scanAgent struct {
873		id string
874		r  *spi.ScanReporter
875	}
876	
877	// scanProgress renders one live line while enumerateAll scans providers in parallel:
878	//
879	//	🔍  Scanning agents…  ⠹  codex 18420 · claude 3195 · cursor 10
880	//
881	// Codex and Claude (the long poles) report sessions-found via spi.ProgressEnumerator; every
882	// provider snaps to its final count via markDone when it finishes. Counts are sessions, not raw
883	// files (warmup/sidechain transcripts are excluded). On a non-TTY it prints periodic plain lines.
884	// A nil *scanProgress is fully silent — the background warm passes nil.
885	type scanProgress struct {
886		agents []*scanAgent
887		byID   map[string]*scanAgent
888		tty    bool
889		spin   int
890	
891		stopCh     chan struct{}
892		doneCh     chan struct{}
893		linesDrawn int
894	}
895	
896	func newScanProgress(ids []string) *scanProgress {
897		agents := make([]*scanAgent, 0, len(ids))
898		byID := make(map[string]*scanAgent, len(ids))
899		for _, id := range ids {
900			a := &scanAgent{id: id, r: &spi.ScanReporter{}}
901			agents = append(agents, a)
902			byID[id] = a
903		}
904		return &scanProgress{
905			agents: agents,
906			byID:   byID,
907			tty:    isTerminal(os.Stderr),
908			stopCh: make(chan struct{}),
909			doneCh: make(chan struct{}),
910		}
911	}
912	
913	// reporterFor returns a provider's live reporter (nil-safe: a nil scanProgress yields nil, which
914	// providers treat as "do not report").
915	func (s *scanProgress) reporterFor(id string) *spi.ScanReporter {
916		if s == nil {
917			return nil
918		}
919		if a := s.byID[id]; a != nil {
920			return a.r
921		}
922		return nil
923	}
924	
925	// markDone reconciles a provider's running count to its final session count — a no-op for the
926	// reporting providers (Codex/Claude), and the only count for providers that never report
927	// (Cursor/Gemini/etc., which appear the moment they finish).
928	func (s *scanProgress) markDone(id string, final int) {
929		if s == nil {
930			return
931		}
932		if a := s.byID[id]; a != nil {
933			if delta := final - int(a.r.Found()); delta != 0 {
934				a.r.Add(delta)
935			}
936		}
937	}
938	
939	func (s *scanProgress) begin() {
940		if s == nil {
941			return
942		}
943		if !s.tty {
944			fprintln(os.Stderr, "\n🔍  Scanning agents…")
945		} else {
946			fprintln(os.Stderr) // blank line above the live line
947			s.draw()            // show immediately, before the first tick
948		}
949		go s.loop()
950	}
951	
952	func (s *scanProgress) end() {
953		if s == nil {
954			return
955		}
956		close(s.stopCh)
957		<-s.doneCh
958		if s.tty && s.linesDrawn > 0 {
959			// Erase the live line so the caller's "✓ Found…" line takes its place.
960			fprint(os.Stderr, "\033[1A\033[2K")
961		}
962	}
963	
964	func (s *scanProgress) loop() {
965		defer close(s.doneCh)
966		interval := 100 * time.Millisecond
967		if !s.tty {
968			interval = 1500 * time.Millisecond
969		}
970		t := time.NewTicker(interval)
971		defer t.Stop()
972		for {
973			select {
974			case <-s.stopCh:
975				return
976			case <-t.C:
977				s.spin++
978				if s.tty {
979					s.draw()
980				} else {
981					fprintf(os.Stderr, "    scanning… %s\n", s.counts())
982				}
983			}
984		}
985	}
986	
987	func (s *scanProgress) draw() {
988		var b strings.Builder
989		if s.linesDrawn > 0 {
990			b.WriteString("\033[1A") // up to the live line
991		}
992		b.WriteString("\033[2K") // clear it, then redraw
993		b.WriteString(s.line())
994		b.WriteString("\n")
995		fprint(os.Stderr, b.String())
996		s.linesDrawn = 1
997	}
998	
999	func (s *scanProgress) line() string {
1000		return fmt.Sprintf("🔍  Scanning agents…  %s  %s", scanSpinner[s.spin%len(scanSpinner)], s.counts())
1001	}
1002	
1003	func (s *scanProgress) counts() string {
1004		var parts []string
1005		for _, a := range s.agents {
1006			found := a.r.Found()
1007			if found == 0 {
1008				continue // not started, or finished with no sessions
1009			}
1010			// Plain integers (no thousands separators), matching the "Found …" summary line.
1011			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("%s %d", a.id, found))
1012		}
1013		if len(parts) == 0 {
1014			return "…"
1015		}
1016		return strings.Join(parts, " · ")
1017	}
1018	
1019	// ---- summary ----
1020	
1021	func printReindexSummary(ids []string, perAgent map[string]int, dbPath string, elapsed time.Duration, indexed, unchanged, projects, unattributed int) {
1022		w := os.Stderr
1023		if unchanged > 0 {
1024			fprintf(w, "\n✓   Indexed %d sessions (%d unchanged) into %s  (%.1fs)\n\n", indexed, unchanged, prettyPath(dbPath), elapsed.Seconds())
1025		} else {
1026			fprintf(w, "\n✓   Indexed %d sessions into %s  (%.1fs)\n\n", indexed, prettyPath(dbPath), elapsed.Seconds())
1027		}
1028		fprintf(w, "      %s\n\n", summarizeCounts(ids, perAgent))
1029		if unattributed > 0 {
1030			fprintf(w, "      %d projects  ·  %d unattributed\n", projects, unattributed)
1031		} else {
1032			fprintf(w, "      %d projects\n", projects)
1033		}
1034		fprintf(w, "\n")
1035	}
1036	
1037	// prettyPath abbreviates the user's home directory to ~ for display.
1038	func prettyPath(p string) string {
1039		if home, err := os.UserHomeDir(); err == nil && strings.HasPrefix(p, home) {
1040			return "~" + p[len(home):]
1041		}
1042		return p
1043	}
1044
```

35	+// Best-effort UI writers. The destination is the user's terminal, so write
36	+// errors are not actionable and are intentionally ignored.
37	+func fprintf(w io.Writer, format string, a ...any) { _, _ = fmt.Fprintf(w, format, a...) }
38	+func fprintln(w io.Writer, a ...any)               { _, _ = fmt.Fprintln(w, a...) }
39	+func fprint(w io.Writer, a ...any)                 { _, _ = fmt.Fprint(w, a...) }
40	+
41	+// resumePlan captures the user's choices from the interactive selection.
42	+type resumePlan struct {
43	+	from      spi.Provider
44	+	fromID    string
45	+	sessionID string
46	+	// fromCwd is the directory the source session was originally launched from
47	+	// (the index's origin_cwd). It can differ from the user's current cwd when a
48	+	// session is picked from another project via the all-projects browser. The
49	+	// source data load must use this, because some providers (e.g. Codex) locate a
50	+	// session by matching its recorded cwd — looking under the current cwd would
51	+	// find nothing. Empty for older index rows; callers fall back to the current cwd.
52	+	fromCwd string
53	+	to      spi.Provider
54	+	toID    string
55	+}
56	+
57	+// agentChoice pairs a registry ID with its provider.
58	+type agentChoice struct {
59	+	id       string
60	+	provider spi.Provider
61	+}
62	+
63	+// CreateResumeCommand builds the `specstory resume` command. It opens the interactive
64	+// picker (a Bubble Tea TUI over the sessions.db index — see docs/RESUME-TUI.md): browse
65	+// the current project's sessions across all agents, pick one, choose a target agent, then
66	+// reconstruct (cross-agent) or native-resume (same agent) and launch with auto-save.
67	+func CreateResumeCommand(cloudURL *string, localTimeZone bool, debugDir string) *cobra.Command {
68	+	longDesc := `Resume a past coding-agent session — in the same agent, or a different one.
69	+
70	+'resume' opens an interactive picker of the sessions in the current project across all
71	+agents. Pick one, choose which installed agent to continue it in, and go. Resuming into a
72	+different agent reconstructs the conversation into that agent's native format first.
73	+
74	+Pass an agent to set the resume target up front, e.g. 'specstory resume codex' — the picker
75	+then skips the target-selection step and resumes straight into that agent. The agent must be
76	+a known, installed provider, or the command errors.`
77	+
78	+	resumeCmd := &cobra.Command{
79	+		Use:   "resume [agent]",
80	+		Short: "Resume a past session, optionally in a different agent",
81	+		Long:  longDesc,
82	+		Args:  cobra.MaximumNArgs(1),
83	+		RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
84	+			config.EnsureDefaultProjectConfig()
85	+			slog.Info("Running in resume mode")
86	+
87	+			registry := factory.GetRegistry()
88	+
89	+			// Optional positional arg pre-selects the target agent (e.g. `resume codex`).
90	+			// An unknown agent name should fail the run rather than be silently ignored, so
91	+			// validate it against the registry up front. When set and valid, the picker skips
92	+			// the target-selection step entirely (see selectResumeViaTUI / beginResume); whether
93	+			// the named agent is actually installed is checked there, where the installed set
94	+			// is known.
95	+			presetTarget := ""
96	+			if len(args) == 1 {
97	+				presetTarget = strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(args[0]))
98	+				if _, err := registry.Get(presetTarget); err != nil {
99	+					return utils.ValidationError{Message: fmt.Sprintf(
100	+						"unknown agent %q. Valid agents: %s", presetTarget, registry.GetProviderList())}
101	+				}
102	+			}
103	+
104	+			// Read flags (subset mirroring run/watch).
105	+			debugRaw, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("debug-raw")
106	+			useLocalTimezone, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("local-time-zone")
107	+			useUTC := !useLocalTimezone
108	+			outputDir, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("output-dir")
109	+			flagDebugDir, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("debug-dir")
110	+			noCloudSync, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-cloud-sync")
111	+			onlyCloudSync, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("only-cloud-sync")
112	+			provenanceEnabled, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("provenance")
113	+			noTelemetryPrompts, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-telemetry-prompts")
114	+
115	+			if flagDebugDir != "" {
116	+				spi.SetDebugBaseDir(flagDebugDir)
117	+			}
118	+
119	+			cwd, err := os.Getwd()
120	+			if err != nil {
121	+				slog.Error("Failed to get current working directory", "error", err)
122	+				return err
123	+			}
124	+
125	+			// Open (building if needed) the session index and run the interactive picker.
126	+			// builtFresh = the index was just rebuilt in the foreground, so the picker skips
127	+			// the background index warm (it would be redundant).
128	+			store, builtFresh, err := openOrBuildResumeIndex()
129	+			if err != nil {
130	+				return err
131	+			}
132	+			defer func() { _ = store.Close() }()
133	+
134	+			projectID, projectName, idErr := utils.ComputeProjectID(cwd)
135	+			if idErr != nil || projectID == "" {
136	+				projectID, projectName = unknownProjectID, filepath.Base(cwd)
137	+			}
138	+
139	+			plan, err := selectResumeViaTUI(registry, store, projectID, projectName, presetTarget, builtFresh)
140	+			if err != nil {
141	+				return err
142	+			}
143	+			if plan == nil {
144	+				// User quit or nothing to resume; the picker already explained why.
145	+				return nil
146	+			}
147	+
148	+			return launchResume(plan, cwd, resumeLaunchOpts{
149	+				outputDir:          outputDir,
150	+				flagDebugDir:       flagDebugDir,
151	+				debugRaw:           debugRaw,
152	+				useUTC:             useUTC,
153	+				noCloudSync:        noCloudSync,
154	+				onlyCloudSync:      onlyCloudSync,
155	+				provenanceEnabled:  provenanceEnabled,
156	+				noTelemetryPrompts: noTelemetryPrompts,
157	+			})
158	+		},
159	+	}
160	+
161	+	resumeCmd.Flags().String("output-dir", "", "custom output directory for markdown files (default: ./.specstory/history)")
162	+	resumeCmd.Flags().String("debug-dir", debugDir, "custom output directory for debug data (default: ./.specstory/debug)")
163	+	resumeCmd.Flags().Bool("only-cloud-sync", false, "skip local markdown file saves, only upload to cloud (requires authentication)")
164	+	resumeCmd.Flags().Bool("no-cloud-sync", false, "disable cloud sync functionality")
165	+	resumeCmd.Flags().Bool("debug-raw", false, "debug mode to output pretty-printed raw data files")
166	+	_ = resumeCmd.Flags().MarkHidden("debug-raw")
167	+	resumeCmd.Flags().Bool("local-time-zone", localTimeZone, "use local timezone for file name and content timestamps (when not present: UTC)")
168	+	resumeCmd.Flags().Bool("provenance", false, "enable AI provenance tracking (correlate file changes to agent activity)")
169	+	_ = resumeCmd.Flags().MarkHidden("provenance")
170	+	resumeCmd.Flags().StringVar(cloudURL, "cloud-url", "", "override the default cloud API base URL")
171	+	_ = resumeCmd.Flags().MarkHidden("cloud-url")
172	+	resumeCmd.Flags().Bool("no-telemetry-prompts", false, "exclude prompt text from telemetry spans, if telemetry is enabled")
173	+
174	+	return resumeCmd
175	+}
176	+
177	+// resumeLaunchOpts carries the resume launch configuration shared by `resume` and
178	+// `search` (whose `r` action resumes a found session through the same path).
179	+type resumeLaunchOpts struct {
180	+	outputDir          string
181	+	flagDebugDir       string
182	+	debugRaw           bool
183	+	useUTC             bool
184	+	noCloudSync        bool
185	+	onlyCloudSync      bool
186	+	provenanceEnabled  bool
187	+	noTelemetryPrompts bool
188	+}
189	+
190	+// launchResume reconstructs (cross-agent) or natively resumes the planned session and
191	+// runs the agent with auto-save + provenance — the shared tail of `resume` and `search`.
192	+func launchResume(plan *resumePlan, cwd string, o resumeLaunchOpts) error {
193	+	// Setup output configuration and project identity (needed for auto-save + cloud).
194	+	outConfig, err := utils.SetupOutputConfig(o.outputDir, o.flagDebugDir)
195	+	if err != nil {
196	+		return err
197	+	}
198	+	if err := utils.EnsureHistoryDirectoryExists(outConfig); err != nil {
199	+		return err
200	+	}
201	+	if _, err := utils.NewProjectIdentityManager(cwd).EnsureProjectIdentity(); err != nil {
202	+		slog.Error("Failed to ensure project identity", "error", err)
203	+	}
204	+
205	+	CheckAndWarnAuthentication(o.noCloudSync)
206	+	if o.onlyCloudSync && !cloud.IsAuthenticated() {
207	+		return utils.ValidationError{Message: "--only-cloud-sync requires authentication. Please run 'specstory login' first"}
208	+	}
209	+
210	+	analytics.SetAgentProviders([]string{plan.to.Name()})
211	+	analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventResumeActivated, analytics.Properties{
212	+		"from_provider": plan.fromID,
213	+		"to_provider":   plan.toID,
214	+		"cross_agent":   plan.fromID != plan.toID,
215	+	})
216	+
217	+	resumeSessionID, err := prepareResumeTarget(plan, cwd, os.Stdout)
218	+	if err != nil {
219	+		return err
220	+	}
221	+
222	+	// Context for graceful cancellation (Ctrl+C).
223	+	ctx, cancel := signal.NotifyContext(context.Background(), os.Interrupt, syscall.SIGTERM)
224	+	defer cancel()
225	+
226	+	// Provenance infrastructure (mirrors run/watch).
227	+	provenanceEngine, provenanceCleanup, err := provenance.StartEngine(o.provenanceEnabled)
228	+	if err != nil {
229	+		return err
230	+	}
231	+	defer provenanceCleanup()
232	+	fsCleanup, err := provenance.StartFSWatcher(ctx, provenanceEngine, cwd)
233	+	if err != nil {
234	+		return err
235	+	}
236	+	defer fsCleanup()
237	+
238	+	// Keep sessions.db current in real time alongside the markdown writes (nil/no-op if the
239	+	// index can't be opened — never block the resumed agent on it).
240	+	liveIndex := NewLiveIndexer(cwd)
241	+	defer liveIndex.Close()
242	+
243	+	// Auto-save callback: identical behavior to run/watch.
244	+	sessionCallback := func(sess *spi.AgentChatSession) {
245	+		if sess == nil {
246	+			return
247	+		}
248	+		_, perr := session.ProcessSingleSession(context.Background(), sess, outConfig, session.ProcessingOptions{
249	+			OnlyCloudSync:      o.onlyCloudSync,
250	+			IsAutosave:         true,
251	+			DebugRaw:           o.debugRaw,
252	+			UseUTC:             o.useUTC,
253	+			NoTelemetryPrompts: o.noTelemetryPrompts,
254	+		})
255	+		if perr != nil {
256	+			slog.Error("Failed to process session update", "sessionId", sess.SessionID, "error", perr)
257	+		}
258	+		// Mirror the markdown write into the restore index (agent we resumed INTO).
259	+		liveIndex.Record(plan.toID, sess)
260	+		provenance.ProcessEvents(ctx, provenanceEngine, sess)
261	+	}
262	+
263	+	slog.Info("Launching resume", "provider", plan.to.Name(), "resumeSessionID", resumeSessionID)
264	+	if err := plan.to.ExecAgentAndWatch(cwd, "", resumeSessionID, o.debugRaw, sessionCallback); err != nil {
265	+		slog.Error("Agent resume failed", "provider", plan.to.Name(), "error", err)
266	+		return err
267	+	}
268	+	return nil
269	+}
270	+
271	+// prepareResumeTarget produces the native session ID to hand to ExecAgentAndWatch.
272	+// Same-agent resume reuses the existing session; cross-agent reconstructs the
273	+// source SessionData into the target's native store and returns the fresh ID.
274	+func prepareResumeTarget(plan *resumePlan, cwd string, out io.Writer) (string, error) {
275	+	// Same agent: native resume on the existing session, no transform.
276	+	if plan.fromID == plan.toID {
277	+		fprintf(out, "\nResuming %s session %s in place...\n", plan.from.Name(), shortID(plan.sessionID))
278	+		return plan.sessionID, nil
279	+	}
280	+
281	+	// Cross-agent: load source SessionData, reconstruct, write into target store.
282	+	// The source must be read from the directory it was originally launched in, not the
283	+	// user's current cwd — a session picked from another project (all-projects browser)
284	+	// lives elsewhere, and providers like Codex locate it by its recorded cwd. The
285	+	// reconstructed session is still written and launched under the current cwd below.
286	+	fromCwd := plan.fromCwd
287	+	if fromCwd == "" {
288	+		fromCwd = cwd
289	+	}
290	+	fromSession, err := plan.from.GetAgentChatSession(fromCwd, plan.sessionID, false)
291	+	if err != nil {
292	+		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to load source session: %w", err)
293	+	}
294	+	if fromSession == nil || fromSession.SessionData == nil {
295	+		return "", fmt.Errorf("source session %s has no data to reconstruct", plan.sessionID)
296	+	}
297	+
298	+	note := fmt.Sprintf("Resumed from a %s session via SpecStory.", plan.from.Name())
299	+	rec, err := plan.to.ReconstructSession(fromSession.SessionData, spi.ReconstructOptions{
300	+		WorkspaceRoot: cwd,
301	+		MigrationNote: note,
302	+	})
303	+	if err != nil {
304	+		if errors.Is(err, spi.ErrReconstructionUnsupported) {
305	+			return "", utils.ValidationError{Message: fmt.Sprintf(
306	+				"%s can't yet be a cross-agent resume target. Choose Claude Code or Codex CLI (or resume in %s itself).",
307	+				plan.to.Name(), plan.from.Name())}
308	+		}
309	+		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to reconstruct session for %s: %w", plan.to.Name(), err)
310	+	}
311	+
312	+	path, err := plan.to.NativeSessionPath(cwd, rec.Filename)
313	+	if err != nil {
314	+		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to resolve target session path: %w", err)
315	+	}
316	+	if err := writeReconstructedSession(path, rec.Content); err != nil {
317	+		return "", err
318	+	}
319	+	// The agent runs in a separate process and resolves the session by reading this file the
320	+	// instant it starts. On a filesystem without strong close-to-open coherence the freshly
321	+	// written file can lag, so confirm it is actually readable before handing the agent a
322	+	// --resume it would otherwise reject as "session not found".
323	+	if err := waitForSessionFileVisible(path, sessionFileVisibleTimeout); err != nil {
324	+		return "", fmt.Errorf("reconstructed %s session is not ready to resume: %w", plan.to.Name(), err)
325	+	}
326	+
327	+	slog.Info("resume: wrote reconstructed session", "path", path, "newID", rec.SessionID)
328	+	fprintf(out, "\nReconstructed %s session into %s as %s.\n", plan.from.Name(), plan.to.Name(), shortID(rec.SessionID))
329	+	return rec.SessionID, nil
330	+}
331	+
332	+// shortID abbreviates a session ID for display.
333	+func shortID(id string) string {
334	+	if len(id) <= 13 {
335	+		return id
336	+	}
337	+	return id[:5] + "..." + id[len(id)-5:]
338	+}
339	+
340	+// sessionFileVisibleTimeout bounds how long resume waits for a just-written reconstructed
341	+// session file to become readable to other processes before launching the agent. It is
342	+// generous enough to absorb the propagation lag of a weak-coherence filesystem (network or
343	+// roaming home, cloud-synced folder, VM/container shared mount) yet short enough not to hang
344	+// an interactive command — on a local disk the very first check passes, so it never waits.
345	+const sessionFileVisibleTimeout = 10 * time.Second
346	+
347	+// writeReconstructedSession durably and atomically writes a reconstructed session file. A
348	+// plain os.WriteFile leaves the data in the page cache and the new directory entry possibly
349	+// non-durable; on a filesystem without strong close-to-open coherence the separately-spawned
350	+// agent process can then fail to see a (large) file and report the session as missing. To
351	+// avoid that, the content is written to a temp file in the SAME directory (so the rename is
352	+// atomic rather than cross-device), fsync'd, renamed into place, and the parent directory is
353	+// fsync'd so the entry itself is durable.
354	+func writeReconstructedSession(path string, content []byte) (err error) {
355	+	dir := filepath.Dir(path)
356	+	if mkErr := os.MkdirAll(dir, 0o755); mkErr != nil {
357	+		return fmt.Errorf("failed to create target session directory: %w", mkErr)
358	+	}
359	+
360	+	tmp, err := os.CreateTemp(dir, ".resume-*.tmp")
361	+	if err != nil {
362	+		return fmt.Errorf("failed to create temp session file: %w", err)
363	+	}
364	+	tmpPath := tmp.Name()
365	+	// Best-effort cleanup of the temp file on any early return; a no-op once it is renamed away.
366	+	defer func() { _ = os.Remove(tmpPath) }()
367	+
368	+	// Preserve the world-readable mode the previous os.WriteFile(…, 0o644) produced
369	+	// (CreateTemp makes the file 0o600).
370	+	if chErr := tmp.Chmod(0o644); chErr != nil {
371	+		_ = tmp.Close()
372	+		return fmt.Errorf("failed to set session file mode: %w", chErr)
373	+	}
374	+	if _, wErr := tmp.Write(content); wErr != nil {
375	+		_ = tmp.Close()
376	+		return fmt.Errorf("failed to write temp session file: %w", wErr)
377	+	}
378	+	// Flush the bytes to the backing store before the rename so a reader cannot observe a
379	+	// renamed-but-empty file.
380	+	if syncErr := tmp.Sync(); syncErr != nil {
381	+		_ = tmp.Close()
382	+		return fmt.Errorf("failed to flush temp session file: %w", syncErr)
383	+	}
384	+	if closeErr := tmp.Close(); closeErr != nil {
385	+		return fmt.Errorf("failed to close temp session file: %w", closeErr)
386	+	}
387	+
388	+	if rnErr := os.Rename(tmpPath, path); rnErr != nil {
389	+		return fmt.Errorf("failed to move session file into place: %w", rnErr)
390	+	}
391	+
392	+	// Fsync the directory so the new entry is durable and propagates. Best-effort: not all
393	+	// platforms permit fsync on a directory handle, and it is a negligible cost on local disks.
394	+	if d, openErr := os.Open(dir); openErr == nil {
395	+		_ = d.Sync()
396	+		_ = d.Close()
397	+	}
398	+	return nil
399	+}
400	+
401	+// waitForSessionFileVisible polls until path is readable — present AND with at least its
402	+// first line available, since on a weak-coherence filesystem a stat can succeed before the
403	+// content propagates — or the timeout elapses, in which case it returns a diagnostic error
404	+// so the caller can fail clearly rather than launch a --resume the agent would reject.
405	+func waitForSessionFileVisible(path string, timeout time.Duration) error {
406	+	deadline := time.Now().Add(timeout)
407	+	for {
408	+		readable, checkErr := sessionFileReadable(path)
409	+		if readable {
410	+			return nil
411	+		}
412	+		if time.Now().After(deadline) {
413	+			return fmt.Errorf("session file %q did not become readable within %s "+
414	+				"(the target filesystem may lack close-to-open coherence): %w", path, timeout, checkErr)
415	+		}
416	+		time.Sleep(50 * time.Millisecond)
417	+	}
418	+}
419	+
420	+// sessionFileReadable reports whether path exists and at least its first line can be read.
421	+// A reconstructed session always has at least one record, so an empty read means the content
422	+// has not propagated yet even though the directory entry exists.
423	+func sessionFileReadable(path string) (bool, error) {
424	+	f, err := os.Open(path)
425	+	if err != nil {
426	+		return false, err
427	+	}
428	+	defer func() { _ = f.Close() }()
429	+
430	+	line, err := bufio.NewReader(f).ReadString('\n')
431	+	if len(line) > 0 {
432	+		return true, nil
433	+	}
434	+	if err == io.EOF || err == nil {
435	+		return false, fmt.Errorf("session file is empty")
436	+	}
437	+	return false, err
438	+}

440	new file mode 100644

442	--- /dev/null
443	+++ b/specstory-cli/pkg/cmd/resume_test.go
444	@@ -0,0 +1,382 @@
445	+package cmd
446	+
447	+import (
448	+	"context"
449	+	"io"
450	+	"os"
451	+	"path/filepath"
452	+	"testing"
453	+	"time"
454	+
455	+	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/sessionindex"
456	+	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
457	+	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
458	+)
459	+
460	+func TestFTSQuery(t *testing.T) {
461	+	cases := []struct{ name, in, want string }{
462	+		{"empty", "", ""},
463	+		{"single word is a prefix", "thank", "thank*"},
464	+		{"bare words are independent prefixes", "thank you", "thank* you*"},
465	+		{"punctuation in a bare word splits into an adjacency phrase", "max-cpu!", "max + cpu*"},
466	+		{"a bare filename is an adjacency phrase with prefix last", "poem.txt", "poem + txt*"},
467	+		{"a quoted filename is a committed phrase, no prefix", `"poem.txt"`, `"poem txt"`},
468	+		{"an open-quoted filename keeps the last token a prefix", `"poem.txt`, "poem + txt*"},
469	+		{"closed phrase is exact adjacency", `"thank you"`, `"thank you"`},
470	+		{"closed single-word phrase has no prefix", `"thank"`, `"thank"`},
471	+		{"open phrase keeps last word a prefix", `"thank yo`, "thank + yo*"},
472	+		{"open single-word phrase is a prefix", `"thank`, "thank*"},
473	+		{"phrase plus trailing bare word", `"thank you" now`, `"thank you" now*`},
474	+		{"bare word before a closed phrase", `please "thank you"`, `please* "thank you"`},
475	+		{"whitespace inside a phrase is collapsed", `"thank    you"`, `"thank you"`},
476	+	}
477	+	for _, c := range cases {
478	+		t.Run(c.name, func(t *testing.T) {
479	+			if got := ftsQuery(c.in); got != c.want {
480	+				t.Errorf("ftsQuery(%q) = %q, want %q", c.in, got, c.want)
481	+			}
482	+		})
483	+	}
484	+}
485	+
486	+func TestQueryReady(t *testing.T) {
487	+	cases := []struct {
488	+		in   string
489	+		want bool
490	+	}{
491	+		{"", false},
492	+		{"a", false},   // one alnum char is below minQueryLen
493	+		{`"a"`, false}, // quotes don't count toward the threshold
494	+		{"ab", true},   // two alnum chars
495	+		{"a!", false},  // punctuation doesn't count
496	+		{"a b", true},  // two alnum chars across words
497	+		{`"hi"`, true}, // alnum inside quotes counts
498	+		{"日本", true},   // letters in other scripts count
499	+	}
500	+	for _, c := range cases {
501	+		if got := queryReady(c.in); got != c.want {
502	+			t.Errorf("queryReady(%q) = %v, want %v", c.in, got, c.want)
503	+		}
504	+	}
505	+}
506	+
507	+func TestShortID(t *testing.T) {
508	+	cases := []struct{ in, want string }{
509	+		{"short", "short"},
510	+		{"1234567890abc", "1234567890abc"}, // <= 13 chars: unchanged
511	+		{"1234567890abcdef", "12345...bcdef"},
512	+	}
513	+	for _, c := range cases {
514	+		if got := shortID(c.in); got != c.want {
515	+			t.Errorf("shortID(%q) = %q, want %q", c.in, got, c.want)
516	+		}
517	+	}
518	+}
519	+
520	+func TestWriteReconstructedSession(t *testing.T) {
521	+	dir := t.TempDir()
522	+	path := filepath.Join(dir, "sub", "session.jsonl")
523	+	content := []byte(`{"type":"user"}` + "\n")
524	+
525	+	if err := writeReconstructedSession(path, content); err != nil {
526	+		t.Fatalf("writeReconstructedSession: %v", err)
527	+	}
528	+
529	+	got, err := os.ReadFile(path)
530	+	if err != nil {
531	+		t.Fatalf("reading written file: %v", err)
532	+	}
533	+	if string(got) != string(content) {
534	+		t.Errorf("content = %q, want %q", got, content)
535	+	}
536	+
537	+	// The atomic-write temp file must not be left behind in the target directory.
538	+	entries, err := os.ReadDir(filepath.Dir(path))
539	+	if err != nil {
540	+		t.Fatal(err)
541	+	}
542	+	for _, e := range entries {
543	+		if e.Name() != "session.jsonl" {
544	+			t.Errorf("unexpected leftover file in target dir: %q", e.Name())
545	+		}
546	+	}
547	+}
548	+
549	+func TestSessionFileReadable(t *testing.T) {
550	+	dir := t.TempDir()
551	+
552	+	missing := filepath.Join(dir, "nope.jsonl")
553	+	if ok, _ := sessionFileReadable(missing); ok {
554	+		t.Error("missing file reported readable")
555	+	}
556	+
557	+	empty := filepath.Join(dir, "empty.jsonl")
558	+	if err := os.WriteFile(empty, nil, 0o644); err != nil {
559	+		t.Fatal(err)
560	+	}
561	+	if ok, _ := sessionFileReadable(empty); ok {
562	+		t.Error("empty file reported readable")
563	+	}
564	+
565	+	full := filepath.Join(dir, "full.jsonl")
566	+	if err := os.WriteFile(full, []byte(`{"type":"user"}`+"\n"), 0o644); err != nil {
567	+		t.Fatal(err)
568	+	}
569	+	if ok, err := sessionFileReadable(full); !ok {
570	+		t.Errorf("file with content reported not readable: %v", err)
571	+	}
572	+}
573	+
574	+func TestWaitForSessionFileVisible(t *testing.T) {
575	+	dir := t.TempDir()
576	+
577	+	present := filepath.Join(dir, "present.jsonl")
578	+	if err := os.WriteFile(present, []byte(`{"type":"user"}`+"\n"), 0o644); err != nil {
579	+		t.Fatal(err)
580	+	}
581	+	if err := waitForSessionFileVisible(present, time.Second); err != nil {
582	+		t.Errorf("expected an already-present file to be visible: %v", err)
583	+	}
584	+
585	+	// A file that never appears must time out with a diagnostic error rather than block forever.
586	+	if err := waitForSessionFileVisible(filepath.Join(dir, "never.jsonl"), 100*time.Millisecond); err == nil {
587	+		t.Error("expected timeout error for a file that never becomes visible")
588	+	}
589	+}
590	+
591	+// TestBeginResumeWithPresetSkipsTargetStep verifies the `resume <agent>` contract: when a
592	+// target agent was pre-selected, choosing a session resumes immediately into that agent
593	+// rather than prompting for a target.
594	+func TestBeginResumeWithPresetSkipsTargetStep(t *testing.T) {
595	+	sess := &sessionindex.Session{SessionID: "s1", Agent: "codex"}
596	+	m := sessionTUI{presetTo: "claude"}
597	+
598	+	next, cmd := m.beginResume(sess)
599	+	rm := next.(sessionTUI)
600	+
601	+	if cmd == nil {
602	+		t.Error("expected an immediate quit command when a target is preset")
603	+	}
604	+	if rm.mode == modeTarget {
605	+		t.Error("a preset target must skip the target-selection step")
606	+	}
607	+	if rm.result.session != sess {
608	+		t.Errorf("result session = %v, want the chosen session", rm.result.session)
609	+	}
610	+	if rm.result.targetID != "claude" {
611	+		t.Errorf("result target = %q, want %q", rm.result.targetID, "claude")
612	+	}
613	+}
614	+
615	+// TestBeginResumeWithoutPresetEntersTargetStep verifies the default flow: with no preset,
616	+// choosing a session advances to the target-selection step (no immediate resume).
617	+func TestBeginResumeWithoutPresetEntersTargetStep(t *testing.T) {
618	+	sess := &sessionindex.Session{SessionID: "s1", Agent: "codex"}
619	+	m := sessionTUI{}
620	+
621	+	next, cmd := m.beginResume(sess)
622	+	rm := next.(sessionTUI)
623	+
624	+	if cmd != nil {
625	+		t.Error("expected no immediate quit without a preset target")
626	+	}
627	+	if rm.mode != modeTarget {
628	+		t.Errorf("mode = %v, want modeTarget", rm.mode)
629	+	}
630	+	if rm.chosen != sess {
631	+		t.Error("chosen session must be recorded before target selection")
632	+	}
633	+}
634	+
635	+// fakeProvider is a minimal spi.Provider for exercising prepareResumeTarget. It records
636	+// the projectPath it is asked to load the source session from, and reconstructs into a
637	+// caller-provided directory so the write/visibility tail succeeds.
638	+type fakeProvider struct {
639	+	name        string
640	+	gotLoadPath string // projectPath captured from GetAgentChatSession
641	+	nativeDir   string // where NativeSessionPath places the reconstructed file
642	+}
643	+
644	+func (f *fakeProvider) Name() string                  { return f.name }
645	+func (f *fakeProvider) Check(string) spi.CheckResult  { return spi.CheckResult{Success: true} }
646	+func (f *fakeProvider) DetectAgent(string, bool) bool { return false }
647	+
648	+func (f *fakeProvider) GetAgentChatSession(projectPath, sessionID string, _ bool) (*spi.AgentChatSession, error) {
649	+	f.gotLoadPath = projectPath
650	+	return &spi.AgentChatSession{SessionID: sessionID, SessionData: &schema.SessionData{}}, nil
651	+}
652	+
653	+func (f *fakeProvider) ReconstructSession(*schema.SessionData, spi.ReconstructOptions) (*spi.ReconstructedSession, error) {
654	+	return &spi.ReconstructedSession{
655	+		SessionID: "new-session-id",
656	+		Filename:  "reconstructed.jsonl",
657	+		Content:   []byte(`{"type":"user"}` + "\n"),
658	+	}, nil
659	+}
660	+
661	+func (f *fakeProvider) NativeSessionPath(_ string, filename string) (string, error) {
662	+	return filepath.Join(f.nativeDir, filename), nil
663	+}
664	+
665	+// Unused-by-these-tests interface methods.
666	+func (f *fakeProvider) GetAgentChatSessions(string, bool, spi.ProgressCallback) ([]spi.AgentChatSession, error) {
667	+	return nil, nil
668	+}
669	+func (f *fakeProvider) ListAgentChatSessions(string) ([]spi.SessionMetadata, error) { return nil, nil }
670	+func (f *fakeProvider) ExecAgentAndWatch(string, string, string, bool, func(*spi.AgentChatSession)) error {
671	+	return nil
672	+}
673	+func (f *fakeProvider) WatchAgent(context.Context, string, bool, func(*spi.AgentChatSession)) error {
674	+	return nil
675	+}
676	+func (f *fakeProvider) ListAllAgentChatSessions() ([]spi.GlobalSessionRef, error) { return nil, nil }
677	+
678	+// TestPrepareResumeTargetLoadsSourceFromOriginCwd guards the cross-project resume fix:
679	+// the source session must be loaded from the directory it was launched in (fromCwd),
680	+// not the user's current cwd, while the reconstructed file is still written under the
681	+// current cwd. Regression test for "source session ... has no data to reconstruct" when
682	+// resuming a session picked from another project via the all-projects browser.
683	+func TestPrepareResumeTargetLoadsSourceFromOriginCwd(t *testing.T) {
684	+	nativeDir := t.TempDir()
685	+	from := &fakeProvider{name: "Codex CLI"}
686	+	to := &fakeProvider{name: "Claude Code", nativeDir: nativeDir}
687	+
688	+	const originCwd = "/Users/jake/dev/tmp/blog-site"
689	+	const currentCwd = "/Users/jake/dev/specstory-website"
690	+
691	+	plan := &resumePlan{
692	+		from:      from,
693	+		fromID:    "codex",
694	+		sessionID: "019c4cdd-917a-74e3-9b2e-fdb45e9eddc5",
695	+		fromCwd:   originCwd,
696	+		to:        to,
697	+		toID:      "claude",
698	+	}
699	+
700	+	newID, err := prepareResumeTarget(plan, currentCwd, io.Discard)
701	+	if err != nil {
702	+		t.Fatalf("prepareResumeTarget returned error: %v", err)
703	+	}
704	+	if newID != "new-session-id" {
705	+		t.Errorf("resume target id = %q, want %q", newID, "new-session-id")
706	+	}
707	+	if from.gotLoadPath != originCwd {
708	+		t.Errorf("source loaded from %q, want origin cwd %q (current cwd %q must not be used)",
709	+			from.gotLoadPath, originCwd, currentCwd)
710	+	}
711	+}
712	+
713	+// TestPrepareResumeTargetFallsBackToCurrentCwd verifies that when the index row carries
714	+// no origin cwd (older rows), the source load falls back to the current cwd rather than
715	+// loading from an empty path.
716	+func TestPrepareResumeTargetFallsBackToCurrentCwd(t *testing.T) {
717	+	nativeDir := t.TempDir()
718	+	from := &fakeProvider{name: "Codex CLI"}
719	+	to := &fakeProvider{name: "Claude Code", nativeDir: nativeDir}
720	+
721	+	const currentCwd = "/Users/jake/dev/blog-site"
722	+
723	+	plan := &resumePlan{
724	+		from:      from,
725	+		fromID:    "codex",
726	+		sessionID: "sid",
727	+		fromCwd:   "", // older index row: no origin cwd recorded
728	+		to:        to,
729	+		toID:      "claude",
730	+	}
731	+
732	+	if _, err := prepareResumeTarget(plan, currentCwd, io.Discard); err != nil {
733	+		t.Fatalf("prepareResumeTarget returned error: %v", err)
734	+	}
735	+	if from.gotLoadPath != currentCwd {
736	+		t.Errorf("source loaded from %q, want fallback to current cwd %q", from.gotLoadPath, currentCwd)
737	+	}
738	+}
739	+
740	+// TestLiveIndexerRecord exercises real-time indexing (watch/run/resume): a live session is
741	+// upserted into the index, its FTS body is searchable, repeat records with no new activity are
742	+// skipped, and new activity updates the same row in place.
743	+func TestLiveIndexerRecord(t *testing.T) {
744	+	store, err := sessionindex.Open(filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "sessions.db"))
745	+	if err != nil {
746	+		t.Fatalf("open index: %v", err)
747	+	}
748	+	defer func() { _ = store.Close() }()
749	+
750	+	li := &LiveIndexer{
751	+		store:       store,
752	+		cwd:         "/work/proj",
753	+		projectID:   "proj-1",
754	+		projectName: "proj",
755	+		indexedAt:   "2026-06-25T00:00:00Z",
756	+		lastSeen:    map[string]string{},
757	+	}
758	+
759	+	msg := func(role, ts, text string) schema.Message {
760	+		return schema.Message{
761	+			Role:      role,
762	+			Timestamp: ts,
763	+			Content:   []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: text}},
764	+		}
765	+	}
766	+	sess := func(msgs ...schema.Message) *spi.AgentChatSession {
767	+		return &spi.AgentChatSession{
768	+			SessionID: "sess-1",
769	+			CreatedAt: "2026-06-25T00:00:00Z",
770	+			Slug:      "hello-world",
771	+			SessionData: &schema.SessionData{
772	+				Provider:  schema.ProviderInfo{Name: "Claude Code"},
773	+				Exchanges: []schema.Exchange{{Messages: msgs}},
774	+			},
775	+		}
776	+	}
777	+	user1 := msg(schema.RoleUser, "2026-06-25T00:01:00Z", "index this please")
778	+	agent1 := msg(schema.RoleAgent, "2026-06-25T00:02:00Z", "done indexing")
779	+
780	+	// First record: one user + one agent message → one fully-derived row.
781	+	li.Record("claude", sess(user1, agent1))
782	+	rows, err := store.ListByProject("proj-1")
783	+	if err != nil {
784	+		t.Fatalf("list: %v", err)
785	+	}
786	+	if len(rows) != 1 {
787	+		t.Fatalf("want 1 row, got %d", len(rows))
788	+	}
789	+	r := rows[0]
790	+	if r.Agent != "claude" || r.SessionID != "sess-1" || r.OriginCwd != "/work/proj" {
791	+		t.Errorf("identity wrong: agent=%q session=%q cwd=%q", r.Agent, r.SessionID, r.OriginCwd)
792	+	}
793	+	if r.UserTurns != 1 || r.TotalTurns != 2 {
794	+		t.Errorf("turns = %d/%d, want 1/2", r.UserTurns, r.TotalTurns)
795	+	}
796	+	if r.UpdatedAt != "2026-06-25T00:02:00Z" {
797	+		t.Errorf("updatedAt = %q, want last message timestamp", r.UpdatedAt)
798	+	}
799	+
800	+	// The conversation body is full-text searchable immediately.
801	+	if hits, _ := store.Search(ftsQuery("indexing"), "proj-1"); len(hits) != 1 {
802	+		t.Errorf("search for body word returned %d hits, want 1", len(hits))
803	+	}
804	+
805	+	// Dedup guard: re-recording with no new activity is a no-op.
806	+	li.Record("claude", sess(user1, agent1))
807	+	if rows, _ := store.ListByProject("proj-1"); len(rows) != 1 {
808	+		t.Errorf("dedup: want 1 row, got %d", len(rows))
809	+	}
810	+
811	+	// New activity (a later message) updates the same row in place.
812	+	li.Record("claude", sess(user1, agent1, msg(schema.RoleUser, "2026-06-25T00:03:00Z", "more")))
813	+	rows, _ = store.ListByProject("proj-1")
814	+	if len(rows) != 1 {
815	+		t.Fatalf("want 1 row after update, got %d", len(rows))
816	+	}
817	+	if rows[0].UserTurns != 2 || rows[0].TotalTurns != 3 || rows[0].UpdatedAt != "2026-06-25T00:03:00Z" {
818	+		t.Errorf("after update: turns=%d/%d updatedAt=%q, want 2/3 and advanced timestamp",
819	+			rows[0].UserTurns, rows[0].TotalTurns, rows[0].UpdatedAt)
820	+	}
821	+
822	+	// A nil indexer (index failed to open) is safe to use.
823	+	var nilLI *LiveIndexer
824	+	nilLI.Record("claude", sess(user1))
825	+	nilLI.Close()
826	+}
827	diff --git a/specstory-cli/pkg/cmd/search.go b/specstory-cli/pkg/cmd/search.go
828	new file mode 100644
829	index 0000000..79b3add
830	--- /dev/null
831	+++ b/specstory-cli/pkg/cmd/search.go
832	@@ -0,0 +1,121 @@
833	+package cmd
834	+
835	+import (
836	+	"fmt"
837	+	"os"
838	+	"path/filepath"
839	+	"strings"
840	+
841	+	tea "charm.land/bubbletea/v2"
842	+	"github.com/spf13/cobra"
843	+
844	+	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/config"
845	+	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/factory"
846	+	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/utils"
847	+)
848	+
849	+// CreateSearchCommand builds `specstory search [query…]`. It is the same interactive UI as
850	+// `specstory resume` (see sessionTUI), entered straight into the all-projects full-text
851	+// search with the input focused: type to search, `space` previews a match (glamour-rendered),
852	+// and `r` resumes it through the same launch path as `resume`. See docs/SESSION-SEARCH.md.
853	+func CreateSearchCommand() *cobra.Command {
854	+	return &cobra.Command{
855	+		Use:   "search [query…]",
856	+		Short: "Search and read your past coding-agent sessions",
857	+		Long: `Full-text search across every session SpecStory has indexed, then read the match.
858	+
859	+'search' opens an interactive search of your session history. Type to search, press space to
860	+preview a session (rendered for the terminal), and press 'r' to resume it in an agent. Anything
861	+after the command pre-seeds the query, e.g. 'specstory search max cpu'.`,
862	+		Args: cobra.ArbitraryArgs,
863	+		RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
864	+			registry := factory.GetRegistry()
865	+			initialQuery := strings.TrimSpace(strings.Join(args, " "))
866	+
867	+			cwd, err := os.Getwd()
868	+			if err != nil {
869	+				return err
870	+			}
871	+
872	+			store, builtFresh, err := openOrBuildResumeIndex()
873	+			if err != nil {
874	+				return err
875	+			}
876	+			defer func() { _ = store.Close() }()
877	+
878	+			if total, _ := store.Count(); total == 0 {
879	+				fprintln(os.Stderr, "\nNo agent sessions indexed yet. Run an agent, then try again (or `specstory reindex`).")
880	+				return nil
881	+			}
882	+
883	+			homeID, homeName, idErr := utils.ComputeProjectID(cwd)
884	+			if idErr != nil || homeID == "" {
885	+				homeID, homeName = unknownProjectID, filepath.Base(cwd)
886	+			}
887	+			homeSessions, _ := store.ListByProject(homeID)
888	+
889	+			agents := map[string]agentMeta{}
890	+			var installed []agentChoice
891	+			for _, id := range registry.ListIDs() {
892	+				prov, err := registry.Get(id)
893	+				if err != nil {
894	+					continue
895	+				}
896	+				agents[id] = agentMeta{name: prov.Name(), accent: colorForAgent(id)}
897	+				if prov.Check("").Success {
898	+					installed = append(installed, agentChoice{id: id, provider: prov})
899	+				}
900	+			}
901	+
902	+			viewMode, lastAgent := "dense", ""
903	+			if cfg, _ := config.Load(nil); cfg != nil {
904	+				viewMode = cfg.GetResumeViewMode()
905	+				lastAgent = cfg.GetResumeLastAgent()
906	+			}
907	+
908	+			// `search` is the same TUI as `resume`, entered straight into the all-projects
909	+			// FTS with the input focused. See newSessionTUI / sessionTUIOpts.
910	+			model := newSessionTUI(store, registry, homeID, homeName, homeSessions, agents, installed, sessionTUIOpts{
911	+				title:         "SpecStory Search",
912	+				lastAgent:     lastAgent,
913	+				viewMode:      viewMode,
914	+				initialQuery:  initialQuery,
915	+				startInSearch: true,
916	+			})
917	+			p := tea.NewProgram(model)
918	+			cancelWarm := startIndexWarm(p, homeID, builtFresh)
919	+			final, err := p.Run()
920	+			cancelWarm() // stop warming the moment search exits (never block on it)
921	+			if err != nil {
922	+				return fmt.Errorf("search failed: %w", err)
923	+			}
924	+			rm := final.(sessionTUI)
925	+			if rm.result.cancelled || rm.result.session == nil {
926	+				return nil // cancelled: nothing to launch
927	+			}
928	+
929	+			// The user asked to resume a found session — launch via the shared path.
930	+			fromProv, err := registry.Get(rm.result.session.Agent)
931	+			if err != nil {
932	+				return fmt.Errorf("unknown source agent %q: %w", rm.result.session.Agent, err)
933	+			}
934	+			toProv, err := registry.Get(rm.result.targetID)
935	+			if err != nil {
936	+				return fmt.Errorf("unknown target agent %q: %w", rm.result.targetID, err)
937	+			}
938	+			_ = config.SaveResumePrefs(viewMode, rm.result.targetID)
939	+
940	+			plan := &resumePlan{
941	+				from:      fromProv,
942	+				fromID:    rm.result.session.Agent,
943	+				sessionID: rm.result.session.SessionID,
944	+				// A search hit can be from another project; resume must load the source from
945	+				// its original cwd, not the current directory.
946	+				fromCwd: rm.result.session.OriginCwd,
947	+				to:      toProv,
948	+				toID:    rm.result.targetID,
949	+			}
950	+			return launchResume(plan, cwd, resumeLaunchOpts{useUTC: true})
951	+		},
952	+	}
953	+}

959	 				fmt.Println()
