```text
1	package cmd
2	
3	import (
4		"strings"
5		"testing"
6	
7		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
8	)
9	
10	func userMsg(ts, text string) schema.Message {
11		return schema.Message{Role: schema.RoleUser, Timestamp: ts,
12			Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: text}}}
13	}
14	func agentMsg(ts, text string) schema.Message {
15		return schema.Message{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Timestamp: ts,
16			Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: text}}}
17	}
18	
19	func sampleData() *schema.SessionData {
20		return &schema.SessionData{
21			Exchanges: []schema.Exchange{
22				{Messages: []schema.Message{userMsg("2026-06-18T10:00:00Z", "fix the bug"), agentMsg("2026-06-18T10:01:00Z", "on it")}},
23				{Messages: []schema.Message{userMsg("2026-06-18T10:05:00Z", "now ship it"), agentMsg("2026-06-18T10:06:00Z", "done")}},
24			},
25		}
26	}
27	
28	func TestCountTurns(t *testing.T) {
29		user, total := countTurns(sampleData())
30		if user != 2 {
31			t.Errorf("user turns = %d; want 2", user)
32		}
33		if total != 4 {
34			t.Errorf("total turns = %d; want 4", total)
35		}
36	}
37	
38	func TestLastTimestamp(t *testing.T) {
39		if got := lastTimestamp(sampleData()); got != "2026-06-18T10:06:00Z" {
40			t.Errorf("lastTimestamp = %q; want the final message ts", got)
41		}
42		// Empty when no messages carry a timestamp.
43		noTS := &schema.SessionData{Exchanges: []schema.Exchange{{Messages: []schema.Message{userMsg("", "hi")}}}}
44		if got := lastTimestamp(noTS); got != "" {
45			t.Errorf("lastTimestamp with no timestamps = %q; want empty", got)
46		}
47	}
48	
49	func TestFlattenBody(t *testing.T) {
50		body := flattenBody(sampleData())
51		for _, want := range []string{"fix the bug", "on it", "now ship it", "done"} {
52			if !strings.Contains(body, want) {
53				t.Errorf("flattened body missing %q; got %q", want, body)
54			}
55		}
56	}
57	
58	func TestProjectIDCacheUnknownForEmptyCwd(t *testing.T) {
59		c := &projectIDCache{m: map[string]projectIDName{}}
60		id, name := c.resolve("")
61		if id != unknownProjectID || name != "" {
62			t.Errorf("resolve(\"\") = (%q,%q); want (unknown, \"\")", id, name)
63		}
64	}
65	
66	func TestProjectIDCacheMemoizes(t *testing.T) {
67		c := &projectIDCache{m: map[string]projectIDName{}}
68		// Pre-seed the cache and confirm resolve returns it without recomputing.
69		c.m["/some/repo"] = projectIDName{id: "abcd-1234", name: "repo"}
70		id, name := c.resolve("/some/repo")
71		if id != "abcd-1234" || name != "repo" {
72			t.Errorf("cached resolve = (%q,%q); want the seeded value", id, name)
73		}
74	}
75	
76	func TestProgressBar(t *testing.T) {
77		cases := []struct {
78			done, total int64
79			wantFull    int // number of █ expected
80		}{
81			{0, 10, 0},
82			{10, 10, 14},
83			{5, 10, 7},
84			{1, 0, 0},    // zero total → no divide-by-zero, empty bar
85			{20, 10, 14}, // clamped, never overflows
86		}
87		for _, tc := range cases {
88			got := progressBar(tc.done, tc.total)
89			if full := strings.Count(got, "█"); full != tc.wantFull {
90				t.Errorf("progressBar(%d,%d) had %d full cells; want %d (%q)", tc.done, tc.total, full, tc.wantFull, got)
91			}
92		}
93	}
94	
95	func TestSummarizeCounts(t *testing.T) {
96		ids := []string{"claude", "codex", "cursor"}
97		got := summarizeCounts(ids, map[string]int{"claude": 683, "codex": 0, "cursor": 49})
98		if got != "claude 683 · cursor 49" {
99			t.Errorf("summarizeCounts = %q; want zeros omitted, registry order", got)
100		}
101	}
102

21		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/config"
22		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/provenance"
23		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/session"
24		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
25		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/factory"
26		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/utils"
27	)
28	
29	// Best-effort UI writers. The destination is the user's terminal, so write
30	// errors are not actionable and are intentionally ignored.
31	func fprintf(w io.Writer, format string, a ...any) { _, _ = fmt.Fprintf(w, format, a...) }
32	func fprintln(w io.Writer, a ...any)               { _, _ = fmt.Fprintln(w, a...) }
33	func fprint(w io.Writer, a ...any)                 { _, _ = fmt.Fprint(w, a...) }
34	
35	// resumePlan captures the user's choices from the interactive selection.
36	type resumePlan struct {
37		from      spi.Provider
38		fromID    string
39		sessionID string
40		// fromCwd is the directory the source session was originally launched from
41		// (the index's origin_cwd). It can differ from the user's current cwd when a
42		// session is picked from another project via the all-projects browser. The
43		// source data load must use this, because some providers (e.g. Codex) locate a
44		// session by matching its recorded cwd — looking under the current cwd would
45		// find nothing. Empty for older index rows; callers fall back to the current cwd.
46		fromCwd string
47		to      spi.Provider
48		toID    string
49	}
50	
51	// agentChoice pairs a registry ID with its provider.
52	type agentChoice struct {
53		id       string
54		provider spi.Provider
55	}
56	
57	// CreateResumeCommand builds the `specstory resume` command. It opens the interactive
58	// picker (a Bubble Tea TUI over the sessions.db index — see docs/RESUME-TUI.md): browse
59	// the current project's sessions across all agents, pick one, choose a target agent, then
60	// reconstruct (cross-agent) or native-resume (same agent) and launch with auto-save.
61	func CreateResumeCommand(cloudURL *string, localTimeZone bool, debugDir string) *cobra.Command {
62		longDesc := `Resume a past coding-agent session — in the same agent, or a different one.
63	
64	'resume' opens an interactive picker of the sessions in the current project across all
65	agents. Pick one, choose which installed agent to continue it in, and go. Resuming into a
66	different agent reconstructs the conversation into that agent's native format first.
67	
68	Pass an agent to set the resume target up front, e.g. 'specstory resume codex' — the picker
69	then skips the target-selection step and resumes straight into that agent. The agent must be
70	a known, installed provider, or the command errors.`
71	
72		resumeCmd := &cobra.Command{
73			Use:   "resume [agent]",
74			Short: "Resume a past session, optionally in a different agent",
75			Long:  longDesc,
76			Args:  cobra.MaximumNArgs(1),
77			RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
78				config.EnsureDefaultProjectConfig()
79				slog.Info("Running in resume mode")
80	
81				registry := factory.GetRegistry()
82	
83				// Optional positional arg pre-selects the target agent (e.g. `resume codex`).
84				// An unknown agent name should fail the run rather than be silently ignored, so
85				// validate it against the registry up front. When set and valid, the picker skips
86				// the target-selection step entirely (see selectResumeViaTUI / beginResume); whether
87				// the named agent is actually installed is checked there, where the installed set
88				// is known.
89				presetTarget := ""
90				if len(args) == 1 {
91					presetTarget = strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(args[0]))
92					if _, err := registry.Get(presetTarget); err != nil {
93						return utils.ValidationError{Message: fmt.Sprintf(
94							"unknown agent %q. Valid agents: %s", presetTarget, registry.GetProviderList())}
95					}
96				}
97	
98				// Read flags (subset mirroring run/watch).
99				debugRaw, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("debug-raw")
100				useLocalTimezone, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("local-time-zone")
```
</details></tool-use>

35	// resumePlan captures the user's choices from the interactive selection.
36	type resumePlan struct {
37		from      spi.Provider
38		fromID    string
39		sessionID string
40		// fromCwd is the directory the source session was originally launched from
41		// (the index's origin_cwd). It can differ from the user's current cwd when a
42		// session is picked from another project via the all-projects browser. The
43		// source data load must use this, because some providers (e.g. Codex) locate a
44		// session by matching its recorded cwd — looking under the current cwd would
45		// find nothing. Empty for older index rows; callers fall back to the current cwd.
46		fromCwd string
47		to      spi.Provider
48		toID    string
49	}
50	
51	// agentChoice pairs a registry ID with its provider.
52	type agentChoice struct {

56	
57	// CreateResumeCommand builds the `specstory resume` command. It opens the interactive
58	// picker (a Bubble Tea TUI over the sessions.db index — see docs/RESUME-TUI.md): browse
59	// the current project's sessions across all agents, pick one, choose a target agent, then
60	// reconstruct (cross-agent) or native-resume (same agent) and launch with auto-save.
61	func CreateResumeCommand(cloudURL *string, localTimeZone bool, debugDir string) *cobra.Command {
62		longDesc := `Resume a past coding-agent session — in the same agent, or a different one.
63	
64	'resume' opens an interactive picker of the sessions in the current project across all
65	agents. Pick one, choose which installed agent to continue it in, and go. Resuming into a
66	different agent reconstructs the conversation into that agent's native format first.
67	
68	Pass an agent to set the resume target up front, e.g. 'specstory resume codex' — the picker
69	then skips the target-selection step and resumes straight into that agent. The agent must be
70	a known, installed provider, or the command errors.`
71	
72		resumeCmd := &cobra.Command{
73			Use:   "resume [agent]",
74			Short: "Resume a past session, optionally in a different agent",
75			Long:  longDesc,
76			Args:  cobra.MaximumNArgs(1),
77			RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
78				config.EnsureDefaultProjectConfig()
79				slog.Info("Running in resume mode")
80	
81				registry := factory.GetRegistry()
82	
83				// Optional positional arg pre-selects the target agent (e.g. `resume codex`).
84				// An unknown agent name should fail the run rather than be silently ignored, so
85				// validate it against the registry up front. When set and valid, the picker skips
86				// the target-selection step entirely (see selectResumeViaTUI / beginResume); whether
87				// the named agent is actually installed is checked there, where the installed set
88				// is known.
89				presetTarget := ""
90				if len(args) == 1 {
91					presetTarget = strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(args[0]))
92					if _, err := registry.Get(presetTarget); err != nil {
93						return utils.ValidationError{Message: fmt.Sprintf(
94							"unknown agent %q. Valid agents: %s", presetTarget, registry.GetProviderList())}
95					}
96				}
97	
98				// Read flags (subset mirroring run/watch).
99				debugRaw, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("debug-raw")
100				useLocalTimezone, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("local-time-zone")
101				useUTC := !useLocalTimezone
102				outputDir, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("output-dir")
103				flagDebugDir, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("debug-dir")
104				noCloudSync, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-cloud-sync")
105				onlyCloudSync, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("only-cloud-sync")
106				provenanceEnabled, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("provenance")
107				noTelemetryPrompts, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-telemetry-prompts")
108	
109				if flagDebugDir != "" {
110					spi.SetDebugBaseDir(flagDebugDir)
111				}
112	
113				cwd, err := os.Getwd()
114				if err != nil {
115					slog.Error("Failed to get current working directory", "error", err)
116					return err
117				}
118	
119				// Open (building if needed) the session index and run the interactive picker.
120				// builtFresh = the index was just rebuilt in the foreground, so the picker skips
121				// the background index warm (it would be redundant).
122				store, builtFresh, err := openOrBuildResumeIndex()
123				if err != nil {
124					return err
125				}
126				defer func() { _ = store.Close() }()
127	
128				projectID, projectName, idErr := utils.ComputeProjectID(cwd)
129				if idErr != nil || projectID == "" {
130					projectID, projectName = unknownProjectID, filepath.Base(cwd)
131				}
132	
133				plan, err := selectResumeViaTUI(registry, store, projectID, projectName, presetTarget, builtFresh)
134				if err != nil {
135					return err
136				}
137				if plan == nil {
138					// User quit or nothing to resume; the picker already explained why.
139					return nil
140				}
141	
142				return launchResume(plan, cwd, resumeLaunchOpts{
143					outputDir:          outputDir,
144					flagDebugDir:       flagDebugDir,
145					debugRaw:           debugRaw,
146					useUTC:             useUTC,
147					noCloudSync:        noCloudSync,
148					onlyCloudSync:      onlyCloudSync,
149					provenanceEnabled:  provenanceEnabled,
150					noTelemetryPrompts: noTelemetryPrompts,
151				})
152			},
153		}
154	
155		resumeCmd.Flags().String("output-dir", "", "custom output directory for markdown files (default: ./.specstory/history)")
156		resumeCmd.Flags().String("debug-dir", debugDir, "custom output directory for debug data (default: ./.specstory/debug)")
157		resumeCmd.Flags().Bool("only-cloud-sync", false, "skip local markdown file saves, only upload to cloud (requires authentication)")
158		resumeCmd.Flags().Bool("no-cloud-sync", false, "disable cloud sync functionality")
159		resumeCmd.Flags().Bool("debug-raw", false, "debug mode to output pretty-printed raw data files")
160		_ = resumeCmd.Flags().MarkHidden("debug-raw")
161		resumeCmd.Flags().Bool("local-time-zone", localTimeZone, "use local timezone for file name and content timestamps (when not present: UTC)")
162		resumeCmd.Flags().Bool("provenance", false, "enable AI provenance tracking (correlate file changes to agent activity)")
163		_ = resumeCmd.Flags().MarkHidden("provenance")
164		resumeCmd.Flags().StringVar(cloudURL, "cloud-url", "", "override the default cloud API base URL")
165		_ = resumeCmd.Flags().MarkHidden("cloud-url")
166		resumeCmd.Flags().Bool("no-telemetry-prompts", false, "exclude prompt text from telemetry spans, if telemetry is enabled")
167	
168		return resumeCmd
169	}
170	
171	// resumeLaunchOpts carries the resume launch configuration shared by `resume` and
172	// `search` (whose `r` action resumes a found session through the same path).
173	type resumeLaunchOpts struct {
174		outputDir          string
175		flagDebugDir       string
176		debugRaw           bool
177		useUTC             bool
178		noCloudSync        bool
179		onlyCloudSync      bool
180		provenanceEnabled  bool
181		noTelemetryPrompts bool
182	}
183	
184	// launchResume reconstructs (cross-agent) or natively resumes the planned session and
185	// runs the agent with auto-save + provenance — the shared tail of `resume` and `search`.
186	func launchResume(plan *resumePlan, cwd string, o resumeLaunchOpts) error {
187		// Setup output configuration and project identity (needed for auto-save + cloud).
188		outConfig, err := utils.SetupOutputConfig(o.outputDir, o.flagDebugDir)
189		if err != nil {
190			return err
191		}
192		if err := utils.EnsureHistoryDirectoryExists(outConfig); err != nil {
193			return err
194		}
195		if _, err := utils.NewProjectIdentityManager(cwd).EnsureProjectIdentity(); err != nil {
196			slog.Error("Failed to ensure project identity", "error", err)
197		}
198	
199		CheckAndWarnAuthentication(o.noCloudSync)
200		if o.onlyCloudSync && !cloud.IsAuthenticated() {
201			return utils.ValidationError{Message: "--only-cloud-sync requires authentication. Please run 'specstory login' first"}
202		}
203	
204		analytics.SetAgentProviders([]string{plan.to.Name()})
205		analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventResumeActivated, analytics.Properties{
206			"from_provider": plan.fromID,
207			"to_provider":   plan.toID,
208			"cross_agent":   plan.fromID != plan.toID,
209		})
210	
211		resumeSessionID, err := prepareResumeTarget(plan, cwd, os.Stdout)
212		if err != nil {
213			return err
214		}
215	
216		// Context for graceful cancellation (Ctrl+C).
217		ctx, cancel := signal.NotifyContext(context.Background(), os.Interrupt, syscall.SIGTERM)
218		defer cancel()
219	
220		// Provenance infrastructure (mirrors run/watch).
221		provenanceEngine, provenanceCleanup, err := provenance.StartEngine(o.provenanceEnabled)
222		if err != nil {
223			return err
224		}
225		defer provenanceCleanup()
226		fsCleanup, err := provenance.StartFSWatcher(ctx, provenanceEngine, cwd)
227		if err != nil {
228			return err
229		}
230		defer fsCleanup()
231	
232		// Keep sessions.db current in real time alongside the markdown writes (nil/no-op if the
233		// index can't be opened — never block the resumed agent on it).
234		liveIndex := NewLiveIndexer(cwd)
235		defer liveIndex.Close()
236	
237		// Auto-save callback: identical behavior to run/watch.
238		sessionCallback := func(sess *spi.AgentChatSession) {
239			if sess == nil {
240				return
241			}
242			_, perr := session.ProcessSingleSession(context.Background(), sess, outConfig, session.ProcessingOptions{
243				OnlyCloudSync:      o.onlyCloudSync,
244				IsAutosave:         true,
245				DebugRaw:           o.debugRaw,
246				UseUTC:             o.useUTC,
247				NoTelemetryPrompts: o.noTelemetryPrompts,
248			})
249			if perr != nil {
250				slog.Error("Failed to process session update", "sessionId", sess.SessionID, "error", perr)
251			}
252			// Mirror the markdown write into the restore index (agent we resumed INTO).
253			liveIndex.Record(plan.toID, sess)
254			provenance.ProcessEvents(ctx, provenanceEngine, sess)
255		}
256	
257		slog.Info("Launching resume", "provider", plan.to.Name(), "resumeSessionID", resumeSessionID)
258		if err := plan.to.ExecAgentAndWatch(cwd, "", resumeSessionID, o.debugRaw, sessionCallback); err != nil {
259			slog.Error("Agent resume failed", "provider", plan.to.Name(), "error", err)
260			return err
261		}
262		return nil
263	}
264	
265	// prepareResumeTarget produces the native session ID to hand to ExecAgentAndWatch.
266	// Same-agent resume reuses the existing session; cross-agent reconstructs the
267	// source SessionData into the target's native store and returns the fresh ID.
268	func prepareResumeTarget(plan *resumePlan, cwd string, out io.Writer) (string, error) {
269		// Same agent: native resume on the existing session, no transform.
270		if plan.fromID == plan.toID {
271			fprintf(out, "\nResuming %s session %s in place...\n", plan.from.Name(), shortID(plan.sessionID))
272			return plan.sessionID, nil
273		}
274	
275		// Cross-agent: load source SessionData, reconstruct, write into target store.
276		// The source must be read from the directory it was originally launched in, not the
277		// user's current cwd — a session picked from another project (all-projects browser)
278		// lives elsewhere, and providers like Codex locate it by its recorded cwd. The
279		// reconstructed session is still written and launched under the current cwd below.
280		fromCwd := plan.fromCwd
281		if fromCwd == "" {
282			fromCwd = cwd
283		}
284		fromSession, err := plan.from.GetAgentChatSession(fromCwd, plan.sessionID, false)
285		if err != nil {
286			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to load source session: %w", err)
287		}
288		if fromSession == nil || fromSession.SessionData == nil {
289			return "", fmt.Errorf("source session %s has no data to reconstruct", plan.sessionID)
290		}
291	
292		note := fmt.Sprintf("Resumed from a %s session via SpecStory.", plan.from.Name())
293		rec, err := plan.to.ReconstructSession(fromSession.SessionData, spi.ReconstructOptions{
294			WorkspaceRoot: cwd,
295			MigrationNote: note,
296		})
297		if err != nil {
298			if errors.Is(err, spi.ErrReconstructionUnsupported) {
299				return "", utils.ValidationError{Message: fmt.Sprintf(
300					"%s can't yet be a cross-agent resume target. Choose Claude Code or Codex CLI (or resume in %s itself).",
301					plan.to.Name(), plan.from.Name())}
302			}
303			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to reconstruct session for %s: %w", plan.to.Name(), err)
304		}
305	
306		path, err := plan.to.NativeSessionPath(cwd, rec.Filename)
307		if err != nil {
308			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to resolve target session path: %w", err)
309		}
310		if err := writeReconstructedSession(path, rec.Content); err != nil {
311			return "", err
312		}
313		// The agent runs in a separate process and resolves the session by reading this file the
314		// instant it starts. On a filesystem without strong close-to-open coherence the freshly
315		// written file can lag, so confirm it is actually readable before handing the agent a
316		// --resume it would otherwise reject as "session not found".
317		if err := waitForSessionFileVisible(path, sessionFileVisibleTimeout); err != nil {
318			return "", fmt.Errorf("reconstructed %s session is not ready to resume: %w", plan.to.Name(), err)
319		}
320	
321		slog.Info("resume: wrote reconstructed session", "path", path, "newID", rec.SessionID)
322		fprintf(out, "\nReconstructed %s session into %s as %s.\n", plan.from.Name(), plan.to.Name(), shortID(rec.SessionID))
323		return rec.SessionID, nil
324	}
325	
326	// shortID abbreviates a session ID for display.
327	func shortID(id string) string {
328		if len(id) <= 13 {
329			return id
330		}
331		return id[:5] + "..." + id[len(id)-5:]
332	}
333	
334	// sessionFileVisibleTimeout bounds how long resume waits for a just-written reconstructed
335	// session file to become readable to other processes before launching the agent. It is
336	// generous enough to absorb the propagation lag of a weak-coherence filesystem (network or
337	// roaming home, cloud-synced folder, VM/container shared mount) yet short enough not to hang
338	// an interactive command — on a local disk the very first check passes, so it never waits.
339	const sessionFileVisibleTimeout = 10 * time.Second
340	
341	// writeReconstructedSession durably and atomically writes a reconstructed session file. A
342	// plain os.WriteFile leaves the data in the page cache and the new directory entry possibly
343	// non-durable; on a filesystem without strong close-to-open coherence the separately-spawned
344	// agent process can then fail to see a (large) file and report the session as missing. To
345	// avoid that, the content is written to a temp file in the SAME directory (so the rename is
346	// atomic rather than cross-device), fsync'd, renamed into place, and the parent directory is
347	// fsync'd so the entry itself is durable.
348	func writeReconstructedSession(path string, content []byte) (err error) {
349		dir := filepath.Dir(path)
350		if mkErr := os.MkdirAll(dir, 0o755); mkErr != nil {
351			return fmt.Errorf("failed to create target session directory: %w", mkErr)
352		}
353	
354		tmp, err := os.CreateTemp(dir, ".resume-*.tmp")
355		if err != nil {
356			return fmt.Errorf("failed to create temp session file: %w", err)
357		}
358		tmpPath := tmp.Name()
359		// Best-effort cleanup of the temp file on any early return; a no-op once it is renamed away.
360		defer func() { _ = os.Remove(tmpPath) }()
361	
362		// Preserve the world-readable mode the previous os.WriteFile(…, 0o644) produced
363		// (CreateTemp makes the file 0o600).
364		if chErr := tmp.Chmod(0o644); chErr != nil {
365			_ = tmp.Close()
366			return fmt.Errorf("failed to set session file mode: %w", chErr)
367		}
368		if _, wErr := tmp.Write(content); wErr != nil {
369			_ = tmp.Close()
370			return fmt.Errorf("failed to write temp session file: %w", wErr)
371		}
372		// Flush the bytes to the backing store before the rename so a reader cannot observe a
373		// renamed-but-empty file.
374		if syncErr := tmp.Sync(); syncErr != nil {
375			_ = tmp.Close()
376			return fmt.Errorf("failed to flush temp session file: %w", syncErr)
377		}
378		if closeErr := tmp.Close(); closeErr != nil {
379			return fmt.Errorf("failed to close temp session file: %w", closeErr)
380		}
381	
382		if rnErr := os.Rename(tmpPath, path); rnErr != nil {
383			return fmt.Errorf("failed to move session file into place: %w", rnErr)
384		}
385	
386		// Fsync the directory so the new entry is durable and propagates. Best-effort: not all
387		// platforms permit fsync on a directory handle, and it is a negligible cost on local disks.
388		if d, openErr := os.Open(dir); openErr == nil {
389			_ = d.Sync()
390			_ = d.Close()
391		}
392		return nil
393	}
394	
395	// waitForSessionFileVisible polls until path is readable — present AND with at least its
396	// first line available, since on a weak-coherence filesystem a stat can succeed before the
397	// content propagates — or the timeout elapses, in which case it returns a diagnostic error
398	// so the caller can fail clearly rather than launch a --resume the agent would reject.
399	func waitForSessionFileVisible(path string, timeout time.Duration) error {
400		deadline := time.Now().Add(timeout)
401		for {
402			readable, checkErr := sessionFileReadable(path)
403			if readable {
404				return nil
405			}
406			if time.Now().After(deadline) {
407				return fmt.Errorf("session file %q did not become readable within %s "+
408					"(the target filesystem may lack close-to-open coherence): %w", path, timeout, checkErr)
409			}
410			time.Sleep(50 * time.Millisecond)
411		}
412	}
413	
414	// sessionFileReadable reports whether path exists and at least its first line can be read.
415	// A reconstructed session always has at least one record, so an empty read means the content
416	// has not propagated yet even though the directory entry exists.
417	func sessionFileReadable(path string) (bool, error) {
418		f, err := os.Open(path)
419		if err != nil {
420			return false, err
421		}
422		defer func() { _ = f.Close() }()
423	
424		line, err := bufio.NewReader(f).ReadString('\n')
425		if len(line) > 0 {
426			return true, nil
427		}
428		if err == io.EOF || err == nil {
429			return false, fmt.Errorf("session file is empty")
430		}
431		return false, err
432	}
433

16	func TestFTSQuery(t *testing.T) {
17		cases := []struct{ name, in, want string }{
18			{"empty", "", ""},
19			{"single word is a prefix", "thank", "thank*"},
20			{"bare words are independent prefixes", "thank you", "thank* you*"},
21			{"punctuation in a bare word splits into an adjacency phrase", "max-cpu!", "max + cpu*"},
22			{"a bare filename is an adjacency phrase with prefix last", "poem.txt", "poem + txt*"},
23			{"a quoted filename is a committed phrase, no prefix", `"poem.txt"`, `"poem txt"`},
24			{"an open-quoted filename keeps the last token a prefix", `"poem.txt`, "poem + txt*"},
25			{"closed phrase is exact adjacency", `"thank you"`, `"thank you"`},
26			{"closed single-word phrase has no prefix", `"thank"`, `"thank"`},
27			{"open phrase keeps last word a prefix", `"thank yo`, "thank + yo*"},
28			{"open single-word phrase is a prefix", `"thank`, "thank*"},
29			{"phrase plus trailing bare word", `"thank you" now`, `"thank you" now*`},
30			{"bare word before a closed phrase", `please "thank you"`, `please* "thank you"`},
31			{"whitespace inside a phrase is collapsed", `"thank    you"`, `"thank you"`},
32		}
33		for _, c := range cases {
34			t.Run(c.name, func(t *testing.T) {
35				if got := ftsQuery(c.in); got != c.want {
36					t.Errorf("ftsQuery(%q) = %q, want %q", c.in, got, c.want)
37				}
38			})
39		}
40	}
41	
42	func TestQueryReady(t *testing.T) {
43		cases := []struct {
44			in   string
45			want bool
46		}{
47			{"", false},
48			{"a", false},   // one alnum char is below minQueryLen
49			{`"a"`, false}, // quotes don't count toward the threshold
50			{"ab", true},   // two alnum chars
51			{"a!", false},  // punctuation doesn't count
52			{"a b", true},  // two alnum chars across words
53			{`"hi"`, true}, // alnum inside quotes counts
54			{"日本", true},   // letters in other scripts count
55		}
56		for _, c := range cases {
57			if got := queryReady(c.in); got != c.want {
58				t.Errorf("queryReady(%q) = %v, want %v", c.in, got, c.want)
59			}
60		}
61	}
62	
63	func TestShortID(t *testing.T) {
64		cases := []struct{ in, want string }{
65			{"short", "short"},
66			{"1234567890abc", "1234567890abc"}, // <= 13 chars: unchanged
67			{"1234567890abcdef", "12345...bcdef"},
68		}
69		for _, c := range cases {
70			if got := shortID(c.in); got != c.want {
71				t.Errorf("shortID(%q) = %q, want %q", c.in, got, c.want)
72			}
73		}
74	}

76	func TestWriteReconstructedSession(t *testing.T) {
77		dir := t.TempDir()
78		path := filepath.Join(dir, "sub", "session.jsonl")
79		content := []byte(`{"type":"user"}` + "\n")
80	
81		if err := writeReconstructedSession(path, content); err != nil {
82			t.Fatalf("writeReconstructedSession: %v", err)
83		}
84	
85		got, err := os.ReadFile(path)
86		if err != nil {
87			t.Fatalf("reading written file: %v", err)
88		}
89		if string(got) != string(content) {
90			t.Errorf("content = %q, want %q", got, content)
91		}
92	
93		// The atomic-write temp file must not be left behind in the target directory.
94		entries, err := os.ReadDir(filepath.Dir(path))
95		if err != nil {
96			t.Fatal(err)
97		}
98		for _, e := range entries {
99			if e.Name() != "session.jsonl" {
100				t.Errorf("unexpected leftover file in target dir: %q", e.Name())
101			}
102		}
103	}
104	
105	func TestSessionFileReadable(t *testing.T) {
106		dir := t.TempDir()
107	
108		missing := filepath.Join(dir, "nope.jsonl")
109		if ok, _ := sessionFileReadable(missing); ok {
110			t.Error("missing file reported readable")
111		}
112	
113		empty := filepath.Join(dir, "empty.jsonl")
114		if err := os.WriteFile(empty, nil, 0o644); err != nil {
115			t.Fatal(err)
116		}
117		if ok, _ := sessionFileReadable(empty); ok {
118			t.Error("empty file reported readable")
119		}
120	
121		full := filepath.Join(dir, "full.jsonl")
122		if err := os.WriteFile(full, []byte(`{"type":"user"}`+"\n"), 0o644); err != nil {
123			t.Fatal(err)
124		}
125		if ok, err := sessionFileReadable(full); !ok {
126			t.Errorf("file with content reported not readable: %v", err)
127		}
128	}
129	
130	func TestWaitForSessionFileVisible(t *testing.T) {
131		dir := t.TempDir()
132	
133		present := filepath.Join(dir, "present.jsonl")
134		if err := os.WriteFile(present, []byte(`{"type":"user"}`+"\n"), 0o644); err != nil {
135			t.Fatal(err)
136		}
137		if err := waitForSessionFileVisible(present, time.Second); err != nil {
138			t.Errorf("expected an already-present file to be visible: %v", err)
139		}
140	
141		// A file that never appears must time out with a diagnostic error rather than block forever.
142		if err := waitForSessionFileVisible(filepath.Join(dir, "never.jsonl"), 100*time.Millisecond); err == nil {
143			t.Error("expected timeout error for a file that never becomes visible")
144		}
145	}

147	// TestBeginResumeWithPresetSkipsTargetStep verifies the `resume <agent>` contract: when a
148	// target agent was pre-selected, choosing a session resumes immediately into that agent
149	// rather than prompting for a target.
150	func TestBeginResumeWithPresetSkipsTargetStep(t *testing.T) {
151		sess := &sessionindex.Session{SessionID: "s1", Agent: "codex"}
152		m := sessionTUI{presetTo: "claude"}
153	
154		next, cmd := m.beginResume(sess)
155		rm := next.(sessionTUI)
156	
157		if cmd == nil {
158			t.Error("expected an immediate quit command when a target is preset")
159		}
160		if rm.mode == modeTarget {
161			t.Error("a preset target must skip the target-selection step")
162		}
163		if rm.result.session != sess {
164			t.Errorf("result session = %v, want the chosen session", rm.result.session)
165		}
166		if rm.result.targetID != "claude" {
167			t.Errorf("result target = %q, want %q", rm.result.targetID, "claude")
168		}
169	}
170	
171	// TestBeginResumeWithoutPresetEntersTargetStep verifies the default flow: with no preset,
172	// choosing a session advances to the target-selection step (no immediate resume).
173	func TestBeginResumeWithoutPresetEntersTargetStep(t *testing.T) {
174		sess := &sessionindex.Session{SessionID: "s1", Agent: "codex"}
175		m := sessionTUI{}
176	
177		next, cmd := m.beginResume(sess)
178		rm := next.(sessionTUI)
179	
180		if cmd != nil {
181			t.Error("expected no immediate quit without a preset target")
182		}
183		if rm.mode != modeTarget {
184			t.Errorf("mode = %v, want modeTarget", rm.mode)
185		}
186		if rm.chosen != sess {
187			t.Error("chosen session must be recorded before target selection")
188		}
189	}

191	// fakeProvider is a minimal spi.Provider for exercising prepareResumeTarget. It records
192	// the projectPath it is asked to load the source session from, and reconstructs into a
193	// caller-provided directory so the write/visibility tail succeeds.
194	type fakeProvider struct {
195		name        string
196		gotLoadPath string // projectPath captured from GetAgentChatSession
197		nativeDir   string // where NativeSessionPath places the reconstructed file
198	}
199	
200	func (f *fakeProvider) Name() string                  { return f.name }
201	func (f *fakeProvider) Check(string) spi.CheckResult  { return spi.CheckResult{Success: true} }
202	func (f *fakeProvider) DetectAgent(string, bool) bool { return false }
203	
204	func (f *fakeProvider) GetAgentChatSession(projectPath, sessionID string, _ bool) (*spi.AgentChatSession, error) {
205		f.gotLoadPath = projectPath
206		return &spi.AgentChatSession{SessionID: sessionID, SessionData: &schema.SessionData{}}, nil
207	}
208	
209	func (f *fakeProvider) ReconstructSession(*schema.SessionData, spi.ReconstructOptions) (*spi.ReconstructedSession, error) {
210		return &spi.ReconstructedSession{
211			SessionID: "new-session-id",
212			Filename:  "reconstructed.jsonl",
213			Content:   []byte(`{"type":"user"}` + "\n"),
214		}, nil
215	}
216	
217	func (f *fakeProvider) NativeSessionPath(_ string, filename string) (string, error) {
218		return filepath.Join(f.nativeDir, filename), nil
219	}

223		return nil, nil
224	}

227		return nil
228	}

230		return nil
231	}
232	func (f *fakeProvider) ListAllAgentChatSessions() ([]spi.GlobalSessionRef, error) { return nil, nil }
233	
234	// TestPrepareResumeTargetLoadsSourceFromOriginCwd guards the cross-project resume fix:
235	// the source session must be loaded from the directory it was launched in (fromCwd),
236	// not the user's current cwd, while the reconstructed file is still written under the
237	// current cwd. Regression test for "source session ... has no data to reconstruct" when
238	// resuming a session picked from another project via the all-projects browser.
239	func TestPrepareResumeTargetLoadsSourceFromOriginCwd(t *testing.T) {
240		nativeDir := t.TempDir()
241		from := &fakeProvider{name: "Codex CLI"}
242		to := &fakeProvider{name: "Claude Code", nativeDir: nativeDir}
243	
244		const originCwd = "/Users/jake/dev/tmp/blog-site"
245		const currentCwd = "/Users/jake/dev/specstory-website"
246	
247		plan := &resumePlan{
248			from:      from,
249			fromID:    "codex",
250			sessionID: "019c4cdd-917a-74e3-9b2e-fdb45e9eddc5",
251			fromCwd:   originCwd,
252			to:        to,
253			toID:      "claude",
254		}
255	
256		newID, err := prepareResumeTarget(plan, currentCwd, io.Discard)
257		if err != nil {
258			t.Fatalf("prepareResumeTarget returned error: %v", err)
259		}
260		if newID != "new-session-id" {
261			t.Errorf("resume target id = %q, want %q", newID, "new-session-id")
262		}
263		if from.gotLoadPath != originCwd {
264			t.Errorf("source loaded from %q, want origin cwd %q (current cwd %q must not be used)",
265				from.gotLoadPath, originCwd, currentCwd)
266		}
267	}
268	
269	// TestPrepareResumeTargetFallsBackToCurrentCwd verifies that when the index row carries
270	// no origin cwd (older rows), the source load falls back to the current cwd rather than
271	// loading from an empty path.
272	func TestPrepareResumeTargetFallsBackToCurrentCwd(t *testing.T) {
273		nativeDir := t.TempDir()
274		from := &fakeProvider{name: "Codex CLI"}
275		to := &fakeProvider{name: "Claude Code", nativeDir: nativeDir}
276	
277		const currentCwd = "/Users/jake/dev/blog-site"
278	
279		plan := &resumePlan{
280			from:      from,
281			fromID:    "codex",
282			sessionID: "sid",
283			fromCwd:   "", // older index row: no origin cwd recorded
284			to:        to,
285			toID:      "claude",
286		}
287	
288		if _, err := prepareResumeTarget(plan, currentCwd, io.Discard); err != nil {
289			t.Fatalf("prepareResumeTarget returned error: %v", err)
290		}
291		if from.gotLoadPath != currentCwd {
292			t.Errorf("source loaded from %q, want fallback to current cwd %q", from.gotLoadPath, currentCwd)
293		}
294	}
295	
296	// TestLiveIndexerRecord exercises real-time indexing (watch/run/resume): a live session is
297	// upserted into the index, its FTS body is searchable, repeat records with no new activity are
298	// skipped, and new activity updates the same row in place.
299	func TestLiveIndexerRecord(t *testing.T) {
300		store, err := sessionindex.Open(filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "sessions.db"))
301		if err != nil {
302			t.Fatalf("open index: %v", err)
303		}
304		defer func() { _ = store.Close() }()
305	
306		li := &LiveIndexer{
307			store:       store,
308			cwd:         "/work/proj",
309			projectID:   "proj-1",
310			projectName: "proj",
311			indexedAt:   "2026-06-25T00:00:00Z",
312			lastSeen:    map[string]string{},
313		}
314	
315		msg := func(role, ts, text string) schema.Message {
316			return schema.Message{
317				Role:      role,
318				Timestamp: ts,
319				Content:   []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: text}},
320			}
321		}
322		sess := func(msgs ...schema.Message) *spi.AgentChatSession {
323			return &spi.AgentChatSession{
324				SessionID: "sess-1",
325				CreatedAt: "2026-06-25T00:00:00Z",
326				Slug:      "hello-world",
327				SessionData: &schema.SessionData{
328					Provider:  schema.ProviderInfo{Name: "Claude Code"},
329					Exchanges: []schema.Exchange{{Messages: msgs}},
330				},
331			}
332		}
333		user1 := msg(schema.RoleUser, "2026-06-25T00:01:00Z", "index this please")
334		agent1 := msg(schema.RoleAgent, "2026-06-25T00:02:00Z", "done indexing")
335	
336		// First record: one user + one agent message → one fully-derived row.
337		li.Record("claude", sess(user1, agent1))
338		rows, err := store.ListByProject("proj-1")
339		if err != nil {
340			t.Fatalf("list: %v", err)
341		}
342		if len(rows) != 1 {
343			t.Fatalf("want 1 row, got %d", len(rows))
344		}
345		r := rows[0]
346		if r.Agent != "claude" || r.SessionID != "sess-1" || r.OriginCwd != "/work/proj" {
347			t.Errorf("identity wrong: agent=%q session=%q cwd=%q", r.Agent, r.SessionID, r.OriginCwd)
348		}
349		if r.UserTurns != 1 || r.TotalTurns != 2 {
350			t.Errorf("turns = %d/%d, want 1/2", r.UserTurns, r.TotalTurns)
351		}
352		if r.UpdatedAt != "2026-06-25T00:02:00Z" {
353			t.Errorf("updatedAt = %q, want last message timestamp", r.UpdatedAt)
354		}
355	
356		// The conversation body is full-text searchable immediately.
357		if hits, _ := store.Search(ftsQuery("indexing"), "proj-1"); len(hits) != 1 {
358			t.Errorf("search for body word returned %d hits, want 1", len(hits))
359		}
360	
361		// Dedup guard: re-recording with no new activity is a no-op.
362		li.Record("claude", sess(user1, agent1))
363		if rows, _ := store.ListByProject("proj-1"); len(rows) != 1 {
364			t.Errorf("dedup: want 1 row, got %d", len(rows))
365		}
366	
367		// New activity (a later message) updates the same row in place.
368		li.Record("claude", sess(user1, agent1, msg(schema.RoleUser, "2026-06-25T00:03:00Z", "more")))
369		rows, _ = store.ListByProject("proj-1")
370		if len(rows) != 1 {
371			t.Fatalf("want 1 row after update, got %d", len(rows))
372		}
373		if rows[0].UserTurns != 2 || rows[0].TotalTurns != 3 || rows[0].UpdatedAt != "2026-06-25T00:03:00Z" {
374			t.Errorf("after update: turns=%d/%d updatedAt=%q, want 2/3 and advanced timestamp",
375				rows[0].UserTurns, rows[0].TotalTurns, rows[0].UpdatedAt)
376		}
377	
378		// A nil indexer (index failed to open) is safe to use.
379		var nilLI *LiveIndexer
380		nilLI.Record("claude", sess(user1))
381		nilLI.Close()
382	}
383
```

	if fromSession == nil || fromSession.SessionData == nil {

	note := fmt.Sprintf("Resumed from a %s session via SpecStory.", plan.from.Name())
	rec, err := plan.to.ReconstructSession(fromSession.SessionData, spi.ReconstructOptions{
		WorkspaceRoot: cwd,
		MigrationNote: note,
	})
	if err != nil {

				"%s can't yet be a cross-agent resume target. Choose Claude Code or Codex CLI (or resume in %s itself).",
				plan.to.Name(), plan.from.Name())}
		}
		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to reconstruct session for %s: %w", plan.to.Name(), err)

+package cmd
+
+import (
+	"context"
+	"fmt"
+	"image/color"
+	"log/slog"
+	"os"
+	"sort"
+	"strings"
+	"time"
+	"unicode"
+
+	"charm.land/bubbles/v2/textinput"
+	"charm.land/bubbles/v2/viewport"
+	tea "charm.land/bubbletea/v2"
+	"charm.land/lipgloss/v2"
+	"github.com/charmbracelet/glamour"
+
+	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/config"
+	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/session"
+	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/sessionindex"
+	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/factory"
+	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/utils"
+)
+
+// sessionTUIResult is what the picker hands back: the chosen session and target agent,
+// or a cancel. It is read off the final model after the program exits.
+type sessionTUIResult struct {
+	session   *sessionindex.Session
+	targetID  string
+	cancelled bool
+}
+
+// tuiMode is the picker's top-level screen.
+type tuiMode int
+
+const (
+	modeList     tuiMode = iota // browsing the session list (current or a drilled-in project)
+	modeTarget                  // choosing which agent to resume into
+	modeProjects                // the all-projects browser (Stage B)
+)
+
+// agentMeta carries an agent's display name + accent color for the list.
+type agentMeta struct {
+	name   string
+	accent color.Color
+}
+
+// sessionTUI is the shared model behind BOTH `specstory resume` and `specstory search`.
+// The two commands are the same UI with different entry points: resume opens on the current
+// project's session list; search opens straight into the all-projects FTS with the input
+// focused. Everything else — keys, preview, agent filter, dense/sparse, the target-agent
+// step — is identical by construction. See docs/RESUME-TUI.md and docs/SESSION-SEARCH.md.
+type sessionTUI struct {
+	store       *sessionindex.Store
+	registry    *factory.Registry // re-parses a session for the glamour preview
+	title       string            // header title, e.g. "SpecStory Resume" / "SpecStory Search"
+	projectID   string
+	projectName string
+	agents      map[string]agentMeta // provider id -> display meta
+	installed   []agentChoice        // installed agents, for the target step
+	presetTo    string               // pre-selected target (from `resume <agent>`), or ""
+	lastAgent   string               // default target (last resumed), or ""
+
+	// homeProjectID/Name + homeSessions are the current directory's project; the picker
+	// can drill into other projects via the browser and toggle back to "home" with tab.
+	homeProjectID   string
+	homeProjectName string
+	homeSessions    []sessionindex.Session
+
+	all              []sessionindex.Session // sessions for the active project (projectID), newest first
+	filtered         []sessionindex.Session // after agent filter + search
+	filteredSnippets map[string]string      // match snippets for visible filtered rows, keyed by agent/session
+	cursor           int                    // index into filtered
+	top              int                    // first visible row (scroll)
+	inBrowser        bool                   // the active session list was reached via the browser
+
+	agentCycle  []string // "" (all) followed by each present agent id
+	agentFilter string   // "" = all
+	viewMode    string   // "dense" | "sparse"
+
+	// all-projects browser (Stage B)
+	projects         []sessionindex.ProjectSummary // all projects, most recent first
+	projFiltered     []sessionindex.ProjectSummary // after project-name search
+	projCursor       int
+	projTop          int
+	projectsLoaded   bool
+	startedInBrowser bool // launched straight into the browser (empty home project)
+	startedInSearch  bool // launched straight into the all-projects search (`specstory search`)
+	projSearching    bool
+	projSearch       textinput.Model
+	projSearchQuery  string
+
```

1	package cmd
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"os"
6		"path/filepath"
7		"strings"
8	
9		tea "charm.land/bubbletea/v2"
10		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
11	
12		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/config"
13		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/factory"
14		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/utils"
15	)
16	
17	// CreateSearchCommand builds `specstory search [query…]`. It is the same interactive UI as
18	// `specstory resume` (see sessionTUI), entered straight into the all-projects full-text
19	// search with the input focused: type to search, `space` previews a match (glamour-rendered),
20	// and `r` resumes it through the same launch path as `resume`. See docs/SESSION-SEARCH.md.
21	func CreateSearchCommand() *cobra.Command {
22		return &cobra.Command{
23			Use:   "search [query…]",
24			Short: "Search and read your past coding-agent sessions",
25			Long: `Full-text search across every session SpecStory has indexed, then read the match.
26	
27	'search' opens an interactive search of your session history. Type to search, press space to
28	preview a session (rendered for the terminal), and press 'r' to resume it in an agent. Anything
29	after the command pre-seeds the query, e.g. 'specstory search max cpu'.`,
30			Args: cobra.ArbitraryArgs,
31			RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
32				registry := factory.GetRegistry()
33				initialQuery := strings.TrimSpace(strings.Join(args, " "))
34	
35				cwd, err := os.Getwd()
36				if err != nil {
37					return err
38				}
39	
40				store, builtFresh, err := openOrBuildResumeIndex()
41				if err != nil {
42					return err
43				}
44				defer func() { _ = store.Close() }()
45	
46				if total, _ := store.Count(); total == 0 {
47					fprintln(os.Stderr, "\nNo agent sessions indexed yet. Run an agent, then try again (or `specstory reindex`).")
48					return nil
49				}
50	
51				homeID, homeName, idErr := utils.ComputeProjectID(cwd)
52				if idErr != nil || homeID == "" {
53					homeID, homeName = unknownProjectID, filepath.Base(cwd)
54				}
55				homeSessions, _ := store.ListByProject(homeID)
56	
57				agents := map[string]agentMeta{}
58				var installed []agentChoice
59				for _, id := range registry.ListIDs() {
60					prov, err := registry.Get(id)
61					if err != nil {
62						continue
63					}
64					agents[id] = agentMeta{name: prov.Name(), accent: colorForAgent(id)}
65					if prov.Check("").Success {
66						installed = append(installed, agentChoice{id: id, provider: prov})
67					}
68				}
69	
70				viewMode, lastAgent := "dense", ""
71				if cfg, _ := config.Load(nil); cfg != nil {
72					viewMode = cfg.GetResumeViewMode()
73					lastAgent = cfg.GetResumeLastAgent()
74				}
75	
76				// `search` is the same TUI as `resume`, entered straight into the all-projects
77				// FTS with the input focused. See newSessionTUI / sessionTUIOpts.
78				model := newSessionTUI(store, registry, homeID, homeName, homeSessions, agents, installed, sessionTUIOpts{
79					title:         "SpecStory Search",
80					lastAgent:     lastAgent,
81					viewMode:      viewMode,
82					initialQuery:  initialQuery,
83					startInSearch: true,
84				})
85				p := tea.NewProgram(model)
86				cancelWarm := startIndexWarm(p, homeID, builtFresh)
87				final, err := p.Run()
88				cancelWarm() // stop warming the moment search exits (never block on it)
89				if err != nil {
90					return fmt.Errorf("search failed: %w", err)
91				}
92				rm := final.(sessionTUI)
93				if rm.result.cancelled || rm.result.session == nil {
94					return nil // cancelled: nothing to launch
95				}
96	
97				// The user asked to resume a found session — launch via the shared path.
98				fromProv, err := registry.Get(rm.result.session.Agent)
99				if err != nil {
100					return fmt.Errorf("unknown source agent %q: %w", rm.result.session.Agent, err)
101				}
102				toProv, err := registry.Get(rm.result.targetID)
103				if err != nil {
104					return fmt.Errorf("unknown target agent %q: %w", rm.result.targetID, err)
105				}
106				_ = config.SaveResumePrefs(viewMode, rm.result.targetID)
107	
108				plan := &resumePlan{
109					from:      fromProv,
110					fromID:    rm.result.session.Agent,
111					sessionID: rm.result.session.SessionID,
112					// A search hit can be from another project; resume must load the source from
113					// its original cwd, not the current directory.
114					fromCwd: rm.result.session.OriginCwd,
115					to:      toProv,
116					toID:    rm.result.targetID,
117				}
118				return launchResume(plan, cwd, resumeLaunchOpts{useUTC: true})
119			},
120		}
121	}
122
