```text
31	+)
32	+
33	+const (
34	+	// unknownProjectID buckets sessions whose originating cwd could not be resolved
35	+	// (a few Claude warmups; all Cursor sessions until the deferred cwd recovery lands).
36	+	unknownProjectID = "unknown"
37	+
38	+	// reindexWriteBatch is how many sessions the writer goroutine commits per transaction.
39	+	reindexWriteBatch = 200
40	+
41	+	// reindexVersion is the logic version stamped on every indexed row. Bump it whenever
42	+	// the parse/flatten/derivation logic changes so the next reindex re-parses everything
43	+	// even when files are unchanged. It is part of the freshness fingerprint.
44	+	//   2: skip slash-command noise when choosing a Claude session title (fixes UUID titles)
45	+	//   3: also skip "[Request interrupted by user…]" markers
46	+	//   4: canonicalize (case-fold) the workspace_id path hash + prefer a persisted
47	+	//      .project.json workspace_id, so a project's id no longer varies with path casing
48	+	//   5: parse Codex bodies from the enumerated NativePath (was a by-id tree search), so a
49	+	//      session's body and its freshness fingerprint now derive from the same file
50	+	//   6: populate sessions.fts_rowid (the O(1) link to each session's FTS row) — existing
51	+	//      rows have it NULL until re-parsed, so bump to repopulate the whole index once
52	+	reindexVersion = 6
53	+)
54	+
55	+// CreateReindexCommand builds the `specstory reindex` command: a full, from-scratch
56	+// rebuild of the restore index (~/.specstory/sessions.db) of every coding-agent session
57	+// SpecStory can find, across all projects and providers. See docs/SESSIONS-DB.md.
58	+func CreateReindexCommand() *cobra.Command {
59	+	cmd := &cobra.Command{
60	+		Use:   "reindex",
61	+		Short: "Rebuild the restore index of all known agent sessions",
62	+		Long: `Rebuild the restore index used by 'specstory resume'.
63	+
64	+'reindex' enumerates every session across all installed agents and projects and writes a
65	+searchable index to ~/.specstory/sessions.db. It is incremental: a session whose native
66	+file is unchanged since it was last indexed is skipped, so re-runs are fast. Use --force to
67	+re-index everything regardless. The index is a derived cache: it is safe to delete.`,
68	+		Args: cobra.NoArgs,
69	+		RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
70	+			force, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("force")
71	+			return runReindex(force)
72	+		},
73	+	}
74	+	cmd.Flags().Bool("force", false, "re-index every session even if unchanged")
75	+	return cmd
76	+}
77	+
78	+// reindexItem is one unit of work: a session to parse and index. size/mtime are the
79	+// native file's stat, captured during the freshness check and reused (no re-stat).
80	+type reindexItem struct {
81	+	agent string
82	+	prov  spi.Provider
83	+	ref   spi.GlobalSessionRef
84	+	size  int64
85	+	mtime int64
86	+	isNew bool // no existing index row → writer can skip the standalone-FTS delete
87	+}
88	+
89	+func runReindex(force bool) error {
90	+	start := time.Now()
91	+
92	+	// Graceful Ctrl+C: stop feeding work, flush what's written, exit cleanly.
93	+	ctx, cancel := signal.NotifyContext(context.Background(), os.Interrupt, syscall.SIGTERM)
94	+	defer cancel()
95	+
96	+	dbPath, err := sessionindex.DefaultPath()
97	+	if err != nil {
98	+		return err
99	+	}
100	+	store, err := sessionindex.Open(dbPath)
101	+	if err != nil {
102	+		return fmt.Errorf("opening restore index: %w", err)
103	+	}
104	+	defer func() { _ = store.Close() }()
105	+
106	+	registry := factory.GetRegistry()
107	+
108	+	// ---- Phase 1: enumerate every provider concurrently, then dedup ----
109	+	ids, provs, perProvider := enumerateAll(registry, true)
110	+
111	+	// Existing fingerprints, so unchanged sessions can be skipped (the incremental path).
112	+	fingerprints := map[string]sessionindex.Fingerprint{}
113	+	if !force {
114	+		if fps, err := store.Fingerprints(); err != nil {
115	+			slog.Warn("reindex: could not load fingerprints, re-indexing all", "error", err)
116	+		} else {
117	+			fingerprints = fps
118	+		}
119	+	}
120	+
121	+	best, order, foundPerAgent := dedupRefs(ids, provs, perProvider)
122	+	found := len(order)
123	+	cache := &projectIDCache{m: map[string]projectIDName{}}
124	+	work, totals, unchanged := selectWork(order, best, fingerprints, "", force, cache)
125	+
126	+	if found == 0 {
127	+		fprintln(os.Stderr, "No agent sessions found to index.")
128	+		return nil
129	+	}
130	+	fprintf(os.Stderr, "✓   Found %d sessions  ·  %s\n", found, summarizeCounts(ids, foundPerAgent))
131	+	if len(work) == 0 {
132	+		fprintf(os.Stderr, "✓   All %d sessions already up to date.  (%.1fs)\n", found, time.Since(start).Seconds())
133	+		return nil
134	+	}
135	+	if unchanged > 0 {
136	+		fprintf(os.Stderr, "    %d unchanged · indexing %d…\n\n", unchanged, len(work))
137	+	} else {
138	+		fprintln(os.Stderr, "")
139	+	}
140	+
141	+	// ---- Phase 2+3: parse (worker pool) → write (single goroutine), with live progress ----
142	+	prog := newReindexProgress(ids, totals)
143	+	prog.begin()
144	+	indexedAt := start.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)
145	+	writeErr := processWork(ctx, store, work, cache, indexedAt, min(runtime.NumCPU(), 12), prog)
146	+	prog.end()
147	+
148	+	if writeErr != nil {
149	+		return fmt.Errorf("writing restore index: %w", writeErr)
150	+	}
151	+
152	+	// ---- Summary (counts reflect the WHOLE index, not just this run's work) ----
153	+	elapsed := time.Since(start)
154	+	projects, _ := store.ProjectCount(unknownProjectID)
155	+	unattributed, _ := store.UnattributedCount(unknownProjectID)
156	+	printReindexSummary(ids, foundPerAgent, dbPath, elapsed, prog.totalDone(), unchanged, projects, unattributed)
157	+	analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventReindexCompleted, analytics.Properties{
158	+		"sessions":     len(work),
159	+		"unchanged":    unchanged,
160	+		"projects":     projects,
161	+		"unattributed": unattributed,
162	+		"duration_ms":  elapsed.Milliseconds(),
163	+	})
164	+	return nil
165	+}
166	+
167	+// ---- reindex engine (shared by the foreground command and the background warm) ----
168	+
169	+// enumerateAll concurrently lists every installed provider's sessions. The returned slices
170	+// are index-aligned: provs[i]/perProvider[i] correspond to ids[i] (a nil provs[i] marks a
171	+// provider that failed to load). When visible, it renders a live "Scanning agents…" line
172	+// (foreground reindex); the background warm passes visible=false and stays silent.
173	+func enumerateAll(registry *factory.Registry, visible bool) (ids []string, provs []spi.Provider, perProvider [][]spi.GlobalSessionRef) {
174	+	ids = registry.ListIDs()
175	+	perProvider = make([][]spi.GlobalSessionRef, len(ids))
176	+	provs = make([]spi.Provider, len(ids))
177	+
178	+	var scan *scanProgress
179	+	if visible {
180	+		scan = newScanProgress(ids)
181	+		scan.begin()
182	+		defer scan.end()
183	+	}
184	+
185	+	var ewg sync.WaitGroup
186	+	for i, id := range ids {
187	+		prov, err := registry.Get(id)
188	+		if err != nil {
189	+			continue
190	+		}
191	+		provs[i] = prov
192	+		reporter := scan.reporterFor(id)
193	+		ewg.Add(1)
194	+		go func(i int, id string, prov spi.Provider, reporter *spi.ScanReporter) {
195	+			defer ewg.Done()
196	+			var refs []spi.GlobalSessionRef
197	+			var err error
198	+			// Providers that can report progress (Codex, the long pole) tick the live line; the
199	+			// rest snap to their final count via markDone when they finish.
200	+			if pe, ok := prov.(spi.ProgressEnumerator); ok {
201	+				refs, err = pe.ListAllAgentChatSessionsProgress(reporter)
202	+			} else {
203	+				refs, err = prov.ListAllAgentChatSessions()
204	+			}
205	+			if err != nil {
206	+				slog.Warn("reindex: enumeration failed", "provider", id, "error", err)
207	+			}
208	+			perProvider[i] = refs
209	+			scan.markDone(id, len(refs))
210	+		}(i, id, prov, reporter)
211	+	}
212	+	ewg.Wait()
213	+	return ids, provs, perProvider
214	+}
215	+
216	+// dedupRefs collapses enumerated refs by (agent, session_id), keeping the freshest file. A
217	+// resumed session can span MULTIPLE native files sharing one id; they all map to one
218	+// (agent, session_id) row, so without dedup the duplicates ping-pong — each run, one file's
219	+// stat never matches the fingerprint the other file just stored, re-indexing both forever.
220	+// Keeping the freshest by mtime makes the fingerprint stable. Sessions with no native id are
221	+// dropped (unresumable, and would collide on the PK). order is first-seen order (deterministic
222	+// processing); foundPerAgent is the distinct-session count per agent (for the "Found" line).
223	+func dedupRefs(ids []string, provs []spi.Provider, perProvider [][]spi.GlobalSessionRef) (best map[string]reindexItem, order []string, foundPerAgent map[string]int) {
224	+	best = map[string]reindexItem{}
225	+	foundPerAgent = map[string]int{}
226	+	for i, id := range ids {
227	+		if provs[i] == nil {
228	+			continue
229	+		}
230	+		for _, ref := range perProvider[i] {
231	+			if strings.TrimSpace(ref.SessionID) == "" {
232	+				slog.Debug("reindex: skipping session with no id", "agent", id, "path", ref.NativePath)
233	+				continue
234	+			}
235	+			size, mtime := statNative(ref.NativePath)
236	+			item := reindexItem{agent: id, prov: provs[i], ref: ref, size: size, mtime: mtime}
237	+			key := sessionindex.FingerprintKey(id, ref.SessionID)
238	+			if cur, ok := best[key]; ok {
239	+				if mtime > cur.mtime {
240	+					best[key] = item
241	+				}
242	+				continue
243	+			}
244	+			best[key] = item
245	+			order = append(order, key)
246	+		}
247	+	}
248	+	for _, key := range order {
249	+		foundPerAgent[best[key].agent]++
250	+	}
251	+	return best, order, foundPerAgent
252	+}
253	+
254	+// selectWork applies the incremental fingerprint skip (and an optional project filter) to the
255	+// deduped set. projectFilter "" means all projects; otherwise only sessions whose originating
256	+// cwd resolves to that project id are kept (used by the background warm's current-project
257	+// pass). totals is the per-agent to-do count (drives the foreground progress bars).
258	+func selectWork(order []string, best map[string]reindexItem, fingerprints map[string]sessionindex.Fingerprint, projectFilter string, force bool, cache *projectIDCache) (work []reindexItem, totals map[string]int, unchanged int) {
259	+	totals = map[string]int{}
260	+	for _, key := range order {
261	+		item := best[key]
262	+		if projectFilter != "" {
263	+			if pid, _ := cache.resolve(item.ref.OriginCwd); pid != projectFilter {
264	+				continue
265	+			}
266	+		}
267	+		fp, ok := fingerprints[key]
268	+		if ok && fp.Size == item.size && fp.Mtime == item.mtime && fp.Version == reindexVersion {
269	+			unchanged++
270	+			continue // already indexed and unchanged
271	+		}
272	+		// A session absent from the fingerprint set has no sessions row, hence no sessions_fts
273	+		// row either (the pair is written atomically) — so the writer can skip the O(rows)
274	+		// standalone-FTS delete. Under --force the fingerprint map is deliberately empty, so
275	+		// absence proves nothing; keep the delete to avoid duplicate FTS rows.
276	+		item.isNew = !force && !ok
277	+		work = append(work, item)
278	+		totals[item.agent]++
279	+	}
280	+	return work, totals, unchanged
281	+}
282	+
283	+// reindexReporter receives per-session progress from processWork. The foreground command
284	+// passes *reindexProgress (live bars); the background warm passes nopReporter (silent).
285	+type reindexReporter interface {
286	+	observe(projectID string)
287	+	inc(agent string)
288	+}
289	+
290	+// nopReporter is the silent reporter used by the background warm.
291	+type nopReporter struct{}
292	+
293	+func (nopReporter) observe(string) {}
294	+func (nopReporter) inc(string)     {}
295	+
296	+// processWork parses each work item (worker pool) and writes the results to the store via a
297	+// single writer goroutine, reporting progress through rep. It stops feeding work when ctx is
298	+// cancelled; the writer flushes what it already received. Returns the writer's error, if any.
299	+func processWork(ctx context.Context, store *sessionindex.Store, work []reindexItem, cache *projectIDCache, indexedAt string, workers int, rep reindexReporter) error {
300	+	if len(work) == 0 {
301	+		return nil
302	+	}
303	+	if workers < 1 {
304	+		workers = 1
305	+	}
306	+
307	+	// Schedule the largest sessions first. A few huge Codex files (hundreds of MB) would
308	+	// otherwise be end-of-run stragglers that leave most worker cores idle; starting them
309	+	// first overlaps them with the long tail of small sessions. Order does not affect the
310	+	// resulting index.
311	+	sort.SliceStable(work, func(i, j int) bool { return work[i].size > work[j].size })
312	+
313	+	sessionsCh := make(chan sessionindex.Session, 256)
314	+	var writeErr error
315	+	writerDone := make(chan struct{})
316	+	go func() {
317	+		defer close(writerDone)
318	+		writeErr = drainToStore(store, sessionsCh)
319	+	}()
320	+
321	+	workCh := make(chan reindexItem)
322	+	var wwg sync.WaitGroup
323	+	for w := 0; w < workers; w++ {
324	+		wwg.Add(1)
325	+		go func() {
326	+			defer wwg.Done()
327	+			for item := range workCh {
328	+				sess := buildSession(item, cache, indexedAt)
329	+				rep.observe(sess.ProjectID)
330	+				sessionsCh <- sess
331	+				rep.inc(item.agent)
332	+			}
333	+		}()
334	+	}
335	+
336	+	for _, item := range work {
337	+		select {
338	+		case <-ctx.Done():
339	+			goto closing
340	+		case workCh <- item:
341	+		}
342	+	}
343	+closing:
344	+	close(workCh)
345	+	wwg.Wait()
346	+	close(sessionsCh)
347	+	<-writerDone
348	+	return writeErr
349	+}
350	+
351	+// warmIndexInBackground keeps ~/.specstory/sessions.db warm as a side effect of resume/search.
352	+// It runs two SILENT incremental passes over its own writer handle: the current project first
353	+// (so its rows refresh quickly, and the open TUI re-queries via onProjectWarmed), then the full
354	+// corpus. It enumerates once and reuses the result for both passes. It stops promptly when ctx
355	+// is cancelled (the TUI exited); an abandoned write batch is safe under WAL. Errors are logged
356	+// at debug and never surfaced — warmth is best-effort.
357	+func warmIndexInBackground(ctx context.Context, dbPath, currentProjectID string, onProjectWarmed func()) {
358	+	store, err := sessionindex.Open(dbPath)
359	+	if err != nil {
360	+		slog.Debug("warm: could not open index", "error", err)
361	+		return
362	+	}
363	+	defer func() { _ = store.Close() }()
364	+
365	+	registry := factory.GetRegistry()
366	+	ids, provs, perProvider := enumerateAll(registry, false)
367	+	if ctx.Err() != nil {
368	+		return
369	+	}
370	+	best, order, _ := dedupRefs(ids, provs, perProvider)
371	+	cache := &projectIDCache{m: map[string]projectIDName{}}
372	+	indexedAt := time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)
373	+
374	+	// Gentle: fewer workers than a foreground reindex, since the user is interacting.
375	+	workers := min(runtime.NumCPU(), 4)
376	+
377	+	// Pass 1 — the current project, so its rows are fresh almost immediately.
378	+	fps, _ := store.Fingerprints()
379	+	work, _, _ := selectWork(order, best, fps, currentProjectID, false, cache)
380	+	if err := processWork(ctx, store, work, cache, indexedAt, workers, nopReporter{}); err != nil {
381	+		slog.Debug("warm: current-project pass failed", "error", err)
382	+	}
383	+	if ctx.Err() != nil {
384	+		return
385	+	}
386	+	if onProjectWarmed != nil {
387	+		onProjectWarmed() // let the open TUI re-query the (now fresh) current project
388	+	}
389	+
390	+	// Pass 2 — everything else. Reload fingerprints so pass-1's writes now register as
391	+	// unchanged and are skipped, leaving only the rest of the corpus to parse.
392	+	fps, _ = store.Fingerprints()
393	+	work, _, _ = selectWork(order, best, fps, "", false, cache)
394	+	if err := processWork(ctx, store, work, cache, indexedAt, workers, nopReporter{}); err != nil {
395	+		slog.Debug("warm: full pass failed", "error", err)
396	+	}
397	+}
398	+
399	+// ---- real-time indexing (watch / run / resume launch) ----
400	+
401	+// LiveIndexer keeps ~/.specstory/sessions.db current in real time, alongside the markdown
402	+// files that watch/run/resume already write as a session changes. It holds one writer handle
403	+// for the command's lifetime and upserts a single row per detected update, reusing the same
404	+// content derivation as reindex. The project is resolved once (a watch/run has a fixed cwd).
405	+//
406	+// Everything is best-effort: real-time indexing must never disrupt the agent or the markdown
407	+// write, so construction failures yield a nil *LiveIndexer (whose methods are no-ops) and
408	+// per-update errors are logged at debug. A live row carries no native-file fingerprint
409	+// (Size/Mtime/NativePath are unknown without enumerating), so the next reindex re-verifies and
410	+// rewrites it with a stable fingerprint — the live row is correct and queryable in the interim.
411	+type LiveIndexer struct {
412	+	store       *sessionindex.Store
413	+	cwd         string
414	+	projectID   string
415	+	projectName string
416	+	indexedAt   string
417	+
418	+	mu       sync.Mutex
419	+	lastSeen map[string]string // session id -> last indexed UpdatedAt, to skip no-op upserts
420	+}
421	+
422	+// NewLiveIndexer opens the index for writing and resolves the project for cwd. It returns nil
423	+// (not an error) when the index can't be opened, so the caller keeps running with real-time
424	+// indexing simply disabled. The caller owns Close.
425	+func NewLiveIndexer(cwd string) *LiveIndexer {
426	+	dbPath, err := sessionindex.DefaultPath()
427	+	if err != nil {
428	+		slog.Debug("live index: no db path; real-time indexing disabled", "error", err)
429	+		return nil
430	+	}
431	+	store, err := sessionindex.Open(dbPath)
432	+	if err != nil {
433	+		slog.Debug("live index: cannot open index; real-time indexing disabled", "error", err)
434	+		return nil
435	+	}
436	+	projectID, projectName, err := utils.ComputeProjectID(cwd)
437	+	if err != nil || projectID == "" {
438	+		projectID, projectName = unknownProjectID, ""
439	+	}
440	+	return &LiveIndexer{
441	+		store:       store,
442	+		cwd:         cwd,
443	+		projectID:   projectID,
444	+		projectName: projectName,
445	+		indexedAt:   time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
446	+		lastSeen:    map[string]string{},
447	+	}
448	+}
449	+
450	+// Record upserts the live session into the index. agentID is the provider's registry id (e.g.
451	+// "claude"). It skips the write when the session's last activity hasn't advanced since the
452	+// previous record (the watcher can fire repeatedly without new content). Safe on a nil
453	+// receiver, and serialized so concurrent provider watchers don't contend on the single writer.
454	+func (li *LiveIndexer) Record(agentID string, sess *spi.AgentChatSession) {
455	+	if li == nil || sess == nil || sess.SessionData == nil {
456	+		return
457	+	}
458	+	data := sess.SessionData
459	+
460	+	updatedAt := sess.CreatedAt
461	+	if ts := lastTimestamp(data); ts != "" {
462	+		updatedAt = ts
463	+	}
464	+
465	+	li.mu.Lock()
466	+	defer li.mu.Unlock()
467	+
468	+	// Nothing advanced since the last write → skip the (FTS-heavy) upsert.
469	+	if updatedAt != "" && li.lastSeen[sess.SessionID] == updatedAt {
470	+		return
471	+	}
472	+
473	+	// Skip the whole-table FTS delete (session_id/agent are UNINDEXED, so it scans the entire
474	+	// FTS) when this session has no existing row to replace. Only the first write of a session
475	+	// in this process can be new, and even then only if it isn't already in the index from a
476	+	// prior run — so we check by primary key just once, before lastSeen records the write.
477	+	isNew := false
478	+	if _, written := li.lastSeen[sess.SessionID]; !written {
479	+		if exists, err := li.store.Exists(agentID, sess.SessionID); err == nil {
480	+			isNew = !exists
481	+		}
482	+	}
483	+
484	+	userTurns, totalTurns := countTurns(data)
485	+	row := sessionindex.Session{
486	+		ProjectID:    li.projectID,
487	+		ProjectName:  li.projectName,
488	+		Agent:        agentID,
489	+		SessionID:    sess.SessionID,
490	+		CreatedAt:    sess.CreatedAt,
491	+		UpdatedAt:    updatedAt,
492	+		UserTurns:    userTurns,
493	+		TotalTurns:   totalTurns,
494	+		Slug:         sess.Slug,
495	+		OriginCwd:    li.cwd,
496	+		IndexVersion: reindexVersion,
497	+		IndexedAt:    li.indexedAt,
498	+		Body:         flattenBody(data),
499	+		IsNew:        isNew,
500	+	}
501	+	if err := li.store.Upsert(row); err != nil {
502	+		slog.Debug("live index: upsert failed", "sessionId", sess.SessionID, "error", err)
503	+		return
504	+	}
505	+	li.lastSeen[sess.SessionID] = updatedAt
506	+}
507	+
508	+// Close releases the writer handle. Safe on a nil receiver.
509	+func (li *LiveIndexer) Close() {
510	+	if li == nil {
511	+		return
512	+	}
513	+	if err := li.store.Close(); err != nil {
514	+		slog.Debug("live index: close failed", "error", err)
515	+	}
516	+}
517	+
518	+// buildSession turns one enumeration ref into a fully-built index row. Every session
519	+// yields a row from its metadata; the body + turn counts + last-activity time are
520	+// ENRICHED via a full parse only when the originating cwd lets us re-locate the
521	+// session (so Cursor, whose cwd is unknown for now, lands metadata-only). Nothing is
522	+// dropped.
523	+func buildSession(item reindexItem, cache *projectIDCache, indexedAt string) sessionindex.Session {
524	+	ref := item.ref
525	+	projectID, projectName := cache.resolve(ref.OriginCwd)
526	+
527	+	sess := sessionindex.Session{
528	+		ProjectID:    projectID,
529	+		ProjectName:  projectName,
530	+		Agent:        item.agent,
531	+		SessionID:    ref.SessionID,
532	+		CreatedAt:    ref.CreatedAt,
533	+		UpdatedAt:    ref.CreatedAt, // default; overwritten by last-turn timestamp below
534	+		Slug:         ref.Slug,
535	+		Name:         ref.Name,
536	+		NativePath:   ref.NativePath,
537	+		OriginCwd:    ref.OriginCwd,
538	+		Size:         item.size, // captured during the freshness check (no re-stat)
539	+		Mtime:        item.mtime,
540	+		IndexVersion: reindexVersion,
541	+		IndexedAt:    indexedAt,
542	+		IsNew:        item.isNew,
543	+	}
544	+
545	+	if ref.OriginCwd == "" {
546	+		return sess // cannot re-locate without a cwd → metadata-only
547	+	}
548	+	full, err := parseFullSession(item.prov, ref)
549	+	if err != nil || full == nil || full.SessionData == nil {
550	+		slog.Debug("reindex: full parse unavailable, indexing metadata only",
551	+			"agent", item.agent, "session", ref.SessionID, "error", err)
552	+		return sess
553	+	}
554	+
555	+	data := full.SessionData
556	+	sess.Body = flattenBody(data)
557	+	sess.UserTurns, sess.TotalTurns = countTurns(data)
558	+	if ts := lastTimestamp(data); ts != "" {
559	+		sess.UpdatedAt = ts
560	+	}
561	+	return sess
562	+}
563	+
564	+// parseFullSession reads a session's full data for indexing. It prefers a provider's
565	+// path-keyed parse (spi.PathSessionReader) using the ref's already-known NativePath, which
566	+// avoids the provider's by-id discovery search — for Codex that search walks the entire
567	+// ~/.codex/sessions tree, so this is the difference between an O(N) and an O(N²) reindex.
568	+// Providers without the capability (or refs lacking a NativePath) fall back to the by-id
569	+// lookup. Parsing the known NativePath is also more consistent: the row's freshness
570	+// fingerprint is stat'd from NativePath, so the body now comes from that same file.
571	+func parseFullSession(prov spi.Provider, ref spi.GlobalSessionRef) (*spi.AgentChatSession, error) {
572	+	if pr, ok := prov.(spi.PathSessionReader); ok && ref.NativePath != "" {
573	+		return pr.GetAgentChatSessionByPath(ref.NativePath, ref.OriginCwd, false)
574	+	}
575	+	return prov.GetAgentChatSession(ref.OriginCwd, ref.SessionID, false)
576	+}
577	+
578	+// drainToStore writes parsed sessions to the index in batched transactions.
579	+func drainToStore(store *sessionindex.Store, ch <-chan sessionindex.Session) error {
580	+	batch := make([]sessionindex.Session, 0, reindexWriteBatch)
581	+	flush := func() error {
582	+		if err := store.UpsertBatch(batch); err != nil {
583	+			return err
584	+		}
585	+		batch = batch[:0]
586	+		return nil
587	+	}
588	+	for sess := range ch {
589	+		batch = append(batch, sess)
590	+		if len(batch) >= reindexWriteBatch {
591	+			if err := flush(); err != nil {
592	+				return err
593	+			}
594	+		}
595	+	}
596	+	return flush()
597	+}
598	+
599	+// ---- session field derivation ----
600	+
601	+// flattenBody renders SessionData to plain user/agent text for full-text indexing,
602	+// reusing the reconstruction flattener (synthetic noise already stripped). No
603	+// migration note is prepended — this is index content, not a resumed transcript.
604	+func flattenBody(data *schema.SessionData) string {
605	+	turns := spi.FlattenSessionData(data, "")
606	+	var b strings.Builder
607	+	for _, t := range turns {
608	+		if t.Text == "" {
609	+			continue
610	+		}
611	+		if b.Len() > 0 {
612	+			b.WriteString("\n\n")
613	+		}
614	+		b.WriteString(t.Text)
615	+	}
616	+	return b.String()
617	+}
618	+
619	+// countTurns returns (user prompts, all messages) across the session's exchanges.
620	+func countTurns(data *schema.SessionData) (userTurns, totalTurns int) {
621	+	for _, ex := range data.Exchanges {
622	+		for _, m := range ex.Messages {
623	+			totalTurns++
624	+			if m.Role == schema.RoleUser {
625	+				userTurns++
626	+			}
627	+		}
628	+	}
629	+	return userTurns, totalTurns
630	+}
631	+
632	+// lastTimestamp returns the last non-empty message timestamp in the session, or "".
633	+func lastTimestamp(data *schema.SessionData) string {
634	+	last := ""
635	+	for _, ex := range data.Exchanges {
636	+		for _, m := range ex.Messages {
637	+			if m.Timestamp != "" {
638	+				last = m.Timestamp
639	+			}
640	+		}
641	+	}
642	+	return last
643	+}
644	+
645	+// statNative returns the native file's size (bytes) and mtime (epoch ms), or zeros.
646	+func statNative(path string) (size, mtimeMs int64) {
647	+	info, err := os.Stat(path)
648	+	if err != nil {
649	+		return 0, 0
650	+	}
651	+	return info.Size(), info.ModTime().UnixMilli()
652	+}
653	+
654	+// ---- project identity cache (cwd -> project id/name) ----
655	+
656	+type projectIDName struct{ id, name string }
657	+
658	+// projectIDCache memoizes ComputeProjectID by cwd, since many sessions share a cwd and
659	+// each resolution walks the filesystem to the git root.
660	+type projectIDCache struct {
661	+	mu sync.Mutex
662	+	m  map[string]projectIDName
663	+}
664	+
665	+func (c *projectIDCache) resolve(cwd string) (string, string) {
666	+	if strings.TrimSpace(cwd) == "" {
667	+		return unknownProjectID, ""
668	+	}
669	+	c.mu.Lock()
670	+	defer c.mu.Unlock()
671	+	if v, ok := c.m[cwd]; ok {
672	+		return v.id, v.name
673	+	}
674	+	id, name, err := utils.ComputeProjectID(cwd)
675	+	if err != nil || id == "" {
676	+		id, name = unknownProjectID, ""
677	+	}
678	+	c.m[cwd] = projectIDName{id, name}
679	+	return id, name
680	+}
681	+
682	+// ---- helpers ----
683	+
684	+// summarizeCounts renders "claude 683 · codex 70 · …" in registry order, omitting zeros.
685	+func summarizeCounts(ids []string, totals map[string]int) string {
686	+	var parts []string
687	+	for _, id := range ids {
688	+		if totals[id] > 0 {
689	+			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("%s %d", id, totals[id]))
690	+		}
691	+	}
692	+	return strings.Join(parts, " · ")
693	+}
694	+
695	+// ---- live progress (per-agent bars) ----
696	+
697	+// reindexProgress renders live per-agent progress. Workers bump atomic per-agent
698	+// counters and observe project ids; a single render goroutine redraws the block in
699	+// place on a TTY (ANSI cursor-up), or prints periodic plain lines otherwise. No worker
700	+// draws directly. See docs/SESSIONS-DB.md "Reindex Progress UX".
701	+type reindexProgress struct {
702	+	agents []string          // agents with >0 sessions, in registry order
703	+	totals map[string]int    // per-agent session totals
704	+	done   map[string]*int64 // per-agent completed counters (atomic)
705	+	tty    bool
706	+
707	+	mu       sync.Mutex
708	+	projects map[string]struct{} // distinct non-unknown project ids seen
709	+
710	+	stopCh     chan struct{}
711	+	doneCh     chan struct{}
712	+	linesDrawn int
713	+}
714	+
715	+func newReindexProgress(ids []string, totals map[string]int) *reindexProgress {
716	+	done := make(map[string]*int64)
717	+	var agents []string
718	+	for _, id := range ids {
719	+		if totals[id] > 0 {
720	+			agents = append(agents, id)
721	+			done[id] = new(int64)
722	+		}
723	+	}
724	+	return &reindexProgress{
725	+		agents:   agents,
726	+		totals:   totals,
727	+		done:     done,
728	+		tty:      isTerminal(os.Stderr),
729	+		projects: map[string]struct{}{},
730	+		stopCh:   make(chan struct{}),
731	+		doneCh:   make(chan struct{}),
732	+	}
733	+}
734	+
735	+func (p *reindexProgress) begin() {
736	+	go p.loop()
737	+}
738	+
739	+func (p *reindexProgress) end() {
740	+	close(p.stopCh)
741	+	<-p.doneCh
742	+	p.draw(true) // final frame at 100%
743	+}
744	+
745	+func (p *reindexProgress) inc(agent string) {
746	+	if c := p.done[agent]; c != nil {
747	+		atomic.AddInt64(c, 1)
748	+	}
749	+}
750	+
751	+func (p *reindexProgress) observe(projectID string) {
752	+	// Unknown-project sessions are not counted toward the live "projects" total (the
753	+	// final unattributed count comes from the DB at summary time).
754	+	if projectID == unknownProjectID {
755	+		return
756	+	}
757	+	p.mu.Lock()
758	+	p.projects[projectID] = struct{}{}
759	+	p.mu.Unlock()
760	+}
761	+
762	+func (p *reindexProgress) projectCount() int {
763	+	p.mu.Lock()
764	+	defer p.mu.Unlock()
765	+	return len(p.projects)
766	+}
767	+
768	+func (p *reindexProgress) totalDone() int {
769	+	var n int64
770	+	for _, c := range p.done {
771	+		n += atomic.LoadInt64(c)
772	+	}
773	+	return int(n)
774	+}
775	+
776	+func (p *reindexProgress) grandTotal() int {
777	+	n := 0
778	+	for _, a := range p.agents {
779	+		n += p.totals[a]
780	+	}
781	+	return n
782	+}
783	+
784	+func (p *reindexProgress) loop() {
785	+	defer close(p.doneCh)
786	+	interval := 100 * time.Millisecond
787	+	if !p.tty {
788	+		interval = 1500 * time.Millisecond
789	+	}
790	+	ticker := time.NewTicker(interval)
791	+	defer ticker.Stop()
792	+	for {
793	+		select {
794	+		case <-p.stopCh:
795	+			return
796	+		case <-ticker.C:
797	+			p.draw(false)
798	+		}
799	+	}
800	+}
801	+
802	+func (p *reindexProgress) draw(final bool) {
803	+	if !p.tty {
804	+		if !final { // the final state is covered by the summary line
805	+			fprintf(os.Stderr, "indexed %d/%d…\n", p.totalDone(), p.grandTotal())
806	+		}
807	+		return
808	+	}
809	+
810	+	lines := p.frameLines()
811	+	var b strings.Builder
812	+	if p.linesDrawn > 0 {
813	+		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "\033[%dA", p.linesDrawn) // move cursor up to the block's top
814	+	}
815	+	for _, ln := range lines {
816	+		b.WriteString("\033[2K") // clear the line, then redraw it
817	+		b.WriteString(ln)
818	+		b.WriteString("\n")
819	+	}
820	+	fprint(os.Stderr, b.String())
821	+	p.linesDrawn = len(lines)
822	+}
823	+
824	+func (p *reindexProgress) frameLines() []string {
825	+	doneN, totalN := p.totalDone(), p.grandTotal()
826	+	lines := []string{
827	+		fmt.Sprintf("Indexing  %d/%d · %d projects", doneN, totalN, p.projectCount()),
828	+		"",
829	+	}
830	+
831	+	nameW := 0
832	+	for _, a := range p.agents {
833	+		if len(a) > nameW {
834	+			nameW = len(a)
835	+		}
836	+	}
837	+	for _, a := range p.agents {
838	+		d := atomic.LoadInt64(p.done[a])
839	+		t := int64(p.totals[a])
840	+		check := ""
841	+		if d >= t {
842	+			check = " ✓"
843	+		}
844	+		lines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf("  %-*s %s %d/%d%s", nameW, a, progressBar(d, t), d, t, check))
845	+	}
846	+	return lines
847	+}
848	+
849	+// progressBar renders a fixed-width unicode bar, e.g. ▕███████░░░░░░░▏.
850	+func progressBar(done, total int64) string {
851	+	const w = 14
852	+	filled := 0
853	+	if total > 0 {
854	+		filled = int(done * int64(w) / total)
855	+	}
856	+	if filled > w {
857	+		filled = w
858	+	}
859	+	return "▕" + strings.Repeat("█", filled) + strings.Repeat("░", w-filled) + "▏"
860	+}
861	+
862	+// isTerminal reports whether f is a character device (an interactive terminal),
863	+// using only the standard library (no isatty dependency).
864	+func isTerminal(f *os.File) bool {
865	+	info, err := f.Stat()
866	+	if err != nil {
867	+		return false
868	+	}
869	+	return info.Mode()&os.ModeCharDevice != 0
870	+}
871	+
872	+// ---- scan progress (live "Scanning agents…" line) ----
873	+
874	+// scanSpinner is the braille spinner cycled while enumerating.
875	+var scanSpinner = []string{"⠋", "⠙", "⠹", "⠸", "⠼", "⠴", "⠦", "⠧", "⠇", "⠏"}
876	+
877	+// scanAgent holds one provider's live enumeration counter.
878	+type scanAgent struct {
879	+	id string
880	+	r  *spi.ScanReporter
881	+}
882	+
883	+// scanProgress renders one live line while enumerateAll scans providers in parallel:
884	+//
885	+//	🔍  Scanning agents…  ⠹  codex 18420 · claude 3195 · cursor 10
886	+//
887	+// Codex and Claude (the long poles) report sessions-found via spi.ProgressEnumerator; every
888	+// provider snaps to its final count via markDone when it finishes. Counts are sessions, not raw
889	+// files (warmup/sidechain transcripts are excluded). On a non-TTY it prints periodic plain lines.
890	+// A nil *scanProgress is fully silent — the background warm passes nil.
891	+type scanProgress struct {
892	+	agents []*scanAgent
893	+	byID   map[string]*scanAgent
894	+	tty    bool
895	+	spin   int
896	+
897	+	stopCh     chan struct{}
898	+	doneCh     chan struct{}
899	+	linesDrawn int
900	+}
901	+
902	+func newScanProgress(ids []string) *scanProgress {
903	+	agents := make([]*scanAgent, 0, len(ids))
904	+	byID := make(map[string]*scanAgent, len(ids))
905	+	for _, id := range ids {
906	+		a := &scanAgent{id: id, r: &spi.ScanReporter{}}
907	+		agents = append(agents, a)
908	+		byID[id] = a
909	+	}
910	+	return &scanProgress{
911	+		agents: agents,
912	+		byID:   byID,
913	+		tty:    isTerminal(os.Stderr),
914	+		stopCh: make(chan struct{}),
915	+		doneCh: make(chan struct{}),
916	+	}
917	+}
918	+
919	+// reporterFor returns a provider's live reporter (nil-safe: a nil scanProgress yields nil, which
920	+// providers treat as "do not report").
921	+func (s *scanProgress) reporterFor(id string) *spi.ScanReporter {
922	+	if s == nil {
923	+		return nil
924	+	}
925	+	if a := s.byID[id]; a != nil {
926	+		return a.r
927	+	}
928	+	return nil
929	+}
930	+
931	+// markDone reconciles a provider's running count to its final session count — a no-op for the
932	+// reporting providers (Codex/Claude), and the only count for providers that never report
933	+// (Cursor/Gemini/etc., which appear the moment they finish).
934	+func (s *scanProgress) markDone(id string, final int) {
935	+	if s == nil {
936	+		return
937	+	}
938	+	if a := s.byID[id]; a != nil {
939	+		if delta := final - int(a.r.Found()); delta != 0 {
940	+			a.r.Add(delta)
941	+		}
942	+	}
943	+}
944	+
945	+func (s *scanProgress) begin() {
946	+	if s == nil {
947	+		return
948	+	}
949	+	if !s.tty {
950	+		fprintln(os.Stderr, "\n🔍  Scanning agents…")
951	+	} else {
952	+		fprintln(os.Stderr) // blank line above the live line
953	+		s.draw()            // show immediately, before the first tick
954	+	}
955	+	go s.loop()
956	+}
957	+
958	+func (s *scanProgress) end() {
959	+	if s == nil {
960	+		return
961	+	}
962	+	close(s.stopCh)
963	+	<-s.doneCh
964	+	if s.tty && s.linesDrawn > 0 {
965	+		// Erase the live line so the caller's "✓ Found…" line takes its place.
966	+		fprint(os.Stderr, "\033[1A\033[2K")
967	+	}
968	+}
969	+
970	+func (s *scanProgress) loop() {
971	+	defer close(s.doneCh)
972	+	interval := 100 * time.Millisecond
973	+	if !s.tty {
974	+		interval = 1500 * time.Millisecond
975	+	}
976	+	t := time.NewTicker(interval)
977	+	defer t.Stop()
978	+	for {
979	+		select {
980	+		case <-s.stopCh:
981	+			return
982	+		case <-t.C:
983	+			s.spin++
984	+			if s.tty {
985	+				s.draw()
986	+			} else {
987	+				fprintf(os.Stderr, "    scanning… %s\n", s.counts())
988	+			}
989	+		}
990	+	}
991	+}
992	+
993	+func (s *scanProgress) draw() {
994	+	var b strings.Builder
995	+	if s.linesDrawn > 0 {
996	+		b.WriteString("\033[1A") // up to the live line
997	+	}
998	+	b.WriteString("\033[2K") // clear it, then redraw
999	+	b.WriteString(s.line())
1000	+	b.WriteString("\n")
1001	+	fprint(os.Stderr, b.String())
1002	+	s.linesDrawn = 1
1003	+}
1004	+
1005	+func (s *scanProgress) line() string {
1006	+	return fmt.Sprintf("🔍  Scanning agents…  %s  %s", scanSpinner[s.spin%len(scanSpinner)], s.counts())
1007	+}
1008	+
1009	+func (s *scanProgress) counts() string {
1010	+	var parts []string
1011	+	for _, a := range s.agents {
1012	+		found := a.r.Found()
1013	+		if found == 0 {
1014	+			continue // not started, or finished with no sessions
1015	+		}
1016	+		// Plain integers (no thousands separators), matching the "Found …" summary line.
1017	+		parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("%s %d", a.id, found))
1018	+	}
1019	+	if len(parts) == 0 {
1020	+		return "…"
1021	+	}
1022	+	return strings.Join(parts, " · ")
1023	+}
1024	+
1025	+// ---- summary ----
1026	+
1027	+func printReindexSummary(ids []string, perAgent map[string]int, dbPath string, elapsed time.Duration, indexed, unchanged, projects, unattributed int) {
1028	+	w := os.Stderr
1029	+	if unchanged > 0 {
1030	+		fprintf(w, "\n✓   Indexed %d sessions (%d unchanged) into %s  (%.1fs)\n\n", indexed, unchanged, prettyPath(dbPath), elapsed.Seconds())
1031	+	} else {
1032	+		fprintf(w, "\n✓   Indexed %d sessions into %s  (%.1fs)\n\n", indexed, prettyPath(dbPath), elapsed.Seconds())
1033	+	}
1034	+	fprintf(w, "      %s\n\n", summarizeCounts(ids, perAgent))
1035	+	if unattributed > 0 {
1036	+		fprintf(w, "      %d projects  ·  %d unattributed\n", projects, unattributed)
1037	+	} else {
1038	+		fprintf(w, "      %d projects\n", projects)
1039	+	}
1040	+	fprintf(w, "\n")
1041	+}
1042	+
1043	+// prettyPath abbreviates the user's home directory to ~ for display.
1044	+func prettyPath(p string) string {
1045	+	if home, err := os.UserHomeDir(); err == nil && strings.HasPrefix(p, home) {
1046	+		return "~" + p[len(home):]
1047	+	}
1048	+	return p
1049	+}

1056	+package cmd
1057	+

1061	+
1062	+	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
1063	+)
1064	+
1065	+func userMsg(ts, text string) schema.Message {
1066	+	return schema.Message{Role: schema.RoleUser, Timestamp: ts,
1067	+		Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: text}}}
1068	+}
1069	+func agentMsg(ts, text string) schema.Message {
1070	+	return schema.Message{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Timestamp: ts,
1071	+		Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: text}}}
1072	+}
1073	+
1074	+func sampleData() *schema.SessionData {
1075	+	return &schema.SessionData{
1076	+		Exchanges: []schema.Exchange{
1077	+			{Messages: []schema.Message{userMsg("2026-06-18T10:00:00Z", "fix the bug"), agentMsg("2026-06-18T10:01:00Z", "on it")}},
1078	+			{Messages: []schema.Message{userMsg("2026-06-18T10:05:00Z", "now ship it"), agentMsg("2026-06-18T10:06:00Z", "done")}},
1079	+		},
1080	+	}
1081	+}
1082	+
1083	+func TestCountTurns(t *testing.T) {
1084	+	user, total := countTurns(sampleData())
1085	+	if user != 2 {
1086	+		t.Errorf("user turns = %d; want 2", user)
1087	+	}
1088	+	if total != 4 {
1089	+		t.Errorf("total turns = %d; want 4", total)
1090	+	}
1091	+}
1092	+
1093	+func TestLastTimestamp(t *testing.T) {
1094	+	if got := lastTimestamp(sampleData()); got != "2026-06-18T10:06:00Z" {
1095	+		t.Errorf("lastTimestamp = %q; want the final message ts", got)
1096	+	}
1097	+	// Empty when no messages carry a timestamp.
1098	+	noTS := &schema.SessionData{Exchanges: []schema.Exchange{{Messages: []schema.Message{userMsg("", "hi")}}}}
1099	+	if got := lastTimestamp(noTS); got != "" {
1100	+		t.Errorf("lastTimestamp with no timestamps = %q; want empty", got)
1101	+	}
1102	+}
1103	+
1104	+func TestFlattenBody(t *testing.T) {
1105	+	body := flattenBody(sampleData())
1106	+	for _, want := range []string{"fix the bug", "on it", "now ship it", "done"} {
1107	+		if !strings.Contains(body, want) {
1108	+			t.Errorf("flattened body missing %q; got %q", want, body)
1109	+		}
1110	+	}
1111	+}
1112	+
1113	+func TestProjectIDCacheUnknownForEmptyCwd(t *testing.T) {
1114	+	c := &projectIDCache{m: map[string]projectIDName{}}
1115	+	id, name := c.resolve("")
1116	+	if id != unknownProjectID || name != "" {
1117	+		t.Errorf("resolve(\"\") = (%q,%q); want (unknown, \"\")", id, name)
1118	+	}
1119	+}
1120	+
1121	+func TestProjectIDCacheMemoizes(t *testing.T) {
1122	+	c := &projectIDCache{m: map[string]projectIDName{}}
1123	+	// Pre-seed the cache and confirm resolve returns it without recomputing.
1124	+	c.m["/some/repo"] = projectIDName{id: "abcd-1234", name: "repo"}
1125	+	id, name := c.resolve("/some/repo")
1126	+	if id != "abcd-1234" || name != "repo" {
1127	+		t.Errorf("cached resolve = (%q,%q); want the seeded value", id, name)
1128	+	}
1129	+}
1130	+
1131	+func TestProgressBar(t *testing.T) {
1132	+	cases := []struct {
1133	+		done, total int64
1134	+		wantFull    int // number of █ expected
1135	+	}{
1136	+		{0, 10, 0},
1137	+		{10, 10, 14},
1138	+		{5, 10, 7},
1139	+		{1, 0, 0},    // zero total → no divide-by-zero, empty bar
1140	+		{20, 10, 14}, // clamped, never overflows
1141	+	}
1142	+	for _, tc := range cases {
1143	+		got := progressBar(tc.done, tc.total)
1144	+		if full := strings.Count(got, "█"); full != tc.wantFull {
1145	+			t.Errorf("progressBar(%d,%d) had %d full cells; want %d (%q)", tc.done, tc.total, full, tc.wantFull, got)
1146	+		}
1147	+	}
1148	+}
1149	+
1150	+func TestSummarizeCounts(t *testing.T) {

1153	+	if got != "claude 683 · cursor 49" {
1154	+		t.Errorf("summarizeCounts = %q; want zeros omitted, registry order", got)
```

```text
// flattenBody renders SessionData to plain user/agent text for full-text indexing,
// reusing the reconstruction flattener (synthetic noise already stripped). No
// migration note is prepended — this is index content, not a resumed transcript.
func flattenBody(data *schema.SessionData) string {
	turns := spi.FlattenSessionData(data, "")
	var b strings.Builder
	for _, t := range turns {
		if t.Text == "" {
			continue
		}
```

```text
29	// truncateSessionID shortens a UUID to first5...last5 for display.
30	// Full IDs are available in --json output; the short form is enough
31	// to visually distinguish sessions.
32	func truncateSessionID(id string) string {
33		if len(id) <= 13 {
34			return id
35		}
36		return id[:5] + "..." + id[len(id)-5:]
37	}
38	
39	// CreateWatchCommand dynamically creates the watch command with provider information.
40	// cloudURL binds to the parent's --cloud-url flag so PersistentPreRunE can apply it.
41	// localTimeZone and debugDir are passed from the global config/flag values.
42	func CreateWatchCommand(cloudURL *string, localTimeZone bool, debugDir string) *cobra.Command {
43		registry := factory.GetRegistry()
44		ids := registry.ListIDs()
45		providerList := registry.GetProviderList()
46	
47		// Build dynamic examples
48		examples := `
49	# Watch all registered agent providers for activity
50	specstory watch`
51	
52		if len(ids) > 0 {
53			examples += "\n\n# Watch for activity from a specific agent"
54			for _, id := range ids {
55				examples += fmt.Sprintf("\nspecstory watch %s", id)
56			}
57		}
58	
59		examples += `
60	
61	# Watch with custom output directory
62	specstory watch --output-dir ~/my-sessions`
63	
64		longDesc := `Watch for coding agent activity in the current directory and auto-save markdown files.
65	
66	Unlike 'run', this command does not launch a coding agent - it only monitors for agent activity.
67	Use this when you want to run the agent separately, but still want auto-saved markdown files.
68	
69	By default, 'watch' is for activity from all registered agent providers. Specify a specific agent ID to watch for activity from only that agent.`
70		if providerList != "No providers registered" {
71			longDesc += "\n\nAvailable provider IDs: " + providerList + "."
72		}
73	
74		watchCmd := &cobra.Command{
75			Use:     "watch [provider-id]",
76			Aliases: []string{"w"},
77			Short:   "Watch for coding agent activity with auto-save",
78			Long:    longDesc,
79			Example: examples,
80			Args:    cobra.MaximumNArgs(1), // Accept 0 or 1 argument (provider ID)
81			RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
82				config.EnsureDefaultProjectConfig()
83				slog.Info("Running in watch mode")
84	
85				registry := factory.GetRegistry()
86	
87				// Read flags from the command
88				debugRaw, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("debug-raw")
89				useLocalTimezone, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("local-time-zone")
90				useUTC := !useLocalTimezone
91				jsonOutput, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
92				outputDir, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("output-dir")
93				flagDebugDir, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("debug-dir")
94				noCloudSync, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-cloud-sync")
95				onlyCloudSync, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("only-cloud-sync")
96				provenanceEnabled, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("provenance")
97				noTelemetryPrompts, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-telemetry-prompts")
98	
99				// Apply debug dir override from flag if provided
100				if flagDebugDir != "" {
101					spi.SetDebugBaseDir(flagDebugDir)
102				}
103	
104				// Setup output configuration
105				config, err := utils.SetupOutputConfig(outputDir, flagDebugDir)
106				if err != nil {
107					return err
108				}
109	
110				// Ensure history directory exists for watch mode
111				if err := utils.EnsureHistoryDirectoryExists(config); err != nil {
112					return err
113				}
114	
115				// Initialize project identity (needed for cloud sync)
116				cwd, err := os.Getwd()
117				if err != nil {
118					slog.Error("Failed to get current working directory", "error", err)
119					return err
120				}
121				if _, err := utils.NewProjectIdentityManager(cwd).EnsureProjectIdentity(); err != nil {
122					// Log error but don't fail the command
123					slog.Error("Failed to ensure project identity", "error", err)
124				}
125	
126				// Check authentication for cloud sync
127				CheckAndWarnAuthentication(noCloudSync)
128	
129				// Validate that --only-cloud-sync requires authentication
130				if onlyCloudSync && !cloud.IsAuthenticated() {
131					return utils.ValidationError{Message: "--only-cloud-sync requires authentication. Please run 'specstory login' first"}
132				}
133	
134				// Start provenance engine if enabled (used in later phases for event correlation)
135				provenanceEngine, provenanceCleanup, err := provenance.StartEngine(provenanceEnabled)
136				if err != nil {
137					return err
138				}
139				defer provenanceCleanup()
140	
141				providerIDs := registry.ListIDs()
142				if len(providerIDs) == 0 {
143					return fmt.Errorf("no providers registered")
144				}
145				if len(args) > 0 {
146					providerIDs = []string{args[0]}
147				}
148	
149				// Collect provider names for analytics
150				providers := make(map[string]spi.Provider)
151				for _, id := range providerIDs {
152					if provider, err := registry.Get(id); err == nil {
153						providers[id] = provider
154					} else {
155						return fmt.Errorf("no provider %s found", id)
156					}
157				}
158				var providerNames []string
159				// Get all provider names from the providers map
160				for _, provider := range providers {
161					providerNames = append(providerNames, provider.Name())
162				}
163				analytics.SetAgentProviders(providerNames)
164	
165				// Track watch command activation
166				analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventWatchActivated, nil)
167	
168				// Create context for graceful cancellation (Ctrl+C handling)
169				// This allows providers to clean up resources when user presses Ctrl+C
170				ctx, cancel := signal.NotifyContext(context.Background(), os.Interrupt, syscall.SIGTERM)
171				defer cancel()
172	
173				// Start filesystem watcher for provenance correlation if enabled (uses signal context for Ctrl+C)
174				fsCleanup, err := provenance.StartFSWatcher(ctx, provenanceEngine, cwd)
175				if err != nil {
176					return err
177				}
178				defer fsCleanup()
179	
180				if !log.IsSilent() && !jsonOutput {
181					fmt.Println()
182					agentWord := "agents"
183					if len(providerNames) == 1 {
184						agentWord = "agent"
185					}
186					fmt.Println("👀 Watching for activity from " + agentWord + ": " + strings.Join(providerNames, ", "))
187					fmt.Println("   Press Ctrl+C to stop watching")
188					fmt.Println()
189				}
190	
191				// Keep sessions.db current in real time alongside the markdown writes (nil/no-op
192				// if the index can't be opened — never block the watcher on it).
193				liveIndex := NewLiveIndexer(cwd)
194				defer liveIndex.Close()
195	
196				// Track sessions we've seen to suppress initial-scan output.
197				// Existing sessions found at startup get their markdown refreshed
198				// but don't produce output — only new activity does.
199				var seenMu gosync.Mutex
200				seenSessions := make(map[string]bool)
201	
202				// Session callback for watch mode output
203				sessionCallback := func(providerID string, sess *spi.AgentChatSession) {
204					// Check if markdown file already exists to determine if this is an update or creation
205					fileFullPath := session.BuildSessionFilePath(sess, config.GetHistoryDir(), useUTC)
206					_, fileExistsErr := os.Stat(fileFullPath)
207					fileExists := fileExistsErr == nil
208	
209					// Process the session (write markdown and sync to cloud)
210					// Don't show output during watch mode
211					// This is autosave mode (true)
212					markdownSize, err := session.ProcessSingleSession(ctx, sess, config, session.ProcessingOptions{
213						OnlyCloudSync:      onlyCloudSync,
214						IsAutosave:         true,
215						DebugRaw:           debugRaw,
216						UseUTC:             useUTC,
217						NoTelemetryPrompts: noTelemetryPrompts,
218					})
219					if err != nil {
220						// Log error but continue - don't fail the whole watch
221						// In watch mode, we prioritize keeping the watcher running.
222						// Failed markdown writes or cloud syncs can be retried later via
223						// the sync command, so we just log and continue.
224						slog.Error("Failed to process session update",
225							"sessionId", sess.SessionID,
226							"provider", providerID,
227							"error", err)
228						return
229					}
230	
231					// Mirror the markdown write into the restore index in real time.
232					liveIndex.Record(providerID, sess)
233	
234					// Suppress output for existing sessions seen for the first time (initial scan).
235					// New sessions (!fileExists) always get output since they represent real activity.
236					seenMu.Lock()
237					firstSeen := !seenSessions[sess.SessionID]
238					seenSessions[sess.SessionID] = true
239					seenMu.Unlock()
240					if firstSeen && fileExists {
241						return
242					}
243	
244					// Output formatted line to stdout for watch mode
245					if !log.IsSilent() {
246						// Determine if this was an update or creation
247						action := "updated"
248						if !fileExists {
249							action = "created"
250						}
```
