```text
1	package cmd
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"
6		"image/color"
7		"log/slog"
8		"os"
9		"sort"
10		"strings"
11		"time"
12		"unicode"
13	
14		"charm.land/bubbles/v2/textinput"
15		"charm.land/bubbles/v2/viewport"
16		tea "charm.land/bubbletea/v2"
17		"charm.land/lipgloss/v2"
18		"github.com/charmbracelet/glamour"
19	
20		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/config"
21		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/session"
22		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/sessionindex"
23		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/factory"
24		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/utils"
25	)
26	
27	// sessionTUIResult is what the picker hands back: the chosen session and target agent,
28	// or a cancel. It is read off the final model after the program exits.
29	type sessionTUIResult struct {
30		session   *sessionindex.Session
31		targetID  string
32		cancelled bool
33	}
34	
35	// tuiMode is the picker's top-level screen.
36	type tuiMode int
37	
38	const (
39		modeList     tuiMode = iota // browsing the session list (current or a drilled-in project)
40		modeTarget                  // choosing which agent to resume into
41		modeProjects                // the all-projects browser (Stage B)
42	)
43	
44	// agentMeta carries an agent's display name + accent color for the list.
45	type agentMeta struct {
46		name   string
47		accent color.Color
48	}
49	
50	// sessionTUI is the shared model behind BOTH `specstory resume` and `specstory search`.
51	// The two commands are the same UI with different entry points: resume opens on the current
52	// project's session list; search opens straight into the all-projects FTS with the input
53	// focused. Everything else — keys, preview, agent filter, dense/sparse, the target-agent
54	// step — is identical by construction. See docs/RESUME-TUI.md and docs/SESSION-SEARCH.md.
55	type sessionTUI struct {
56		store       *sessionindex.Store
57		registry    *factory.Registry // re-parses a session for the glamour preview
58		title       string            // header title, e.g. "SpecStory Resume" / "SpecStory Search"
59		projectID   string
60		projectName string
61		agents      map[string]agentMeta // provider id -> display meta
62		installed   []agentChoice        // installed agents, for the target step
63		presetTo    string               // pre-selected target (from `resume <agent>`), or ""
64		lastAgent   string               // default target (last resumed), or ""
65	
66		// homeProjectID/Name + homeSessions are the current directory's project; the picker
67		// can drill into other projects via the browser and toggle back to "home" with tab.
68		homeProjectID   string
69		homeProjectName string
70		homeSessions    []sessionindex.Session
71	
72		all              []sessionindex.Session // sessions for the active project (projectID), newest first
73		filtered         []sessionindex.Session // after agent filter + search
74		filteredSnippets map[string]string      // match snippets for visible filtered rows, keyed by agent/session
75		cursor           int                    // index into filtered
76		top              int                    // first visible row (scroll)
77		inBrowser        bool                   // the active session list was reached via the browser
78	
79		agentCycle  []string // "" (all) followed by each present agent id
80		agentFilter string   // "" = all
81		viewMode    string   // "dense" | "sparse"
82	
83		// all-projects browser (Stage B)
84		projects         []sessionindex.ProjectSummary // all projects, most recent first
85		projFiltered     []sessionindex.ProjectSummary // after project-name search
86		projCursor       int
87		projTop          int
88		projectsLoaded   bool
89		startedInBrowser bool // launched straight into the browser (empty home project)
90		startedInSearch  bool // launched straight into the all-projects search (`specstory search`)
91		projSearching    bool
92		projSearch       textinput.Model
93		projSearchQuery  string
94	
95		// global session search: FTS across all projects, or scoped to a single project with tab
96		// (the highlighted hit's project, else the current directory's). Opened with / in the
97		// browser or as `search`.
98		globalActive    bool
99		globalSearching bool
100		globalScopeID   string // "" = all projects; else the project id the search is scoped to
101		globalScopeName string // display name for the scoped project
102		globalInput     textinput.Model
103		globalQuery     string
104		globalResults   []sessionindex.Session
105		globalSnippets  map[string]string
106		globalCursor    int
107		globalTop       int
108	
109		searching   bool
110		search      textinput.Model
111		searchQuery string
112		searchSeq   int // bumped per search keystroke; debounced FTS results must match it
113		snippetSeq  int // bumped per lazy snippet request; stale snippet results are discarded
114		// searchCancel aborts the in-flight FTS query when a newer keystroke supersedes it,
115		// freeing the database connection (a broad prefix query can rank the whole corpus).
116		searchCancel context.CancelFunc
117	
118		// previewing shows a glamour-rendered, scrollable reader for the highlighted session,
119		// identical to search's reader (see openPreview / renderPreview).
120		previewing    bool
121		reader        viewport.Model
122		readerSession *sessionindex.Session
123	
124		mode         tuiMode
125		chosen       *sessionindex.Session
126		targetCursor int
127	
128		width, height int
129		result        sessionTUIResult
130	}
131	
132	// sessionTUIOpts carries the per-command entry configuration. The only real differences
133	// between `resume` and `search` live here: the header title, what the positional arg means
134	// (presetTo vs. initialQuery), and whether to open in the all-projects search.
135	type sessionTUIOpts struct {
136		title         string // header title ("SpecStory Resume" / "SpecStory Search")
137		presetTo      string // resume: pre-selected target agent (from `resume <agent>`)
138		lastAgent     string // default target (last resumed), or ""
139		viewMode      string // "dense" | "sparse"
140		initialQuery  string // search: pre-seed the all-projects query
141		startInSearch bool   // search: open in the all-projects FTS with the input focused
142	}
143	
144	func newSessionTUI(store *sessionindex.Store, registry *factory.Registry, projectID, projectName string,
145		sessions []sessionindex.Session, agents map[string]agentMeta, installed []agentChoice, opts sessionTUIOpts) sessionTUI {
146	
147		ti := textinput.New()
148		ti.Prompt = "/ "
149		pi := textinput.New()
150		pi.Prompt = "p "
151		gi := textinput.New()
152		gi.Prompt = "/ "
153	
154		m := sessionTUI{
155			store:           store,
156			registry:        registry,
157			title:           opts.title,
158			reader:          viewport.New(),
159			projectID:       projectID,
160			projectName:     projectName,
161			homeProjectID:   projectID,
162			homeProjectName: projectName,
163			homeSessions:    sessions,
164			agents:          agents,
165			installed:       installed,
166			presetTo:        opts.presetTo,
167			lastAgent:       opts.lastAgent,
168			all:             sessions,
169			viewMode:        opts.viewMode,
170			search:          ti,
171			projSearch:      pi,
172			globalInput:     gi,
173		}
174		m.rebuildAgentCycle()
175		m.applyFilter()
176	
177		switch {
178		case opts.startInSearch:
179			// `search`: land directly in the all-projects FTS, input focused (so the user types
180			// immediately, exactly like before). Init fires the pre-seeded query, if any. The
181			// search IS the root view here, so esc quits rather than unwinding into the browser.
182			m.startedInSearch = true
183			m.enterBrowser()
184			m.globalActive = true
185			m.globalSearching = true
186			m.globalQuery = opts.initialQuery
187			m.globalInput.SetValue(opts.initialQuery)
188			m.globalInput.Focus() // focus state persists on the stored model; Init returns the blink+query cmds
189			if queryReady(m.globalQuery) {
190				m.searchSeq = 1
191			}
192		case len(sessions) == 0:
193			// `resume` with an empty current project → open straight into the all-projects browser.
194			m.startedInBrowser = true
195			m.enterBrowser()
196		}
197		return m
198	}
199	
200	func (m sessionTUI) Init() tea.Cmd {
201		// Search starts focused in the all-projects input; kick the blink and any pre-seeded query.
202		if m.globalSearching {
203			cmds := []tea.Cmd{m.globalInput.Focus()}
204			if queryReady(m.globalQuery) {
205				cmds = append(cmds, searchDebounce(m.searchSeq, modeProjects))
206			}
207			return tea.Batch(cmds...)
208		}
209		return nil
210	}
211	
212	func (m sessionTUI) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
213		switch msg := msg.(type) {
214		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
215			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
216			m.reader.SetWidth(m.width)
217			m.reader.SetHeight(m.previewHeight())
218			return m, nil
219		case indexWarmedMsg:
220			return m.refreshAfterWarm()
221		case searchDebounceMsg:
222			// Fire the actual query only if no newer keystroke has arrived (debounce).
223			if msg.seq == m.searchSeq {
224				if m.searchCancel != nil {
225					m.searchCancel() // abort any prior in-flight query, freeing its connection
226				}
227				ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
228				m.searchCancel = cancel
229				return m, m.runSearch(msg.seq, msg.kind, ctx)
230			}
231			return m, nil
232		case searchResultMsg:
233			// Apply only the latest query's results (discard stale async results).
234			if msg.seq == m.searchSeq {
235				m.applySearchResults(msg.kind, msg.sessions)
236				return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(msg.kind)
237			}
238			return m, nil
239		case snippetResultMsg:
240			if msg.seq == m.snippetSeq {
241				if msg.kind == modeProjects {
242					if m.globalSnippets == nil {
243						m.globalSnippets = map[string]string{}
244					}
245					for key, snip := range msg.snippets {
246						m.globalSnippets[key] = snip
247					}
248				} else {
249					if m.filteredSnippets == nil {
250						m.filteredSnippets = map[string]string{}
251					}
252					for key, snip := range msg.snippets {
253						m.filteredSnippets[key] = snip
254					}
255				}
256			}
257			return m, nil
258		case tea.KeyPressMsg:
259			switch {
260			case m.previewing:
261				// The preview is a top-level overlay: it opens over the list OR the global
262				// results, so it must be checked before any mode-specific routing.
263				return m.updatePreview(msg)
264			case m.mode == modeTarget:
265				return m.updateTarget(msg)
266			case m.mode == modeProjects:
267				switch {
268				case m.globalSearching:
269					return m.updateGlobalSearch(msg)
270				case m.globalActive:
271					return m.updateGlobalResults(msg)
272				case m.projSearching:
273					return m.updateProjectSearch(msg)
274				default:
275					return m.updateProjects(msg)
276				}
277			case m.searching:
278				return m.updateSearch(msg)
279			default:
280				return m.updateList(msg)
281			}
282		}
283		return m, nil
284	}
285	
286	// refreshAfterWarm re-queries the index after the background current-project warm pass lands,
287	// so newly created or changed sessions for THIS project appear without a restart. It refreshes
288	// in place only when the home project's session list is what's on screen (preserving the
289	// cursor by session id), or re-runs a settled cross-project search to fold in the fresh rows.
290	// In any other view it just caches the refreshed sessions for when the user returns home.
291	func (m sessionTUI) refreshAfterWarm() (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
292		sessions, err := m.store.ListByProject(m.homeProjectID)
293		if err != nil {
294			slog.Debug("resume: refresh after warm failed", "error", err)
295			return m, nil
296		}
297		m.homeSessions = sessions
298	
299		// In-place refresh when the home project's list is the active view.
300		if m.mode == modeList && !m.inBrowser && m.projectID == m.homeProjectID {
301			selID := ""
302			if sel := m.selected(); sel != nil {
303				selID = sel.SessionID
304			}
305			m.all = sessions
306			m.rebuildAgentCycle()
307			m.applyFilter()
308			m.cursor = indexOfSession(m.filtered, selID)
309			m.clampScroll()
310			return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeList)
311		}
312	
313		// A settled cross-project search (not mid-typing): re-run it so refreshed rows show up.
314		// While typing, the user's own debounced queries already see the fresh data.
315		if m.mode == modeProjects && m.globalActive && !m.globalSearching && queryReady(m.globalQuery) {
316			m.searchSeq++
317			return m, searchDebounce(m.searchSeq, modeProjects)
318		}
319		return m, nil
320	}
321	
322	// indexOfSession returns the position of the session with id in list, or 0 when not present
323	// (so a refreshed list with a deleted selection lands safely at the top).
324	func indexOfSession(list []sessionindex.Session, id string) int {
325		if id == "" {
326			return 0
327		}
328		for i := range list {
329			if list[i].SessionID == id {
330				return i
331			}
332		}
333		return 0
334	}
335	
336	// updateList handles keys while browsing the session list.
337	func (m sessionTUI) updateList(msg tea.KeyPressMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
338		switch msg.String() {
339		case "q", "ctrl+c":
340			m.result = sessionTUIResult{cancelled: true}
341			return m, tea.Quit
342		case "esc":
343			// In a drilled-in project, esc returns to the browser; at home it quits.
344			if m.inBrowser {
345				m.enterBrowser()
346				return m, nil
347			}
348			m.result = sessionTUIResult{cancelled: true}
349			return m, tea.Quit
350		case "tab":
351			// Toggle scope: home list ↔ all-projects browser.
352			m.enterBrowser()
353			return m, nil
354		case "up", "k":
355			m.moveCursor(-1)
356			return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeList)
357		case "down", "j":
358			m.moveCursor(1)
359			return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeList)
360		case "pgup":
361			m.moveCursor(-m.listHeight())
362			return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeList)
363		case "pgdown":
364			m.moveCursor(m.listHeight())
365			return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeList)
366		case "home", "g":
367			m.cursor, m.top = 0, 0
368			return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeList)
369		case "end", "G":
370			m.cursor = len(m.filtered) - 1
371			m.clampScroll()
372			return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeList)
373		case "r":
374			// Resume the highlighted session (enter is deliberately a no-op here, so a stray
375			// return can't accidentally launch an agent).
376			if sel := m.selected(); sel != nil {
377				return m.beginResume(sel)
378			}
379		case " ", "space":
380			if sel := m.selected(); sel != nil {
381				return m.openPreview(sel)
382			}
383		case "/":
384			m.searching = true
385			m.search.SetValue(m.searchQuery)
386			return m, m.search.Focus()
387		case "a":
388			return m, m.cycleAgent()
389		case "v":
390			m.toggleViewMode()
391			return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeList)
392		}
393		return m, nil
394	}
395	
396	// updateSearch handles the full-text search input. The typed character is applied to the
397	// input immediately (instant feedback); the FTS query runs async + debounced so a slow
398	// query never blocks typing. See searchDebounceMsg / searchResultMsg.
399	func (m sessionTUI) updateSearch(msg tea.KeyPressMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
400		switch msg.String() {
401		case "esc":
402			m.searching = false
403			m.search.Blur()
404			m.searchQuery = ""
405			m.searchSeq++ // invalidate any in-flight search
406			m.snippetSeq++
407			m.applyFilter()
408			return m, nil
409		case "enter":
410			m.searching = false
411			m.search.Blur()
412			return m, nil
413		case "up", "down", "pgup", "pgdown":
414			// Arrows do nothing inside the one-line input, so commit the search and let the same
415			// key move the now-focused list — the user reaches a result without a separate enter.
416			m.searching = false
417			m.search.Blur()
418			return m.updateList(msg)
419		}
420		var cmd tea.Cmd
421		m.search, cmd = m.search.Update(msg)
422		m.searchQuery = m.search.Value()
423		m.searchSeq++
424		if !queryReady(m.searchQuery) {
425			// Too short to search: show the full (agent-filtered) list synchronously.
426			m.snippetSeq++
427			m.applyFilter()
428			return m, cmd
429		}
430		return m, tea.Batch(cmd, searchDebounce(m.searchSeq, modeList))
431	}
432	
433	// updatePreview handles keys while the glamour preview reader is open. Close keys return to
434	// the underlying screen (list or global results); `r` resumes the previewed session;
435	// everything else (↑↓ pgup/pgdn) scrolls the viewport.
436	func (m sessionTUI) updatePreview(msg tea.KeyPressMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
437		switch msg.String() {
438		case " ", "space", "esc", "q":
439			m.previewing = false
440			m.readerSession = nil
441			return m, nil
442		case "r":
443			if m.readerSession != nil {
444				sess := m.readerSession
445				m.previewing = false
446				m.readerSession = nil
447				return m.beginResume(sess)
448			}
449			return m, nil
450		}
451		var cmd tea.Cmd
452		m.reader, cmd = m.reader.Update(msg)
453		return m, cmd
454	}
455	
456	// openPreview loads the highlighted session into the glamour reader. Identical to search's
457	// openReader so the two previews render the same way.
458	func (m sessionTUI) openPreview(s *sessionindex.Session) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
459		m.readerSession = s
460		m.reader.SetWidth(m.width)
461		m.reader.SetHeight(m.previewHeight())
462		m.reader.SetContent(renderGlamour(sessionMarkdown(m.registry, m.store, s), m.width))
463		m.reader.GotoTop()
464		m.previewing = true
465		return m, nil
466	}
467	
468	// previewHeight is the viewport height inside the preview chrome (title + two rules + footer).
469	func (m sessionTUI) previewHeight() int {
470		const chrome = 4
471		h := m.height - chrome
472		if h < 1 {
473			h = 1
474		}
475		return h
476	}
477	
478	// updateTarget handles the target-agent selection step.
479	func (m sessionTUI) updateTarget(msg tea.KeyPressMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
480		switch msg.String() {
481		case "esc", "q":
482			m.mode = modeList
483			m.chosen = nil
484		case "ctrl+c":
485			m.result = sessionTUIResult{cancelled: true}
486			return m, tea.Quit
487		case "up", "k":
488			if m.targetCursor > 0 {
489				m.targetCursor--
490			}
491		case "down", "j":
492			if m.targetCursor < len(m.installed)-1 {
493				m.targetCursor++
494			}
495		case "enter":
496			m.result = sessionTUIResult{session: m.chosen, targetID: m.installed[m.targetCursor].id}
497			return m, tea.Quit
498		}
499		return m, nil
500	}
501	
502	// ---- list mechanics ----
503	
504	func (m *sessionTUI) moveCursor(delta int) {
505		if len(m.filtered) == 0 {
506			return
507		}
508		m.cursor += delta
509		if m.cursor < 0 {
510			m.cursor = 0
511		}
512		if m.cursor > len(m.filtered)-1 {
513			m.cursor = len(m.filtered) - 1
514		}
515		m.clampScroll()
516	}
517	
518	func (m *sessionTUI) clampScroll() {
519		h := m.listHeight()
520		if m.cursor < m.top {
521			m.top = m.cursor
522		}
523		if m.cursor >= m.top+h {
524			m.top = m.cursor - h + 1
525		}
526		if m.top < 0 {
527			m.top = 0
528		}
529	}
530	
531	func (m sessionTUI) selected() *sessionindex.Session {
532		if m.cursor < 0 || m.cursor >= len(m.filtered) {
533			return nil
534		}
535		return &m.filtered[m.cursor]
536	}
537	
538	// rowsPerSession is how many terminal lines one list row occupies in each view mode.
539	func (m sessionTUI) rowsPerSession() int {
540		if m.viewMode == "sparse" {
541			return 2
542		}
543		return 1
544	}
545	
546	// listHeight is how many sessions fit in the list region (height minus chrome).
547	func (m sessionTUI) listHeight() int {
548		const chrome = 5 // header(1) + two rules(2) + footer(1) + margin
549		avail := m.height - chrome
550		if avail < 1 {
551			avail = 1
552		}
553		n := avail / m.rowsPerSession()
554		if n < 1 {
555			n = 1
556		}
557		return n
558	}
559	
560	func (m *sessionTUI) rebuildAgentCycle() {
561		present := map[string]bool{}
562		for _, s := range m.all {
563			present[s.Agent] = true
564		}
565		ids := make([]string, 0, len(present))
566		for id := range present {
567			ids = append(ids, id)
568		}
569		sort.Strings(ids)
570		m.agentCycle = append([]string{""}, ids...)
571	}
572	
573	func (m *sessionTUI) cycleAgent() tea.Cmd {
574		// In the all-projects search, cycle over every known agent and re-run the query; in a
575		// session list, cycle over the agents actually present and re-filter in place.
576		if m.mode == modeProjects && m.globalActive {
577			m.agentFilter = nextInCycle(m.allAgentCycle(), m.agentFilter)
578			m.searchSeq++
579			return searchDebounce(m.searchSeq, modeProjects)
580		}
581		m.agentFilter = nextInCycle(m.agentCycle, m.agentFilter)
582		m.applyFilter()
583		return m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeList)
584	}
585	
586	// allAgentCycle is the agent-filter ring for cross-project search: "" then every known agent.
587	func (m sessionTUI) allAgentCycle() []string {
588		ids := make([]string, 0, len(m.agents))
589		for id := range m.agents {
590			ids = append(ids, id)
591		}
592		sort.Strings(ids)
593		return append([]string{""}, ids...)
594	}
595	
596	// nextInCycle returns the element after cur in cycle, wrapping around.
597	func nextInCycle(cycle []string, cur string) string {
598		idx := 0
599		for i, id := range cycle {
600			if id == cur {
601				idx = i
602				break
603			}
604		}
605		return cycle[(idx+1)%len(cycle)]
606	}
607	
608	func (m *sessionTUI) toggleViewMode() {
609		if m.viewMode == "sparse" {
610			m.viewMode = "dense"
611		} else {
612			m.viewMode = "sparse"
613		}
614		m.clampScroll()
615	}
616	
617	// applyFilter rebuilds the visible list from the agent filter and search query. When a
618	// search is active, snippets are fetched lazily for the visible rows; otherwise the list
619	// is the project's sessions filtered by agent and snippets are cleared.
620	func (m *sessionTUI) applyFilter() {
621		m.filtered = m.filtered[:0]
622		m.filteredSnippets = nil
623	
624		if queryReady(m.searchQuery) {
625			var out []sessionindex.Session
626			if sessions, err := m.store.Search(ftsQuery(m.searchQuery), m.projectID); err == nil {
627				for _, s := range sessions {
628					if m.agentFilter == "" || s.Agent == m.agentFilter {
629						out = append(out, s)
630					}
631				}
632			}
633			m.filtered = out
634			m.filteredSnippets = map[string]string{}
635		} else {
636			out := make([]sessionindex.Session, 0, len(m.all))
637			for _, s := range m.all {
638				if m.agentFilter == "" || s.Agent == m.agentFilter {
639					out = append(out, s)
640				}
641			}
642			m.filtered = out
643		}
644	
645		if m.cursor > len(m.filtered)-1 {
646			m.cursor = len(m.filtered) - 1
647		}
648		if m.cursor < 0 {
649			m.cursor = 0
650		}
651		m.top = 0
652		m.clampScroll()
653	}
654	
655	// beginResume advances from a chosen session toward launch. When a target agent was
656	// pre-selected via `resume <agent>`, that choice is honored immediately and the picker exits
657	// — the user is never asked to pick a target. Otherwise it moves to the target-selection step.
658	func (m sessionTUI) beginResume(sess *sessionindex.Session) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
659		m.chosen = sess
660		if m.presetTo != "" {
661			m.result = sessionTUIResult{session: sess, targetID: m.presetTo}
662			return m, tea.Quit
663		}
664		m.mode = modeTarget
665		m.targetCursor = m.defaultTargetIndex()
666		return m, nil
667	}
668	
669	func (m sessionTUI) defaultTargetIndex() int {
670		// Prefer the last-resumed agent, else the chosen session's own agent (same-agent resume).
671		// An explicit preset (`resume <agent>`) never reaches here — beginResume skips the
672		// target-selection step entirely in that case.
673		want := m.lastAgent
674		if want == "" && m.chosen != nil {
675			want = m.chosen.Agent
676		}
677		for i, a := range m.installed {
678			if a.id == want {
679				return i
680			}
681		}
682		return 0
683	}
684	
685	// ftsQuery turns free-form input into a safe FTS5 prefix query (alnum tokens only).
686	// minQueryLen is the shortest query we run a full-text search for. A single-character
687	// prefix (e.g. "t*") matches almost the entire corpus, and ranking + snippet generation
688	// over that set is pathologically slow (~80s on a ~800-session index), so we never fire
689	// it. Shared by resume and search so both behave the same.
690	const minQueryLen = 2
691	
692	// queryReady reports whether input has enough searchable characters (letters/digits, the
693	// same runes ftsQuery keeps) to run an FTS query. Below minQueryLen we don't search.
694	func queryReady(input string) bool {
695		n := 0
696		for _, r := range input {
697			if unicode.IsLetter(r) || unicode.IsDigit(r) {
698				n++
699				if n >= minQueryLen {
700					return true
701				}
702			}
703		}
704		return false
705	}
706	
707	// ftsQuery turns free-form input into an FTS5 query string. Three shapes:
708	//
709	//   - Bare words become independent prefix terms, AND-ed (matched anywhere, any order) —
710	//     a loose search:  thank you      -> thank* you*
711	//   - A double-quoted span becomes a phrase: tokens adjacent and in order —
712	//     an exact phrase:  "thank you"    -> "thank you"
713	//   - A still-open quote (the user is mid-typing the phrase) keeps the final token a prefix,
714	//     so results keep updating live:  "thank yo    -> thank + yo*
715	//
716	// A single typed word that contains punctuation (e.g. a filename, "poem.txt") is itself an
717	// adjacency phrase. The index's unicode61 tokenizer splits on every non-alphanumeric run, so
718	// "poem.txt" is stored as the two adjacent tokens poem, txt — never the fused "poemtxt". We
719	// must tokenize the query the same way, or the term can't match what was indexed. So a bare
720	// "poem.txt" becomes the phrase poem + txt* (adjacent, prefix the last token for type-ahead);
721	// quoted, it is the committed phrase "poem txt" (adjacent, no prefix).
722	//
723	// Only letters and digits survive tokenization, so no FTS5 syntax character from the raw
724	// input can reach MATCH — every query this builds is safe to execute.
725	func ftsQuery(input string) string {
726		// Splitting on '"' yields alternating runs: even indexes are outside quotes, odd indexes
727		// are inside. An odd-indexed run that is also the LAST run means the closing quote hasn't
728		// been typed yet (an open phrase), so its final token stays a prefix for live type-ahead.
729		segs := strings.Split(input, `"`)
730		var parts []string
731		for i, seg := range segs {
732			switch {
733			case i%2 == 0: // outside quotes: each word is its own loose, prefix-terminated term
734				for _, f := range strings.Fields(seg) {
735					if toks := fieldTokens(f); len(toks) > 0 {
736						parts = append(parts, strings.Join(toks, " + ")+"*")
737					}
738				}
739			case i == len(segs)-1: // open phrase (still typing): adjacency across all tokens + prefix last
740				if toks := segmentTokens(seg); len(toks) > 0 {
741					parts = append(parts, strings.Join(toks, " + ")+"*")
742				}
743			default: // closed phrase: exact adjacency, no prefix (a committed phrase)
744				if toks := segmentTokens(seg); len(toks) > 0 {
745					parts = append(parts, `"`+strings.Join(toks, " ")+`"`)
746				}
747			}
748		}
749		return strings.Join(parts, " ")
750	}
751	
752	// fieldTokens splits one whitespace-delimited field into its FTS5 tokens the way the index's
753	// default unicode61 tokenizer does: every run of non-alphanumeric characters is a token
754	// boundary. So "poem.txt" -> [poem, txt] and "max-cpu!" -> [max, cpu]. Mirroring the index
755	// tokenizer here is the whole point — previously punctuation was stripped and the pieces
756	// fused into one token ("poemtxt") that the index, holding poem and txt separately, never had.
757	func fieldTokens(field string) []string {
758		var tokens []string
759		var b strings.Builder
760		flush := func() {
761			if b.Len() > 0 {
762				tokens = append(tokens, b.String())
763				b.Reset()
764			}
765		}
766		for _, r := range field {
767			if unicode.IsLetter(r) || unicode.IsDigit(r) {
768				b.WriteRune(r)
769			} else {
770				flush()
771			}
772		}
773		flush()
774		return tokens
775	}
776	
777	// segmentTokens tokenizes a whole quoted segment into one flat, ordered token list — both the
778	// whitespace between words and any punctuation within them are token boundaries (unicode61),
779	// so the phrase matches the adjacency the index actually stored.
780	func segmentTokens(seg string) []string {
781		var tokens []string
782		for _, f := range strings.Fields(seg) {
783			tokens = append(tokens, fieldTokens(f)...)
784		}
785		return tokens
786	}
787	
788	// ---- rendering ----
789	
790	var (
791		styDim    = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("240"))
792		styFaint  = lipgloss.NewStyle().Faint(true)
793		styBold   = lipgloss.NewStyle().Bold(true)
794		styCursor = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("213")).Bold(true)
795		stySel    = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("255")).Bold(true)
796	)
797	
798	func (m sessionTUI) View() tea.View {
799		var content string
800		switch {
801		case m.previewing:
802			content = m.renderPreview()
803		case m.mode == modeTarget:
804			content = m.renderTarget()
805		case m.mode == modeProjects && m.globalActive:
806			content = m.renderGlobalResults()
807		case m.mode == modeProjects:
808			content = m.renderProjects()
809		default:
810			content = m.renderListScreen()
811		}
812		v := tea.NewView(content)
813		v.AltScreen = true
814		return v
815	}
816	
817	func (m sessionTUI) renderListScreen() string {
818		var b strings.Builder
819		b.WriteString(m.renderHeader())
820		b.WriteString("\n")
821		b.WriteString(strings.Repeat("─", m.lineWidth()))
822		b.WriteString("\n")
823		b.WriteString(m.renderRows())
824		b.WriteString("\n")
825		b.WriteString(strings.Repeat("─", m.lineWidth()))
826		b.WriteString("\n")
827		b.WriteString(m.renderFooter())
828		return b.String()
829	}
830	
831	func (m sessionTUI) lineWidth() int {
832		if m.width < 20 {
833			return 80
834		}
835		return m.width
836	}
837	
838	// headerLeft is the shared "<title> · <scope>" left segment used by every screen.
839	func (m sessionTUI) headerLeft(scope string) string {
840		return styBold.Render(m.title) + styDim.Render(" · ") + scope
841	}
842	
843	// projectScope labels the current project, e.g. "project: getspecstory" (name in white).
844	func (m sessionTUI) projectScope() string {
845		return styDim.Render("project: ") + stySel.Render(m.projectName)
846	}
847	
848	// agentScope mirrors the project-scope label: an unstyled "all agents" phrase (rendered like
849	// "all projects") when unfiltered, else "agent: <name>" matching projectScope's "project: <name>".
850	func (m sessionTUI) agentScope() string {
851		if m.agentFilter == "" {
852			return "all agents"
853		}
854		return styDim.Render("agent: ") + stySel.Render(m.agentName(m.agentFilter))
855	}
856	
857	func (m sessionTUI) renderHeader() string {
858		// Agent filter sits left, right after the project, rather than tucked in the far corner.
859		left := m.headerLeft(m.projectScope()) + styDim.Render("  ·  ") + m.agentScope()
860		return headerRow(left, "", m.lineWidth())
861	}
862	
863	func (m sessionTUI) renderRows() string {
864		if len(m.filtered) == 0 {
865			return styFaint.Render("  No sessions match.")
866		}
867		h := m.listHeight()
868		end := min(m.top+h, len(m.filtered))
869		var b strings.Builder
870		for i := m.top; i < end; i++ {
871			b.WriteString(m.sessionRow(m.filtered[i], i == m.cursor, m.snippetAt(i)))
872			if i < end-1 {
873				b.WriteString("\n")
874			}
875		}
876		return b.String()
877	}
878	
879	// snippetAt returns the search snippet for filtered row i, or "" (no active search).
880	func (m sessionTUI) snippetAt(i int) string {
881		if i >= 0 && i < len(m.filtered) {
882			s := m.filtered[i]
883			return m.filteredSnippets[sessionindex.FingerprintKey(s.Agent, s.SessionID)]
884		}
885		return ""
886	}
887	
888	// sessionRow renders one list row. When snippet is non-empty (search active) the row
889	// shows the highlighted match context instead of the session title.
890	func (m sessionTUI) sessionRow(s sessionindex.Session, selected bool, snippet string) string {
891		cursor := "  "
892		if selected {
893			cursor = styCursor.Render("▸ ")
894		}
895		agent := m.agentTag(s.Agent)
896		when := fmt.Sprintf("%-4s", relativeTime(s.UpdatedAt))
897	
898		if m.viewMode == "sparse" {
899			turns := styDim.Render(fmt.Sprintf("%d prompts", s.UserTurns))
900			label := renderName(sessionTitle(s), selected, m.lineWidth()-24)
901			if snippet != "" {
902				label = renderSnippet(snippet, m.lineWidth()-26)
903			}
904			head := cursor + agent + "  " + label + "   " + turns
905			sub := "    " + styFaint.Render(fmt.Sprintf("%s ago · %s", relativeTime(s.UpdatedAt), shortID(s.SessionID)))
906			return head + "\n" + sub
907		}
908		turns := styDim.Render(fmt.Sprintf("%4d", s.UserTurns))
909		// A year-stamped date ("Dec 31 '25") is wider than the 4-col slot; shrink the label by the
910		// overflow so the right-hand turns column can't get pushed off the line and wrap.
911		extra := len(when) - 4
912		if extra < 0 {
913			extra = 0
914		}
915		label := renderName(sessionTitle(s), selected, m.lineWidth()-22-extra)
916		if snippet != "" {
917			label = renderSnippet(snippet, m.lineWidth()-24-extra)
918		}
919		return cursor + agent + " " + styDim.Render(when) + "  " + label + "  " + turns
920	}
921	
922	// renderSnippet renders a FTS snippet (matched terms wrapped in the control-char marks)
923	// with the matches highlighted, collapsing whitespace and clipping to maxWidth columns.
924	func renderSnippet(snip string, maxWidth int) string {
925		if maxWidth < 8 {
926			maxWidth = 8
927		}
928		snip = strings.NewReplacer("\n", " ", "\t", " ", "\r", " ").Replace(snip)
929		hl := lipgloss.NewStyle().Bold(true).Foreground(lipgloss.Color("232")).Background(lipgloss.Color("221"))
930	
931		var b strings.Builder
932		var seg strings.Builder
933		matched := false
934		visible := 0
935		flush := func() {
936			if seg.Len() == 0 {
937				return
938			}
939			if matched {
940				b.WriteString(hl.Render(seg.String()))
941			} else {
942				b.WriteString(seg.String())
943			}
944			seg.Reset()
945		}
946		for _, r := range snip {
947			switch r {
948			case '\x02':
949				flush()
950				matched = true
951				continue
952			case '\x03':
953				flush()
954				matched = false
955				continue
956			}
957			if visible >= maxWidth {
958				flush()
959				b.WriteString("…")
960				return b.String()
961			}
962			seg.WriteRune(r)
963			visible++
964		}
965		flush()
966		return b.String()
967	}
968	
969	func renderName(name string, selected bool, width int) string {
970		if width < 8 {
971			width = 8
972		}
973		t := truncate(name, width)
974		if selected {
975			return stySel.Render(t)
976		}
977		return t
978	}
979	
980	func (m sessionTUI) renderFooter() string {
981		if m.searching {
982			return m.search.View() + "    " + styFaint.Render("esc clear · enter apply")
983		}
984		scopeKey := "tab all-projects"
985		if m.inBrowser {
986			scopeKey = "tab/esc back"
987		}
988		keys := []string{"↑↓ move", "r resume", "space preview", "/ search", "a agent", scopeKey, "v " + m.viewMode, "q quit"}
989		return styDim.Render(strings.Join(keys, " · "))
990	}
991	
992	func (m sessionTUI) renderPreview() string {
993		var b strings.Builder
994		left := styBold.Render("Preview")
995		if s := m.readerSession; s != nil {
996			left += styDim.Render(" · ") + m.agentTag(s.Agent) +
997				styDim.Render(" · ") + sessionTitle(*s)
998		}
999		b.WriteString(left)
1000		b.WriteString("\n")
1001		b.WriteString(strings.Repeat("─", m.lineWidth()))
1002		b.WriteString("\n")
1003		b.WriteString(m.reader.View())
1004		b.WriteString("\n")
1005		b.WriteString(strings.Repeat("─", m.lineWidth()))
1006		b.WriteString("\n")
1007		b.WriteString(styDim.Render(strings.Join([]string{"↑↓ scroll", "pgup/pgdn page", "r resume", "space/esc close"}, " · ")))
1008		return b.String()
1009	}
1010	
1011	func (m sessionTUI) renderTarget() string {
1012		var b strings.Builder
1013		b.WriteString(styBold.Render("Resume into which agent?"))
1014		if m.chosen != nil {
1015			b.WriteString(styDim.Render("   " + sessionTitle(*m.chosen)))
1016		}
1017		b.WriteString("\n\n")
1018		for i, a := range m.installed {
1019			cursor := "   "
1020			label := a.provider.Name()
1021			if m.chosen != nil && a.id == m.chosen.Agent {
1022				label += styFaint.Render(" (same agent — native resume)")
1023			}
1024			if i == m.targetCursor {
1025				cursor = styCursor.Render(" ▸ ")
1026				label = stySel.Render(a.provider.Name()) + label[len(a.provider.Name()):]
1027			}
1028			b.WriteString(cursor + label + "\n")
1029		}
1030		b.WriteString("\n")
1031		b.WriteString(styDim.Render(strings.Join([]string{"↑↓ move", "↵ resume", "esc back"}, " · ")))
1032		return b.String()
1033	}
1034	
1035	// ---- helpers ----
1036	
1037	func (m sessionTUI) agentName(id string) string {
1038		if a, ok := m.agents[id]; ok {
1039			return a.name
1040		}
1041		return id
1042	}
1043	
1044	func (m sessionTUI) agentTag(id string) string {
1045		label := fmt.Sprintf("%-8s", id)
1046		if a, ok := m.agents[id]; ok {
1047			return lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(a.accent).Render(label)
1048		}
1049		return label
1050	}
1051	
1052	// sessionTitle is the human label for a session: name, then slug, then short id.
1053	func sessionTitle(s sessionindex.Session) string {
1054		switch {
1055		case strings.TrimSpace(s.Name) != "":
1056			return s.Name
1057		case strings.TrimSpace(s.Slug) != "":
1058			return s.Slug
1059		default:
1060			return shortID(s.SessionID)
1061		}
1062	}
1063	
1064	// truncate shortens s to n runes with an ellipsis.
1065	func truncate(s string, n int) string {
1066		r := []rune(s)
1067		if len(r) <= n {
1068			return s
1069		}
1070		if n < 1 {
1071			return ""
1072		}
1073		return string(r[:n-1]) + "…"
1074	}
1075	
1076	// relativeTime renders an ISO 8601 timestamp as a compact "2m"/"3h"/"5d"/"Jun 2".
1077	func relativeTime(iso string) string {
1078		t, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, iso)
1079		if err != nil {
1080			return ""
1081		}
1082		d := time.Since(t)
1083		switch {
1084		case d < time.Minute:
1085			return "now"
1086		case d < time.Hour:
1087			return fmt.Sprintf("%dm", int(d.Minutes()))
1088		case d < 24*time.Hour:
1089			return fmt.Sprintf("%dh", int(d.Hours()))
1090		case d < 7*24*time.Hour:
1091			return fmt.Sprintf("%dd", int(d.Hours()/24))
1092		default:
1093			lt := t.Local()
1094			if lt.Year() == time.Now().Year() {
1095				return lt.Format("Jan 2")
1096			}
1097			// Disambiguate prior years so "Dec 31" can't be mistaken for this year: "Dec 31 '25".
1098			return lt.Format("Jan 2 '06")
1099		}
1100	}
1101	
1102	// headerRow lays out a left and right segment across width with a gap between.
1103	func headerRow(left, right string, width int) string {
1104		gap := width - lipgloss.Width(left) - lipgloss.Width(right)
1105		if gap < 1 {
1106			gap = 1
1107		}
1108		return left + strings.Repeat(" ", gap) + right
1109	}
1110	
1111	// renderGlamour renders markdown to styled terminal output for the preview reader.
1112	func renderGlamour(md string, width int) string {
1113		if width < 20 {
1114			width = 80
1115		}
1116		r, err := glamour.NewTermRenderer(glamour.WithStandardStyle("dark"), glamour.WithWordWrap(width-2))
1117		if err != nil {
1118			return md
1119		}
1120		out, err := r.Render(md)
1121		if err != nil {
1122			return md
1123		}
1124		return out
1125	}
1126	
1127	// sessionMarkdown returns the session as markdown for the preview — the real specstory
1128	// render when the session can be re-parsed (needs a cwd), else the plain FTS body.
1129	func sessionMarkdown(registry *factory.Registry, store *sessionindex.Store, s *sessionindex.Session) string {
1130		if s.OriginCwd != "" {
1131			if prov, err := registry.Get(s.Agent); err == nil {
1132				if full, err := prov.GetAgentChatSession(s.OriginCwd, s.SessionID, false); err == nil &&
1133					full != nil && full.SessionData != nil {
1134					if md, err := session.GenerateMarkdownFromAgentSession(full.SessionData, false, true); err == nil {
1135						return md
1136					}
1137				}
1138			}
1139		}
1140		if body, _ := store.SessionBody(s.Agent, s.SessionID); strings.TrimSpace(body) != "" {
1141			return "```\n" + body + "\n```"
1142		}
1143		return "_(no readable content for this session)_"
1144	}
1145	
1146	// ---- integration with the resume command ----
1147	
1148	// openOrBuildResumeIndex opens sessions.db, building it first (with the normal reindex
1149	// UI) when it is missing or empty, then proceeding straight into the picker. builtFresh
1150	// reports whether a full foreground build just ran — when it did, the index is already
1151	// fully current, so callers skip the background warm (it would be redundant).
1152	func openOrBuildResumeIndex() (store *sessionindex.Store, builtFresh bool, err error) {
1153		dbPath, err := sessionindex.DefaultPath()
1154		if err != nil {
1155			return nil, false, err
1156		}
1157		if _, statErr := os.Stat(dbPath); statErr == nil {
1158			s, err := sessionindex.OpenReader(dbPath)
1159			if err != nil {
1160				return nil, false, err
1161			}
1162			if n, _ := s.Count(); n > 0 {
1163				return s, false, nil
1164			}
1165			_ = s.Close() // empty → rebuild below
1166		}
1167		if err := runReindex(false); err != nil {
1168			return nil, false, err
1169		}
1170		s, err := sessionindex.OpenReader(dbPath)
1171		return s, true, err
1172	}
1173	
1174	// indexWarmedMsg is sent to the running TUI when the background warm finishes refreshing the
1175	// CURRENT project (the first warm pass). The model re-queries so new/changed sessions for this
1176	// project appear without a restart. The subsequent full pass is silent (no refresh).
1177	type indexWarmedMsg struct{}
1178	
1179	// startIndexWarm launches the background two-pass index warm for projectID, refreshing the
1180	// running TUI (via p.Send) when the current-project pass completes. The returned cancel func
1181	// should be invoked when the TUI exits: the warm is best-effort and must never block process
1182	// exit. When builtFresh is true the index was just rebuilt in the foreground, so there is
1183	// nothing to warm and this only returns a cancel func.
1184	func startIndexWarm(p *tea.Program, projectID string, builtFresh bool) context.CancelFunc {
1185		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
1186		if builtFresh {
1187			return cancel
1188		}
1189		dbPath, err := sessionindex.DefaultPath()
1190		if err != nil {
1191			slog.Debug("warm: no db path; skipping", "error", err)
1192			return cancel
1193		}
1194		go warmIndexInBackground(ctx, dbPath, projectID, func() { p.Send(indexWarmedMsg{}) })
1195		return cancel
1196	}
1197	
1198	// selectResumeViaTUI runs the picker for the current project and returns the chosen
1199	// resume plan (or nil if the user cancelled). On a successful selection it persists the
1200	// view-mode and target-agent preferences to the user config.
1201	func selectResumeViaTUI(registry *factory.Registry, store *sessionindex.Store, projectID, projectName, presetTo string, builtFresh bool) (*resumePlan, error) {
1202		sessions, err := store.ListByProject(projectID)
1203		if err != nil {
1204			return nil, fmt.Errorf("loading sessions: %w", err)
1205		}
1206		// An empty current project is fine — the picker opens in the all-projects browser.
1207		// Only bail when the whole index is empty (nothing to resume anywhere).
1208		if total, _ := store.Count(); total == 0 {
1209			fprintln(os.Stderr, "\nNo agent sessions indexed yet. Run an agent here, then try again (or `specstory reindex`).")
1210			return nil, nil
1211		}
1212	
1213		agents := map[string]agentMeta{}
1214		var installed []agentChoice
1215		for _, id := range registry.ListIDs() {
1216			prov, err := registry.Get(id)
1217			if err != nil {
1218				continue
1219			}
1220			agents[id] = agentMeta{name: prov.Name(), accent: colorForAgent(id)}
1221			if prov.Check("").Success {
1222				installed = append(installed, agentChoice{id: id, provider: prov})
1223			}
1224		}
1225		if len(installed) == 0 {
1226			fprintln(os.Stderr, "\nNo installed agents found to resume into.")
1227			return nil, nil
1228		}
1229	
1230		// A preset target (`resume <agent>`) must be installed to resume into. The command already
1231		// confirmed it is a known provider; here, with the installed set known, confirm it is
1232		// available and resolve it to its canonical ID. When set, the picker skips the
1233		// target-selection step entirely (see beginResume).
1234		if presetTo != "" {
1235			resolved := ""
1236			for _, a := range installed {
1237				if strings.EqualFold(a.id, presetTo) {
1238					resolved = a.id
1239					break
1240				}
1241			}
1242			if resolved == "" {
1243				return nil, utils.ValidationError{Message: fmt.Sprintf(
1244					"agent %q is not installed, so it can't be a resume target.", presetTo)}
1245			}
1246			presetTo = resolved
1247		}
1248	
1249		viewMode, lastAgent := "dense", ""
1250		if cfg, _ := config.Load(nil); cfg != nil {
1251			viewMode = cfg.GetResumeViewMode()
1252			lastAgent = cfg.GetResumeLastAgent()
1253		}
1254	
1255		model := newSessionTUI(store, registry, projectID, projectName, sessions, agents, installed, sessionTUIOpts{
1256			title:     "SpecStory Resume",
1257			presetTo:  presetTo,
1258			lastAgent: lastAgent,
1259			viewMode:  viewMode,
1260		})
1261		p := tea.NewProgram(model)
1262		cancelWarm := startIndexWarm(p, projectID, builtFresh)
1263		final, err := p.Run()
1264		cancelWarm() // stop warming the moment the picker exits (never block on it)
1265		if err != nil {
1266			return nil, fmt.Errorf("resume picker failed: %w", err)
1267		}
1268		rm := final.(sessionTUI)
1269		if rm.result.cancelled || rm.result.session == nil {
1270			return nil, nil
1271		}
1272	
1273		fromID := rm.result.session.Agent
1274		fromProv, err := registry.Get(fromID)
1275		if err != nil {
1276			return nil, fmt.Errorf("unknown source agent %q: %w", fromID, err)
1277		}
1278		toProv, err := registry.Get(rm.result.targetID)
1279		if err != nil {
1280			return nil, fmt.Errorf("unknown target agent %q: %w", rm.result.targetID, err)
1281		}
1282	
1283		if err := config.SaveResumePrefs(rm.viewMode, rm.result.targetID); err != nil {
1284			slog.Debug("resume: could not save prefs", "error", err)
1285		}
1286	
1287		return &resumePlan{
1288			from:      fromProv,
1289			fromID:    fromID,
1290			sessionID: rm.result.session.SessionID,
1291			fromCwd:   rm.result.session.OriginCwd,
1292			to:        toProv,
1293			toID:      rm.result.targetID,
1294		}, nil
1295	}
1296	
1297	// colorForAgent returns a stable accent color per provider for the list.
1298	func colorForAgent(id string) color.Color {
1299		switch id {
1300		case "claude":
1301			return lipgloss.Color("170") // purple
1302		case "codex":
1303			return lipgloss.Color("42") // green
1304		case "cursor":
1305			return lipgloss.Color("39") // blue
1306		case "gemini":
1307			return lipgloss.Color("45") // cyan
1308		case "droid":
1309			return lipgloss.Color("214") // orange
1310		case "deepseek":
1311			return lipgloss.Color("203") // red
1312		default:
1313			return lipgloss.Color("250")
1314		}
1315	}
1316	
1317	// ---- all-projects browser (Stage B) ----
1318	
1319	// enterBrowser switches to the all-projects browser, loading the project rollup lazily.
1320	func (m *sessionTUI) enterBrowser() {
1321		if !m.projectsLoaded {
1322			if ps, err := m.store.ListProjects(); err == nil {
1323				m.projects = ps
1324			}
1325			m.projectsLoaded = true
1326		}
1327		m.applyProjectFilter()
1328		m.mode = modeProjects
1329	}
1330	
1331	// gotoHome returns the session list to the current directory's project.
1332	func (m *sessionTUI) gotoHome() {
1333		m.projectID, m.projectName = m.homeProjectID, m.homeProjectName
1334		m.all = m.homeSessions
1335		m.inBrowser = false
1336		m.agentFilter, m.searchQuery = "", ""
1337		m.search.SetValue("")
1338		m.cursor, m.top = 0, 0
1339		m.rebuildAgentCycle()
1340		m.applyFilter()
1341		m.mode = modeList
1342	}
1343	
1344	// drillInto opens a project's session list from the browser.
1345	func (m *sessionTUI) drillInto(p sessionindex.ProjectSummary) {
1346		sessions, err := m.store.ListByProject(p.ProjectID)
1347		if err != nil {
1348			return
1349		}
1350		m.projectID = p.ProjectID
1351		m.projectName = projectDisplayName(p)
1352		m.all = sessions
1353		m.inBrowser = true
1354		m.agentFilter, m.searchQuery = "", ""
1355		m.search.SetValue("")
1356		m.cursor, m.top = 0, 0
1357		m.rebuildAgentCycle()
1358		m.applyFilter()
1359		m.mode = modeList
1360	}
1361	
1362	func (m sessionTUI) updateProjects(msg tea.KeyPressMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
1363		switch msg.String() {
1364		case "q", "ctrl+c":
1365			m.result = sessionTUIResult{cancelled: true}
1366			return m, tea.Quit
1367		case "esc":
1368			if m.startedInBrowser {
1369				m.result = sessionTUIResult{cancelled: true}
1370				return m, tea.Quit
1371			}
1372			m.gotoHome()
1373		case "tab":
1374			if !m.startedInBrowser {
1375				m.gotoHome()
1376			}
1377		case "up", "k":
1378			m.moveProjCursor(-1)
1379		case "down", "j":
1380			m.moveProjCursor(1)
1381		case "pgup":
1382			m.moveProjCursor(-m.projectsHeight())
1383		case "pgdown":
1384			m.moveProjCursor(m.projectsHeight())
1385		case "home", "g":
1386			m.projCursor, m.projTop = 0, 0
1387		case "end", "G":
1388			m.projCursor = len(m.projFiltered) - 1
1389			m.clampProjScroll()
1390		case "enter":
1391			if m.projCursor >= 0 && m.projCursor < len(m.projFiltered) {
1392				m.drillInto(m.projFiltered[m.projCursor])
1393			}
1394		case "/":
1395			// FTS over sessions across ALL projects (consistent with / in a session list).
1396			m.globalActive = true
1397			m.globalSearching = true
1398			m.globalInput.SetValue(m.globalQuery)
1399			return m, m.globalInput.Focus()
1400		case "p":
1401			// Filter the project list by name.
1402			m.projSearching = true
1403			m.projSearch.SetValue(m.projSearchQuery)
1404			return m, m.projSearch.Focus()
1405		}
1406		return m, nil
1407	}
1408	
1409	func (m sessionTUI) updateProjectSearch(msg tea.KeyPressMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
1410		switch msg.String() {
1411		case "esc":
1412			m.projSearching = false
1413			m.projSearch.Blur()
1414			m.projSearchQuery = ""
1415			m.applyProjectFilter()
1416			return m, nil
1417		case "enter":
1418			m.projSearching = false
1419			m.projSearch.Blur()
1420			return m, nil
1421		}
1422		var cmd tea.Cmd
1423		m.projSearch, cmd = m.projSearch.Update(msg)
1424		m.projSearchQuery = m.projSearch.Value()
1425		m.applyProjectFilter()
1426		return m, cmd
1427	}
1428	
1429	func (m *sessionTUI) applyProjectFilter() {
1430		q := strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(m.projSearchQuery))
1431		if q == "" {
1432			m.projFiltered = m.projects
1433		} else {
1434			out := make([]sessionindex.ProjectSummary, 0, len(m.projects))
1435			for _, p := range m.projects {
1436				if strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(projectDisplayName(p)), q) {
1437					out = append(out, p)
1438				}
1439			}
1440			m.projFiltered = out
1441		}
1442		if m.projCursor > len(m.projFiltered)-1 {

1444		}
1445		if m.projCursor < 0 {
1446			m.projCursor = 0
1447		}
1448		m.projTop = 0
1449	}
1450	
1451	func (m *sessionTUI) moveProjCursor(delta int) {
1452		if len(m.projFiltered) == 0 {
1453			return
1454		}
1455		m.projCursor += delta
1456		if m.projCursor < 0 {
1457			m.projCursor = 0
1458		}
1459		if m.projCursor > len(m.projFiltered)-1 {
1460			m.projCursor = len(m.projFiltered) - 1
1461		}
1462		m.clampProjScroll()
1463	}
1464	
1465	func (m *sessionTUI) clampProjScroll() {
1466		h := m.projectsHeight()
1467		if m.projCursor < m.projTop {
1468			m.projTop = m.projCursor
1469		}
1470		if m.projCursor >= m.projTop+h {
1471			m.projTop = m.projCursor - h + 1
1472		}
1473		if m.projTop < 0 {
1474			m.projTop = 0
1475		}
1476	}
1477	
1478	// projectsHeight reserves room for chrome plus a few date-bucket header lines.
1479	func (m sessionTUI) projectsHeight() int {
1480		const chrome = 9
1481		avail := m.height - chrome
1482		if avail < 1 {
1483			avail = 1
1484		}
1485		return avail
1486	}
1487	
1488	func (m sessionTUI) renderProjects() string {
1489		var b strings.Builder
1490	
1491		left := m.headerLeft("all projects")
1492		right := styDim.Render(fmt.Sprintf("%d projects", len(m.projFiltered)))
1493		b.WriteString(headerRow(left, right, m.lineWidth()) + "\n")
1494		b.WriteString(strings.Repeat("─", m.lineWidth()) + "\n")
1495	
1496		if len(m.projFiltered) == 0 {
1497			b.WriteString(styFaint.Render("  No projects match."))
1498		} else {
1499			h := m.projectsHeight()
1500			end := min(m.projTop+h, len(m.projFiltered))
1501			lastBucket := ""
1502			for i := m.projTop; i < end; i++ {
1503				p := m.projFiltered[i]
1504				if bucket := dateBucket(p.LastActivity); bucket != lastBucket {
1505					b.WriteString(styFaint.Render("  ── "+bucket) + "\n")
1506					lastBucket = bucket
1507				}
1508				b.WriteString(m.projectRow(p, i == m.projCursor) + "\n")
1509			}
1510		}
1511	
1512		b.WriteString(strings.Repeat("─", m.lineWidth()) + "\n")
1513		if m.projSearching {
1514			b.WriteString(m.projSearch.View() + "    " + styFaint.Render("esc clear · enter apply"))
1515			return b.String()
1516		}
1517		keys := []string{"↑↓ move", "↵ open", "/ search sessions", "p filter projects"}
1518		if !m.startedInBrowser {
1519			keys = append(keys, "tab this project")
1520		}
1521		keys = append(keys, "q quit")
1522		b.WriteString(styDim.Render(strings.Join(keys, " · ")))
1523		return b.String()
1524	}
1525

1527		cursor := "  "
1528		if selected {
1529			cursor = styCursor.Render("▸ ")
```

```text
1537	// agentCountChips renders "claude 12 · codex 3" with colored agent tags.
1538	func (m sessionTUI) agentCountChips(counts map[string]int) string {
1539		ids := make([]string, 0, len(counts))
1540		for id := range counts {
1541			ids = append(ids, id)
1542		}
1543		sort.Strings(ids)
1544		parts := make([]string, 0, len(ids))
1545		for _, id := range ids {
1546			tag := id
1547			if a, ok := m.agents[id]; ok {
1548				tag = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(a.accent).Render(id)
1549			}
1550			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("%s %d", tag, counts[id]))
1551		}
1552		return strings.Join(parts, styDim.Render(" · "))
1553	}
1554	
1555	func projectDisplayName(p sessionindex.ProjectSummary) string {
1556		if strings.TrimSpace(p.ProjectName) != "" {
1557			return p.ProjectName
1558		}
1559		if p.ProjectID == unknownProjectID || p.ProjectID == "" {
1560			return "(unknown project)"
1561		}
1562		return p.ProjectID
1563	}
1564	
1565	// dateBucket groups a timestamp into a relative bucket for the browser rollup.
1566	func dateBucket(iso string) string {
1567		t, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, iso)
1568		if err != nil {
1569			return "Older"
1570		}
1571		switch d := dayDiff(t.Local()); {
1572		case d <= 0:
1573			return "Today"
1574		case d == 1:
1575			return "Yesterday"
1576		case d < 7:
1577			return "Previous 7 days"
1578		case d < 30:
1579			return "Previous 30 days"
1580		default:
1581			return "Older"
1582		}
1583	}
1584	
1585	// dayDiff returns whole calendar days between now and t (both local), now - t.
1586	func dayDiff(t time.Time) int {
1587		now := time.Now()
1588		ny, nm, nd := now.Date()
1589		ty, tm, td := t.Date()
1590		a := time.Date(ny, nm, nd, 0, 0, 0, 0, now.Location())
1591		c := time.Date(ty, tm, td, 0, 0, 0, 0, now.Location())
1592		return int(a.Sub(c).Hours() / 24)
1593	}
1594	
1595	// ---- global session search (FTS across all projects, from the browser) ----
1596	
1597	func (m sessionTUI) updateGlobalSearch(msg tea.KeyPressMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
1598		switch msg.String() {
1599		case "esc":
1600			// In `search`, the search is the root → esc quits. In `resume` (reached via the
1601			// browser), esc backs out to the browser.
1602			if m.startedInSearch {
1603				m.result = sessionTUIResult{cancelled: true}
1604				return m, tea.Quit
1605			}
1606			m.searchSeq++
1607			m.exitGlobal()
1608			return m, nil
1609		case "tab":
1610			return m, m.toggleGlobalScope()
1611		case "enter":
1612			m.globalSearching = false // commit → browse the results
1613			m.globalInput.Blur()
1614			return m, nil
1615		case "up", "down", "pgup", "pgdown":
1616			// Arrows are useless in the one-line input, so commit and let the same key move into
1617			// the results — reach a hit without a separate enter.
1618			m.globalSearching = false
1619			m.globalInput.Blur()
1620			return m.updateGlobalResults(msg)
1621		}
1622		var cmd tea.Cmd
1623		m.globalInput, cmd = m.globalInput.Update(msg)
1624		m.globalQuery = m.globalInput.Value()
1625		m.searchSeq++
1626		if !queryReady(m.globalQuery) {
1627			// Too short to search the whole corpus: no results until the 2nd character.
1628			m.globalResults = nil
1629			m.globalSnippets = nil
1630			m.snippetSeq++
1631			return m, cmd
1632		}
1633		return m, tea.Batch(cmd, searchDebounce(m.searchSeq, modeProjects))
1634	}
1635	
1636	func (m sessionTUI) updateGlobalResults(msg tea.KeyPressMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
1637		switch msg.String() {
1638		case "q", "ctrl+c":
1639			m.result = sessionTUIResult{cancelled: true}
1640			return m, tea.Quit
1641		case "esc":
1642			// In `search`, the search is the root → esc quits. In `resume` (reached via the
1643			// browser), esc backs out to the browser.
1644			if m.startedInSearch {
1645				m.result = sessionTUIResult{cancelled: true}
1646				return m, tea.Quit
1647			}
1648			m.exitGlobal()
1649		case "tab":
1650			return m, m.toggleGlobalScope()
1651		case "/":
1652			m.globalSearching = true
1653			m.globalInput.SetValue(m.globalQuery)
1654			return m, m.globalInput.Focus()
1655		case "up", "k":
1656			m.moveGlobalCursor(-1)
1657			return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeProjects)
1658		case "down", "j":
1659			m.moveGlobalCursor(1)
1660			return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeProjects)
1661		case "pgup":
1662			m.moveGlobalCursor(-m.globalHeight())
1663			return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeProjects)
1664		case "pgdown":
1665			m.moveGlobalCursor(m.globalHeight())
1666			return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeProjects)
1667		case "home", "g":
1668			m.globalCursor, m.globalTop = 0, 0
1669			return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeProjects)
1670		case "end", "G":
1671			m.globalCursor = len(m.globalResults) - 1
1672			m.clampGlobalScroll()
1673			return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeProjects)
1674		case "r":
1675			// Resume the highlighted hit (enter is a no-op, mirroring the list).
1676			if sel := m.globalSelected(); sel != nil {
1677				return m.beginResume(sel)
1678			}
1679		case " ", "space":
1680			if sel := m.globalSelected(); sel != nil {
1681				return m.openPreview(sel)
1682			}
1683		case "a":
1684			return m, m.cycleAgent()
1685		case "v":
1686			m.toggleViewMode()
1687			m.clampGlobalScroll() // toggleViewMode clamps the list; the global list scrolls separately
1688			return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeProjects)
1689		}
1690		return m, nil
1691	}
1692	
1693	// globalSelected returns the highlighted cross-project hit, or nil.
1694	func (m sessionTUI) globalSelected() *sessionindex.Session {
1695		if m.globalCursor < 0 || m.globalCursor >= len(m.globalResults) {
1696			return nil
1697		}
1698		return &m.globalResults[m.globalCursor]
1699	}
1700	
1701	// toggleGlobalScope flips the cross-project search between all projects and a single project
1702	// (tab): the highlighted hit's project when a result is selected, else the current directory's
1703	// project (nothing to point at). A second tab widens back to all projects. It then re-runs the
1704	// query from the top against the new scope.
1705	func (m *sessionTUI) toggleGlobalScope() tea.Cmd {
1706		switch {
1707		case m.globalScopeID != "":
1708			// Already scoped → widen back to all projects.
1709			m.globalScopeID, m.globalScopeName = "", ""
1710		case m.globalSelected() != nil:
1711			// A result is highlighted → scope to its project.
1712			sel := m.globalSelected()
1713			m.globalScopeID, m.globalScopeName = sel.ProjectID, sessionProject(*sel)
1714		default:
1715			// No results to point at → scope to the current directory's project.
1716			m.globalScopeID, m.globalScopeName = m.homeProjectID, m.homeProjectName
1717		}
1718		m.globalCursor, m.globalTop = 0, 0
1719		m.searchSeq++
1720		if !queryReady(m.globalQuery) {
1721			m.globalResults = nil
1722			m.globalSnippets = nil
1723			m.snippetSeq++
1724			return nil
1725		}
1726		return searchDebounce(m.searchSeq, modeProjects)
1727	}
1728	
1729	func (m *sessionTUI) exitGlobal() {
1730		m.globalActive = false
1731		m.globalSearching = false
1732		m.globalInput.Blur()
1733		m.globalInput.SetValue("")
1734		m.globalQuery = ""
1735		m.globalScopeID, m.globalScopeName = "", "" // re-enter search at all-projects scope
1736		m.globalResults = nil
1737		m.globalSnippets = nil
1738		m.snippetSeq++
1739		m.globalCursor, m.globalTop = 0, 0
1740	}
1741	
1742	func (m *sessionTUI) moveGlobalCursor(delta int) {
1743		if len(m.globalResults) == 0 {
1744			return
1745		}
1746		m.globalCursor += delta
1747		if m.globalCursor < 0 {
1748			m.globalCursor = 0
1749		}
1750		if m.globalCursor > len(m.globalResults)-1 {
1751			m.globalCursor = len(m.globalResults) - 1
1752		}
1753		m.clampGlobalScroll()
1754	}
1755	
1756	func (m *sessionTUI) clampGlobalScroll() {
1757		h := m.globalHeight()
1758		if m.globalCursor < m.globalTop {
1759			m.globalTop = m.globalCursor
1760		}
1761		if m.globalCursor >= m.globalTop+h {
1762			m.globalTop = m.globalCursor - h + 1
1763		}
1764		if m.globalTop < 0 {
1765			m.globalTop = 0
1766		}
1767	}
1768	
1769	func (m sessionTUI) globalHeight() int {
1770		const chrome = 6
1771		avail := m.height - chrome
1772		if avail < 1 {
1773			avail = 1
1774		}
1775		n := avail / m.rowsPerSession()
1776		if n < 1 {
1777			n = 1
1778		}
1779		return n
1780	}
1781	
1782	func (m sessionTUI) renderGlobalResults() string {
1783		var b strings.Builder
1784	
1785		scope := "all projects"
1786		if m.globalScopeID != "" {
1787			scope = styDim.Render("project: ") + stySel.Render(m.globalScopeName)
1788		}
1789		left := m.headerLeft(scope) + styDim.Render("  ·  ") + m.agentScope()
1790		if q := strings.TrimSpace(m.globalQuery); q != "" && !m.globalSearching {
1791			left += styDim.Render(" · ") + stySel.Render(q)
1792		}
1793		right := styDim.Render(fmt.Sprintf("%d matches", len(m.globalResults)))
1794		b.WriteString(headerRow(left, right, m.lineWidth()) + "\n")
1795		b.WriteString(strings.Repeat("─", m.lineWidth()) + "\n")
1796	
1797		scopeWord := "all projects"
1798		if m.globalScopeID != "" {
1799			scopeWord = "this project"
1800		}
1801		switch {
1802		case strings.TrimSpace(m.globalQuery) == "":
1803			b.WriteString(styFaint.Render("  Type to search sessions across " + scopeWord + "."))
1804		case len(m.globalResults) == 0:
1805			b.WriteString(styFaint.Render("  No matches."))
1806		default:
1807			h := m.globalHeight()
1808			end := min(m.globalTop+h, len(m.globalResults))
1809			for i := m.globalTop; i < end; i++ {
1810				s := m.globalResults[i]
1811				snip := m.globalSnippets[sessionindex.FingerprintKey(s.Agent, s.SessionID)]
1812				b.WriteString(m.globalRow(s, i == m.globalCursor, snip))
1813				if i < end-1 {
1814					b.WriteString("\n")
1815				}
1816			}
1817		}
1818	
1819		b.WriteString("\n" + strings.Repeat("─", m.lineWidth()) + "\n")
1820		// esc quits when search is the root (`specstory search`); otherwise it backs out to the
1821		// browser it was opened from (`specstory resume`).
1822		escHint := "esc back"
1823		if m.startedInSearch {
1824			escHint = "esc quit"
1825		}
1826		scopeKey := "tab this project"
1827		if m.globalScopeID != "" {
1828			scopeKey = "tab all projects"
1829		}
1830		if m.globalSearching {
1831			// While typing: esc quits (search root) or cancels back to the browser (resume). Only
1832			// offer "enter browse results" once there's actually something to browse.
1833			inputHint := "esc quit"
1834			if !m.startedInSearch {
1835				inputHint = "esc cancel"
1836			}
1837			inputHint += " · " + scopeKey
1838			if len(m.globalResults) > 0 {
1839				inputHint += " · enter browse results"
1840			}
1841			b.WriteString(m.globalInput.View() + "    " + styFaint.Render(inputHint))
1842			return b.String()
1843		}
1844		keys := []string{"↑↓ move", "r resume", "space preview", "a agent", "v " + m.viewMode, "/ edit search", scopeKey, escHint, "q quit"}
1845		b.WriteString(styDim.Render(strings.Join(keys, " · ")))
1846		return b.String()
1847	}
1848	
1849	// globalRow renders a cross-project search hit: agent · time · project · highlighted match
1850	// snippet. Honors the dense/sparse view mode, matching the project session list.
1851	func (m sessionTUI) globalRow(s sessionindex.Session, selected bool, snippet string) string {
1852		cursor := "  "
1853		if selected {
1854			cursor = styCursor.Render("▸ ")
1855		}
1856		agent := m.agentTag(s.Agent)
1857		proj := fmt.Sprintf("%-18s", truncate(sessionProject(s), 18))
1858	
1859		if m.viewMode == "sparse" {
1860			label := renderName(sessionTitle(s), selected, m.lineWidth()-30)
1861			if snippet != "" {
1862				label = renderSnippet(snippet, m.lineWidth()-32)
1863			}
1864			head := cursor + agent + "  " + styFaint.Render(proj) + "  " + label
1865			sub := "    " + styFaint.Render(fmt.Sprintf("%s ago · %s", relativeTime(s.UpdatedAt), shortID(s.SessionID)))
1866			return head + "\n" + sub
1867		}
1868	
1869		when := fmt.Sprintf("%-4s", relativeTime(s.UpdatedAt))
1870		label := renderName(sessionTitle(s), selected, m.lineWidth()-46)
1871		if snippet != "" {
1872			label = renderSnippet(snippet, m.lineWidth()-48)
1873		}
1874		return cursor + agent + " " + styDim.Render(when) + "  " + styFaint.Render(proj) + "  " + label
1875	}
1876	
1877	func sessionProject(s sessionindex.Session) string {
1878		if strings.TrimSpace(s.ProjectName) != "" {
1879			return s.ProjectName
1880		}
1881		if s.ProjectID == unknownProjectID || s.ProjectID == "" {
1882			return "(unknown)"
1883		}
1884		return s.ProjectID
1885	}
1886	
1887	// ---- async, debounced full-text search ----
1888	
1889	// searchDebounceMsg fires after a brief typing pause; the query runs only if its seq is
1890	// still current. searchResultMsg carries the async FTS results back to the model. Both
1891	// keep typing instant: the input updates synchronously while the query runs off-thread.
1892	type searchDebounceMsg struct {
1893		seq  int
1894		kind tuiMode // modeList (project-scoped) or modeProjects (global)
1895	}
1896	
1897	type searchResultMsg struct {
1898		seq      int
1899		kind     tuiMode
1900		sessions []sessionindex.Session
1901	}
1902	
1903	type snippetResultMsg struct {
1904		seq      int
1905		kind     tuiMode
1906		snippets map[string]string
1907	}
1908	
1909	const searchDebounceDelay = 50 * time.Millisecond
1910	
1911	// searchDebounce schedules a debounce tick. On fire, the model checks the seq and decides
1912	// whether to actually run the query (so only the last keystroke in a burst queries).
1913	func searchDebounce(seq int, kind tuiMode) tea.Cmd {
1914		return tea.Tick(searchDebounceDelay, func(time.Time) tea.Msg {
1915			return searchDebounceMsg{seq: seq, kind: kind}
1916		})
1917	}
1918	
1919	// runSearch returns a command that performs the FTS query off the UI thread.
1920	func (m sessionTUI) runSearch(seq int, kind tuiMode, ctx context.Context) tea.Cmd {
1921		store := m.store
1922		query, projectID := m.searchQuery, m.projectID
1923		if kind == modeProjects {
1924			query, projectID = m.globalQuery, m.globalScopeID // "" = all projects, else scoped
1925		}
1926		fq := ftsQuery(query)
1927		return func() tea.Msg {
1928			if !queryReady(query) {
1929				return searchResultMsg{seq: seq, kind: kind}
1930			}
1931			sessions, _ := store.SearchContext(ctx, fq, projectID)
1932			return searchResultMsg{seq: seq, kind: kind, sessions: sessions}
1933		}
1934	}
1935	
1936	// applySearchResults installs async FTS results into the matching list (scoped or global).
1937	func (m *sessionTUI) applySearchResults(kind tuiMode, sessions []sessionindex.Session) {
1938		if kind == modeProjects {
1939			m.globalSnippets = map[string]string{}
1940			var out []sessionindex.Session
1941			for _, s := range sessions {
1942				if m.agentFilter == "" || s.Agent == m.agentFilter {
1943					out = append(out, s)
1944				}
1945			}
1946			m.globalResults = out
1947			if m.globalCursor > len(m.globalResults)-1 {
1948				m.globalCursor = len(m.globalResults) - 1
1949			}
1950			if m.globalCursor < 0 {
1951				m.globalCursor = 0
1952			}
1953			m.globalTop = 0
1954			return
1955		}
1956	
1957		var out []sessionindex.Session
1958		for _, s := range sessions {
1959			if m.agentFilter == "" || s.Agent == m.agentFilter {
1960				out = append(out, s)
1961			}
1962		}
1963		m.filtered = out
1964		m.filteredSnippets = map[string]string{}
1965		if m.cursor > len(m.filtered)-1 {
1966			m.cursor = len(m.filtered) - 1
1967		}
1968		if m.cursor < 0 {
1969			m.cursor = 0
1970		}
1971		m.top = 0
1972		m.clampScroll()
1973	}
1974	
1975	func (m *sessionTUI) requestVisibleSnippets(kind tuiMode) tea.Cmd {
1976		var query string
1977		var rows []sessionindex.Session
1978		if kind == modeProjects {
1979			if !queryReady(m.globalQuery) || len(m.globalResults) == 0 {
1980				return nil
1981			}
1982			h := m.globalHeight()
1983			end := min(m.globalTop+h, len(m.globalResults))
1984			if m.globalTop >= end {
1985				return nil
1986			}
1987			query = m.globalQuery
1988			rows = append([]sessionindex.Session(nil), m.globalResults[m.globalTop:end]...)
1989		} else {
1990			if !queryReady(m.searchQuery) || len(m.filtered) == 0 {
1991				return nil
1992			}
1993			h := m.listHeight()
1994			end := min(m.top+h, len(m.filtered))
1995			if m.top >= end {
1996				return nil
1997			}
1998			query = m.searchQuery
1999			rows = append([]sessionindex.Session(nil), m.filtered[m.top:end]...)
2000		}
2001	
2002		store := m.store
2003		fq := ftsQuery(query)
2004		m.snippetSeq++
2005		seq := m.snippetSeq
2006		return func() tea.Msg {
2007			snips, _ := store.SnippetsContext(context.Background(), fq, rows)
2008			return snippetResultMsg{seq: seq, kind: kind, snippets: snips}
2009		}
2010	}
```

```text
180				if !log.IsSilent() && !jsonOutput {
181					fmt.Println()
182					agentWord := "agents"
183					if len(providerNames) == 1 {
184						agentWord = "agent"
185					}
186					fmt.Println("👀 Watching for activity from " + agentWord + ": " + strings.Join(providerNames, ", "))
187					fmt.Println("   Press Ctrl+C to stop watching")
188					fmt.Println()
189				}
190	
191				// Keep sessions.db current in real time alongside the markdown writes (nil/no-op
192				// if the index can't be opened — never block the watcher on it).
193				liveIndex := NewLiveIndexer(cwd)
194				defer liveIndex.Close()
195	
196				// Track sessions we've seen to suppress initial-scan output.
197				// Existing sessions found at startup get their markdown refreshed
198				// but don't produce output — only new activity does.
199				var seenMu gosync.Mutex
200				seenSessions := make(map[string]bool)
201	
202				// Session callback for watch mode output
203				sessionCallback := func(providerID string, sess *spi.AgentChatSession) {
204					// Check if markdown file already exists to determine if this is an update or creation
205					fileFullPath := session.BuildSessionFilePath(sess, config.GetHistoryDir(), useUTC)
206					_, fileExistsErr := os.Stat(fileFullPath)
207					fileExists := fileExistsErr == nil
208	
209					// Process the session (write markdown and sync to cloud)
210					// Don't show output during watch mode
211					// This is autosave mode (true)
212					markdownSize, err := session.ProcessSingleSession(ctx, sess, config, session.ProcessingOptions{
213						OnlyCloudSync:      onlyCloudSync,
214						IsAutosave:         true,
215						DebugRaw:           debugRaw,
216						UseUTC:             useUTC,
217						NoTelemetryPrompts: noTelemetryPrompts,
218					})
219					if err != nil {
220						// Log error but continue - don't fail the whole watch
221						// In watch mode, we prioritize keeping the watcher running.
222						// Failed markdown writes or cloud syncs can be retried later via
223						// the sync command, so we just log and continue.
224						slog.Error("Failed to process session update",
225							"sessionId", sess.SessionID,
226							"provider", providerID,
227							"error", err)
228						return
229					}
230	
231					// Mirror the markdown write into the restore index in real time.
232					liveIndex.Record(providerID, sess)
233	
234					// Suppress output for existing sessions seen for the first time (initial scan).
235					// New sessions (!fileExists) always get output since they represent real activity.
236					seenMu.Lock()
237					firstSeen := !seenSessions[sess.SessionID]
238					seenSessions[sess.SessionID] = true
239					seenMu.Unlock()
240					if firstSeen && fileExists {
241						return
242					}
243	
244					// Output formatted line to stdout for watch mode
245					if !log.IsSilent() {
246						// Determine if this was an update or creation
247						action := "updated"
248						if !fileExists {
249							action = "created"
250						}
251	
252						// Get timestamps from session data
253						startTime := sess.CreatedAt
254						endTime := startTime
255						if sess.SessionData != nil && sess.SessionData.UpdatedAt != "" {
256							endTime = sess.SessionData.UpdatedAt
257						}
258	
259						// Count messages by role
260						userPrompts := 0
261						agentActivity := 0
262						if sess.SessionData != nil {
263							for _, exchange := range sess.SessionData.Exchanges {
264								for _, msg := range exchange.Messages {
265									if msg.Role == schema.RoleUser {
266										userPrompts++
267									} else {
268										agentActivity++
269									}
270								}
271							}
272						}
273	
274						// Output the formatted line
275						if jsonOutput {
276							record := map[string]interface{}{
277								"timestamp":          time.Now().Format(time.RFC3339),
278								"action":             action,
279								"session_id":         sess.SessionID,
```

```text
150	@@ -9,6 +9,51 @@ import (
151	 	"strings"
152	 )
153	 
154	+// projectDirNameRegex matches any character that is not alphanumeric or a dash.
155	+// Claude Code replaces each such character with a dash to map a working directory
156	+// to a project folder name under ~/.claude/projects.
157	+var projectDirNameRegex = regexp.MustCompile(`[^a-zA-Z0-9-]`)
158	+
159	+// encodeProjectDirName converts a real (symlink-resolved) path into Claude Code's
160	+// project directory name: non-alphanumeric/dash characters become dashes, with a
161	+// guaranteed leading dash. Example: "/Users/sean/app" -> "-Users-sean-app".
162	+func encodeProjectDirName(realPath string) string {
163	+	name := projectDirNameRegex.ReplaceAllString(realPath, "-")
164	+	if !strings.HasPrefix(name, "-") {
165	+		name = "-" + name
166	+	}
167	+	return name
168	+}
169	+
170	+// resolveClaudeProjectDir returns ~/.claude/projects/<encoded> for the given
171	+// project path, resolving symlinks, WITHOUT requiring the directory to exist.
172	+// Used when writing a reconstructed session into the store (the caller creates
173	+// the directory). Distinct from GetClaudeCodeProjectDir, which requires the
174	+// projects directory to already exist.
175	+func resolveClaudeProjectDir(projectPath string) (string, error) {
176	+	homeDir, err := os.UserHomeDir()
177	+	if err != nil {
178	+		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get user home directory: %v", err)
179	+	}
180	+
181	+	cwd := projectPath
182	+	if cwd == "" {
183	+		cwd, err = os.Getwd()
184	+		if err != nil {
185	+			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get current working directory: %v", err)
186	+		}
187	+	}
188	+
189	+	// Resolve symlinks to match Claude Code's behavior; fall back to the raw path
190	+	// if resolution fails (e.g., a path component does not exist yet).
191	+	realPath, err := filepath.EvalSymlinks(cwd)
192	+	if err != nil {
193	+		realPath = cwd
194	+	}
195	+
196	+	return filepath.Join(homeDir, ".claude", "projects", encodeProjectDirName(realPath)), nil
197	+}
198	+

238	 // isSyntheticMessage checks if a message is synthetic/internal and should be skipped
239	-// when looking for the first real user message.
240	-// Currently filters warmup messages and Claude Code session title generation prompts.
241	+// when looking for the first real user message (e.g. for the session title/slug).
242	+// Filters warmup prompts, title-generation prompts, and slash-command noise (the
243	+// command invocation, its local stdout/stderr, and the local-command caveat) — these
244	+// are conversation scaffolding, not the user's actual prompt. Without this, a session
245	+// that opens with a slash command gets an empty title and falls back to its UUID.
246	 func isSyntheticMessage(content string) bool {
247	+	// warmup and the <TEXTBLOCK> title-generation wrapper can be embedded anywhere
248	+	// in the record, so match them with Contains.
249	 	if strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(content), "warmup") {
250	 		return true
251	 	}
252	-	// Claude Code wraps the real user prompt in <TEXTBLOCK> tags for title generation
253	 	if strings.Contains(content, "<TEXTBLOCK>") {
254	 		return true
255	 	}
256	-	return false
257	+	// The slash-command scaffolding tags (shared with reconstruction via
258	+	// spi.SyntheticCommandTags) and the interrupt marker REPLACE the prompt, so they
259	+	// appear at the very START of the record. Match by prefix so a real prompt that
260	+	// merely mentions a tag mid-sentence is still treated as real. The interrupt
261	+	// marker's "…for tool use" variant is caught by the same prefix.
262	+	trimmed := strings.TrimSpace(content)
263	+	for _, marker := range spi.SyntheticCommandTags {
264	+		if strings.HasPrefix(trimmed, marker) {
265	+			return true
266	+		}
267	+	}
268	+	return strings.HasPrefix(trimmed, "[Request interrupted by user")
269	 }

396	+// extractCommandName pulls the slash command from a Claude Code command record, e.g.
397	+// "<command-name>/code-review</command-name>…" -> "/code-review". Falls back to the
398	+// bare command-message name (prefixed with "/"). Returns "" when none is present.
399	+func extractCommandName(content string) string {
400	+	if c := between(content, "<command-name>", "</command-name>"); c != "" {
401	+		return c
402	+	}
403	+	if c := between(content, "<command-message>", "</command-message>"); c != "" {

411	+// between returns the text between the first open/close markers, trimmed, or "".
412	+func between(s, openTag, closeTag string) string {
413	+	_, after, ok := strings.Cut(s, openTag)
414	+	if !ok {
415	+		return ""
416	+	}
417	+	inner, _, ok := strings.Cut(after, closeTag)
418	+	if !ok {
419	+		return ""
420	+	}
421	+	return strings.TrimSpace(inner)
422	+}

435	+	}
```
