443	+		CreatedAt: scan.timestamp,
444	+		Slug:      spi.GenerateFilenameFromUserMessage(scan.firstUserMessage),
445	+		Name:      spi.GenerateReadableName(scan.firstUserMessage),
446	 	}, nil
447	 }
448	+
449	+// ListAllAgentChatSessions enumerates every Claude Code session across all projects. It is the
450	+// no-progress form of ListAllAgentChatSessionsProgress.
451	+func (p *Provider) ListAllAgentChatSessions() ([]spi.GlobalSessionRef, error) {
452	+	return p.ListAllAgentChatSessionsProgress(nil)
453	+}
454	+
455	+// ListAllAgentChatSessionsProgress enumerates every Claude Code session across all projects by
456	+// walking ~/.claude/projects/*/ for *.jsonl (the originating cwd comes from inside each session;
457	+// the project directory name is a lossy, irreversible encoding of the path), reporting scan
458	+// progress into r (nil-safe). Headers are scanned in parallel across CPUs; output order is
459	+// irrelevant (reindex dedups and sorts later). Implements spi.ProgressEnumerator. See
460	+// docs/SESSIONS-DB.md.
461	+func (p *Provider) ListAllAgentChatSessionsProgress(r *spi.ScanReporter) ([]spi.GlobalSessionRef, error) {
462	+	projectsDir, err := GetClaudeCodeProjectsDir()
463	+	if err != nil {
464	+		// No projects directory yet → nothing to enumerate (not an error).
465	+		return []spi.GlobalSessionRef{}, nil
466	+	}
467	+
468	+	// Phase 1: collect file paths (dirents only — no opens).
469	+	var paths []string
470	+	walkErr := filepath.WalkDir(projectsDir, func(path string, d os.DirEntry, err error) error {
471	+		if err != nil {
472	+			return nil // skip unreadable entries rather than abort the whole sweep
473	+		}
474	+		if d.IsDir() || !strings.HasSuffix(path, ".jsonl") {
475	+			return nil
476	+		}
477	+		paths = append(paths, path)
478	+		return nil
479	+	})
480	+	if walkErr != nil {
481	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to walk claude projects: %w", walkErr)
482	+	}
483	+
484	+	// Phase 2: scan session headers in parallel — each file's read+parse is independent and
485	+	// CPU-bound, so this scales across cores.
486	+	workers := min(runtime.NumCPU(), 12)
487	+	if workers < 1 {
488	+		workers = 1
489	+	}
490	+	pathCh := make(chan string, workers)
491	+	var refs []spi.GlobalSessionRef
492	+	var mu sync.Mutex
493	+	var wg sync.WaitGroup
494	+	for i := 0; i < workers; i++ {
495	+		wg.Add(1)
496	+		go func() {
497	+			defer wg.Done()
498	+			for path := range pathCh {
499	+				scan, scanErr := scanClaudeSession(path)
500	+				if scanErr != nil {
501	+					slog.Warn("reindex: failed to scan claude session", "file", path, "error", scanErr)
502	+					continue
503	+				}
504	+				if !scan.foundRealMessage {
505	+					continue // warmup-only or sidechain-only transcript (not a session)
506	+				}
507	+				ref := spi.GlobalSessionRef{
508	+					SessionID:  scan.sessionID,
509	+					CreatedAt:  scan.timestamp,
510	+					Slug:       spi.GenerateFilenameFromUserMessage(scan.firstUserMessage),
511	+					Name:       spi.GenerateReadableName(scan.firstUserMessage),
512	+					NativePath: path,
513	+					OriginCwd:  scan.cwd,
514	+				}
515	+				mu.Lock()
516	+				refs = append(refs, ref)
517	+				mu.Unlock()
518	+				r.Add(1) // count sessions found, not files scanned (warmup/sidechain files yield none)
519	+			}
520	+		}()
521	+	}
522	+	for _, path := range paths {
523	+		pathCh <- path
524	+	}
525	+	close(pathCh)
526	+	wg.Wait()
527	+	return refs, nil
528	+}

538	+func TestIsSyntheticMessage_SlashCommandNoise(t *testing.T) {
539	+	synthetic := []string{
540	+		"warmup",
541	+		"<TEXTBLOCK>real prompt</TEXTBLOCK>",
542	+		"<command-name>/code-review</command-name>",
543	+		"  <local-command-stdout>Cancelled</local-command-stdout>",
544	+		"<local-command-caveat>Caveat: …</local-command-caveat>",
545	+		"<command-message>today</command-message>",
546	+		"[Request interrupted by user]",
547	+		"[Request interrupted by user for tool use]",
548	+	}
549	+	for _, c := range synthetic {
550	+		if !isSyntheticMessage(c) {
551	+			t.Errorf("expected synthetic: %q", c)
552	+		}
553	+	}
554	+	real := []string{
555	+		"read @docs/SESSION-PORTABILITY.md task to follow",
556	+		"fix the bug in the parser",
557	+		"I have a <command-name> mentioned mid-sentence", // not a prefix → real
558	+	}
559	+	for _, c := range real {
560	+		if isSyntheticMessage(c) {
561	+			t.Errorf("expected real: %q", c)
562	+		}
563	+	}
564	+}
565	+
566	+func TestExtractCommandName(t *testing.T) {
567	+	cases := []struct{ in, want string }{
568	+		{"<command-name>/code-review</command-name>\n<command-message>code-review</command-message>", "/code-review"},
569	+		{"<command-message>today</command-message>", "/today"},
570	+		{"no command here", ""},
571	+	}
572	+	for _, c := range cases {
573	+		if got := extractCommandName(c.in); got != c.want {
574	+			t.Errorf("extractCommandName(%q) = %q, want %q", c.in, got, c.want)
575	+		}
576	+	}
577	+}
578	diff --git a/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/claudecode/reconstruct.go b/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/claudecode/reconstruct.go
579	new file mode 100644
580	index 0000000..a57010d
581	--- /dev/null
582	+++ b/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/claudecode/reconstruct.go
583	@@ -0,0 +1,127 @@
584	+package claudecode
585	+
586	+import (
587	+	"bytes"
588	+	"encoding/json"
589	+	"fmt"
590	+	"path/filepath"
591	+	"time"
592	+
593	+	"github.com/google/uuid"
594	+
595	+	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
596	+	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
597	+)
598	+
599	+const (
600	+	// reconstructedClaudeModel is a placeholder model label for reconstructed
601	+	// assistant records. It is only a historical display value — on resume Claude
602	+	// Code uses its own configured model for the next turn.
603	+	reconstructedClaudeModel = "claude-opus-4-8"
604	+
605	+	// reconstructedClaudeVersion mirrors the `version` field Claude Code writes on
606	+	// its records. A concrete value matching observed sessions maximizes the
607	+	// chance the loader accepts a synthesized file.
608	+	reconstructedClaudeVersion = "2.1.178"
609	+)
610	+
611	+// ReconstructSession rebuilds a Claude Code native JSONL session from the neutral
612	+// SessionData so `claude --resume <id>` can continue the conversation.
613	+//
614	+// The forward parser collapses Claude's parentUuid DAG and drops the API
615	+// message.id and thinking signatures, so reconstruction synthesizes a fresh,
616	+// linear parentUuid chain of plain user/assistant text records (tool calls and
617	+// thinking are already flattened into agent text by FlattenSessionData). This is
618	+// the structure validated by the Stage 1 spike. See docs/SESSION-PORTABILITY.md.
619	+func (p *Provider) ReconstructSession(data *schema.SessionData, opts spi.ReconstructOptions) (*spi.ReconstructedSession, error) {
620	+	if data == nil {
621	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("cannot reconstruct nil session data")
622	+	}
623	+
624	+	// Prefer the explicit target workspace; fall back to the session's own root.
625	+	cwd := opts.WorkspaceRoot
626	+	if cwd == "" {
627	+		cwd = data.WorkspaceRoot
628	+	}
629	+
630	+	turns := spi.FlattenSessionData(data, opts.MigrationNote)
631	+	if len(turns) == 0 {
632	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("session has no content to reconstruct")
633	+	}
634	+
635	+	newID := uuid.NewString()
636	+	base := time.Now().UTC()
637	+
638	+	var buf bytes.Buffer
639	+	enc := json.NewEncoder(&buf)
640	+	// Preserve <, >, & in conversation/markdown text rather than \u-escaping them.
641	+	enc.SetEscapeHTML(false)
642	+
643	+	var prevUUID string
644	+	for i, turn := range turns {
645	+		recUUID := uuid.NewString()
646	+		ts := base.Add(time.Duration(i) * time.Second).Format("2006-01-02T15:04:05.000Z")
647	+
648	+		rec := map[string]interface{}{
649	+			"isSidechain": false,
650	+			"userType":    "external",
651	+			"cwd":         cwd,
652	+			"sessionId":   newID,
653	+			"version":     reconstructedClaudeVersion,
654	+			"gitBranch":   "",
655	+			"uuid":        recUUID,
656	+			"timestamp":   ts,
657	+		}
658	+
659	+		// Linear chain: the first record roots the DAG (parentUuid null) and
660	+		// carries provenance back to the source session.
661	+		if prevUUID == "" {
662	+			rec["parentUuid"] = nil
663	+			rec["specstorySourceSessionId"] = data.SessionID
664	+		} else {
665	+			rec["parentUuid"] = prevUUID
666	+		}
667	+
668	+		if turn.Role == schema.RoleUser {
669	+			rec["type"] = "user"
670	+			rec["message"] = map[string]interface{}{
671	+				"role":    "user",
672	+				"content": turn.Text,
673	+			}
674	+		} else {
675	+			rec["type"] = "assistant"
676	+			rec["message"] = map[string]interface{}{
677	+				"id":          "msg_" + recUUID,
678	+				"type":        "message",
679	+				"role":        "assistant",
680	+				"model":       reconstructedClaudeModel,
681	+				"content":     []map[string]interface{}{{"type": "text", "text": turn.Text}},
682	+				"stop_reason": "end_turn",
683	+				"usage":       map[string]interface{}{"input_tokens": 1, "output_tokens": 1},
684	+			}
685	+		}
686	+
687	+		if err := enc.Encode(rec); err != nil {
688	+			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to encode reconstructed record: %w", err)
689	+		}
690	+
691	+		prevUUID = recUUID
692	+	}
693	+
694	+	return &spi.ReconstructedSession{
695	+		SessionID: newID,
696	+		Filename:  newID + ".jsonl",
697	+		Content:   buf.Bytes(),
698	+	}, nil
699	+}
700	+
701	+// NativeSessionPath returns where a reconstructed session file belongs in Claude
702	+// Code's store: ~/.claude/projects/<cwd-encoded>/<filename>. The directory is not
703	+// required to exist (the caller creates it).
704	+func (p *Provider) NativeSessionPath(projectPath string, filename string) (string, error) {
705	+	dir, err := resolveClaudeProjectDir(projectPath)
706	+	if err != nil {
707	+		return "", err
708	+	}
709	+	return filepath.Join(dir, filename), nil
710	+}

712	new file mode 100644
713	index 0000000..cdd1e66
714	--- /dev/null
715	+++ b/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/claudecode/reconstruct_test.go
716	@@ -0,0 +1,154 @@
717	+package claudecode
718	+
719	+import (
720	+	"bufio"
721	+	"bytes"
722	+	"encoding/json"
723	+	"path/filepath"
724	+	"strings"
725	+	"testing"
726	+
727	+	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
728	+	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
729	+)
730	+
731	+func strptr(s string) *string { return &s }
732	+
733	+// reconstructSampleData returns a SessionData exercising user text, agent text,
734	+// thinking, and a tool call (all of which flatten to user/agent text turns).
735	+func reconstructSampleData() *schema.SessionData {
736	+	fm := "Wrote `hello.c`:\n\n```c\n#include <stdio.h>\nint main(void){ printf(\"Hello, World!\\n\"); }\n```"
737	+	return &schema.SessionData{
738	+		SchemaVersion: "1.0",
739	+		Provider:      schema.ProviderInfo{ID: "claude-code", Name: "Claude Code", Version: "x"},
740	+		SessionID:     "orig-claude-123",
741	+		CreatedAt:     "2026-06-17T10:00:00.000Z",
742	+		WorkspaceRoot: "/tmp/proj",
743	+		Exchanges: []schema.Exchange{
744	+			{
745	+				ExchangeID: "orig-claude-123:0",
746	+				Messages: []schema.Message{
747	+					{Role: schema.RoleUser, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "Create a Hello World in C."}}},
748	+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Model: "claude-x", Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "I'll create it."}}},
749	+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeThinking, Text: "Need a main function."}}},
750	+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Tool: &schema.ToolInfo{Name: "Write", Type: schema.ToolTypeWrite, FormattedMarkdown: strptr(fm)}},
751	+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "Done."}}},
752	+				},
753	+			},
754	+		},
755	+	}
756	+}
757	+
758	+func parseClaudeJSONL(t *testing.T, content []byte) []JSONLRecord {
759	+	t.Helper()
760	+	var records []JSONLRecord
761	+	scanner := bufio.NewScanner(bytes.NewReader(content))
762	+	scanner.Buffer(make([]byte, 0, 64*1024), 16*1024*1024)
763	+	for scanner.Scan() {
764	+		line := strings.TrimSpace(scanner.Text())
765	+		if line == "" {
766	+			continue
767	+		}
768	+		var m map[string]interface{}
769	+		if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &m); err != nil {
770	+			t.Fatalf("invalid JSONL line %q: %v", line, err)
771	+		}
772	+		records = append(records, JSONLRecord{Data: m})
773	+	}
774	+	if err := scanner.Err(); err != nil {
775	+		t.Fatalf("scanning content: %v", err)
776	+	}
777	+	return records
778	+}
779	+
780	+// TestReconstructSession_RoundTrip reconstructs a session to native Claude JSONL,
781	+// re-parses it through the forward path, and asserts the flattened transcript is
782	+// preserved.
783	+func TestReconstructSession_RoundTrip(t *testing.T) {
784	+	data := reconstructSampleData()
785	+	expected := spi.FlattenSessionData(data, "")
786	+
787	+	out, err := NewProvider().ReconstructSession(data, spi.ReconstructOptions{WorkspaceRoot: "/tmp/proj"})
788	+	if err != nil {
789	+		t.Fatalf("ReconstructSession: %v", err)
790	+	}
791	+	if out.SessionID == "" {
792	+		t.Fatal("expected a fresh session ID")
793	+	}
794	+	if !strings.HasSuffix(out.Filename, ".jsonl") {
795	+		t.Errorf("filename %q should end in .jsonl", out.Filename)
796	+	}
797	+
798	+	records := parseClaudeJSONL(t, out.Content)
799	+	regenerated, err := GenerateAgentSession(Session{SessionUuid: out.SessionID, Records: records}, "/tmp/proj")
800	+	if err != nil {
801	+		t.Fatalf("GenerateAgentSession: %v", err)
802	+	}
803	+
804	+	actual := spi.FlattenSessionData(regenerated, "")
805	+	if len(actual) != len(expected) {
806	+		t.Fatalf("round-trip produced %d turns, want %d\n got: %+v\nwant: %+v", len(actual), len(expected), actual, expected)
807	+	}
808	+	for i := range expected {
809	+		if actual[i] != expected[i] {
810	+			t.Errorf("turn %d mismatch:\n got: %+v\nwant: %+v", i, actual[i], expected[i])
811	+		}
812	+	}
813	+}
814	+
815	+// TestReconstructSession_Chain verifies the records form a valid linear parentUuid
816	+// chain rooted at null, all sharing the fresh session ID.
817	+func TestReconstructSession_Chain(t *testing.T) {
818	+	out, err := NewProvider().ReconstructSession(reconstructSampleData(), spi.ReconstructOptions{WorkspaceRoot: "/tmp/proj"})
819	+	if err != nil {
820	+		t.Fatalf("ReconstructSession: %v", err)
821	+	}
822	+
823	+	records := parseClaudeJSONL(t, out.Content)
824	+	if len(records) == 0 {
825	+		t.Fatal("no records produced")
826	+	}
827	+
828	+	var prev string
829	+	for i, r := range records {
830	+		if sid, _ := r.Data["sessionId"].(string); sid != out.SessionID {
831	+			t.Errorf("record %d sessionId = %q, want %q", i, sid, out.SessionID)
832	+		}
833	+		parent := r.Data["parentUuid"]
834	+		if i == 0 {
835	+			if parent != nil {
836	+				t.Errorf("first record parentUuid = %v, want nil", parent)
837	+			}
838	+			if src, _ := r.Data["specstorySourceSessionId"].(string); src != "orig-claude-123" {
839	+				t.Errorf("first record provenance = %q, want orig-claude-123", src)
840	+			}
841	+		} else if ps, _ := parent.(string); ps != prev {
842	+			t.Errorf("record %d parentUuid = %v, want %q", i, parent, prev)
843	+		}
844	+		prev, _ = r.Data["uuid"].(string)
845	+	}
846	+}
847	+
848	+func TestNativeSessionPath(t *testing.T) {
849	+	path, err := NewProvider().NativeSessionPath("/tmp/some-claude-proj", "abc.jsonl")
850	+	if err != nil {
851	+		t.Fatalf("NativeSessionPath: %v", err)
852	+	}
853	+	if !strings.Contains(path, filepath.Join(".claude", "projects")) {
854	+		t.Errorf("path %q should be under .claude/projects", path)
855	+	}
856	+	if !strings.HasSuffix(path, "abc.jsonl") {
857	+		t.Errorf("path %q should end with the filename", path)
858	+	}
859	+	// The project segment encodes the cwd (non-alphanumerics become dashes).
860	+	if !strings.Contains(path, "-tmp-some-claude-proj") {
861	+		t.Errorf("path %q should contain the encoded project dir", path)
862	+	}
863	+}
864	+
865	+func TestReconstructSession_Empty(t *testing.T) {
866	+	_, err := NewProvider().ReconstructSession(&schema.SessionData{SessionID: "x", WorkspaceRoot: "/tmp"}, spi.ReconstructOptions{})
867	+	if err == nil {
868	+		t.Fatal("expected error reconstructing a session with no content")
869	+	}
870	+}

889	+// GetAgentChatSessionByPath parses a single Codex session directly from its native file
890	+// path, implementing spi.PathSessionReader. It skips findCodexSessions' whole-tree walk —
891	+// which is what makes the by-id GetAgentChatSession O(N) per call, and a reindex of every
892	+// session O(N²) — by reading session_meta from the known file and handing off to the same
893	+// processSessionToAgentChat the by-id path uses. originCwd is the workspace root, matching
894	+// what GetAgentChatSession passes as projectPath.
895	+func (p *Provider) GetAgentChatSessionByPath(nativePath string, originCwd string, debugRaw bool) (*spi.AgentChatSession, error) {
896	+	meta, err := loadCodexSessionMeta(nativePath)
897	+	if err != nil {
898	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to load codex session meta: %w", err)
899	+	}
900	+	// debugRaw is only set when a caller wants provider-specific raw dumps + RawData; reindex
901	+	// (the only caller) never does. Honor it via the full path so that behavior is unchanged.
902	+	if debugRaw {
903	+		info := codexSessionInfo{
904	+			SessionID:   strings.TrimSpace(meta.Payload.ID),
905	+			SessionPath: nativePath,
906	+			Meta:        meta,
907	+		}
908	+		return processSessionToAgentChat(&info, originCwd, debugRaw)
909	+	}
910	+	// Index fast path: read records only (skip the whole-file rawData string reindex never
911	+	// reads) and skip slug derivation (reindex uses the slug from enumeration). The SessionData
912	+	// produced here is identical to the full path's.
913	+	records, err := readSessionRecords(nativePath)
914	+	if err != nil {
915	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to read session data: %w", err)
916	+	}
917	+	if len(records) == 0 {
918	+		return nil, nil
919	+	}
920	+	sessionData, err := GenerateAgentSession(records, originCwd)
921	+	if err != nil {
922	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to generate SessionData: %w", err)
923	+	}
924	+	return &spi.AgentChatSession{
925	+		SessionID:   strings.TrimSpace(meta.Payload.ID),
926	+		CreatedAt:   meta.Timestamp,
927	+		SessionData: sessionData,
928	+	}, nil
929	+}
930	+
931	 // ExecAgentAndWatch executes the Codex CLI in interactive mode and monitors for session updates.
932	 // The function blocks until the Codex CLI exits. During execution, it watches for JSONL file changes
933	 // and invokes sessionCallback for each update, enabling real-time markdown generation and cloud sync.
934	@@ -576,9 +620,23 @@ func findCodexSessions(projectPath string, targetSessionID string, stopOnFirst b
935	 	return sessions, nil
936	 }
937	 
938	-// readSessionRawData reads all JSONL lines from a Codex CLI session file and returns
939	-// both the parsed records and the raw JSONL content.
940	+// readSessionRawData reads all JSONL records and the raw JSONL content from a Codex session.
941	 func readSessionRawData(sessionPath string) ([]map[string]interface{}, string, error) {
942	+	return readCodexJSONL(sessionPath, true)
943	+}
944	+
945	+// readSessionRecords reads only the parsed JSONL records, skipping construction of the raw
946	+// JSONL string. reindex parses on this path: the raw content (up to the full file size — some
947	+// Codex sessions are hundreds of MB) is never read when indexing, so building it is pure waste.
948	+func readSessionRecords(sessionPath string) ([]map[string]interface{}, error) {
949	+	records, _, err := readCodexJSONL(sessionPath, false)
950	+	return records, err
951	+}
952	+
953	+// readCodexJSONL reads a Codex session's JSONL records, and — when collectRaw is true — the raw
954	+// JSONL content as one string. Callers that only need records pass collectRaw=false to skip the
955	+// whole-file string builder.
956	+func readCodexJSONL(sessionPath string, collectRaw bool) ([]map[string]interface{}, string, error) {
957	 	file, err := os.Open(sessionPath)
958	 	if err != nil {
959	 		return nil, "", fmt.Errorf("failed to open session file: %w", err)
960	@@ -650,9 +708,11 @@ func readSessionRawData(sessionPath string) ([]map[string]interface{}, string, e
961	 			continue
962	 		}
963	 
964	-		// Add to raw data
965	-		rawBuilder.WriteString(line)
966	-		rawBuilder.WriteString("\n")
967	+		// Accumulate raw JSONL only when the caller asked for it (reindex does not).
968	+		if collectRaw {
969	+			rawBuilder.WriteString(line)
970	+			rawBuilder.WriteString("\n")
971	+		}
972	 
973	 		// Parse JSON
974	 		var record map[string]interface{}
975	@@ -959,3 +1019,192 @@ func extractCodexSessionMetadata(sessionInfo *codexSessionInfo) (*spi.SessionMet
976	 		Name:      name,
977	 	}, nil
978	 }
979	+
980	+// codexSessionHeader is the minimal metadata enumeration needs from a session file: the
981	+// session_meta fields plus the first user message (for slug/name), produced in a SINGLE pass.
982	+type codexSessionHeader struct {
983	+	sessionID        string
984	+	cwd              string
985	+	createdAt        string
986	+	firstUserMessage string
987	+}
988	+
989	+// userMessageMarker is the cheap substring screen for a Codex user_message record. Every real
990	+// user_message record contains it (it is the payload "type" value), so screening on it before a
991	+// full JSON parse never misses one; a rare false positive (the literal text inside an injected
992	+// context record) just costs one extra parse that codexUserMessageText then rejects.
993	+const userMessageMarker = `"user_message"`
994	+
995	+// scanCodexSessionHeader reads a Codex session file ONCE and extracts the session_meta
996	+// (id/cwd/timestamp from line 1) and the first user message. It deliberately avoids fully
997	+// parsing the large injected-context records (environment, instructions, AGENTS.md — often MB
998	+// each) that precede the first user message: each line is first cheaply screened for the
999	+// user_message marker, and only a candidate line is JSON-decoded. The result is identical to the
1000	+// prior loadCodexSessionMeta + extractCodexSessionMetadata pair, in one pass instead of two opens.
1001	+// Returns (nil, nil) for a session with no user message — treated as empty, as before.
1002	+func scanCodexSessionHeader(sessionPath string) (*codexSessionHeader, error) {
1003	+	file, err := os.Open(sessionPath)
1004	+	if err != nil {
1005	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to open session file: %w", err)
1006	+	}
1007	+	defer func() { _ = file.Close() }()
1008	+
1009	+	reader := bufio.NewReader(file)
1010	+	h := &codexSessionHeader{}
1011	+	lineNum := 0
1012	+	for {
1013	+		line, readErr := reader.ReadString('\n')
1014	+		if readErr != nil && readErr != io.EOF {
1015	+			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to read line: %w", readErr)
1016	+		}
1017	+		lineNum++
1018	+		trimmed := strings.TrimSpace(line)
1019	+
1020	+		if trimmed != "" {
1021	+			if lineNum == 1 {
1022	+				// Line 1 is session_meta. Keep loadCodexSessionMeta's 64 KB first-line limit so
1023	+				// the set of indexed sessions is unchanged from the Scanner-based path.
1024	+				if len(trimmed) > bufio.MaxScanTokenSize {
1025	+					return nil, errors.New("codex session meta exceeds line limit")
1026	+				}
1027	+				var meta codexSessionMeta
1028	+				if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(trimmed), &meta); err != nil {
1029	+					return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to parse codex session meta: %w", err)
1030	+				}
1031	+				if meta.Type != "session_meta" {
1032	+					return nil, fmt.Errorf("unexpected codex session record type: %s", meta.Type)
1033	+				}
1034	+				h.sessionID = strings.TrimSpace(meta.Payload.ID)
1035	+				h.cwd = meta.Payload.CWD
1036	+				h.createdAt = meta.Timestamp
1037	+			} else if strings.Contains(trimmed, userMessageMarker) {
1038	+				// Cheap screen passed: only now pay for a full parse. The big context records
1039	+				// before the first user message lack the marker and are skipped without a parse.
1040	+				if msg := codexUserMessageText(trimmed); msg != "" {
1041	+					h.firstUserMessage = msg
1042	+					break
1043	+				}
1044	+			}
1045	+		}
1046	+
1047	+		if readErr == io.EOF {
1048	+			break
1049	+		}
1050	+	}
1051	+
1052	+	if h.sessionID == "" {
1053	+		return nil, errors.New("codex session meta not found")
1054	+	}
1055	+	if h.firstUserMessage == "" {
1056	+		return nil, nil // no user message → empty session, excluded from enumeration
1057	+	}
1058	+	return h, nil
1059	+}
1060	+
1061	+// codexUserMessageText returns the message text when line is an event_msg user_message with a
1062	+// non-empty message, else "". It is only called for lines that passed the userMessageMarker
1063	+// screen, and applies the same structural checks extractCodexSessionMetadata used.
1064	+func codexUserMessageText(line string) string {
1065	+	var record map[string]interface{}
1066	+	if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &record); err != nil {
1067	+		return ""
1068	+	}
1069	+	if recordType, _ := record["type"].(string); recordType != "event_msg" {
1070	+		return ""
1071	+	}
1072	+	payload, ok := record["payload"].(map[string]interface{})
1073	+	if !ok {
1074	+		return ""
1075	+	}
1076	+	if payloadType, _ := payload["type"].(string); payloadType != "user_message" {
1077	+		return ""
1078	+	}
1079	+	message, _ := payload["message"].(string)
1080	+	return message
1081	+}
1082	+
1083	+// ListAllAgentChatSessions enumerates every Codex CLI session across all projects. It is the
1084	+// no-progress form of ListAllAgentChatSessionsProgress.
1085	+func (p *Provider) ListAllAgentChatSessions() ([]spi.GlobalSessionRef, error) {
1086	+	return p.ListAllAgentChatSessionsProgress(nil)
1087	+}
1088	+
1089	+// ListAllAgentChatSessionsProgress enumerates every Codex CLI session across all projects by
1090	+// walking ~/.codex/sessions for *.jsonl rollouts (Codex sessions are not project-keyed by
1091	+// directory; the originating cwd lives in the session_meta record), reporting scan progress into r
1092	+// (nil-safe). The per-file header read is the dominant cost of `specstory reindex` (parsing each
1093	+// file's injected context), so headers are scanned in parallel across CPUs; output order is
1094	+// irrelevant (reindex dedups and sorts later). Implements spi.ProgressEnumerator. See
1095	+// docs/SESSIONS-DB.md.
1096	+func (p *Provider) ListAllAgentChatSessionsProgress(r *spi.ScanReporter) ([]spi.GlobalSessionRef, error) {
1097	+	homeDir, err := osUserHomeDir()
1098	+	if err != nil {
1099	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to determine home directory: %w", err)
1100	+	}
1101	+	sessionsRoot := codexSessionsRoot(homeDir)
1102	+	if _, err := os.Stat(sessionsRoot); err != nil {
1103	+		// No sessions directory yet → nothing to enumerate (not an error).
1104	+		return []spi.GlobalSessionRef{}, nil
1105	+	}
1106	+
1107	+	// Phase 1: collect file paths (dirents only — no opens).
1108	+	var paths []string
1109	+	walkErr := filepath.WalkDir(sessionsRoot, func(path string, d os.DirEntry, err error) error {
1110	+		if err != nil {
1111	+			return nil // skip unreadable entries rather than abort the sweep
1112	+		}
1113	+		if d.IsDir() || filepath.Ext(path) != ".jsonl" {
1114	+			return nil
1115	+		}
1116	+		paths = append(paths, path)
1117	+		return nil
1118	+	})
1119	+	if walkErr != nil {
1120	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to walk codex sessions: %w", walkErr)
1121	+	}
1122	+
1123	+	// Phase 2: scan headers in parallel. Each file's single-pass read is CPU-bound (JSON) and the
1124	+	// files are independent, so this scales across cores.
1125	+	workers := min(runtime.NumCPU(), 12)
1126	+	if workers < 1 {
1127	+		workers = 1
1128	+	}
1129	+	pathCh := make(chan string, workers)
1130	+	refs := make([]spi.GlobalSessionRef, 0, len(paths))
1131	+	var mu sync.Mutex
1132	+	var wg sync.WaitGroup
1133	+	for i := 0; i < workers; i++ {
1134	+		wg.Add(1)
1135	+		go func() {
1136	+			defer wg.Done()
1137	+			for path := range pathCh {
1138	+				h, err := scanCodexSessionHeader(path)
1139	+				if err != nil {
1140	+					slog.Debug("reindex: failed to scan codex session header", "path", path, "error", err)
1141	+					continue
1142	+				}
1143	+				if h == nil {
1144	+					continue // empty session (no user message)
1145	+				}
1146	+				ref := spi.GlobalSessionRef{
1147	+					SessionID:  h.sessionID,
1148	+					CreatedAt:  h.createdAt,
1149	+					Slug:       spi.GenerateFilenameFromUserMessage(h.firstUserMessage),
1150	+					Name:       spi.GenerateReadableName(h.firstUserMessage),
1151	+					NativePath: path,
1152	+					OriginCwd:  h.cwd,
1153	+				}
1154	+				mu.Lock()
1155	+				refs = append(refs, ref)
1156	+				mu.Unlock()
1157	+				r.Add(1) // count sessions found, not files scanned (warmup/empty files yield none)
1158	+			}
1159	+		}()
1160	+	}
1161	+	for _, path := range paths {
1162	+		pathCh <- path
1163	+	}
1164	+	close(pathCh)
1165	+	wg.Wait()
1166	+	return refs, nil
1167	+}
1168	diff --git a/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/codexcli/reconstruct.go b/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/codexcli/reconstruct.go
1169	new file mode 100644
1170	index 0000000..b52bf14
1171	--- /dev/null
1172	+++ b/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/codexcli/reconstruct.go
1173	@@ -0,0 +1,160 @@
1174	+package codexcli

1195	+// codexTimestamp formats a time the way Codex records do (RFC3339 millis, Z).
1196	+func codexTimestamp(t time.Time) string {
1197	+	return t.Format("2006-01-02T15:04:05.000Z")

1199	+
1200	+// ReconstructSession rebuilds a Codex CLI native rollout from the neutral
1201	+// SessionData so `codex resume <id>` can continue the conversation.
1202	+//
1203	+// Codex resume replays the response_item model transcript, while the forward
1204	+// parser reads the event_msg UI stream — so reconstruction emits BOTH for each
1205	+// turn: a response_item message (what the agent replays) and a matching event_msg
1206	+// (what the UI and our own re-parse read). Tool calls and thinking are already
1207	+// flattened into agent text by FlattenSessionData.
1208	+//
1209	+// We do NOT synthesize an environment_context block: Codex injects its own fresh
1210	+// environment context on every resume, consistent with the cross-provider policy
1211	+// that the target supplies its own preamble. See docs/SESSION-PORTABILITY.md.
1212	+func (p *Provider) ReconstructSession(data *schema.SessionData, opts spi.ReconstructOptions) (*spi.ReconstructedSession, error) {
1213	+	if data == nil {
1214	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("cannot reconstruct nil session data")
1215	+	}

1235	+	var buf bytes.Buffer

1237	+	enc.SetEscapeHTML(false)
1238	+
1239	+	encode := func(rec map[string]interface{}) error {
1240	+		return enc.Encode(rec)
1241	+	}
1242	+
1243	+	// session_meta must be the first record; it also carries provenance.
1244	+	metaTS := codexTimestamp(base)
1245	+	if err := encode(map[string]interface{}{
1246	+		"timestamp": metaTS,
1247	+		"type":      "session_meta",
1248	+		"payload": map[string]interface{}{

1250	+			"timestamp":                metaTS,

1258	+	}); err != nil {
1259	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to encode session_meta: %w", err)
1260	+	}
1261	+
1262	+	for i, turn := range turns {
1263	+		ts := codexTimestamp(base.Add(time.Duration(i+1) * time.Second))
1264	+
1265	+		var role, contentType, eventType string
1266	+		if turn.Role == schema.RoleUser {
1267	+			role, contentType, eventType = "user", "input_text", "user_message"
1268	+		} else {
1269	+			role, contentType, eventType = "assistant", "output_text", "agent_message"
1270	+		}
1271	+
1272	+		// response_item: the model-facing transcript the agent replays on resume.
1273	+		if err := encode(codexResponseItemMessage(ts, role, contentType, turn.Text)); err != nil {
1274	+			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to encode response_item: %w", err)
1275	+		}
1276	+		// event_msg: the UI/history stream the forward parser reads.
1277	+		if err := encode(codexEventMessage(ts, eventType, turn.Text)); err != nil {
1278	+			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to encode event_msg: %w", err)
1279	+		}
1280	+	}

1291	+// NativeSessionPath returns where a reconstructed rollout belongs in Codex's
1292	+// store: ~/.codex/sessions/YYYY/MM/DD/<filename>, dated today. Codex sessions are
1293	+// not project-scoped by directory, so projectPath is unused. The directory is not
1294	+// required to exist (the caller creates it).
1295	+func (p *Provider) NativeSessionPath(projectPath string, filename string) (string, error) {
1296	+	homeDir, err := os.UserHomeDir()
1297	+	if err != nil {
1298	+		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get user home directory: %w", err)
1299	+	}
1300	+	now := time.Now()
1301	+	dir := filepath.Join(
1302	+		codexSessionsRoot(homeDir),
1303	+		fmt.Sprintf("%04d", now.Year()),
1304	+		fmt.Sprintf("%02d", int(now.Month())),
1305	+		fmt.Sprintf("%02d", now.Day()),
1306	+	)
1307	+	return filepath.Join(dir, filename), nil
1308	+}

1310	+// codexResponseItemMessage builds a response_item "message" record.
1311	+func codexResponseItemMessage(ts, role, contentType, text string) map[string]interface{} {
1312	+	return map[string]interface{}{
1313	+		"timestamp": ts,
1314	+		"type":      "response_item",
1315	+		"payload": map[string]interface{}{
1316	+			"type":    "message",
1317	+			"role":    role,
1318	+			"content": []map[string]interface{}{{"type": contentType, "text": text}},
1319	+		},
1320	+	}
1321	+}
1322	+

1329	+			"type":    eventType,
1330	+			"message": message,
1331	+		},
1332	+	}

1356	+// reconstructSampleData exercises user text, agent text, thinking, and a tool
1357	+// call carrying both a summary and formatted markdown.
1358	+func reconstructSampleData() *schema.SessionData {

1360	+	fm := "```\nhello.c\nhello\n```"
1361	+	return &schema.SessionData{

1366	+		WorkspaceRoot: "/tmp/proj",

1371	+					{Role: schema.RoleUser, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "Create a hello world in D."}}},
1372	+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "Adding hello.d."}}},
1373	+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeThinking, Text: "Check for a compiler."}}},
1374	+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Tool: &schema.ToolInfo{Name: "exec_command", Type: schema.ToolTypeShell, Summary: strptr(summary), FormattedMarkdown: strptr(fm)}},
1375	+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "Created hello.d."}}},
1376	+				},
1377	+			},
1378	+		},
1379	+	}
1380	+}
1381	+
1382	+func parseCodexJSONL(t *testing.T, content []byte) []map[string]interface{} {
1383	+	t.Helper()
1384	+	var records []map[string]interface{}
1385	+	scanner := bufio.NewScanner(bytes.NewReader(content))
1386	+	scanner.Buffer(make([]byte, 0, 64*1024), 16*1024*1024)
1387	+	for scanner.Scan() {
1388	+		line := strings.TrimSpace(scanner.Text())
1389	+		if line == "" {
1390	+			continue
1391	+		}
1392	+		var m map[string]interface{}
1393	+		if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &m); err != nil {
1394	+			t.Fatalf("invalid JSONL line %q: %v", line, err)
1395	+		}
1396	+		records = append(records, m)
1397	+	}
1398	+	if err := scanner.Err(); err != nil {
1399	+		t.Fatalf("scanning content: %v", err)
1400	+	}
1401	+	return records
1402	+}
1403	+
1404	+// TestReconstructSession_RoundTrip reconstructs to native Codex rollout format,
1405	+// re-parses through the forward path, and asserts the flattened transcript is
1406	+// preserved.
1407	+func TestReconstructSession_RoundTrip(t *testing.T) {
1408	+	data := reconstructSampleData()
1409	+	expected := spi.FlattenSessionData(data, "")
1410	+
1411	+	out, err := NewProvider().ReconstructSession(data, spi.ReconstructOptions{WorkspaceRoot: "/tmp/proj"})
1412	+	if err != nil {
1413	+		t.Fatalf("ReconstructSession: %v", err)
1414	+	}
1415	+	if out.SessionID == "" {
1416	+		t.Fatal("expected a fresh session ID")
1417	+	}
1418	+	if !strings.HasPrefix(out.Filename, "rollout-") || !strings.HasSuffix(out.Filename, ".jsonl") {
1419	+		t.Errorf("filename %q should look like rollout-<ts>-<id>.jsonl", out.Filename)
1420	+	}
1421	+
1422	+	records := parseCodexJSONL(t, out.Content)
1423	+	regenerated, err := GenerateAgentSession(records, "/tmp/proj")
1424	+	if err != nil {
1425	+		t.Fatalf("GenerateAgentSession: %v", err)
1426	+	}
1427	+
1428	+	actual := spi.FlattenSessionData(regenerated, "")
1429	+	if len(actual) != len(expected) {
1430	+		t.Fatalf("round-trip produced %d turns, want %d\n got: %+v\nwant: %+v", len(actual), len(expected), actual, expected)
1431	+	}
1432	+	for i := range expected {
1433	+		if actual[i] != expected[i] {
1434	+			t.Errorf("turn %d mismatch:\n got: %+v\nwant: %+v", i, actual[i], expected[i])
1435	+		}
1436	+	}
1437	+}
1438	+
1439	+// TestReconstructSession_Structure checks the first record is session_meta with a
1440	+// matching id and provenance, that there is exactly one user_message event, and
1441	+// that no synthesized environment_context is emitted (Codex injects its own).
1442	+func TestReconstructSession_Structure(t *testing.T) {
1443	+	out, err := NewProvider().ReconstructSession(reconstructSampleData(), spi.ReconstructOptions{WorkspaceRoot: "/tmp/proj"})
1444	+	if err != nil {
1445	+		t.Fatalf("ReconstructSession: %v", err)
1446	+	}
1447	+
1448	+	records := parseCodexJSONL(t, out.Content)
1449	+	if len(records) == 0 {
1450	+		t.Fatal("no records produced")
1451	+	}
1452	+
1453	+	meta := records[0]
1454	+	if meta["type"] != "session_meta" {
1455	+		t.Fatalf("first record type = %v, want session_meta", meta["type"])
1456	+	}
1457	+	payload, _ := meta["payload"].(map[string]interface{})
1458	+	if payload["id"] != out.SessionID {
1459	+		t.Errorf("session_meta id = %v, want %q", payload["id"], out.SessionID)
1460	+	}
1461	+	if payload["specstorySourceSessionId"] != "orig-codex-123" {
1462	+		t.Errorf("provenance = %v, want orig-codex-123", payload["specstorySourceSessionId"])
1463	+	}
1464	+
1465	+	// Exactly one user_message event per user turn; no synthesized environment_context anywhere.
1466	+	userEvents := 0
1467	+	for _, r := range records {
1468	+		if strings.Contains(toJSON(t, r), "environment_context") {
1469	+			t.Error("reconstruction should not synthesize an environment_context block")
1470	+		}
1471	+		if r["type"] != "event_msg" {
1472	+			continue
1473	+		}
1474	+		p, _ := r["payload"].(map[string]interface{})
1475	+		if p["type"] == "user_message" {
1476	+			userEvents++
1477	+		}
1478	+	}
1479	+	if userEvents != 1 {
1480	+		t.Errorf("got %d user_message events, want 1", userEvents)
1481	+	}
1482	+}
1483	+
1484	+func toJSON(t *testing.T, v map[string]interface{}) string {
1485	+	t.Helper()
1486	+	b, err := json.Marshal(v)
1487	+	if err != nil {
1488	+		t.Fatalf("marshal: %v", err)
1489	+	}
1490	+	return string(b)
1491	+}
1492	+

+
+// ListAllAgentChatSessions enumerates every session in this provider's native store,
+// regardless of project. See docs/SESSIONS-DB.md.
+//
+// Cursor stores sessions at ~/.cursor/chats/<projectHash>/<sessionID>/store.db, where
+// projectHash = md5(canonical project path). Cursor records NO workspace path inside
+// the store (the meta blob has only agent/model fields), and md5 is one-way — so
+// OriginCwd cannot be filled from the store alone and is left empty here. The hash is
+// embedded in NativePath, so `specstory reindex` recovers the cwd by reverse-matching
+// that hash against the project paths the other providers surface.
+func (p *Provider) ListAllAgentChatSessions() ([]spi.GlobalSessionRef, error) {
+	chatsDir, err := GetCursorChatsDir()
+	if err != nil {
+		return nil, err
+	}
+	hashEntries, err := os.ReadDir(chatsDir)
+	if err != nil {
+		if os.IsNotExist(err) {
+			return []spi.GlobalSessionRef{}, nil
+		}
+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to read cursor chats directory: %w", err)
+	}
+
+	var refs []spi.GlobalSessionRef
+	for _, hashEntry := range hashEntries {
+		if !hashEntry.IsDir() {
+			continue
+		}
+		hashDir := filepath.Join(chatsDir, hashEntry.Name())
+		sessionIDs, err := GetCursorSessionDirs(hashDir)
+		if err != nil {
+			continue
+		}
+		for _, sessionID := range sessionIDs {
+			if !HasStoreDB(hashDir, sessionID) {
+				continue
+			}
+			sessionPath := filepath.Join(hashDir, sessionID)
+			metadata, err := extractCursorSessionMetadata(sessionPath, sessionID)
+			if err != nil {
...

 `./pkg/providers/claudecode/jsonl_parser.go`

```text
535	
536		return mergedDags
537	}
538	
539	// buildDAGs constructs parent/child DAGs from records
540	func (p *JSONLParser) buildDAGs(records []JSONLRecord) [][]JSONLRecord {
541		// Index every record by uuid AND build a parentUuid->children adjacency map in one pass.
542		// Precomputing children here is what keeps DAG building O(N): the traversal below can then
543		// look a node's children up directly. The previous version re-scanned the entire record set
544		// to find children at every node it visited, making a whole-project parse O(N²) — tens of
545		// seconds for a project with ~25k records (the dominant cost of `specstory sync`).
546		recordByUuid := make(map[string]JSONLRecord, len(records))
547		childrenByParent := make(map[string][]JSONLRecord)
548		roots := []JSONLRecord{}
549		for _, record := range records {
550			uuid, ok := record.Data["uuid"].(string)
551			if !ok {
552				continue // a record with no uuid can be neither traversed nor linked
553			}
554			recordByUuid[uuid] = record
555			if parentUuid, ok := record.Data["parentUuid"].(string); ok {
556				childrenByParent[parentUuid] = append(childrenByParent[parentUuid], record)
557			} else if record.Data["parentUuid"] == nil {
558				// A nil parentUuid marks a root node (the start of a conversation).
559				roots = append(roots, record)
560			}
561		}
562	
563		// Sort each child list by timestamp once, giving the same deterministic traversal order the
564		// per-node sort used to — but paid O(N log N) total across the forest instead of per visit.
565		for parentUuid := range childrenByParent {
566			children := childrenByParent[parentUuid]
567			sort.Slice(children, func(i, j int) bool {
568				tsI, _ := children[i].Data["timestamp"].(string)
569				tsJ, _ := children[j].Data["timestamp"].(string)
570				return tsI < tsJ
571			})
572		}
573	
574		// Build a DAG for each root
575		dags := [][]JSONLRecord{}
576		for _, root := range roots {
577			dag := p.buildDAGFromRoot(root, childrenByParent)
578			if len(dag) > 0 {
579				dags = append(dags, dag)
580			}
581		}
582	
583		return dags
584	}
585	
586	// buildDAGFromRoot builds a DAG starting from a root node, following the precomputed
587	// parentUuid->children adjacency (already timestamp-sorted in buildDAGs).
588	func (p *JSONLParser) buildDAGFromRoot(root JSONLRecord, childrenByParent map[string][]JSONLRecord) []JSONLRecord {
589		dag := []JSONLRecord{}
590		visited := make(map[string]bool)
591	
592		var traverse func(node JSONLRecord)
593		traverse = func(node JSONLRecord) {
594			uuid, ok := node.Data["uuid"].(string)
595			if !ok || visited[uuid] {
596				return
597			}
598	
599			visited[uuid] = true
600			dag = append(dag, node)
601	
602			// Children are precomputed and already sorted by timestamp.
603			for _, child := range childrenByParent[uuid] {
604				traverse(child)
605			}
606		}
607	
608		traverse(root)
609		return dag
610	}
611	
612	// flattenDAG flattens a DAG into an array ordered by timestamp
613	func (p *JSONLParser) flattenDAG(dag []JSONLRecord) []JSONLRecord {
614		// Sort by timestamp, with parent-child relationships as tiebreaker
615		sort.Slice(dag, func(i, j int) bool {
616			tsI, _ := dag[i].Data["timestamp"].(string)
617			tsJ, _ := dag[j].Data["timestamp"].(string)
618			if tsI != tsJ {
619				return tsI < tsJ
620			}
621			// If timestamps are equal, check parent-child relationship
622			uuidI, _ := dag[i].Data["uuid"].(string)
623			uuidJ, _ := dag[j].Data["uuid"].(string)
624			parentI, _ := dag[i].Data["parentUuid"].(string)
625			parentJ, _ := dag[j].Data["parentUuid"].(string)
626	
627			// If j is the parent of i, j should come first
628			if parentI == uuidJ {
629				return false
630			}
631			// If i is the parent of j, i should come first
632			if parentJ == uuidI {
633				return true
634			}
```

266	// DetectAgent checks if Claude Code has been used in the given project directory
267	func (p *Provider) DetectAgent(projectPath string, helpOutput bool) bool {
268		// Get the Claude Code project directory for the given path
269		claudeProjectPath, err := GetClaudeCodeProjectDir(projectPath)
270		if err != nil {
271			slog.Debug("DetectAgent: Failed to get Claude Code project directory", "error", err)
272			return false
273		}
274	
275		// Check if the Claude Code project directory exists
276		if info, err := os.Stat(claudeProjectPath); err == nil && info.IsDir() {
277			slog.Debug("DetectAgent: Claude Code project found", "path", claudeProjectPath)
278			return true
279		}
280	
281		slog.Debug("DetectAgent: No Claude Code project found", "expected_path", claudeProjectPath)
282	
283		// If helpOutput is requested, provide helpful guidance
284		if helpOutput {
285			fmt.Println() // Add visual separation
286			log.UserWarn("No Claude Code project found for this directory.\n")
287			log.UserMessage("Claude Code hasn't created a project folder for your current directory yet.\n")
288			log.UserMessage("This happens when Claude Code hasn't been run in this directory.\n\n")
289			log.UserMessage("To fix this:\n")
290			log.UserMessage("  1. Run 'specstory run' to start Claude Code in this directory\n")
291			log.UserMessage("  2. Or run Claude Code directly with `claude`, then try syncing again\n\n")
292			log.UserMessage("Expected project folder: %s\n", claudeProjectPath)
293			fmt.Println() // Add trailing newline
294		}
295	
296		return false
297	}
298	
299	// GetAgentChatSessions retrieves all chat sessions for the given project path
300	func (p *Provider) GetAgentChatSessions(projectPath string, debugRaw bool, progress spi.ProgressCallback) ([]spi.AgentChatSession, error) {
301		// Get the Claude Code project directory
302		claudeProjectDir, err := GetClaudeCodeProjectDir(projectPath)
303		if err != nil {
304			return nil, err
305		}
306	
307		// Check if project directory exists
308		if _, err := os.Stat(claudeProjectDir); os.IsNotExist(err) {
309			return []spi.AgentChatSession{}, nil // No sessions if no project
310		}
311	
312		// Parse JSONL files with progress callback
313		parser := NewJSONLParser()
314		err = parser.ParseProjectSessionsWithProgress(claudeProjectDir, progress)
315		if err != nil {
316			return nil, err
317		}
318	
319		// Convert sessions to AgentChatSession structs
320		result := make([]spi.AgentChatSession, 0, len(parser.Sessions))
321		for _, session := range parser.Sessions {
322			// Skip empty sessions (no records)
323			if len(session.Records) == 0 {
324				continue
325			}
326	
327			// Process session (filter warmup, generate SessionData)
328			chatSession := processSession(session, projectPath, debugRaw)
329			if chatSession != nil {
330				result = append(result, *chatSession)
331			}
332		}
333	
334		return result, nil
335	}
336	
337	// GetAgentChatSession retrieves a single chat session by ID for the given project path
338	func (p *Provider) GetAgentChatSession(projectPath string, sessionID string, debugRaw bool) (*spi.AgentChatSession, error) {
339		// Get the Claude Code project directory
340		claudeProjectDir, err := GetClaudeCodeProjectDir(projectPath)
341		if err != nil {
342			return nil, err
343		}
344	
345		// Check if project directory exists
346		if _, err := os.Stat(claudeProjectDir); os.IsNotExist(err) {
347			return nil, nil // No sessions if no project
348		}
349	
350		// Parse only the session we're looking for
351		parser := NewJSONLParser()
352		err = parser.ParseSingleSession(claudeProjectDir, sessionID)
353		if err != nil {
354			// If session not found, return nil (not an error)
355			if strings.Contains(err.Error(), "not found") {
356				return nil, nil
357			}
358			return nil, err
359		}
360	
361		// Find the session
362		sessionPointer := parser.FindSession(sessionID)
363		if sessionPointer == nil {
364			return nil, nil
365		}
366		session := *sessionPointer
367	
368		// Skip empty sessions (no records)
369		if len(session.Records) == 0 {
370			return nil, nil
371		}
372	
373		// Process session (filter warmup, generate SessionData)
374		return processSession(session, projectPath, debugRaw), nil
375	}
376	
377	// ExecAgentAndWatch executes Claude Code in interactive mode and watches for session updates
378	func (p *Provider) ExecAgentAndWatch(projectPath string, customCommand string, resumeSessionID string, debugRaw bool, sessionCallback func(*spi.AgentChatSession)) error {
379		slog.Info("ExecAgentAndWatch: Starting Claude Code execution and monitoring",
380			"projectPath", projectPath,
381			"customCommand", customCommand,
382			"resumeSessionID", resumeSessionID,
383			"debugRaw", debugRaw)
384	
385		// Validate resume session ID if provided (Claude Code uses UUID format)
386		if resumeSessionID != "" {
387			resumeSessionID = strings.TrimSpace(resumeSessionID)

1	diff --git a/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/cursorcli/provider.go b/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/cursorcli/provider.go
2	index 5fcf971..3cef8cb 100644
3	--- a/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/cursorcli/provider.go
4	+++ b/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/cursorcli/provider.go
5	@@ -652,3 +652,61 @@ func extractCursorSessionMetadata(sessionPath string, sessionID string) (*spi.Se
6	 		Name:      name,
7	 	}, nil
8	 }
9	+
10	+// ListAllAgentChatSessions enumerates every session in this provider's native store,
11	+// regardless of project. See docs/SESSIONS-DB.md.
12	+//
13	+// Cursor stores sessions at ~/.cursor/chats/<projectHash>/<sessionID>/store.db, where
14	+// projectHash = md5(canonical project path). Cursor records NO workspace path inside
15	+// the store (the meta blob has only agent/model fields), and md5 is one-way — so
16	+// OriginCwd cannot be filled from the store alone and is left empty here. The hash is
17	+// embedded in NativePath, so `specstory reindex` recovers the cwd by reverse-matching
18	+// that hash against the project paths the other providers surface.
19	+func (p *Provider) ListAllAgentChatSessions() ([]spi.GlobalSessionRef, error) {
20	+	chatsDir, err := GetCursorChatsDir()
21	+	if err != nil {
22	+		return nil, err
23	+	}
24	+	hashEntries, err := os.ReadDir(chatsDir)
25	+	if err != nil {
26	+		if os.IsNotExist(err) {
27	+			return []spi.GlobalSessionRef{}, nil
28	+		}
29	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to read cursor chats directory: %w", err)
30	+	}
31	+
32	+	var refs []spi.GlobalSessionRef
33	+	for _, hashEntry := range hashEntries {
34	+		if !hashEntry.IsDir() {
35	+			continue
36	+		}
37	+		hashDir := filepath.Join(chatsDir, hashEntry.Name())
38	+		sessionIDs, err := GetCursorSessionDirs(hashDir)
39	+		if err != nil {
40	+			continue
41	+		}
42	+		for _, sessionID := range sessionIDs {
43	+			if !HasStoreDB(hashDir, sessionID) {
44	+				continue
45	+			}
46	+			sessionPath := filepath.Join(hashDir, sessionID)
47	+			metadata, err := extractCursorSessionMetadata(sessionPath, sessionID)
48	+			if err != nil {
49	+				slog.Warn("reindex: failed to extract cursor session metadata", "sessionID", sessionID, "error", err)
50	+				continue
51	+			}
52	+			if metadata == nil {
53	+				continue // empty session
54	+			}
55	+			refs = append(refs, spi.GlobalSessionRef{
56	+				SessionID:  metadata.SessionID,
57	+				CreatedAt:  metadata.CreatedAt,
58	+				Slug:       metadata.Slug,
59	+				Name:       metadata.Name,
60	+				NativePath: filepath.Join(sessionPath, "store.db"),
61	+				OriginCwd:  "", // not stored by Cursor; recovered at reindex via the hash in NativePath
62	+			})
63	+		}
64	+	}
65	+	return refs, nil
66	+}
67	diff --git a/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/cursorcli/reconstruct.go b/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/cursorcli/reconstruct.go
68	new file mode 100644
69	index 0000000..af0c03a
70	--- /dev/null
71	+++ b/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/cursorcli/reconstruct.go
72	@@ -0,0 +1,222 @@
73	+package cursorcli
74	+
75	+import (
76	+	"bytes"
77	+	"crypto/sha256"
78	+	"database/sql"
79	+	"encoding/binary"
80	+	"encoding/hex"
81	+	"encoding/json"
82	+	"fmt"
83	+	"os"
84	+	"path/filepath"
85	+	"time"
86	+
87	+	"github.com/google/uuid"
88	+
89	+	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
90	+	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
91	+)
92	+
93	+// Cursor stores a session as a SQLite store.db: a content-addressed Merkle-DAG of
94	+// blobs where each blob's id == sha256(data). Conversation messages are PLAIN JSON
95	+// blobs; small protobuf "index" nodes thread them into the DAG; `meta['0']` carries
96	+// the head pointer (`latestRootBlobId`). Tool calls and thinking are already
97	+// flattened into agent text by FlattenSessionData, so reconstruction emits only
98	+// user/assistant text-message blobs. The structure below (messages + root + a
99	+// field-2 metadata subtree of title/empty/preview nodes, with NO system message)
100	+// was validated against `cursor-agent --resume`. See docs/SESSION-PORTABILITY.md.
101	+
102	+// blobID returns the content-addressed id Cursor uses: sha256(data), hex-encoded.
103	+func blobID(data []byte) string {
104	+	sum := sha256.Sum256(data)
105	+	return hex.EncodeToString(sum[:])
106	+}
107	+
108	+// pbField encodes a protobuf length-delimited field (wire type 2). fieldNum must be < 16.
109	+func pbField(fieldNum int, payload []byte) []byte {
110	+	out := []byte{byte(fieldNum<<3 | 2)}
111	+	var lenBuf [binary.MaxVarintLen64]byte
112	+	n := binary.PutUvarint(lenBuf[:], uint64(len(payload)))
113	+	out = append(out, lenBuf[:n]...)
114	+	return append(out, payload...)
115	+}
116	+
117	+// pbRef encodes a length-delimited field carrying a 32-byte blob id reference.
118	+func pbRef(fieldNum int, idHex string) []byte {
119	+	raw, _ := hex.DecodeString(idHex)
120	+	return pbField(fieldNum, raw)
121	+}
122	+
123	+// cursorTextPart / message structs marshal to Cursor's plain-JSON message shape.
124	+type cursorTextPart struct {
125	+	Type string `json:"type"`
126	+	Text string `json:"text"`
127	+}
128	+type cursorUserMsg struct {
129	+	Role    string           `json:"role"`
130	+	Content []cursorTextPart `json:"content"`
131	+}
132	+type cursorAsstMsg struct {
133	+	ID      string           `json:"id"`
134	+	Role    string           `json:"role"`
135	+	Content []cursorTextPart `json:"content"`
136	+}
137	+
138	+// marshalCompact JSON-encodes v without HTML escaping and without a trailing newline.
139	+func marshalCompact(v interface{}) ([]byte, error) {
140	+	var buf bytes.Buffer
141	+	enc := json.NewEncoder(&buf)
142	+	enc.SetEscapeHTML(false)
143	+	if err := enc.Encode(v); err != nil {
144	+		return nil, err
145	+	}
146	+	return bytes.TrimRight(buf.Bytes(), "\n"), nil
147	+}
148	+
149	+// ReconstructSession rebuilds a Cursor CLI native store.db from the neutral SessionData.
150	+func (p *Provider) ReconstructSession(data *schema.SessionData, opts spi.ReconstructOptions) (*spi.ReconstructedSession, error) {
151	+	if data == nil {
152	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("cannot reconstruct nil session data")
153	+	}
154	+
155	+	turns := spi.FlattenSessionData(data, opts.MigrationNote)
156	+	if len(turns) == 0 {
157	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("session has no content to reconstruct")
158	+	}
159	+
160	+	newID := uuid.NewString()
161	+	name := data.Slug
162	+	if name == "" {
163	+		name = "Resumed session"
164	+	}
165	+
166	+	// blobs accumulates every blob (id -> data); helper interns by content address.
167	+	blobs := map[string][]byte{}
168	+	put := func(b []byte) string { id := blobID(b); blobs[id] = b; return id }
169	+
170	+	// 1. Message blobs (plain JSON), in order. No system message.
171	+	var msgIDs []string
172	+	var lastAssistantText string
173	+	asstSeq := 0
174	+	for _, turn := range turns {
175	+		var raw []byte
176	+		var err error
177	+		if turn.Role == schema.RoleUser {
178	+			raw, err = marshalCompact(cursorUserMsg{Role: "user", Content: []cursorTextPart{{Type: "text", Text: turn.Text}}})
179	+		} else {
180	+			asstSeq++
181	+			raw, err = marshalCompact(cursorAsstMsg{ID: fmt.Sprintf("%d", asstSeq), Role: "assistant", Content: []cursorTextPart{{Type: "text", Text: turn.Text}}})
182	+			lastAssistantText = turn.Text
183	+		}
184	+		if err != nil {
185	+			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to encode message: %w", err)
186	+		}
187	+		msgIDs = append(msgIDs, put(raw))
188	+	}
189	+
190	+	// 2. Field-2 metadata subtree: title leaf, empty placeholder, preview node.
191	+	titleID := put(append(pbField(1, []byte(name)), pbField(2, []byte(uuid.NewString()))...))
192	+	emptyID := put(pbField(3, nil))                                     // {f3:""}
193	+	previewID := put(pbField(1, pbField(1, []byte(lastAssistantText)))) // {f1:{f1:text}}
194	+	metaNodeID := put(concat(pbRef(1, titleID), pbRef(2, emptyID), pbRef(2, previewID)))
195	+
196	+	// 3. Root node: field 1 = ordered message refs, field 2 = metadata node.
197	+	var root []byte
198	+	for _, mid := range msgIDs {
199	+		root = append(root, pbRef(1, mid)...)
200	+	}
201	+	root = append(root, pbRef(2, metaNodeID)...)
202	+	rootID := put(root)
203	+
204	+	// 4. meta['0'] head record (hex-encoded JSON).
205	+	metaJSON, err := marshalCompact(map[string]interface{}{
206	+		"agentId":          newID,
207	+		"latestRootBlobId": rootID,
208	+		"name":             name,
209	+		"mode":             "default",
210	+		"createdAt":        time.Now().UTC().UnixMilli(),
211	+	})
212	+	if err != nil {
213	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to encode meta: %w", err)
214	+	}
215	+
216	+	// 5. Materialize the SQLite store.db and return its bytes. SQLite needs a file
217	+	// handle, so we build in a temp file (created and removed within this call).
218	+	content, err := buildStoreDB(blobs, hex.EncodeToString(metaJSON))
219	+	if err != nil {
220	+		return nil, err
221	+	}
222	+
223	+	return &spi.ReconstructedSession{
224	+		SessionID: newID,
225	+		Filename:  filepath.Join(newID, "store.db"),
226	+		Content:   content,
227	+	}, nil
228	+}
229	+
230	+// concat joins byte slices.
231	+func concat(parts ...[]byte) []byte {
232	+	var out []byte
233	+	for _, p := range parts {
234	+		out = append(out, p...)
235	+	}
236	+	return out
237	+}
238	+
239	+// buildStoreDB writes the blobs + meta into a SQLite database and returns its bytes.
240	+func buildStoreDB(blobs map[string][]byte, metaHex string) ([]byte, error) {
241	+	tmp, err := os.CreateTemp("", "specstory-cursor-*.db")
242	+	if err != nil {
243	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to create temp db: %w", err)
244	+	}
245	+	tmpPath := tmp.Name()
246	+	_ = tmp.Close()
247	+	defer func() { _ = os.Remove(tmpPath) }()
248	+
249	+	db, err := sql.Open("sqlite", tmpPath)
250	+	if err != nil {
251	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to open temp db: %w", err)
252	+	}
253	+	if _, err := db.Exec("CREATE TABLE blobs (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, data BLOB)"); err != nil {
254	+		_ = db.Close()
255	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to create blobs table: %w", err)
256	+	}
257	+	if _, err := db.Exec("CREATE TABLE meta (key TEXT PRIMARY KEY, value TEXT)"); err != nil {
258	+		_ = db.Close()
259	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to create meta table: %w", err)
260	+	}
261	+	for id, data := range blobs {
262	+		if _, err := db.Exec("INSERT INTO blobs (id, data) VALUES (?, ?)", id, data); err != nil {
263	+			_ = db.Close()
264	+			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to insert blob: %w", err)
265	+		}
266	+	}
267	+	if _, err := db.Exec("INSERT INTO meta (key, value) VALUES ('0', ?)", metaHex); err != nil {
268	+		_ = db.Close()
269	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to insert meta: %w", err)
270	+	}
271	+	if err := db.Close(); err != nil {
272	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to close temp db: %w", err)
273	+	}
274	+
275	+	return os.ReadFile(tmpPath)
276	+}
277	+
278	+// NativeSessionPath returns where a reconstructed store.db belongs in Cursor's store:
279	+// ~/.cursor/chats/<md5(canonical cwd)>/<session-id>/store.db. The filename already
280	+// includes the session-id subdir, so this joins it under the project hash dir.
281	+func (p *Provider) NativeSessionPath(projectPath string, filename string) (string, error) {
282	+	if projectPath == "" {
283	+		var err error
284	+		projectPath, err = os.Getwd()
285	+		if err != nil {
286	+			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get current working directory: %w", err)
287	+		}
288	+	}
289	+	hashDir, err := GetProjectHashDir(projectPath)
290	+	if err != nil {
291	+		return "", err
292	+	}
293	+	return filepath.Join(hashDir, filename), nil
294	+}
295	diff --git a/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/cursorcli/reconstruct_test.go b/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/cursorcli/reconstruct_test.go
296	new file mode 100644
297	index 0000000..f4b162e
298	--- /dev/null
299	+++ b/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/cursorcli/reconstruct_test.go
300	@@ -0,0 +1,183 @@
301	+package cursorcli
302	+
303	+import (
304	+	"crypto/sha256"
305	+	"database/sql"
306	+	"encoding/hex"
307	+	"encoding/json"
308	+	"os"
309	+	"path/filepath"
310	+	"strings"
311	+	"testing"
312	+
313	+	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
314	+	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
315	+)
316	+
317	+func strptr(s string) *string { return &s }
318	+
319	+func reconstructSampleData() *schema.SessionData {
320	+	fm := "Created `hello.rs`:\n\n```rust\nfn main() { println!(\"Hello, World!\"); }\n```"
321	+	return &schema.SessionData{
322	+		SchemaVersion: "1.0",
323	+		Provider:      schema.ProviderInfo{ID: "cursor", Name: "Cursor CLI", Version: "x"},
324	+		SessionID:     "orig-cursor-123",
325	+		CreatedAt:     "2026-06-18T10:00:00.000Z",
326	+		WorkspaceRoot: "/tmp/proj",
327	+		Slug:          "hello world in rust",
328	+		Exchanges: []schema.Exchange{
329	+			{
330	+				ExchangeID: "orig-cursor-123:0",
331	+				Messages: []schema.Message{
332	+					{Role: schema.RoleUser, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "Create a hello world in Rust."}}},
333	+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "I'll create it."}}},
334	+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Tool: &schema.ToolInfo{Name: "write", Type: schema.ToolTypeWrite, FormattedMarkdown: strptr(fm)}},
335	+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "Done."}}},
336	+				},
337	+			},
338	+		},
339	+	}
340	+}
341	+
342	+// openStoreBytes writes the reconstructed store.db bytes to a temp file and reads
343	+// back its blobs + meta head record.
344	+func openStoreBytes(t *testing.T, content []byte) (map[string][]byte, map[string]interface{}) {
345	+	t.Helper()
346	+	path := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "store.db")
347	+	if err := os.WriteFile(path, content, 0o644); err != nil {
348	+		t.Fatalf("write store: %v", err)
349	+	}
350	+	db, err := sql.Open("sqlite", path+"?mode=ro")
351	+	if err != nil {
352	+		t.Fatalf("open store: %v", err)
353	+	}
354	+	defer func() { _ = db.Close() }()
355	+
356	+	blobs := map[string][]byte{}
357	+	rows, err := db.Query("SELECT id, data FROM blobs")
358	+	if err != nil {
359	+		t.Fatalf("query blobs: %v", err)
360	+	}
361	+	for rows.Next() {
362	+		var id string
363	+		var data []byte
364	+		if err := rows.Scan(&id, &data); err != nil {
365	+			t.Fatalf("scan: %v", err)
366	+		}
367	+		blobs[id] = data
368	+	}
369	+	_ = rows.Close()
370	+
371	+	var metaHex string
372	+	if err := db.QueryRow("SELECT value FROM meta WHERE key='0'").Scan(&metaHex); err != nil {
373	+		t.Fatalf("meta: %v", err)
374	+	}
375	+	metaBytes, err := hex.DecodeString(metaHex)
376	+	if err != nil {
377	+		t.Fatalf("meta hex: %v", err)
378	+	}
379	+	var meta map[string]interface{}
380	+	if err := json.Unmarshal(metaBytes, &meta); err != nil {
381	+		t.Fatalf("meta json: %v", err)
382	+	}
383	+	return blobs, meta
384	+}
385	+
386	+// refsOf scans a protobuf node for 32-byte blob-id references in the given field.
387	+func refsOf(node []byte, fieldNum int) []string {
388	+	tag := byte(fieldNum<<3 | 2)
389	+	var refs []string
390	+	for i := 0; i+34 <= len(node); i++ {
391	+		if node[i] == tag && node[i+1] == 0x20 {
392	+			refs = append(refs, hex.EncodeToString(node[i+2:i+34]))
393	+		}
394	+	}
395	+	return refs
396	+}
397	+
398	+// TestReconstructSession_Structure validates the store.db has the content-addressed
399	+// shape Cursor requires: sha256 ids, a head pointer, and a root whose field-1 lists
400	+// the message blobs in order with a field-2 metadata node.
401	+func TestReconstructSession_Structure(t *testing.T) {
402	+	data := reconstructSampleData()
403	+	expected := spi.FlattenSessionData(data, "") // user + 3 agent turns
404	+
405	+	out, err := NewProvider().ReconstructSession(data, spi.ReconstructOptions{WorkspaceRoot: "/tmp/proj"})
406	+	if err != nil {
407	+		t.Fatalf("ReconstructSession: %v", err)
408	+	}
409	+	if out.SessionID == "" || filepath.Base(out.Filename) != "store.db" {
410	+		t.Fatalf("unexpected id/filename: %q / %q", out.SessionID, out.Filename)
411	+	}
412	+
413	+	blobs, meta := openStoreBytes(t, out.Content)
414	+
415	+	// Every blob is content-addressed by sha256.
416	+	for id, d := range blobs {
417	+		if sum := sha256.Sum256(d); hex.EncodeToString(sum[:]) != id {
418	+			t.Errorf("blob id %s != sha256(data)", id)
419	+		}
420	+	}
421	+
422	+	head, _ := meta["latestRootBlobId"].(string)
423	+	root, ok := blobs[head]
424	+	if !ok {
425	+		t.Fatalf("head %q not present in blobs", head)
426	+	}
427	+	if meta["agentId"] != out.SessionID {
428	+		t.Errorf("meta agentId %v != session id %q", meta["agentId"], out.SessionID)
429	+	}
430	+
431	+	// Root field 1 = message blobs in order; verify roles + text match the turns.
432	+	msgRefs := refsOf(root, 1)
433	+	if len(msgRefs) != len(expected) {
434	+		t.Fatalf("root references %d messages, want %d", len(msgRefs), len(expected))
435	+	}
436	+	for i, ref := range msgRefs {
437	+		var m struct {
438	+			Role    string `json:"role"`
439	+			Content []struct {
440	+				Text string `json:"text"`
441	+			} `json:"content"`
442	+		}
443	+		if err := json.Unmarshal(blobs[ref], &m); err != nil {
444	+			t.Fatalf("message %d not JSON: %v", i, err)
445	+		}
446	+		wantRole := "assistant"
447	+		if expected[i].Role == schema.RoleUser {
448	+			wantRole = "user"
449	+		}
450	+		if m.Role != wantRole {
451	+			t.Errorf("message %d role = %q, want %q", i, m.Role, wantRole)
452	+		}
453	+		if len(m.Content) == 0 || m.Content[0].Text != expected[i].Text {
454	+			t.Errorf("message %d text mismatch:\n got: %q\nwant: %q", i, m.Content, expected[i].Text)
455	+		}
456	+	}
457	+
458	+	// Root field 2 = metadata node, which must resolve.
459	+	metaRefs := refsOf(root, 2)
460	+	if len(metaRefs) != 1 || blobs[metaRefs[0]] == nil {
461	+		t.Errorf("root should reference exactly one metadata node; got %v", metaRefs)
462	+	}
463	+}
464	+
465	+func TestNativeSessionPath(t *testing.T) {
466	+	path, err := NewProvider().NativeSessionPath(t.TempDir(), filepath.Join("sess-id", "store.db"))
467	+	if err != nil {
468	+		t.Fatalf("NativeSessionPath: %v", err)
469	+	}
470	+	if !strings.Contains(path, filepath.Join(".cursor", "chats")) {
471	+		t.Errorf("path %q should be under .cursor/chats", path)
472	+	}
473	+	if !strings.HasSuffix(path, filepath.Join("sess-id", "store.db")) {
474	+		t.Errorf("path %q should end with <session-id>/store.db", path)
475	+	}
476	+}
477	+
478	+func TestReconstructSession_Empty(t *testing.T) {
479	+	_, err := NewProvider().ReconstructSession(&schema.SessionData{SessionID: "x", WorkspaceRoot: "/tmp"}, spi.ReconstructOptions{})
480	+	if err == nil {
481	+		t.Fatal("expected error reconstructing a session with no content")
482	+	}
483	+}
484	diff --git a/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/deepseektui/provider.go b/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/deepseektui/provider.go
485	index 42d3f49..85f727b 100644
486	--- a/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/deepseektui/provider.go
487	+++ b/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/deepseektui/provider.go
488	@@ -318,3 +318,38 @@ func printDetectionHelp() {
489	 	log.UserMessage("No DeepSeek TUI sessions found under ~/.deepseek/sessions yet.\n")
490	 	log.UserMessage("Run DeepSeek TUI inside this project to create a session, then rerun `specstory sync deepseek`.\n")
