492	+
493	+// ListAllAgentChatSessions enumerates every DeepSeek TUI session in
494	+// ~/.deepseek/sessions/. DeepSeek sessions are not project-scoped by directory; the
495	+// originating workspace is recorded in each session's metadata.workspace field. See
496	+// docs/SESSIONS-DB.md.
497	+func (p *Provider) ListAllAgentChatSessions() ([]spi.GlobalSessionRef, error) {
498	+	files, err := listSessionFiles()
499	+	if err != nil {
500	+		if os.IsNotExist(err) {
501	+			return []spi.GlobalSessionRef{}, nil
502	+		}
503	+		return nil, err
504	+	}
505	+
506	+	var refs []spi.GlobalSessionRef
507	+	for _, file := range files {
508	+		metadata, err := extractSessionMetadata(file.Path)
509	+		if err != nil {
510	+			slog.Debug("reindex: failed to extract deepseek session metadata", "path", file.Path, "error", err)
511	+			continue
512	+		}
513	+		if metadata == nil {
514	+			continue // empty session
515	+		}
516	+		refs = append(refs, spi.GlobalSessionRef{
517	+			SessionID:  metadata.SessionID,
518	+			CreatedAt:  metadata.CreatedAt,
519	+			Slug:       metadata.Slug,
520	+			Name:       metadata.Name,
521	+			NativePath: file.Path,
522	+			OriginCwd:  extractWorkspaceFast(file.Path),
523	+		})
524	+	}
525	+	return refs, nil
526	+}
527	diff --git a/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/deepseektui/reconstruct.go b/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/deepseektui/reconstruct.go
528	new file mode 100644
529	index 0000000..e1f5a12
530	--- /dev/null
531	+++ b/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/deepseektui/reconstruct.go
532	@@ -0,0 +1,108 @@
533	+package deepseektui
534	+
535	+import (
536	+	"bytes"
537	+	"encoding/json"
538	+	"fmt"
539	+	"path/filepath"
540	+	"strings"
541	+	"time"
542	+
543	+	"github.com/google/uuid"
544	+
545	+	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
546	+	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
547	+)
548	+
549	+// deepseekTimestamp formats a time the way DeepSeek records do (RFC3339 micros, Z).
550	+func deepseekTimestamp(t time.Time) string {
551	+	return t.Format("2006-01-02T15:04:05.000000Z")
552	+}
553	+
554	+// ReconstructSession rebuilds a DeepSeek TUI native session from the neutral
555	+// SessionData so `deepseek --resume <id>` can continue the conversation.
556	+//
557	+// DeepSeek stores a session as a single JSON document
558	+// `{schema_version, system_prompt, metadata, messages}` where each message is
559	+// `{role, content:[{type:"text",text}]}`. Tool calls and thinking are already
560	+// flattened into agent text by FlattenSessionData, so reconstruction emits only
561	+// `user`/`assistant` text messages. The session is associated with a project via
562	+// metadata.workspace. See docs/SESSION-PORTABILITY.md.
563	+func (p *Provider) ReconstructSession(data *schema.SessionData, opts spi.ReconstructOptions) (*spi.ReconstructedSession, error) {
564	+	if data == nil {
565	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("cannot reconstruct nil session data")
566	+	}
567	+
568	+	cwd := opts.WorkspaceRoot
569	+	if cwd == "" {
570	+		cwd = data.WorkspaceRoot
571	+	}
572	+
573	+	turns := spi.FlattenSessionData(data, opts.MigrationNote)
574	+	if len(turns) == 0 {
575	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("session has no content to reconstruct")
576	+	}
577	+
578	+	newID := uuid.NewString()
579	+	now := deepseekTimestamp(time.Now().UTC())
580	+
581	+	title := strings.TrimSpace(data.Slug)
582	+	if title == "" {
583	+		title = "Resumed session"
584	+	}
585	+
586	+	messages := make([]map[string]interface{}, 0, len(turns))
587	+	for _, turn := range turns {
588	+		role := "assistant"
589	+		if turn.Role == schema.RoleUser {
590	+			role = "user"
591	+		}
592	+		messages = append(messages, map[string]interface{}{
593	+			"role":    role,
594	+			"content": []map[string]interface{}{{"type": "text", "text": turn.Text}},
595	+		})
596	+	}
597	+
598	+	session := map[string]interface{}{
599	+		"schema_version": 1,
600	+		"system_prompt":  "",
601	+		"metadata": map[string]interface{}{
602	+			"id":            newID,
603	+			"title":         title,
604	+			"created_at":    now,
605	+			"updated_at":    now,
606	+			"message_count": len(messages),
607	+			"total_tokens":  0,
608	+			"model":         "",
609	+			"workspace":     cwd,
610	+			"mode":          "agent",
611	+		},
612	+		"messages": messages,
613	+	}
614	+
615	+	var buf bytes.Buffer
616	+	enc := json.NewEncoder(&buf)
617	+	enc.SetEscapeHTML(false)
618	+	enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
619	+	if err := enc.Encode(session); err != nil {
620	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to encode deepseek session: %w", err)
621	+	}
622	+
623	+	return &spi.ReconstructedSession{
624	+		SessionID: newID,
625	+		Filename:  newID + ".json",
626	+		Content:   buf.Bytes(),
627	+	}, nil
628	+}
629	+
630	+// NativeSessionPath returns where a reconstructed session belongs in DeepSeek's
631	+// store: ~/.deepseek/sessions/<filename>. DeepSeek sessions are not project-scoped
632	+// by directory (the project is recorded in metadata.workspace), so projectPath is
633	+// unused. The directory is not required to exist (the caller creates it).
634	+func (p *Provider) NativeSessionPath(projectPath string, filename string) (string, error) {
635	+	dir, err := resolveSessionsDir()
636	+	if err != nil {
637	+		return "", err
638	+	}
639	+	return filepath.Join(dir, filename), nil
640	+}
641	diff --git a/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/deepseektui/reconstruct_test.go b/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/deepseektui/reconstruct_test.go
642	new file mode 100644
643	index 0000000..93bc149
644	--- /dev/null
645	+++ b/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/deepseektui/reconstruct_test.go
646	@@ -0,0 +1,97 @@
647	+package deepseektui
648	+
649	+import (
650	+	"encoding/json"
651	+	"path/filepath"
652	+	"strings"
653	+	"testing"
654	+
655	+	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
656	+	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
657	+)
658	+
659	+func strptr(s string) *string { return &s }
660	+
661	+func reconstructSampleData() *schema.SessionData {
662	+	fm := "Ran `ls`:\n\n```\nfile.txt\n```"
663	+	return &schema.SessionData{
664	+		SchemaVersion: "1.0",
665	+		Provider:      schema.ProviderInfo{ID: "deepseek-tui", Name: "DeepSeek TUI", Version: "x"},
666	+		SessionID:     "orig-deepseek-123",
667	+		CreatedAt:     "2026-06-17T10:00:00.000Z",
668	+		WorkspaceRoot: "/tmp/proj",
669	+		Exchanges: []schema.Exchange{
670	+			{
671	+				ExchangeID: "orig-deepseek-123:0",
672	+				Messages: []schema.Message{
673	+					{Role: schema.RoleUser, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "List the files here."}}},
674	+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "Listing files."}}},
675	+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeThinking, Text: "Use ls."}}},
676	+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Tool: &schema.ToolInfo{Name: "shell", Type: schema.ToolTypeShell, FormattedMarkdown: strptr(fm)}},
677	+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "There is one file."}}},
678	+				},
679	+			},
680	+		},
681	+	}
682	+}
683	+
684	+// TestReconstructSession_RoundTrip reconstructs to native DeepSeek JSON, re-parses
685	+// it through the forward path, and asserts the flattened transcript is preserved.
686	+func TestReconstructSession_RoundTrip(t *testing.T) {
687	+	data := reconstructSampleData()
688	+	expected := spi.FlattenSessionData(data, "")
689	+
690	+	out, err := NewProvider().ReconstructSession(data, spi.ReconstructOptions{WorkspaceRoot: "/tmp/proj"})
691	+	if err != nil {
692	+		t.Fatalf("ReconstructSession: %v", err)
693	+	}
694	+	if out.SessionID == "" || !strings.HasSuffix(out.Filename, ".json") {
695	+		t.Fatalf("unexpected id/filename: %q / %q", out.SessionID, out.Filename)
696	+	}
697	+
698	+	var session dsSession
699	+	if err := json.Unmarshal(out.Content, &session); err != nil {
700	+		t.Fatalf("reconstructed content is not valid dsSession JSON: %v", err)
701	+	}
702	+	if session.Metadata.ID != out.SessionID {
703	+		t.Errorf("metadata.id = %q, want %q", session.Metadata.ID, out.SessionID)
704	+	}
705	+	if session.Metadata.Workspace != "/tmp/proj" {
706	+		t.Errorf("metadata.workspace = %q, want /tmp/proj", session.Metadata.Workspace)
707	+	}
708	+
709	+	regenerated, err := generateAgentSession(&session, "/tmp/proj")
710	+	if err != nil {
711	+		t.Fatalf("generateAgentSession: %v", err)
712	+	}
713	+
714	+	actual := spi.FlattenSessionData(regenerated, "")
715	+	if len(actual) != len(expected) {
716	+		t.Fatalf("round-trip produced %d turns, want %d\n got: %+v\nwant: %+v", len(actual), len(expected), actual, expected)
717	+	}
718	+	for i := range expected {
719	+		if actual[i] != expected[i] {
720	+			t.Errorf("turn %d mismatch:\n got: %+v\nwant: %+v", i, actual[i], expected[i])
721	+		}
722	+	}
723	+}
724	+
725	+func TestNativeSessionPath(t *testing.T) {
726	+	path, err := NewProvider().NativeSessionPath("/tmp/whatever", "abc.json")
727	+	if err != nil {
728	+		t.Fatalf("NativeSessionPath: %v", err)
729	+	}
730	+	if !strings.Contains(path, filepath.Join(".deepseek", "sessions")) {
731	+		t.Errorf("path %q should be under .deepseek/sessions", path)
732	+	}
733	+	if !strings.HasSuffix(path, "abc.json") {
734	+		t.Errorf("path %q should end with the filename", path)
735	+	}
736	+}
737	+
738	+func TestReconstructSession_Empty(t *testing.T) {
739	+	_, err := NewProvider().ReconstructSession(&schema.SessionData{SessionID: "x", WorkspaceRoot: "/tmp"}, spi.ReconstructOptions{})
740	+	if err == nil {
741	+		t.Fatal("expected error reconstructing a session with no content")
742	+	}
743	+}
744	diff --git a/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/droidcli/provider.go b/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/droidcli/provider.go
745	index 6cd55a3..6c63683 100644
746	--- a/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/droidcli/provider.go
747	+++ b/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/droidcli/provider.go
748	@@ -580,3 +580,38 @@ func generateDroidSessionName(session *fdSession) string {
749	 	// No suitable content found
750	 	return ""
751	 }
752	+
753	+// ListAllAgentChatSessions enumerates every Factory Droid session across all projects
754	+// in ~/.factory/sessions/. The originating cwd is read from inside each session
755	+// (parseFactorySession resolves it into WorkspaceRoot, preferring the recorded cwd).
756	+// See docs/SESSIONS-DB.md.
757	+func (p *Provider) ListAllAgentChatSessions() ([]spi.GlobalSessionRef, error) {
758	+	files, err := listSessionFiles()
759	+	if err != nil {
760	+		if os.IsNotExist(err) {
761	+			return []spi.GlobalSessionRef{}, nil
762	+		}
763	+		return nil, err
764	+	}
765	+
766	+	var refs []spi.GlobalSessionRef
767	+	for _, file := range files {
768	+		session, err := parseFactorySession(file.Path)
769	+		if err != nil {
770	+			slog.Debug("reindex: failed to parse droid session", "path", file.Path, "error", err)
771	+			continue
772	+		}
773	+		if session == nil || len(session.Blocks) == 0 {
774	+			continue // empty session
775	+		}
776	+		refs = append(refs, spi.GlobalSessionRef{
777	+			SessionID:  session.ID,
778	+			CreatedAt:  session.CreatedAt,
779	+			Slug:       session.Slug,
780	+			Name:       generateDroidSessionName(session),
781	+			NativePath: file.Path,
782	+			OriginCwd:  strings.TrimSpace(session.WorkspaceRoot),
783	+		})
784	+	}
785	+	return refs, nil
786	+}
787	diff --git a/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/droidcli/reconstruct.go b/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/droidcli/reconstruct.go
788	new file mode 100644
789	index 0000000..a9a32b0
790	--- /dev/null
791	+++ b/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/droidcli/reconstruct.go
792	@@ -0,0 +1,129 @@
793	+package droidcli
794	+
795	+import (
796	+	"bytes"
797	+	"encoding/json"
798	+	"fmt"
799	+	"os"
800	+	"path/filepath"
801	+	"strings"
802	+	"time"
803	+
804	+	"github.com/google/uuid"
805	+
806	+	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
807	+	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
808	+)
809	+
810	+// droidTimestamp formats a time the way Factory Droid records do (RFC3339 millis, Z).
811	+func droidTimestamp(t time.Time) string {
812	+	return t.Format("2006-01-02T15:04:05.000Z")
813	+}
814	+
815	+// ReconstructSession rebuilds a Factory Droid native JSONL session from the neutral
816	+// SessionData so `droid --resume <id>` can continue the conversation.
817	+//
818	+// Droid stores a session as a `session_start` header followed by `parentId`-chained
819	+// `message` records, each `{message:{role, content:[{type:"text",text}]}}`. Tool
820	+// calls and thinking are already flattened into agent text by FlattenSessionData,
821	+// so reconstruction emits only `user`/`assistant` text messages. The store is
822	+// project-scoped (see NativeSessionPath). See docs/SESSION-PORTABILITY.md.
823	+func (p *Provider) ReconstructSession(data *schema.SessionData, opts spi.ReconstructOptions) (*spi.ReconstructedSession, error) {
824	+	if data == nil {
825	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("cannot reconstruct nil session data")
826	+	}
827	+
828	+	cwd := opts.WorkspaceRoot
829	+	if cwd == "" {
830	+		cwd = data.WorkspaceRoot
831	+	}
832	+
833	+	turns := spi.FlattenSessionData(data, opts.MigrationNote)
834	+	if len(turns) == 0 {
835	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("session has no content to reconstruct")
836	+	}
837	+
838	+	newID := uuid.NewString()
839	+	base := time.Now().UTC()
840	+
841	+	title := strings.TrimSpace(data.Slug)
842	+	if title == "" {
843	+		title = "Resumed session"
844	+	}
845	+
846	+	var buf bytes.Buffer
847	+	enc := json.NewEncoder(&buf)
848	+	enc.SetEscapeHTML(false)
849	+
850	+	// session_start header (also carries provenance).
851	+	if err := enc.Encode(map[string]interface{}{
852	+		"type":                      "session_start",
853	+		"id":                        newID,
854	+		"title":                     title,
855	+		"sessionTitle":              title,
856	+		"owner":                     os.Getenv("USER"),
857	+		"version":                   2,
858	+		"cwd":                       cwd,
859	+		"isSessionTitleManuallySet": false,
860	+		"sessionTitleAutoStage":     "first_message",
861	+		"specstorySourceSessionId":  data.SessionID,
862	+	}); err != nil {
863	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to encode session_start: %w", err)
864	+	}
865	+
866	+	var prevID string
867	+	for i, turn := range turns {
868	+		msgID := uuid.NewString()
869	+		role := "assistant"
870	+		if turn.Role == schema.RoleUser {
871	+			role = "user"
872	+		}
873	+
874	+		rec := map[string]interface{}{
875	+			"type":      "message",
876	+			"id":        msgID,
877	+			"timestamp": droidTimestamp(base.Add(time.Duration(i) * time.Second)),
878	+			"message": map[string]interface{}{
879	+				"role":    role,
880	+				"content": []map[string]interface{}{{"type": "text", "text": turn.Text}},
881	+			},
882	+		}
883	+		// Linear chain: the first message roots the thread (no parentId).
884	+		if prevID != "" {
885	+			rec["parentId"] = prevID
886	+		}
887	+
888	+		if err := enc.Encode(rec); err != nil {
889	+			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to encode message: %w", err)
890	+		}
891	+		prevID = msgID
892	+	}
893	+
894	+	return &spi.ReconstructedSession{
895	+		SessionID: newID,
896	+		Filename:  newID + ".jsonl",
897	+		Content:   buf.Bytes(),
898	+	}, nil
899	+}
900	+
901	+// NativeSessionPath returns where a reconstructed session belongs in Droid's store:
902	+// ~/.factory/sessions/<cwd-encoded>/<filename>. The directory is not required to
903	+// exist (the caller creates it).
904	+func (p *Provider) NativeSessionPath(projectPath string, filename string) (string, error) {
905	+	if projectPath == "" {
906	+		var err error
907	+		projectPath, err = os.Getwd()
908	+		if err != nil {
909	+			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get current working directory: %w", err)
910	+		}
911	+	}
912	+
913	+	dir, err := resolveProjectSessionDir(projectPath)
914	+	if err != nil {
915	+		return "", err
916	+	}
917	+	if dir == "" {
918	+		return "", fmt.Errorf("droidcli: cannot resolve session directory for project path")
919	+	}
920	+	return filepath.Join(dir, filename), nil
921	+}
922	diff --git a/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/droidcli/reconstruct_test.go b/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/droidcli/reconstruct_test.go
923	new file mode 100644
924	index 0000000..8bcc522
925	--- /dev/null
926	+++ b/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/droidcli/reconstruct_test.go
927	@@ -0,0 +1,97 @@
928	+package droidcli
929	+
930	+import (
931	+	"os"
932	+	"path/filepath"
933	+	"strings"
934	+	"testing"
935	+
936	+	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
937	+	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
938	+)
939	+
940	+func strptr(s string) *string { return &s }
941	+
942	+func reconstructSampleData() *schema.SessionData {
943	+	fm := "Wrote `hello.c`:\n\n```c\n#include <stdio.h>\nint main(void){ printf(\"Hello, World!\\n\"); }\n```"
944	+	return &schema.SessionData{
945	+		SchemaVersion: "1.0",
946	+		Provider:      schema.ProviderInfo{ID: "droid-cli", Name: "Factory Droid", Version: "x"},
947	+		SessionID:     "orig-droid-123",
948	+		CreatedAt:     "2026-06-17T10:00:00.000Z",
949	+		WorkspaceRoot: "/tmp/proj",
950	+		Exchanges: []schema.Exchange{
951	+			{
952	+				ExchangeID: "orig-droid-123:0",
953	+				Messages: []schema.Message{
954	+					{Role: schema.RoleUser, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "Create a hello world in C."}}},
955	+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "I'll create it."}}},
956	+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeThinking, Text: "Need a main function."}}},
957	+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Tool: &schema.ToolInfo{Name: "Create", Type: schema.ToolTypeWrite, FormattedMarkdown: strptr(fm)}},
958	+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "Done."}}},
959	+				},
960	+			},
961	+		},
962	+	}
963	+}
964	+
965	+// TestReconstructSession_RoundTrip reconstructs to native Droid JSONL, re-parses it
966	+// through the forward path, and asserts the flattened transcript is preserved.
967	+func TestReconstructSession_RoundTrip(t *testing.T) {
968	+	data := reconstructSampleData()
969	+	expected := spi.FlattenSessionData(data, "")
970	+
971	+	out, err := NewProvider().ReconstructSession(data, spi.ReconstructOptions{WorkspaceRoot: "/tmp/proj"})
972	+	if err != nil {
973	+		t.Fatalf("ReconstructSession: %v", err)
974	+	}
975	+	if out.SessionID == "" || !strings.HasSuffix(out.Filename, ".jsonl") {
976	+		t.Fatalf("unexpected id/filename: %q / %q", out.SessionID, out.Filename)
977	+	}
978	+
979	+	tmp := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), out.Filename)
980	+	if err := os.WriteFile(tmp, out.Content, 0o644); err != nil {
981	+		t.Fatalf("write temp: %v", err)
982	+	}
983	+	session, err := parseFactorySession(tmp)
984	+	if err != nil {
985	+		t.Fatalf("parseFactorySession: %v", err)
986	+	}
987	+	regenerated, err := GenerateAgentSession(session, "/tmp/proj")
988	+	if err != nil {
989	+		t.Fatalf("GenerateAgentSession: %v", err)
990	+	}
991	+
992	+	actual := spi.FlattenSessionData(regenerated, "")
993	+	if len(actual) != len(expected) {
994	+		t.Fatalf("round-trip produced %d turns, want %d\n got: %+v\nwant: %+v", len(actual), len(expected), actual, expected)
995	+	}
996	+	for i := range expected {
997	+		if actual[i] != expected[i] {
998	+			t.Errorf("turn %d mismatch:\n got: %+v\nwant: %+v", i, actual[i], expected[i])
999	+		}
1000	+	}
1001	+}
1002	+
1003	+func TestNativeSessionPath(t *testing.T) {
1004	+	path, err := NewProvider().NativeSessionPath("/tmp/some-droid-proj", "abc.jsonl")
1005	+	if err != nil {
1006	+		t.Fatalf("NativeSessionPath: %v", err)
1007	+	}
1008	+	if !strings.Contains(path, filepath.Join(".factory", "sessions")) {
1009	+		t.Errorf("path %q should be under .factory/sessions", path)
1010	+	}
1011	+	if !strings.HasSuffix(path, "abc.jsonl") {
1012	+		t.Errorf("path %q should end with the filename", path)
1013	+	}
1014	+	if !strings.Contains(path, "-tmp-some-droid-proj") {
1015	+		t.Errorf("path %q should contain the encoded project dir", path)
1016	+	}
1017	+}
1018	+
1019	+func TestReconstructSession_Empty(t *testing.T) {
1020	+	_, err := NewProvider().ReconstructSession(&schema.SessionData{SessionID: "x", WorkspaceRoot: "/tmp"}, spi.ReconstructOptions{})
1021	+	if err == nil {
1022	+		t.Fatal("expected error reconstructing a session with no content")
1023	+	}
1024	+}
1025	diff --git a/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/geminicli/provider.go b/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/geminicli/provider.go
1026	index a60bd0e..d726b1c 100644
1027	--- a/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/geminicli/provider.go
1028	+++ b/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/geminicli/provider.go
1029	@@ -416,3 +416,59 @@ func extractGeminiSessionMetadata(session *GeminiSession) *spi.SessionMetadata {
1030	 		Name:      name,
1031	 	}
1032	 }
1033	+
1034	+// ListAllAgentChatSessions enumerates every session in this provider's native store,
1035	+// regardless of project. See docs/SESSIONS-DB.md.
1036	+//
1037	+// Gemini associates a tmp directory (~/.gemini/tmp/<hash>/) with a project via a
1038	+// .project_root marker file holding the project path, which is the originating cwd
1039	+// for every session in that directory.
1040	+func (p *Provider) ListAllAgentChatSessions() ([]spi.GlobalSessionRef, error) {
1041	+	tmpDir, err := GetGeminiTmpDir()
1042	+	if err != nil {
1043	+		return nil, err
1044	+	}
1045	+	entries, err := os.ReadDir(tmpDir)
1046	+	if err != nil {
1047	+		if os.IsNotExist(err) {
1048	+			return []spi.GlobalSessionRef{}, nil
1049	+		}
1050	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to read Gemini tmp directory: %w", err)
1051	+	}
1052	+
1053	+	var refs []spi.GlobalSessionRef
1054	+	for _, entry := range entries {
1055	+		if !entry.IsDir() {
1056	+			continue
1057	+		}
1058	+		dir := filepath.Join(tmpDir, entry.Name())
1059	+
1060	+		// The .project_root marker holds the project path — the originating cwd shared
1061	+		// by every session in this tmp directory.
1062	+		var cwd string
1063	+		if content, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(dir, ".project_root")); err == nil {
1064	+			cwd = strings.TrimSpace(string(content))
1065	+		}
1066	+
1067	+		sessions, err := FindSessions(dir)
1068	+		if err != nil {
1069	+			slog.Debug("reindex: failed to find gemini sessions", "dir", dir, "error", err)
1070	+			continue
1071	+		}
1072	+		for _, session := range sessions {
1073	+			metadata := extractGeminiSessionMetadata(session)
1074	+			if metadata == nil {
1075	+				continue // empty session
1076	+			}
1077	+			refs = append(refs, spi.GlobalSessionRef{
1078	+				SessionID:  metadata.SessionID,
1079	+				CreatedAt:  metadata.CreatedAt,
1080	+				Slug:       metadata.Slug,
1081	+				Name:       metadata.Name,
1082	+				NativePath: session.FilePath,
1083	+				OriginCwd:  cwd,
1084	+			})
1085	+		}
1086	+	}
1087	+	return refs, nil
1088	+}
1089	diff --git a/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/geminicli/reconstruct.go b/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/geminicli/reconstruct.go
1090	new file mode 100644
1091	index 0000000..97537e3
1092	--- /dev/null
1093	+++ b/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/geminicli/reconstruct.go
1094	@@ -0,0 +1,158 @@
1095	+package geminicli
1096	+
1097	+import (
1098	+	"bytes"
1099	+	"encoding/json"
1100	+	"fmt"
1101	+	"os"
1102	+	"path/filepath"
1103	+	"time"
1104	+
1105	+	"github.com/google/uuid"
1106	+
1107	+	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
1108	+	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
1109	+)
1110	+
1111	+// geminiTimestamp formats a time the way Gemini chat records do (RFC3339 millis, Z).
1112	+func geminiTimestamp(t time.Time) string {
1113	+	return t.Format("2006-01-02T15:04:05.000Z")
1114	+}
1115	+
1116	+// ReconstructSession rebuilds a Gemini CLI native chat session from the neutral
1117	+// SessionData so `gemini --resume <id>` can continue the conversation.
1118	+//
1119	+// Gemini stores a session as a single JSON document: a top-level object with
1120	+// `sessionId`/`projectHash`/`startTime`/`lastUpdated`/`kind` and a `messages`
1121	+// array. Each message is `{id, timestamp, type, content}` where a user message's
1122	+// content is `[{text}]` and a gemini message's content is a plain string. Tool
1123	+// calls and thinking are already flattened into agent text by FlattenSessionData,
1124	+// so reconstruction emits only `user`/`gemini` text messages. See
1125	+// docs/SESSION-PORTABILITY.md.
1126	+func (p *Provider) ReconstructSession(data *schema.SessionData, opts spi.ReconstructOptions) (*spi.ReconstructedSession, error) {
1127	+	if data == nil {
1128	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("cannot reconstruct nil session data")
1129	+	}
1130	+
1131	+	cwd := opts.WorkspaceRoot
1132	+	if cwd == "" {
1133	+		cwd = data.WorkspaceRoot
1134	+	}
1135	+
1136	+	turns := spi.FlattenSessionData(data, opts.MigrationNote)
1137	+	if len(turns) == 0 {
1138	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("session has no content to reconstruct")
1139	+	}
1140	+
1141	+	newID := uuid.NewString()
1142	+	base := time.Now().UTC()
1143	+
1144	+	// Best-effort project hash; Gemini stores it but resume keys off the project dir.
1145	+	projectHash, _ := HashProjectPath(cwd)
1146	+
1147	+	messages := make([]map[string]interface{}, 0, len(turns))
1148	+	for i, turn := range turns {
1149	+		msg := map[string]interface{}{
1150	+			"id":        uuid.NewString(),
1151	+			"timestamp": geminiTimestamp(base.Add(time.Duration(i) * time.Second)),
1152	+		}
1153	+		if turn.Role == schema.RoleUser {
1154	+			msg["type"] = "user"
1155	+			msg["content"] = []map[string]interface{}{{"text": turn.Text}}
1156	+		} else {
1157	+			msg["type"] = "gemini"
1158	+			msg["content"] = turn.Text
1159	+		}
1160	+		messages = append(messages, msg)
1161	+	}
1162	+
1163	+	session := map[string]interface{}{
1164	+		"sessionId":   newID,
1165	+		"projectHash": projectHash,
1166	+		"startTime":   geminiTimestamp(base),
1167	+		"lastUpdated": geminiTimestamp(base.Add(time.Duration(len(turns)) * time.Second)),
1168	+		"kind":        "main",
1169	+		"messages":    messages,
1170	+	}
1171	+
1172	+	var buf bytes.Buffer
1173	+	enc := json.NewEncoder(&buf)
1174	+	enc.SetEscapeHTML(false)
1175	+	enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
1176	+	if err := enc.Encode(session); err != nil {
1177	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to encode gemini session: %w", err)
1178	+	}
1179	+
1180	+	// Filename: session-<YYYY-MM-DDTHH-MM>-<first 8 of id>.json (Gemini convention).
1181	+	filename := fmt.Sprintf("session-%s-%s.json", base.Format("2006-01-02T15-04"), newID[:8])
1182	+
1183	+	return &spi.ReconstructedSession{
1184	+		SessionID: newID,
1185	+		Filename:  filename,
1186	+		Content:   buf.Bytes(),
1187	+	}, nil
1188	+}
1189	+
1190	+// NativeSessionPath returns where a reconstructed chat belongs in Gemini's store
1191	+// and, unlike the Claude/Codex resolvers, prepares the project directory: Gemini
1192	+// associates a tmp dir with a project via a `.project_root` marker, so for a
1193	+// project Gemini has never seen we create `~/.gemini/tmp/<name>/` with that marker
1194	+// and a `chats/` subdir. Returns `<projectDir>/chats/<filename>`.
1195	+func (p *Provider) NativeSessionPath(projectPath string, filename string) (string, error) {
1196	+	if projectPath == "" {
1197	+		var err error
1198	+		projectPath, err = osGetwd()
1199	+		if err != nil {
1200	+			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get current working directory: %w", err)
1201	+		}
1202	+	}
1203	+
1204	+	// Reuse the project dir Gemini already associates with this project, if any.
1205	+	dir, err := ResolveGeminiProjectDir(projectPath)
1206	+	if err != nil {
1207	+		dir, err = prepareGeminiProjectDir(projectPath)
1208	+		if err != nil {
1209	+			return "", err
1210	+		}
1211	+	}
1212	+
1213	+	return filepath.Join(dir, "chats", filename), nil
1214	+}
1215	+
1216	+// prepareGeminiProjectDir creates a Gemini project directory for a project that
1217	+// Gemini has not seen yet: ~/.gemini/tmp/<basename>/ (falling back to the hash dir
1218	+// if that basename is taken by a different project), with a `.project_root` marker
1219	+// and a `chats/` subdirectory.
1220	+func prepareGeminiProjectDir(projectPath string) (string, error) {
1221	+	tmpDir, err := GetGeminiTmpDir()
1222	+	if err != nil {
1223	+		return "", err
1224	+	}
1225	+	if err := os.MkdirAll(tmpDir, 0o755); err != nil {
1226	+		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to create gemini tmp dir: %w", err)
1227	+	}
1228	+
1229	+	canonical := canonicalizeProjectPath(projectPath)
1230	+	dir := filepath.Join(tmpDir, filepath.Base(projectPath))
1231	+
1232	+	// If the basename dir exists but belongs to a different project, key off the hash instead.
1233	+	if info, statErr := osStat(dir); statErr == nil && info.IsDir() && !matchesProjectRoot(dir, canonical) {
1234	+		if dir, err = GetGeminiProjectDir(projectPath); err != nil {
1235	+			return "", err
1236	+		}
1237	+	}
1238	+
1239	+	if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(dir, "chats"), 0o755); err != nil {
1240	+		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to create gemini chats dir: %w", err)
1241	+	}
1242	+
1243	+	// Write the project-root marker so Gemini maps this dir to the project on resume.
1244	+	rootFile := filepath.Join(dir, ".project_root")
1245	+	if _, statErr := osStat(rootFile); os.IsNotExist(statErr) {
1246	+		if err := os.WriteFile(rootFile, []byte(canonical), 0o644); err != nil {
1247	+			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to write gemini .project_root: %w", err)
1248	+		}
1249	+	}
1250	+
1251	+	return dir, nil
1252	+}

1258	@@ -0,0 +1,117 @@
1259	+package geminicli
1260	+
1261	+import (
1262	+	"encoding/json"
1263	+	"os"
1264	+	"path/filepath"
1265	+	"strings"
1266	+	"testing"
1267	+
1268	+	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
1269	+	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
1270	+)
1271	+
1272	+func strptr(s string) *string { return &s }
1273	+
1274	+func reconstructSampleData() *schema.SessionData {
1275	+	fm := "Wrote `hello.fs`:\n\n```forth\n.\" Hello, World!\" CR\n```"
1276	+	return &schema.SessionData{
1277	+		SchemaVersion: "1.0",
1278	+		Provider:      schema.ProviderInfo{ID: "gemini-cli", Name: "Gemini CLI", Version: "x"},
1279	+		SessionID:     "orig-gemini-123",
1280	+		CreatedAt:     "2026-06-17T10:00:00.000Z",
1281	+		WorkspaceRoot: "/tmp/proj",
1282	+		Exchanges: []schema.Exchange{
1283	+			{
1284	+				ExchangeID: "orig-gemini-123:0",
1285	+				Messages: []schema.Message{
1286	+					{Role: schema.RoleUser, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "Write hello world in Forth."}}},
1287	+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "I'll create hello.fs."}}},
1288	+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeThinking, Text: "Forth uses dot-quote for strings."}}},
1289	+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Tool: &schema.ToolInfo{Name: "write_file", Type: schema.ToolTypeWrite, FormattedMarkdown: strptr(fm)}},
1290	+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "Created hello.fs."}}},
1291	+				},
1292	+			},
1293	+		},
1294	+	}
1295	+}
1296	+
1297	+// TestReconstructSession_RoundTrip reconstructs to native Gemini chat JSON, re-parses
1298	+// it through the forward path, and asserts the flattened transcript is preserved.
1299	+func TestReconstructSession_RoundTrip(t *testing.T) {
1300	+	data := reconstructSampleData()
1301	+	expected := spi.FlattenSessionData(data, "")
1302	+
1303	+	out, err := NewProvider().ReconstructSession(data, spi.ReconstructOptions{WorkspaceRoot: "/tmp/proj"})
1304	+	if err != nil {
1305	+		t.Fatalf("ReconstructSession: %v", err)
1306	+	}
1307	+	if out.SessionID == "" {
1308	+		t.Fatal("expected a fresh session ID")
1309	+	}
1310	+	if !strings.HasPrefix(out.Filename, "session-") || !strings.HasSuffix(out.Filename, ".json") {
1311	+		t.Errorf("filename %q should look like session-<ts>-<id>.json", out.Filename)
1312	+	}
1313	+
1314	+	var session GeminiSession
1315	+	if err := json.Unmarshal(out.Content, &session); err != nil {
1316	+		t.Fatalf("reconstructed content is not valid GeminiSession JSON: %v", err)
1317	+	}
1318	+	if session.ID != out.SessionID {
1319	+		t.Errorf("session sessionId = %q, want %q", session.ID, out.SessionID)
1320	+	}
1321	+
1322	+	regenerated, err := GenerateAgentSession(&session, "/tmp/proj")
1323	+	if err != nil {
1324	+		t.Fatalf("GenerateAgentSession: %v", err)
1325	+	}
1326	+
1327	+	actual := spi.FlattenSessionData(regenerated, "")
1328	+	if len(actual) != len(expected) {
1329	+		t.Fatalf("round-trip produced %d turns, want %d\n got: %+v\nwant: %+v", len(actual), len(expected), actual, expected)
1330	+	}
1331	+	for i := range expected {
1332	+		if actual[i] != expected[i] {
1333	+			t.Errorf("turn %d mismatch:\n got: %+v\nwant: %+v", i, actual[i], expected[i])
1334	+		}
1335	+	}
1336	+}
1337	+
1338	+// TestNativeSessionPath_CreatesProjectDir verifies that, for a project Gemini has
1339	+// not seen, NativeSessionPath creates the tmp project dir with a .project_root
1340	+// marker and returns a path under chats/.
1341	+func TestNativeSessionPath_CreatesProjectDir(t *testing.T) {
1342	+	home := t.TempDir()
1343	+	project := t.TempDir()
1344	+
1345	+	originalHome := osUserHomeDir
1346	+	osUserHomeDir = func() (string, error) { return home, nil }
1347	+	t.Cleanup(func() { osUserHomeDir = originalHome })
1348	+
1349	+	path, err := NewProvider().NativeSessionPath(project, "session-x.json")
1350	+	if err != nil {
1351	+		t.Fatalf("NativeSessionPath: %v", err)
1352	+	}
1353	+	if filepath.Base(path) != "session-x.json" {
1354	+		t.Errorf("path %q should end with the filename", path)
1355	+	}
1356	+	if filepath.Base(filepath.Dir(path)) != "chats" {
1357	+		t.Errorf("path %q should be under a chats/ dir", path)
1358	+	}
1359	+	// The .project_root marker should exist alongside chats/ and point at the project.
1360	+	rootFile := filepath.Join(filepath.Dir(filepath.Dir(path)), ".project_root")
1361	+	content, err := os.ReadFile(rootFile)
1362	+	if err != nil {
1363	+		t.Fatalf("expected .project_root marker: %v", err)
1364	+	}
1365	+	if !strings.Contains(string(content), filepath.Base(project)) {
1366	+		t.Errorf(".project_root %q should reference the project path", string(content))
1367	+	}
1368	+}
1369	+
1370	+func TestReconstructSession_Empty(t *testing.T) {
1371	+	_, err := NewProvider().ReconstructSession(&schema.SessionData{SessionID: "x", WorkspaceRoot: "/tmp"}, spi.ReconstructOptions{})
1372	+	if err == nil {
1373	+		t.Fatal("expected error reconstructing a session with no content")
1374	+	}
1375	+}
1376

189	+
190	+// SaveResumePrefs persists the resume view mode and last-resumed agent to the
191	+// user-level config (~/.specstory/cli/config.toml). It upserts ONLY the [resume]
192	+// section, preserving the rest of the file (comments and other sections), so the
193	+// self-documenting template stays intact. Pass an empty value to leave that key out.
194	+func SaveResumePrefs(viewMode, lastAgent string) error {
195	+	path := getUserConfigPath()
196	+	if path == "" {
197	+		return fmt.Errorf("could not determine user config path")
198	+	}
199	+	if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(path), 0o755); err != nil {
200	+		return fmt.Errorf("creating config directory: %w", err)
201	+	}
202	+
203	+	data, err := os.ReadFile(path)
204	+	if err != nil {
205	+		if !os.IsNotExist(err) {
206	+			return fmt.Errorf("reading config: %w", err)
207	+		}
208	+		data = []byte(processTemplate(defaultConfigTemplate, "user"))
209	+	}
210	+
211	+	updated := upsertResumeSection(string(data), viewMode, lastAgent)
212	+	if err := os.WriteFile(path, []byte(updated), 0o644); err != nil {
213	+		return fmt.Errorf("writing config: %w", err)
214	+	}
215	+	return nil
216	+}
217	+
218	+// upsertResumeSection replaces the [resume] section of a TOML document with active
219	+// view_mode/last_agent keys, preserving every other line. If no [resume] section
220	+// exists it is appended. Only the [resume] block is rewritten — other sections and
221	+// their comments are untouched.
222	+func upsertResumeSection(content, viewMode, lastAgent string) string {
223	+	var block strings.Builder
224	+	block.WriteString("[resume]\n")
225	+	if viewMode != "" {
226	+		fmt.Fprintf(&block, "view_mode = %q\n", viewMode)
227	+	}
228	+	if lastAgent != "" {
229	+		fmt.Fprintf(&block, "last_agent = %q\n", lastAgent)
230	+	}
231	+
232	+	lines := strings.Split(content, "\n")
233	+	start := -1
234	+	for i, ln := range lines {
235	+		if strings.TrimSpace(ln) == "[resume]" {
236	+			start = i
237	+			break
238	+		}
239	+	}
240	+
241	+	// No existing [resume] section — append it after a blank-line separator.
242	+	if start == -1 {
243	+		out := strings.TrimRight(content, "\n")
244	+		if out != "" {
245	+			out += "\n\n"
246	+		}
247	+		return out + block.String()
248	+	}
249	+
250	+	// Replace from [resume] up to (but not including) the next top-level section.
251	+	end := len(lines)
252	+	for j := start + 1; j < len(lines); j++ {
253	+		t := strings.TrimSpace(lines[j])
254	+		if strings.HasPrefix(t, "[") && strings.HasSuffix(t, "]") {
255	+			end = j
256	+			break
257	+		}
258	+	}
259	+
260	+	var b strings.Builder
261	+	for i := 0; i < start; i++ {
262	+		b.WriteString(lines[i])
263	+		b.WriteString("\n")
264	+	}
265	+	b.WriteString(block.String())
266	+	for i := end; i < len(lines); i++ {
267	+		b.WriteString(lines[i])
268	+		if i < len(lines)-1 {
269	+			b.WriteString("\n")
270	+		}
271	+	}
272	+	return b.String()
273	+}
274	+
275	 // GetProviderCmd returns the custom execution command for a provider, or empty
276	 // string if none is configured. The providerID should match a registered
277	 // provider ID (e.g., "claude", "codex", "cursor", "droid", "gemini").
278	diff --git a/specstory-cli/pkg/config/config_test.go b/specstory-cli/pkg/config/config_test.go
279	index 405dd6e..6b04a78 100644

282	@@ -1279,3 +1279,52 @@ claude_cmd = "claude --project-level"
283	 		}
284	 	})
285	 }

287	+func TestUpsertResumeSection_AppendWhenAbsent(t *testing.T) {
288	+	in := "[local_sync]\n# enabled = false\n"
289	+	out := upsertResumeSection(in, "sparse", "codex")
290	+	if !strings.Contains(out, "[local_sync]") || !strings.Contains(out, "# enabled = false") {
291	+		t.Errorf("existing section/comments not preserved:\n%s", out)
292	+	}
293	+	if !strings.Contains(out, "[resume]") || !strings.Contains(out, `view_mode = "sparse"`) || !strings.Contains(out, `last_agent = "codex"`) {
294	+		t.Errorf("resume section not appended:\n%s", out)
295	+	}
296	+	var cfg Config
297	+	if _, err := toml.Decode(out, &cfg); err != nil {
298	+		t.Fatalf("result is not valid TOML: %v", err)
299	+	}
300	+	if cfg.GetResumeViewMode() != "sparse" || cfg.GetResumeLastAgent() != "codex" {
301	+		t.Errorf("round-trip mismatch: %+v", cfg.Resume)
302	+	}
303	+}

305	+func TestUpsertResumeSection_ReplacePreservingOthers(t *testing.T) {

307	+	out := upsertResumeSection(in, "sparse", "gemini")
308	+	// The other section and its comment must survive untouched.
309	+	if !strings.Contains(out, "[providers]") || !strings.Contains(out, `# claude_cmd = "claude"`) {
310	+		t.Errorf("trailing section clobbered:\n%s", out)
311	+	}
312	+	// Old resume values replaced, not duplicated.
313	+	if strings.Count(out, "[resume]") != 1 {

315	+	}
316	+	var cfg Config

319	+	}
320	+	if cfg.GetResumeViewMode() != "sparse" || cfg.GetResumeLastAgent() != "gemini" {
321	+		t.Errorf("values not replaced: %+v", cfg.Resume)
322	+	}
323	+}

415	 	}

417	 	// The TypeScript version hashes the workspace URI, but in our case
418	-	// we'll hash the absolute path for compatibility
419	-	return m.createHash(absPath)
420	+	// we'll hash the absolute path for compatibility. The path is canonicalized first so
421	+	// the same physical directory yields one id regardless of the casing a caller passes
422	+	// (os.Getwd may echo a lowercase $PWD; a session's recorded cwd may differ in case).
423	+	return m.createHash(canonicalizeWorkspacePath(absPath))

425	+
426	+// caseInsensitiveFilesystem reports whether the host OS treats paths case-insensitively.
427	+// macOS (APFS/HFS+) and Windows (NTFS) are case-insensitive/case-preserving; Linux is
428	+// case-sensitive. It is a var so tests can exercise both behaviors. Windows is included
429	+// even though the app is built only for macOS/Linux today: the in-flight Windows support
430	+// lives on a branch that does not carry this file, so folding case here keeps that branch
431	+// from silently reintroducing case-divergent workspace ids when it merges.
432	+var caseInsensitiveFilesystem = runtime.GOOS == "darwin" || runtime.GOOS == "windows"
433	+
434	+// canonicalizeWorkspacePath normalizes a path before it is hashed into a workspace_id. On
435	+// a case-insensitive filesystem the same directory can be named with different casing,
436	+// which would otherwise hash to different ids for one physical directory; case-folding
437	+// makes the id stable. On case-sensitive filesystems the path is left byte-exact, since
438	+// there two differently-cased paths are genuinely different directories.
439	+func canonicalizeWorkspacePath(path string) string {
440	+	if caseInsensitiveFilesystem {
441	+		return strings.ToLower(path)
442	+	}
443	+	return path
444	 }

502	+
503	+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
504	+// Walk-up identity resolution (shared by ComputeProjectID and the writer).
505	+//
506	+// The stored .specstory/.project.json fragments a monorepo: it is written per
507	+// launch directory, and generateGitID looks for .git at exactly that directory and
508	+// never walks up — so a session launched from a subdirectory gets no git_id and a
509	+// path-based workspace_id all its own. The resolver below instead walks UP to the
510	+// git root and computes identity from there, so every directory inside one repo
511	+// resolves to a single project. See docs/SESSIONS-DB.md.
512	+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
513	+
514	+// originRemoteURLRegex captures the origin remote's url value from a git config file.
515	+var originRemoteURLRegex = regexp.MustCompile(`\[remote "origin"\][^\[]*url\s*=\s*([^\n\r]+)`)
516	+
517	+// readOriginURL returns the origin remote URL from a git config file, or "" if the
518	+// file is unreadable or has no origin remote.
519	+func readOriginURL(gitConfigPath string) string {
520	+	gitConfig, err := os.ReadFile(gitConfigPath)
521	+	if err != nil {
522	+		return ""
523	+	}
524	+	matches := originRemoteURLRegex.FindSubmatch(gitConfig)
525	+	if len(matches) < 2 {
526	+		return ""
527	+	}
528	+	return strings.TrimSpace(string(matches[1]))
529	+}
530	+
531	+// findGitRoot walks up from dir to the nearest ancestor containing a .git entry and
532	+// returns it. The .git entry may be a directory (normal clone) or a FILE (git
533	+// worktree / submodule); both satisfy the search. Returns false if no .git is found
534	+// before the filesystem root. The walk-up is what lets a session launched from a
535	+// monorepo subdirectory resolve to the repo's identity.
536	+func findGitRoot(dir string) (string, bool) {
537	+	if abs, err := filepath.Abs(dir); err == nil {
538	+		dir = abs
539	+	}
540	+	dir = filepath.Clean(dir)
541	+	for {
542	+		if _, err := os.Stat(filepath.Join(dir, ".git")); err == nil {
543	+			return dir, true
544	+		}
545	+		parent := filepath.Dir(dir)
546	+		if parent == dir {
547	+			return "", false // reached the filesystem root without finding .git
548	+		}
549	+		dir = parent
550	+	}
551	+}
552	+
553	+// resolveGitConfigPath returns the path to the git config file that holds a root's
554	+// remotes, handling both a normal `.git` directory and a `.git` FILE (worktrees /
555	+// submodules, whose private gitdir points at a commondir that holds the shared
556	+// config). Returns "" when it cannot be resolved.
557	+func resolveGitConfigPath(gitRoot string) string {
558	+	gitPath := filepath.Join(gitRoot, ".git")
559	+	info, err := os.Stat(gitPath)
560	+	if err != nil {
561	+		return ""
562	+	}
563	+	if info.IsDir() {
564	+		return filepath.Join(gitPath, "config")
565	+	}
566	+
567	+	// `.git` is a file of the form "gitdir: <path>" pointing at the worktree's private
568	+	// git dir; the shared config lives in the common dir, not that private dir.
569	+	data, err := os.ReadFile(gitPath)
570	+	if err != nil {
571	+		return ""
572	+	}
573	+	content := strings.TrimSpace(string(data))
574	+	if !strings.HasPrefix(content, "gitdir:") {
575	+		return ""
576	+	}
577	+	gitDir := strings.TrimSpace(strings.TrimPrefix(content, "gitdir:"))
578	+	if gitDir == "" {
579	+		return ""
580	+	}
581	+	if !filepath.IsAbs(gitDir) {
582	+		gitDir = filepath.Join(gitRoot, gitDir)
583	+	}
584	+	commonDir := gitDir
585	+	if cd, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(gitDir, "commondir")); err == nil {
586	+		c := strings.TrimSpace(string(cd))
587	+		if !filepath.IsAbs(c) {
588	+			c = filepath.Join(gitDir, c)
589	+		}
590	+		commonDir = filepath.Clean(c)
591	+	}
592	+	return filepath.Join(commonDir, "config")
593	+}
594	+
595	+// resolveIdentity computes a project's identity from a starting directory by walking
596	+// up to the git root. It reads only git metadata — never any .specstory/.project.json
597	+// — so it cannot be fooled by the per-directory identity files that fragment a
598	+// monorepo. Returns the git_id (empty when there is no resolvable origin remote), the
599	+// workspace_id (hash of the resolved root), the project name, and the resolved root.
600	+func resolveIdentity(startDir string) (gitID, workspaceID, name, root string) {
601	+	root = startDir
602	+	if gr, ok := findGitRoot(startDir); ok {
603	+		root = gr
604	+	}
605	+
606	+	// A zero-state manager rooted at the resolved root reuses the existing hashing /
607	+	// normalization / repo-name helpers (they do not depend on manager state).
608	+	rooted := &ProjectIdentityManager{projectRoot: root}
609	+	workspaceID = rooted.generateWorkspaceID()
610	+	if cfg := resolveGitConfigPath(root); cfg != "" {
611	+		if originURL := readOriginURL(cfg); originURL != "" {
612	+			gitID = rooted.createHash(rooted.normalizeGitURL(originURL))
613	+			name = rooted.parseGitRemoteURL(originURL)
614	+		}
615	+	}
616	+	if name == "" {
617	+		name = filepath.Base(root)
618	+	}
619	+	return gitID, workspaceID, name, root
620	+}
621	+
622	+// ComputeProjectID resolves the restore-index project identity for a session's
623	+// working directory. It walks up to the git root and computes identity fresh,
624	+// ignoring any stored .specstory/.project.json (which fragments a monorepo) and
625	+// never writing one. Returns the project id (git_id when a remote is resolvable,
626	+// else the path-based workspace_id) and the project name.
627	+func ComputeProjectID(cwd string) (id, name string, err error) {
628	+	if strings.TrimSpace(cwd) == "" {
629	+		return "", "", fmt.Errorf("cannot resolve project id: empty working directory")
630	+	}
631	+	gitID, workspaceID, name, _ := resolveIdentity(cwd)
632	+
633	+	// Prefer a resolvable git_id: it hashes the normalized remote URL, not a path, so it is
634	+	// immune to the case divergence handled below and never fragments a monorepo.
635	+	if gitID != "" {
636	+		return gitID, name, nil
637	+	}
638	+
639	+	// No git remote → use the path-based workspace_id. Prefer an id already persisted in the
640	+	// launch dir's .project.json so every consumer (reindex, resume, cloud sync) agrees on a
641	+	// single value, even for projects whose file predates path canonicalization. Only when no
642	+	// file exists (e.g. a portable session whose recorded cwd is not present locally) do we
643	+	// fall back to the freshly computed — and now canonical — hash.
644	+	if stored := persistedWorkspaceID(cwd); stored != "" {
645	+		return stored, name, nil
646	+	}
647	+	if workspaceID == "" {
648	+		return "", "", fmt.Errorf("cannot resolve project id for %q", cwd)
649	+	}
650	+	return workspaceID, name, nil
651	+}
652	+
653	+// persistedWorkspaceID returns the workspace_id recorded in dir's .specstory/.project.json,
654	+// or "" when there is no readable file or it carries no workspace_id. The file is read from
655	+// the launch directory (where the writer puts it), not the resolved git root.
656	+func persistedWorkspaceID(dir string) string {
657	+	m := &ProjectIdentityManager{projectRoot: dir}
658	+	identity, err := m.ReadProjectIdentity()
659	+	if err != nil || identity == nil {
660	+		return ""
661	+	}
662	+	return identity.WorkspaceID
663	+}
664	diff --git a/specstory-cli/pkg/utils/project_identity_test.go b/specstory-cli/pkg/utils/project_identity_test.go
665	index 0dd97cd..294060f 100644
666	--- a/specstory-cli/pkg/utils/project_identity_test.go
667	+++ b/specstory-cli/pkg/utils/project_identity_test.go
668	@@ -1064,3 +1064,190 @@ func TestProjectIdentityManager_EnsureProjectIdentity_NoSpecstoryDir(t *testing.
669	 		t.Error("Expected .project.json file to be created")
670	 	}
671	 }
672	+
673	+// TestComputeProjectID is the executable spec for walk-up identity resolution:
674	+// the monorepo-subdir + two-clone scenario, plus worktree and remote-less cases.
675	+func TestComputeProjectID(t *testing.T) {
676	+	// newRepo creates a temp dir with a .git/config carrying the given origin url
677	+	// (empty url = a git repo with no remote).
678	+	newRepo := func(t *testing.T, url string) string {
679	+		t.Helper()
680	+		root := t.TempDir()
681	+		gitDir := filepath.Join(root, ".git")
682	+		if err := os.MkdirAll(gitDir, 0o755); err != nil {
683	+			t.Fatal(err)
684	+		}
685	+		cfg := "[core]\n\tbare = false\n"
686	+		if url != "" {
687	+			cfg += "[remote \"origin\"]\n\turl = " + url + "\n"
688	+		}
689	+		if err := os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(gitDir, "config"), []byte(cfg), 0o644); err != nil {
690	+			t.Fatal(err)
691	+		}
692	+		return root
693	+	}
694	+	mkSub := func(t *testing.T, root, rel string) string {
695	+		t.Helper()
696	+		p := filepath.Join(root, rel)
697	+		if err := os.MkdirAll(p, 0o755); err != nil {
698	+			t.Fatal(err)
699	+		}
700	+		return p
701	+	}
702	+	compute := func(t *testing.T, dir string) string {
703	+		t.Helper()
704	+		id, _, err := ComputeProjectID(dir)
705	+		if err != nil {
706	+			t.Fatalf("ComputeProjectID(%q): %v", dir, err)
707	+		}
708	+		return id
709	+	}
710	+
711	+	t.Run("monorepo subdirs collapse to one git_id", func(t *testing.T) {
712	+		root := newRepo(t, "git@github.com:acme/widgets.git")
713	+		a := mkSub(t, root, "a")
714	+		b := mkSub(t, root, "pkg/b")
715	+
716	+		rootID := compute(t, root)
717	+		_, rootName, _ := ComputeProjectID(root)
718	+		if aID := compute(t, a); aID != rootID {
719	+			t.Errorf("subdir a did not collapse: root=%s a=%s", rootID, aID)
720	+		}
721	+		if bID := compute(t, b); bID != rootID {
722	+			t.Errorf("nested subdir b did not collapse: root=%s b=%s", rootID, bID)
723	+		}
724	+		if rootName != "widgets" {
725	+			t.Errorf("project name = %q, want widgets", rootName)
726	+		}
727	+	})
728	+
729	+	t.Run("two clones share git_id across different paths and url forms", func(t *testing.T) {
730	+		ssh := newRepo(t, "git@github.com:acme/widgets.git")
731	+		https := newRepo(t, "https://github.com/acme/widgets.git")
732	+		if compute(t, ssh) != compute(t, https) {
733	+			t.Errorf("clones diverged: ssh=%s https=%s", compute(t, ssh), compute(t, https))
734	+		}
735	+	})
736	+
737	+	t.Run("worktree (.git file) resolves to the main repo git_id", func(t *testing.T) {
738	+		main := newRepo(t, "git@github.com:acme/widgets.git")
739	+		wtGitDir := filepath.Join(main, ".git", "worktrees", "wt")
740	+		if err := os.MkdirAll(wtGitDir, 0o755); err != nil {
741	+			t.Fatal(err)
742	+		}
743	+		// commondir points back to the shared .git (relative to the private gitdir).
744	+		if err := os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(wtGitDir, "commondir"), []byte("../..\n"), 0o644); err != nil {
745	+			t.Fatal(err)
746	+		}
747	+		wt := t.TempDir()
748	+		if err := os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(wt, ".git"), []byte("gitdir: "+wtGitDir+"\n"), 0o644); err != nil {
749	+			t.Fatal(err)
750	+		}
751	+		if compute(t, wt) != compute(t, main) {
752	+			t.Errorf("worktree did not resolve to main repo: main=%s wt=%s", compute(t, main), compute(t, wt))
753	+		}
754	+	})
755	+
756	+	t.Run("remote-less repo: subdirs collapse, distinct repos differ", func(t *testing.T) {
757	+		root := newRepo(t, "")
758	+		a := mkSub(t, root, "a")
759	+		if compute(t, a) != compute(t, root) {
760	+			t.Errorf("remote-less subdir did not collapse: root=%s a=%s", compute(t, root), compute(t, a))
761	+		}
762	+		if compute(t, newRepo(t, "")) == compute(t, root) {
763	+			t.Error("distinct remote-less repos collided")
764	+		}
765	+	})
766	+
767	+	t.Run("no-git dir yields a stable, non-empty id", func(t *testing.T) {
768	+		d := t.TempDir()
769	+		id := compute(t, d)
770	+		if id == "" {
771	+			t.Error("empty id for no-git dir")
772	+		}
773	+		if id2 := compute(t, d); id != id2 {
774	+			t.Errorf("unstable id: %s vs %s", id, id2)
775	+		}
776	+	})
777	+
778	+	t.Run("persisted workspace_id is preferred over the computed hash", func(t *testing.T) {
779	+		// A no-git dir falls back to the path-based workspace_id; an id already persisted in
780	+		// .project.json must win so reindex/resume/cloud all agree on one value even when the
781	+		// file predates path canonicalization.
782	+		d := t.TempDir()
783	+		specDir := filepath.Join(d, SPECSTORY_DIR)
784	+		if err := os.MkdirAll(specDir, 0o755); err != nil {
785	+			t.Fatal(err)
786	+		}
787	+		const sentinel = "dead-beef-cafe-0001"
788	+		body := `{"workspace_id":"` + sentinel + `","workspace_id_at":"2020-01-01T00:00:00Z"}`
789	+		if err := os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(specDir, PROJECT_JSON_FILE), []byte(body), 0o644); err != nil {
790	+			t.Fatal(err)
791	+		}
792	+		if id := compute(t, d); id != sentinel {
793	+			t.Errorf("expected persisted workspace_id %q, got %q", sentinel, id)
794	+		}
795	+	})
796	+
797	+	t.Run("git_id wins over a persisted workspace_id", func(t *testing.T) {
798	+		root := newRepo(t, "git@github.com:acme/widgets.git")
799	+		specDir := filepath.Join(root, SPECSTORY_DIR)
800	+		if err := os.MkdirAll(specDir, 0o755); err != nil {
801	+			t.Fatal(err)
802	+		}
803	+		if err := os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(specDir, PROJECT_JSON_FILE),
804	+			[]byte(`{"workspace_id":"should-not-be-used"}`), 0o644); err != nil {
805	+			t.Fatal(err)
806	+		}
807	+		if id := compute(t, root); id == "should-not-be-used" {
808	+			t.Error("git_id should win over a persisted workspace_id")
809	+		}
810	+	})
811	+
812	+	t.Run("empty cwd errors", func(t *testing.T) {
813	+		if _, _, err := ComputeProjectID(""); err == nil {
814	+			t.Error("expected error for empty cwd")
815	+		}
816	+	})
817	+}
818	+
819	+// TestCanonicalizeWorkspacePath verifies the case-fold rule: paths are lowercased on
820	+// case-insensitive filesystems (macOS/Windows) and left byte-exact otherwise.
821	+func TestCanonicalizeWorkspacePath(t *testing.T) {
822	+	orig := caseInsensitiveFilesystem
823	+	defer func() { caseInsensitiveFilesystem = orig }()
824	+
825	+	const mixed = "/Users/Sean/Source/Proj"
826	+
827	+	caseInsensitiveFilesystem = true
828	+	if got := canonicalizeWorkspacePath(mixed); got != "/users/sean/source/proj" {
829	+		t.Errorf("case-insensitive: got %q, want lowercased", got)
830	+	}
831	+
832	+	caseInsensitiveFilesystem = false
833	+	if got := canonicalizeWorkspacePath(mixed); got != mixed {
834	+		t.Errorf("case-sensitive: got %q, want byte-exact %q", got, mixed)
835	+	}
836	+}
837	+
838	+// TestWorkspaceIDCaseFolding is the regression test for the reported bug: on a
839	+// case-insensitive filesystem, the same physical directory named with different casing
840	+// (e.g. os.Getwd echoing a lowercase $PWD vs a recorded capital-cased cwd) must hash to
841	+// one workspace_id. On a case-sensitive filesystem the two are genuinely distinct.
842	+func TestWorkspaceIDCaseFolding(t *testing.T) {
843	+	orig := caseInsensitiveFilesystem
844	+	defer func() { caseInsensitiveFilesystem = orig }()
845	+
846	+	upper := &ProjectIdentityManager{projectRoot: "/Users/Sean/Source/Proj"}
847	+	lower := &ProjectIdentityManager{projectRoot: "/users/sean/source/proj"}
848	+
849	+	caseInsensitiveFilesystem = true
850	+	if upper.generateWorkspaceID() != lower.generateWorkspaceID() {
851	+		t.Error("case-insensitive FS: differently-cased paths produced different workspace ids")
852	+	}
853	+
854	+	caseInsensitiveFilesystem = false
855	+	if upper.generateWorkspaceID() == lower.generateWorkspaceID() {
856	+		t.Error("case-sensitive FS: differently-cased paths should yield distinct workspace ids")
857	+	}
858	+}
859
```

1	package utils
2	
3	import (
4		"crypto/sha256"
5		"encoding/json"
6		"fmt"
7		"log/slog"
8		"os"
9		"path/filepath"
10		"regexp"
11		"runtime"
12		"strings"
13		"time"
14	
15		"github.com/google/uuid"
16		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/analytics"
17	)
18	
19	const PROJECT_JSON_FILE = ".project.json"
20	
21	// ProjectIdentity represents the project identity stored in .specstory/.project.json
22	type ProjectIdentity struct {
23		WorkspaceID   string `json:"workspace_id"`
24		WorkspaceIDAt string `json:"workspace_id_at"`
25		GitID         string `json:"git_id,omitempty"`
26		GitIDAt       string `json:"git_id_at,omitempty"`
27		ProjectName   string `json:"project_name,omitempty"`
28	}
29	
30	// ProjectIdentityManager handles project identity operations
31	type ProjectIdentityManager struct {
32		projectRoot string
33	}
34	
35	// NewProjectIdentityManager creates a new project identity manager
36	func NewProjectIdentityManager(projectRoot string) *ProjectIdentityManager {
37		return &ProjectIdentityManager{
38			projectRoot: projectRoot,
39		}
40	}
41	
42	// getProjectJSONPath returns the path to .specstory/.project.json
43	func (m *ProjectIdentityManager) getProjectJSONPath() string {
44		return filepath.Join(m.projectRoot, SPECSTORY_DIR, PROJECT_JSON_FILE)
45	}
46	
47	// EnsureProjectIdentity initializes or updates the project identity
48	// Returns true if the identity was created or modified
49	func (m *ProjectIdentityManager) EnsureProjectIdentity() (bool, error) {
50		slog.Debug("Ensuring project identity", "projectRoot", m.projectRoot)
51	
52		// Ensure .specstory directory exists (create if needed)
53		specstoryDir := filepath.Join(m.projectRoot, SPECSTORY_DIR)
54		if err := os.MkdirAll(specstoryDir, 0755); err != nil {
55			return false, fmt.Errorf("failed to create .specstory directory: %w", err)
56		}
57	
58		// Read existing project identity if it exists
59		projectJSONPath := m.getProjectJSONPath()
60		existingIdentity, err := m.ReadProjectIdentity()
61		if err != nil && !os.IsNotExist(err) {
62			return false, fmt.Errorf("failed to read existing project identity: %w", err)
63		}
64	
65		// Resolve identity by walking up to the git root (NOT the launch directory), so a
66		// session run from a monorepo subdirectory attributes to the repo, not a fragment.
67		// resolvedName always falls back to the root's base name, so it is never empty.
68		resolvedGitID, resolvedWorkspaceID, resolvedName, _ := resolveIdentity(m.projectRoot)
69	
70		// Determine what needs to be done
71		var identity ProjectIdentity
72		isModified := false
73	
74		isNewProject := false
75		if existingIdentity == nil {
76			// Case 1: No .project.json yet
77			slog.Debug("No existing project identity found, creating new identity")
78			identity = ProjectIdentity{
79				WorkspaceID:   resolvedWorkspaceID,
80				WorkspaceIDAt: time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
81			}
82			isModified = true
83			isNewProject = true
84		} else {
85			// Case 2 or 3: .project.json exists
86			identity = *existingIdentity
87	
88			// Check if workspace_id is missing (shouldn't happen, but be defensive)
89			if identity.WorkspaceID == "" {
90				slog.Warn("Project identity file exists but has no workspace_id")
91				identity.WorkspaceID = resolvedWorkspaceID
92				identity.WorkspaceIDAt = time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)
93				isModified = true
94			}
95		}
96	
97		// Check if we need to add git_id
98		if identity.GitID == "" && resolvedGitID != "" {
99			identity.GitID = resolvedGitID
100			identity.GitIDAt = time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)
101			isModified = true
102			slog.Debug("Added git_id to project identity", "git_id", resolvedGitID)
103		}
104	
105		// Check if we need to add project_name
106		if identity.ProjectName == "" {
107			identity.ProjectName = resolvedName
108			isModified = true
109			slog.Debug("Added project_name to project identity", "project_name", resolvedName)
110		}
111	
112		// Write the project identity file if modified
113		if isModified {
114			jsonData, err := json.MarshalIndent(identity, "", "  ")
115			if err != nil {
116				return false, fmt.Errorf("failed to marshal project identity: %w", err)
117			}
118	
119			if err := os.WriteFile(projectJSONPath, jsonData, 0644); err != nil {
120				return false, fmt.Errorf("failed to write project identity: %w", err)
121			}
122	
123			slog.Info("Project identity saved", "path", projectJSONPath, "identity", identity)
124	
125			// Track analytics for new project creation
126			if isNewProject {
127				properties := analytics.Properties{
128					"has_git_id":       identity.GitID != "",
129					"has_workspace_id": identity.WorkspaceID != "",
130					"project_name":     identity.ProjectName,
131				}
132	
133				// Determine the ID type being used
134				if identity.GitID != "" {
135					properties["id_type"] = "git"
136				} else {
137					properties["id_type"] = "workspace"
138				}
139	
140				analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventProjectIdentityCreated, properties)
141			}
142	
143			return true, nil
144		}
145	
146		slog.Debug("Project identity is up-to-date, no changes written")
147		return false, nil
148	}
149	
150	// ReadProjectIdentity reads the project identity from .project.json
151	func (m *ProjectIdentityManager) ReadProjectIdentity() (*ProjectIdentity, error) {
152		projectJSONPath := m.getProjectJSONPath()
153	
154		data, err := os.ReadFile(projectJSONPath)
155		if err != nil {
156			if os.IsNotExist(err) {
157				return nil, err
158			}
159			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to read project identity: %w", err)
160		}
161	
162		// Remove trailing commas before closing braces/brackets to handle malformed JSON
163		// This regex finds commas followed by optional whitespace and a closing brace/bracket
164		cleanedData := regexp.MustCompile(`,\s*([}\]])`).ReplaceAll(data, []byte("$1"))
165	
166		var identity ProjectIdentity
167		if err := json.Unmarshal(cleanedData, &identity); err != nil {
168			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to unmarshal project identity: %w", err)
169		}
170	
171		return &identity, nil
172	}
173	
174	// generateWorkspaceID generates a workspace ID by hashing the full project path
175	func (m *ProjectIdentityManager) generateWorkspaceID() string {
176		// Get absolute path to ensure consistency
177		absPath, err := filepath.Abs(m.projectRoot)
178		if err != nil {
179			slog.Warn("Failed to get absolute path, using relative path", "error", err)
180			absPath = m.projectRoot
181		}
182	
183		// The TypeScript version hashes the workspace URI, but in our case
184		// we'll hash the absolute path for compatibility. The path is canonicalized first so
185		// the same physical directory yields one id regardless of the casing a caller passes
186		// (os.Getwd may echo a lowercase $PWD; a session's recorded cwd may differ in case).
187		return m.createHash(canonicalizeWorkspacePath(absPath))
188	}
189	
190	// caseInsensitiveFilesystem reports whether the host OS treats paths case-insensitively.
191	// macOS (APFS/HFS+) and Windows (NTFS) are case-insensitive/case-preserving; Linux is
192	// case-sensitive. It is a var so tests can exercise both behaviors. Windows is included
193	// even though the app is built only for macOS/Linux today: the in-flight Windows support
194	// lives on a branch that does not carry this file, so folding case here keeps that branch
195	// from silently reintroducing case-divergent workspace ids when it merges.
196	var caseInsensitiveFilesystem = runtime.GOOS == "darwin" || runtime.GOOS == "windows"
197	
198	// canonicalizeWorkspacePath normalizes a path before it is hashed into a workspace_id. On
199	// a case-insensitive filesystem the same directory can be named with different casing,
200	// which would otherwise hash to different ids for one physical directory; case-folding
201	// makes the id stable. On case-sensitive filesystems the path is left byte-exact, since
202	// there two differently-cased paths are genuinely different directories.
203	func canonicalizeWorkspacePath(path string) string {
204		if caseInsensitiveFilesystem {
205			return strings.ToLower(path)
206		}
207		return path
208	}
209	
210	// generateGitID generates a git ID by finding and hashing the git origin URL
211	func (m *ProjectIdentityManager) generateGitID() (string, error) {
212		gitConfigPath := filepath.Join(m.projectRoot, ".git", "config")
213	
214		// Check if git config exists
215		if _, err := os.Stat(gitConfigPath); err != nil {
216			return "", fmt.Errorf("no git config found: %w", err)
217		}
218	
219		// Read the origin remote URL from the config (shared with the walk-up resolver).
220		originURL := readOriginURL(gitConfigPath)
221		if originURL == "" {
222			return "", fmt.Errorf("no origin URL found in git config")
223		}
224	
225		// Normalize git URLs to ensure HTTPS and SSH URLs for the same repo generate the same ID
226		normalizedURL := m.normalizeGitURL(originURL)
227	
228		return m.createHash(normalizedURL), nil
229	}
230	
231	// normalizeGitURL normalizes a git URL to ensure HTTPS and SSH URLs for the same repo generate the same ID
232	// It extracts the host and repository path, normalizing different URL formats to a common form
233	func (m *ProjectIdentityManager) normalizeGitURL(gitURL string) string {
234		// Remove .git suffix if present
235		normalized := strings.TrimSuffix(gitURL, ".git")
236	
237		// Handle SSH format (git@host:owner/repo or user@host:owner/repo)
238		if strings.Contains(normalized, "@") && strings.Contains(normalized, ":") {
239			// Split by @ to get the host part
240			parts := strings.SplitN(normalized, "@", 2)
241			if len(parts) == 2 {
242				// Split by : to separate host from path
243				hostAndPath := strings.SplitN(parts[1], ":", 2)
244				if len(hostAndPath) == 2 {
245					// Return normalized as "host/path"
246					return hostAndPath[0] + "/" + hostAndPath[1]
247				}
248			}
249		}
250	
251		// Handle HTTPS and other protocol URLs (https://host/owner/repo, git://host/owner/repo, etc.)
252		if strings.Contains(normalized, "://") {
253			// Remove the protocol
254			protocolIndex := strings.Index(normalized, "://")
255			if protocolIndex != -1 {
256				// Return everything after the protocol
257				return normalized[protocolIndex+3:]
258			}
259		}
260	
261		// Handle implicit format (host/owner/repo) - already normalized
262		return normalized
263	}
264	
265	// createHash creates a hash from the input string matching the TypeScript implementation
266	func (m *ProjectIdentityManager) createHash(input string) string {
267		hash := sha256.Sum256([]byte(input))
268		fullHash := fmt.Sprintf("%x", hash)
269	
270		// Take first 16 characters and format as UUID-like string
271		truncated := fullHash[:16]
272	
273		// Insert dashes after every 4 characters: xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx
274		formatted := fmt.Sprintf("%s-%s-%s-%s",
275			truncated[0:4],
276			truncated[4:8],
277			truncated[8:12],
278			truncated[12:16])
279	
280		return formatted
281	}
282	
283	// GetWorkspaceID returns the workspace ID from the project identity
284	func (m *ProjectIdentityManager) GetWorkspaceID() (string, error) {
285		identity, err := m.ReadProjectIdentity()
286		if err != nil {
287			return "", err
288		}
289		if identity == nil || identity.WorkspaceID == "" {
290			return "", fmt.Errorf("no workspace ID found")
291		}
292		return identity.WorkspaceID, nil
293	}
294	
295	// GetGitID returns the git ID from the project identity
296	func (m *ProjectIdentityManager) GetGitID() (string, error) {
297		identity, err := m.ReadProjectIdentity()
298		if err != nil {
299			return "", err
300		}
301		if identity == nil || identity.GitID == "" {
302			return "", fmt.Errorf("no git ID found")
303		}
304		return identity.GitID, nil
305	}
306	
307	// GetProjectID returns the git ID if available, otherwise the workspace ID
308	func (m *ProjectIdentityManager) GetProjectID() (string, error) {
309		identity, err := m.ReadProjectIdentity()
310		if err != nil {
311			return "", err
312		}
313		if identity == nil {
314			return "", fmt.Errorf("no project identity found")
315		}
316	
317		// Prefer git_id over workspace_id (like the TypeScript implementation)
318		if identity.GitID != "" {
319			return identity.GitID, nil
320		}
321		if identity.WorkspaceID != "" {
322			return identity.WorkspaceID, nil
323		}
324	
325		return "", fmt.Errorf("no project ID available")
326	}
327	
328	// ValidateUUID checks if a string is a valid UUID format
329	func ValidateUUID(s string) bool {
330		_, err := uuid.Parse(s)
331		return err == nil
332	}
333	
334	// IsValidProjectID checks if a string matches the expected project ID format
335	// Project IDs are 16 hex characters formatted as: xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx
336	func IsValidProjectID(s string) bool {
337		// Match the format: 4 groups of 4 hex characters separated by dashes
338		matched, _ := regexp.MatchString(`^[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}$`, s)
339		return matched
340	}
341	
342	// parseGitRemoteURL parses a git remote URL and returns the repository name
343	func (m *ProjectIdentityManager) parseGitRemoteURL(remoteURL string) string {
344		// Remove .git suffix if present
345		withoutDotGit := strings.TrimSuffix(remoteURL, ".git")
346	
347		var repoName string
348	
349		// Case 1: SSH format (git@github.com:owner/repo)
350		if strings.HasPrefix(withoutDotGit, "git@") {
351			// Split by : and get the part after it
352			parts := strings.Split(withoutDotGit, ":")
353			if len(parts) >= 2 {
354				pathParts := strings.Split(parts[1], "/")
355				if len(pathParts) > 0 {
356					repoName = pathParts[len(pathParts)-1]
357				}
358			}
359		} else if matched := regexp.MustCompile(`^(https?|git|git\+ssh)://`).MatchString(withoutDotGit); matched {
360			// Case 2: HTTP-like protocols (http://, https://, git://, git+ssh://)
361			// Remove the protocol part and extract repo name
362			urlParts := strings.Split(withoutDotGit, "/")
363			if len(urlParts) > 0 {
364				repoName = urlParts[len(urlParts)-1]
365			}
366		} else {
367			// Case 3: Implicit HTTPS (github.com/owner/repo)
368			// Match domain/path pattern
369			if matched := regexp.MustCompile(`^[a-zA-Z0-9.-]+/(.+)`).FindStringSubmatch(withoutDotGit); len(matched) > 1 {
370				pathParts := strings.Split(matched[1], "/")
371				if len(pathParts) > 0 {
372					repoName = pathParts[len(pathParts)-1]
373				}
374			}
375		}
376	
377		return repoName
378	}
379	
380	// generateProjectName generates a project name from git remote or directory name
381	func (m *ProjectIdentityManager) generateProjectName() string {
382		// First, try to get from git remote
383		gitConfigPath := filepath.Join(m.projectRoot, ".git", "config")
384		if originURL := readOriginURL(gitConfigPath); originURL != "" {
385			if repoName := m.parseGitRemoteURL(originURL); repoName != "" {
386				return repoName
387			}
388		}
389	
390		// Fallback: use the last component of the project directory
391		return filepath.Base(m.projectRoot)
392	}
393	
394	// GetProjectName returns the project name from the project identity
395	func (m *ProjectIdentityManager) GetProjectName() (string, error) {
396		identity, err := m.ReadProjectIdentity()
397		if err != nil {
398			return "", err
399		}
400		if identity == nil || identity.ProjectName == "" {
401			return "", fmt.Errorf("no project name found")
402		}
403		return identity.ProjectName, nil
404	}
405	
406	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
407	// Walk-up identity resolution (shared by ComputeProjectID and the writer).
408	//
409	// The stored .specstory/.project.json fragments a monorepo: it is written per
410	// launch directory, and generateGitID looks for .git at exactly that directory and
411	// never walks up — so a session launched from a subdirectory gets no git_id and a
412	// path-based workspace_id all its own. The resolver below instead walks UP to the
413	// git root and computes identity from there, so every directory inside one repo
414	// resolves to a single project. See docs/SESSIONS-DB.md.
415	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
416	
417	// originRemoteURLRegex captures the origin remote's url value from a git config file.
418	var originRemoteURLRegex = regexp.MustCompile(`\[remote "origin"\][^\[]*url\s*=\s*([^\n\r]+)`)
419	
420	// readOriginURL returns the origin remote URL from a git config file, or "" if the
421	// file is unreadable or has no origin remote.
422	func readOriginURL(gitConfigPath string) string {
423		gitConfig, err := os.ReadFile(gitConfigPath)
424		if err != nil {
425			return ""
426		}
427		matches := originRemoteURLRegex.FindSubmatch(gitConfig)
428		if len(matches) < 2 {
429			return ""
430		}
431		return strings.TrimSpace(string(matches[1]))
432	}
433	
434	// findGitRoot walks up from dir to the nearest ancestor containing a .git entry and
435	// returns it. The .git entry may be a directory (normal clone) or a FILE (git
436	// worktree / submodule); both satisfy the search. Returns false if no .git is found
437	// before the filesystem root. The walk-up is what lets a session launched from a
438	// monorepo subdirectory resolve to the repo's identity.
439	func findGitRoot(dir string) (string, bool) {
440		if abs, err := filepath.Abs(dir); err == nil {
441			dir = abs
442		}
443		dir = filepath.Clean(dir)
444		for {
445			if _, err := os.Stat(filepath.Join(dir, ".git")); err == nil {
446				return dir, true
447			}
448			parent := filepath.Dir(dir)
449			if parent == dir {
450				return "", false // reached the filesystem root without finding .git
451			}
452			dir = parent
453		}
454	}
455	
456	// resolveGitConfigPath returns the path to the git config file that holds a root's
457	// remotes, handling both a normal `.git` directory and a `.git` FILE (worktrees /
458	// submodules, whose private gitdir points at a commondir that holds the shared
459	// config). Returns "" when it cannot be resolved.
460	func resolveGitConfigPath(gitRoot string) string {
461		gitPath := filepath.Join(gitRoot, ".git")
462		info, err := os.Stat(gitPath)
463		if err != nil {
464			return ""
465		}
466		if info.IsDir() {
467			return filepath.Join(gitPath, "config")
468		}
469	
470		// `.git` is a file of the form "gitdir: <path>" pointing at the worktree's private
471		// git dir; the shared config lives in the common dir, not that private dir.
472		data, err := os.ReadFile(gitPath)
473		if err != nil {
474			return ""
475		}
476		content := strings.TrimSpace(string(data))
477		if !strings.HasPrefix(content, "gitdir:") {
478			return ""
479		}
480		gitDir := strings.TrimSpace(strings.TrimPrefix(content, "gitdir:"))
481		if gitDir == "" {
482			return ""
483		}
484		if !filepath.IsAbs(gitDir) {
485			gitDir = filepath.Join(gitRoot, gitDir)
486		}
487		commonDir := gitDir
488		if cd, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(gitDir, "commondir")); err == nil {
489			c := strings.TrimSpace(string(cd))
490			if !filepath.IsAbs(c) {
491				c = filepath.Join(gitDir, c)
492			}
493			commonDir = filepath.Clean(c)
494		}
495		return filepath.Join(commonDir, "config")
496	}
497	
498	// resolveIdentity computes a project's identity from a starting directory by walking
499	// up to the git root. It reads only git metadata — never any .specstory/.project.json
500	// — so it cannot be fooled by the per-directory identity files that fragment a
501	// monorepo. Returns the git_id (empty when there is no resolvable origin remote), the
502	// workspace_id (hash of the resolved root), the project name, and the resolved root.
503	func resolveIdentity(startDir string) (gitID, workspaceID, name, root string) {
504		root = startDir
505		if gr, ok := findGitRoot(startDir); ok {
506			root = gr
507		}
508	
509		// A zero-state manager rooted at the resolved root reuses the existing hashing /
510		// normalization / repo-name helpers (they do not depend on manager state).
511		rooted := &ProjectIdentityManager{projectRoot: root}
512		workspaceID = rooted.generateWorkspaceID()
513		if cfg := resolveGitConfigPath(root); cfg != "" {
514			if originURL := readOriginURL(cfg); originURL != "" {
515				gitID = rooted.createHash(rooted.normalizeGitURL(originURL))
516				name = rooted.parseGitRemoteURL(originURL)
517			}
518		}
519		if name == "" {
520			name = filepath.Base(root)
521		}
522		return gitID, workspaceID, name, root
523	}
524	
525	// ComputeProjectID resolves the restore-index project identity for a session's
526	// working directory. It walks up to the git root and computes identity fresh,
527	// ignoring any stored .specstory/.project.json (which fragments a monorepo) and
528	// never writing one. Returns the project id (git_id when a remote is resolvable,
529	// else the path-based workspace_id) and the project name.
530	func ComputeProjectID(cwd string) (id, name string, err error) {
531		if strings.TrimSpace(cwd) == "" {
532			return "", "", fmt.Errorf("cannot resolve project id: empty working directory")
533		}
534		gitID, workspaceID, name, _ := resolveIdentity(cwd)
535	
536		// Prefer a resolvable git_id: it hashes the normalized remote URL, not a path, so it is
537		// immune to the case divergence handled below and never fragments a monorepo.
538		if gitID != "" {
539			return gitID, name, nil
540		}
541	
542		// No git remote → use the path-based workspace_id. Prefer an id already persisted in the
543		// launch dir's .project.json so every consumer (reindex, resume, cloud sync) agrees on a
544		// single value, even for projects whose file predates path canonicalization. Only when no
545		// file exists (e.g. a portable session whose recorded cwd is not present locally) do we
546		// fall back to the freshly computed — and now canonical — hash.
547		if stored := persistedWorkspaceID(cwd); stored != "" {
548			return stored, name, nil
549		}
550		if workspaceID == "" {
551			return "", "", fmt.Errorf("cannot resolve project id for %q", cwd)
552		}
553		return workspaceID, name, nil
554	}
555	
556	// persistedWorkspaceID returns the workspace_id recorded in dir's .specstory/.project.json,
557	// or "" when there is no readable file or it carries no workspace_id. The file is read from
558	// the launch directory (where the writer puts it), not the resolved git root.
559	func persistedWorkspaceID(dir string) string {
560		m := &ProjectIdentityManager{projectRoot: dir}
561		identity, err := m.ReadProjectIdentity()
562		if err != nil || identity == nil {
563			return ""
564		}
565		return identity.WorkspaceID
566	}
567
```
</details></tool-use>
