<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./pkg/providers/cursorcli/reconstruct.go`

```text
1	package cursorcli
2	
3	import (
4		"bytes"
5		"crypto/sha256"
6		"database/sql"
7		"encoding/binary"
8		"encoding/hex"
9		"encoding/json"
10		"fmt"
11		"os"
12		"path/filepath"
13		"time"
14	
15		"github.com/google/uuid"
16	
17		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
18		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
19	)
20	
21	// Cursor stores a session as a SQLite store.db: a content-addressed Merkle-DAG of
22	// blobs where each blob's id == sha256(data). Conversation messages are PLAIN JSON
23	// blobs; small protobuf "index" nodes thread them into the DAG; `meta['0']` carries
24	// the head pointer (`latestRootBlobId`). Tool calls and thinking are already
25	// flattened into agent text by FlattenSessionData, so reconstruction emits only
26	// user/assistant text-message blobs. The structure below (messages + root + a
27	// field-2 metadata subtree of title/empty/preview nodes, with NO system message)
28	// was validated against `cursor-agent --resume`. See docs/SESSION-PORTABILITY.md.
29	
30	// blobID returns the content-addressed id Cursor uses: sha256(data), hex-encoded.
31	func blobID(data []byte) string {
32		sum := sha256.Sum256(data)
33		return hex.EncodeToString(sum[:])
34	}
35	
36	// pbField encodes a protobuf length-delimited field (wire type 2). fieldNum must be < 16.
37	func pbField(fieldNum int, payload []byte) []byte {
38		out := []byte{byte(fieldNum<<3 | 2)}
39		var lenBuf [binary.MaxVarintLen64]byte
40		n := binary.PutUvarint(lenBuf[:], uint64(len(payload)))
41		out = append(out, lenBuf[:n]...)
42		return append(out, payload...)
43	}
44	
45	// pbRef encodes a length-delimited field carrying a 32-byte blob id reference.
46	func pbRef(fieldNum int, idHex string) []byte {
47		raw, _ := hex.DecodeString(idHex)
48		return pbField(fieldNum, raw)
49	}
50	
51	// cursorTextPart / message structs marshal to Cursor's plain-JSON message shape.
52	type cursorTextPart struct {
53		Type string `json:"type"`
54		Text string `json:"text"`
55	}
56	type cursorUserMsg struct {
57		Role    string           `json:"role"`
58		Content []cursorTextPart `json:"content"`
59	}
60	type cursorAsstMsg struct {
61		ID      string           `json:"id"`
62		Role    string           `json:"role"`
63		Content []cursorTextPart `json:"content"`
64	}
65	
66	// marshalCompact JSON-encodes v without HTML escaping and without a trailing newline.
67	func marshalCompact(v interface{}) ([]byte, error) {
68		var buf bytes.Buffer
69		enc := json.NewEncoder(&buf)
70		enc.SetEscapeHTML(false)
71		if err := enc.Encode(v); err != nil {
72			return nil, err
73		}
74		return bytes.TrimRight(buf.Bytes(), "\n"), nil
75	}
76	
77	// ReconstructSession rebuilds a Cursor CLI native store.db from the neutral SessionData.
78	func (p *Provider) ReconstructSession(data *schema.SessionData, opts spi.ReconstructOptions) (*spi.ReconstructedSession, error) {
79		if data == nil {
80			return nil, fmt.Errorf("cannot reconstruct nil session data")
81		}
82	
83		turns := spi.FlattenSessionData(data, opts.MigrationNote)
84		if len(turns) == 0 {
85			return nil, fmt.Errorf("session has no content to reconstruct")
86		}
87	
88		newID := uuid.NewString()
89		name := data.Slug
90		if name == "" {
91			name = "Resumed session"
92		}
93	
94		// blobs accumulates every blob (id -> data); helper interns by content address.
95		blobs := map[string][]byte{}
96		put := func(b []byte) string { id := blobID(b); blobs[id] = b; return id }
97	
98		// 1. Message blobs (plain JSON), in order. No system message.
99		var msgIDs []string
100		var lastAssistantText string
101		asstSeq := 0
102		for _, turn := range turns {
103			var raw []byte
104			var err error
105			if turn.Role == schema.RoleUser {
106				raw, err = marshalCompact(cursorUserMsg{Role: "user", Content: []cursorTextPart{{Type: "text", Text: turn.Text}}})
107			} else {
108				asstSeq++
109				raw, err = marshalCompact(cursorAsstMsg{ID: fmt.Sprintf("%d", asstSeq), Role: "assistant", Content: []cursorTextPart{{Type: "text", Text: turn.Text}}})
110				lastAssistantText = turn.Text
111			}
112			if err != nil {
113				return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to encode message: %w", err)
114			}
115			msgIDs = append(msgIDs, put(raw))
116		}
117	
118		// 2. Field-2 metadata subtree: title leaf, empty placeholder, preview node.
119		titleID := put(append(pbField(1, []byte(name)), pbField(2, []byte(uuid.NewString()))...))
120		emptyID := put(pbField(3, nil))                                     // {f3:""}
121		previewID := put(pbField(1, pbField(1, []byte(lastAssistantText)))) // {f1:{f1:text}}
122		metaNodeID := put(concat(pbRef(1, titleID), pbRef(2, emptyID), pbRef(2, previewID)))
123	
124		// 3. Root node: field 1 = ordered message refs, field 2 = metadata node.
125		var root []byte
126		for _, mid := range msgIDs {
127			root = append(root, pbRef(1, mid)...)
128		}
129		root = append(root, pbRef(2, metaNodeID)...)
130		rootID := put(root)
131	
132		// 4. meta['0'] head record (hex-encoded JSON).
133		metaJSON, err := marshalCompact(map[string]interface{}{
134			"agentId":          newID,
135			"latestRootBlobId": rootID,
136			"name":             name,
137			"mode":             "default",
138			"createdAt":        time.Now().UTC().UnixMilli(),
139		})
140		if err != nil {
141			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to encode meta: %w", err)
142		}
143	
144		// 5. Materialize the SQLite store.db and return its bytes. SQLite needs a file
145		// handle, so we build in a temp file (created and removed within this call).
146		content, err := buildStoreDB(blobs, hex.EncodeToString(metaJSON))
147		if err != nil {
148			return nil, err
149		}
150	
151		return &spi.ReconstructedSession{
152			SessionID: newID,
153			Filename:  filepath.Join(newID, "store.db"),
154			Content:   content,
155		}, nil
156	}
157	
158	// concat joins byte slices.
159	func concat(parts ...[]byte) []byte {
160		var out []byte
161		for _, p := range parts {
162			out = append(out, p...)
163		}
164		return out
165	}
166	
167	// buildStoreDB writes the blobs + meta into a SQLite database and returns its bytes.
168	func buildStoreDB(blobs map[string][]byte, metaHex string) ([]byte, error) {
169		tmp, err := os.CreateTemp("", "specstory-cursor-*.db")
170		if err != nil {
171			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to create temp db: %w", err)
172		}
173		tmpPath := tmp.Name()
174		_ = tmp.Close()
175		defer func() { _ = os.Remove(tmpPath) }()
176	
177		db, err := sql.Open("sqlite", tmpPath)
178		if err != nil {
179			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to open temp db: %w", err)
180		}
181		if _, err := db.Exec("CREATE TABLE blobs (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, data BLOB)"); err != nil {
182			_ = db.Close()
183			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to create blobs table: %w", err)
184		}
185		if _, err := db.Exec("CREATE TABLE meta (key TEXT PRIMARY KEY, value TEXT)"); err != nil {
186			_ = db.Close()
187			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to create meta table: %w", err)
188		}
189		for id, data := range blobs {
190			if _, err := db.Exec("INSERT INTO blobs (id, data) VALUES (?, ?)", id, data); err != nil {
191				_ = db.Close()
192				return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to insert blob: %w", err)
193			}
194		}
195		if _, err := db.Exec("INSERT INTO meta (key, value) VALUES ('0', ?)", metaHex); err != nil {
196			_ = db.Close()
197			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to insert meta: %w", err)
198		}
199		if err := db.Close(); err != nil {
200			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to close temp db: %w", err)
201		}
202	
203		return os.ReadFile(tmpPath)
204	}
205	
206	// NativeSessionPath returns where a reconstructed store.db belongs in Cursor's store:
207	// ~/.cursor/chats/<md5(canonical cwd)>/<session-id>/store.db. The filename already
208	// includes the session-id subdir, so this joins it under the project hash dir.
209	func (p *Provider) NativeSessionPath(projectPath string, filename string) (string, error) {
210		if projectPath == "" {
211			var err error
212			projectPath, err = os.Getwd()
213			if err != nil {
214				return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get current working directory: %w", err)
215			}
216		}
217		hashDir, err := GetProjectHashDir(projectPath)
218		if err != nil {
219			return "", err
220		}
221		return filepath.Join(hashDir, filename), nil
222	}
223
```

 `./pkg/providers/deepseektui/reconstruct.go`

```text
1	package deepseektui
2	
3	import (
4		"bytes"
5		"encoding/json"
6		"fmt"
7		"path/filepath"
8		"strings"
9		"time"
10	
11		"github.com/google/uuid"
12	
13		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
14		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
15	)
16	
17	// deepseekTimestamp formats a time the way DeepSeek records do (RFC3339 micros, Z).
18	func deepseekTimestamp(t time.Time) string {
19		return t.Format("2006-01-02T15:04:05.000000Z")
20	}
21	
22	// ReconstructSession rebuilds a DeepSeek TUI native session from the neutral
23	// SessionData so `deepseek --resume <id>` can continue the conversation.
24	//
25	// DeepSeek stores a session as a single JSON document
26	// `{schema_version, system_prompt, metadata, messages}` where each message is
27	// `{role, content:[{type:"text",text}]}`. Tool calls and thinking are already
28	// flattened into agent text by FlattenSessionData, so reconstruction emits only
29	// `user`/`assistant` text messages. The session is associated with a project via
30	// metadata.workspace. See docs/SESSION-PORTABILITY.md.
31	func (p *Provider) ReconstructSession(data *schema.SessionData, opts spi.ReconstructOptions) (*spi.ReconstructedSession, error) {
32		if data == nil {
33			return nil, fmt.Errorf("cannot reconstruct nil session data")
34		}
35	
36		cwd := opts.WorkspaceRoot
37		if cwd == "" {
38			cwd = data.WorkspaceRoot
39		}
40	
41		turns := spi.FlattenSessionData(data, opts.MigrationNote)
42		if len(turns) == 0 {
43			return nil, fmt.Errorf("session has no content to reconstruct")
44		}
45	
46		newID := uuid.NewString()
47		now := deepseekTimestamp(time.Now().UTC())
48	
49		title := strings.TrimSpace(data.Slug)
50		if title == "" {
51			title = "Resumed session"
52		}
53	
54		messages := make([]map[string]interface{}, 0, len(turns))
55		for _, turn := range turns {
56			role := "assistant"
57			if turn.Role == schema.RoleUser {
58				role = "user"
59			}
60			messages = append(messages, map[string]interface{}{
61				"role":    role,
62				"content": []map[string]interface{}{{"type": "text", "text": turn.Text}},
63			})
64		}
65	
66		session := map[string]interface{}{
67			"schema_version": 1,
68			"system_prompt":  "",
69			"metadata": map[string]interface{}{
70				"id":            newID,
71				"title":         title,
72				"created_at":    now,
73				"updated_at":    now,
74				"message_count": len(messages),
75				"total_tokens":  0,
76				"model":         "",
77				"workspace":     cwd,
78				"mode":          "agent",
79			},
80			"messages": messages,
81		}
82	
83		var buf bytes.Buffer
84		enc := json.NewEncoder(&buf)
85		enc.SetEscapeHTML(false)
86		enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
87		if err := enc.Encode(session); err != nil {
88			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to encode deepseek session: %w", err)
89		}
90	
91		return &spi.ReconstructedSession{
92			SessionID: newID,
93			Filename:  newID + ".json",
94			Content:   buf.Bytes(),
95		}, nil
96	}
97	
98	// NativeSessionPath returns where a reconstructed session belongs in DeepSeek's
99	// store: ~/.deepseek/sessions/<filename>. DeepSeek sessions are not project-scoped
100	// by directory (the project is recorded in metadata.workspace), so projectPath is
101	// unused. The directory is not required to exist (the caller creates it).
102	func (p *Provider) NativeSessionPath(projectPath string, filename string) (string, error) {
103		dir, err := resolveSessionsDir()
104		if err != nil {
105			return "", err
106		}
107		return filepath.Join(dir, filename), nil
108	}
109
```

 `./pkg/providers/droidcli/reconstruct.go`

```text
1	package droidcli
2	
3	import (
4		"bytes"
5		"encoding/json"
6		"fmt"
7		"os"
8		"path/filepath"
9		"strings"
10		"time"
11	
12		"github.com/google/uuid"
13	
14		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
15		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
16	)
17	
18	// droidTimestamp formats a time the way Factory Droid records do (RFC3339 millis, Z).
19	func droidTimestamp(t time.Time) string {
20		return t.Format("2006-01-02T15:04:05.000Z")
21	}
22	
23	// ReconstructSession rebuilds a Factory Droid native JSONL session from the neutral
24	// SessionData so `droid --resume <id>` can continue the conversation.
25	//
26	// Droid stores a session as a `session_start` header followed by `parentId`-chained
27	// `message` records, each `{message:{role, content:[{type:"text",text}]}}`. Tool
28	// calls and thinking are already flattened into agent text by FlattenSessionData,
29	// so reconstruction emits only `user`/`assistant` text messages. The store is
30	// project-scoped (see NativeSessionPath). See docs/SESSION-PORTABILITY.md.
31	func (p *Provider) ReconstructSession(data *schema.SessionData, opts spi.ReconstructOptions) (*spi.ReconstructedSession, error) {
32		if data == nil {
33			return nil, fmt.Errorf("cannot reconstruct nil session data")
34		}
35	
36		cwd := opts.WorkspaceRoot
37		if cwd == "" {
38			cwd = data.WorkspaceRoot
39		}
40	
41		turns := spi.FlattenSessionData(data, opts.MigrationNote)
42		if len(turns) == 0 {
43			return nil, fmt.Errorf("session has no content to reconstruct")
44		}
45	
46		newID := uuid.NewString()
47		base := time.Now().UTC()
48	
49		title := strings.TrimSpace(data.Slug)
50		if title == "" {
51			title = "Resumed session"
52		}
53	
54		var buf bytes.Buffer
55		enc := json.NewEncoder(&buf)
56		enc.SetEscapeHTML(false)
57	
58		// session_start header (also carries provenance).
59		if err := enc.Encode(map[string]interface{}{
60			"type":                      "session_start",
61			"id":                        newID,
62			"title":                     title,
63			"sessionTitle":              title,
64			"owner":                     os.Getenv("USER"),
65			"version":                   2,
66			"cwd":                       cwd,
67			"isSessionTitleManuallySet": false,
68			"sessionTitleAutoStage":     "first_message",
69			"specstorySourceSessionId":  data.SessionID,
70		}); err != nil {
71			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to encode session_start: %w", err)
72		}
73	
74		var prevID string
75		for i, turn := range turns {
76			msgID := uuid.NewString()
77			role := "assistant"
78			if turn.Role == schema.RoleUser {
79				role = "user"
80			}
81	
82			rec := map[string]interface{}{
83				"type":      "message",
84				"id":        msgID,
85				"timestamp": droidTimestamp(base.Add(time.Duration(i) * time.Second)),
86				"message": map[string]interface{}{
87					"role":    role,
88					"content": []map[string]interface{}{{"type": "text", "text": turn.Text}},
89				},
90			}
91			// Linear chain: the first message roots the thread (no parentId).
92			if prevID != "" {
93				rec["parentId"] = prevID
94			}
95	
96			if err := enc.Encode(rec); err != nil {
97				return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to encode message: %w", err)
98			}
99			prevID = msgID
100		}
101	
102		return &spi.ReconstructedSession{
103			SessionID: newID,
104			Filename:  newID + ".jsonl",
105			Content:   buf.Bytes(),
106		}, nil
107	}
108	
109	// NativeSessionPath returns where a reconstructed session belongs in Droid's store:
110	// ~/.factory/sessions/<cwd-encoded>/<filename>. The directory is not required to
111	// exist (the caller creates it).
112	func (p *Provider) NativeSessionPath(projectPath string, filename string) (string, error) {
113		if projectPath == "" {
114			var err error
115			projectPath, err = os.Getwd()
116			if err != nil {
117				return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get current working directory: %w", err)
118			}
119		}
120	
121		dir, err := resolveProjectSessionDir(projectPath)
122		if err != nil {
123			return "", err
124		}
125		if dir == "" {
126			return "", fmt.Errorf("droidcli: cannot resolve session directory for project path")
127		}
128		return filepath.Join(dir, filename), nil
129	}
130
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./pkg/providers/claudecode/provider.go`

```text
1	package claudecode
2	
3	import (
4		"bufio"

17		"sync"

19		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/analytics"
20		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/log"
21		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
22	)
23	
24	// Provider implements the SPI Provider interface for Claude Code
25	type Provider struct{}
26	
27	// NewProvider creates a new Claude Code provider instance
28	func NewProvider() *Provider {
29		return &Provider{}
30	}
31	
32	// filterWarmupMessages removes warmup messages (sidechain messages before first real message)
33	func filterWarmupMessages(records []JSONLRecord) []JSONLRecord {
34		// Find first non-sidechain message
35		firstRealMessageIndex := -1
36		for i, record := range records {
37			if isSidechain, ok := record.Data["isSidechain"].(bool); !ok || !isSidechain {
38				firstRealMessageIndex = i
39				break
40			}
41		}
42	
43		// No real messages found
44		if firstRealMessageIndex == -1 {
45			return []JSONLRecord{}
46		}
47	
48		// Return records starting from first real message
49		return records[firstRealMessageIndex:]
50	}
```

<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./pkg/providers/geminicli/reconstruct.go`

```text
1	package geminicli
2	
3	import (
4		"bytes"
5		"encoding/json"
6		"fmt"
7		"os"
8		"path/filepath"
9		"time"
10	
11		"github.com/google/uuid"
12	
13		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
14		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
15	)
16	
17	// geminiTimestamp formats a time the way Gemini chat records do (RFC3339 millis, Z).
18	func geminiTimestamp(t time.Time) string {
19		return t.Format("2006-01-02T15:04:05.000Z")
20	}
21	
22	// ReconstructSession rebuilds a Gemini CLI native chat session from the neutral
23	// SessionData so `gemini --resume <id>` can continue the conversation.
24	//
25	// Gemini stores a session as a single JSON document: a top-level object with
26	// `sessionId`/`projectHash`/`startTime`/`lastUpdated`/`kind` and a `messages`
27	// array. Each message is `{id, timestamp, type, content}` where a user message's
28	// content is `[{text}]` and a gemini message's content is a plain string. Tool
29	// calls and thinking are already flattened into agent text by FlattenSessionData,
30	// so reconstruction emits only `user`/`gemini` text messages. See
31	// docs/SESSION-PORTABILITY.md.
32	func (p *Provider) ReconstructSession(data *schema.SessionData, opts spi.ReconstructOptions) (*spi.ReconstructedSession, error) {
33		if data == nil {
34			return nil, fmt.Errorf("cannot reconstruct nil session data")
35		}
36	
37		cwd := opts.WorkspaceRoot
38		if cwd == "" {
39			cwd = data.WorkspaceRoot
40		}
41	
42		turns := spi.FlattenSessionData(data, opts.MigrationNote)
43		if len(turns) == 0 {
44			return nil, fmt.Errorf("session has no content to reconstruct")
45		}
46	
47		newID := uuid.NewString()
48		base := time.Now().UTC()
49	
50		// Best-effort project hash; Gemini stores it but resume keys off the project dir.
51		projectHash, _ := HashProjectPath(cwd)
52	
53		messages := make([]map[string]interface{}, 0, len(turns))
54		for i, turn := range turns {
55			msg := map[string]interface{}{
56				"id":        uuid.NewString(),
57				"timestamp": geminiTimestamp(base.Add(time.Duration(i) * time.Second)),
58			}
59			if turn.Role == schema.RoleUser {
60				msg["type"] = "user"
61				msg["content"] = []map[string]interface{}{{"text": turn.Text}}
62			} else {
63				msg["type"] = "gemini"
64				msg["content"] = turn.Text
65			}
66			messages = append(messages, msg)
67		}
68	
69		session := map[string]interface{}{
70			"sessionId":   newID,
71			"projectHash": projectHash,
72			"startTime":   geminiTimestamp(base),
73			"lastUpdated": geminiTimestamp(base.Add(time.Duration(len(turns)) * time.Second)),
74			"kind":        "main",
75			"messages":    messages,
76		}
77	
78		var buf bytes.Buffer
79		enc := json.NewEncoder(&buf)
80		enc.SetEscapeHTML(false)
81		enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
82		if err := enc.Encode(session); err != nil {
83			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to encode gemini session: %w", err)
84		}
85	
86		// Filename: session-<YYYY-MM-DDTHH-MM>-<first 8 of id>.json (Gemini convention).
87		filename := fmt.Sprintf("session-%s-%s.json", base.Format("2006-01-02T15-04"), newID[:8])
88	
89		return &spi.ReconstructedSession{
90			SessionID: newID,
91			Filename:  filename,
92			Content:   buf.Bytes(),
93		}, nil
94	}
95	
96	// NativeSessionPath returns where a reconstructed chat belongs in Gemini's store
97	// and, unlike the Claude/Codex resolvers, prepares the project directory: Gemini
98	// associates a tmp dir with a project via a `.project_root` marker, so for a
99	// project Gemini has never seen we create `~/.gemini/tmp/<name>/` with that marker
100	// and a `chats/` subdir. Returns `<projectDir>/chats/<filename>`.
101	func (p *Provider) NativeSessionPath(projectPath string, filename string) (string, error) {
102		if projectPath == "" {
103			var err error
104			projectPath, err = osGetwd()
105			if err != nil {
106				return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get current working directory: %w", err)
107			}
108		}
109	
110		// Reuse the project dir Gemini already associates with this project, if any.
111		dir, err := ResolveGeminiProjectDir(projectPath)
112		if err != nil {
113			dir, err = prepareGeminiProjectDir(projectPath)
114			if err != nil {
115				return "", err
116			}
117		}
118	
119		return filepath.Join(dir, "chats", filename), nil
120	}
121	
122	// prepareGeminiProjectDir creates a Gemini project directory for a project that
123	// Gemini has not seen yet: ~/.gemini/tmp/<basename>/ (falling back to the hash dir
124	// if that basename is taken by a different project), with a `.project_root` marker
125	// and a `chats/` subdirectory.
126	func prepareGeminiProjectDir(projectPath string) (string, error) {
127		tmpDir, err := GetGeminiTmpDir()
128		if err != nil {
129			return "", err
130		}
131		if err := os.MkdirAll(tmpDir, 0o755); err != nil {
132			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to create gemini tmp dir: %w", err)
133		}
134	
135		canonical := canonicalizeProjectPath(projectPath)
136		dir := filepath.Join(tmpDir, filepath.Base(projectPath))
137	
138		// If the basename dir exists but belongs to a different project, key off the hash instead.
139		if info, statErr := osStat(dir); statErr == nil && info.IsDir() && !matchesProjectRoot(dir, canonical) {
140			if dir, err = GetGeminiProjectDir(projectPath); err != nil {
141				return "", err
142			}
143		}
144	
145		if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(dir, "chats"), 0o755); err != nil {
146			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to create gemini chats dir: %w", err)
147		}
148	
149		// Write the project-root marker so Gemini maps this dir to the project on resume.
150		rootFile := filepath.Join(dir, ".project_root")
151		if _, statErr := osStat(rootFile); os.IsNotExist(statErr) {
152			if err := os.WriteFile(rootFile, []byte(canonical), 0o644); err != nil {
153				return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to write gemini .project_root: %w", err)
154			}
155		}
156	
157		return dir, nil
158	}
159
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	// Package config provides configuration management for the SpecStory CLI.
2	// Configuration is loaded with the following priority (highest to lowest):
3	//  1. CLI flags
4	//  2. Local project config: .specstory/cli/config.toml
5	//  3. User-level config: ~/.specstory/cli/config.toml
6	//
7	// Note: For telemetry settings, environment variables (OTEL_*) take highest priority
8	// per OpenTelemetry conventions.
9	package config
10	
11	import (
12		"fmt"
13		"log/slog"
14		"os"
15		"path/filepath"
16		"strings"
17	
18		"github.com/BurntSushi/toml"
19	)
20	
21	const (
22		// ConfigFileName is the name of the configuration file
23		ConfigFileName = "config.toml"
24		// SpecStoryDir is the directory name for SpecStory files
25		SpecStoryDir = ".specstory"
26		// CLIDir is the subdirectory for CLI-specific files (config, auth, etc.)
27		CLIDir = "cli"
28	)
29	
30	// defaultConfigTemplate is the content written to a newly created config file.
31	// All options are commented out so the file is self-documenting but inert.
32	const defaultConfigTemplate = `# SpecStory CLI Configuration
33	#
34	# {u This is the user-level config file for SpecStory CLI.
35	# All settings here apply to every project unless overridden
36	# by a project-level config at ./.specstory/cli/config.toml
37	# or overridden by CLI flags.}
38	# {p This is the project-level config file for SpecStory CLI.
39	# All settings here apply to this project unless overridden by CLI flags.}
40	#
41	# Uncomment (remove the #) the line and edit any setting below to change the default behavior.
42	# For more information, see: https://docs.specstory.com/integrations/terminal-coding-agents/usage
43	
44	[local_sync]
45	# Write markdown files locally. (default: true)
46	# enabled = false # equivalent to --only-cloud-sync
47	
48	# Custom output directory for markdown files.
49	# Default: ./.specstory/history (relative to the project directory)
50	# output_dir = "~/.specstory/history" # equivalent to --output-dir "~/.specstory/history"
51	
52	# Use local timezone for file name and content timestamps (default: false, UTC)
53	# local_time_zone = true # equivalent to --local-time-zone
54	
55	[cloud_sync]
56	# Sync session data to SpecStory Cloud. (default: true, when logged in to SpecStory Cloud)
57	# enabled = false # equivalent to --no-cloud-sync
58	
59	[logging]
60	# Write logs to .specstory/debug/debug.log (default: false)
61	# log = true # equivalent to --log        
62	
63	# Debug-level output, requires console or log (default: false)
64	# debug = true # equivalent to --debug 
65	
66	# Custom output directory for debug data.
67	# Default: ./.specstory/debug (relative to the project directory)
68	# debug_dir = "~/.specstory/debug" # equivalent to --debug-dir "~/.specstory/debug"
69	
70	# Error/warn/info output to stdout (default: false)
71	# console = true # equivalent to --console
72	
73	# Suppress all non-error output (default: false)
74	# silent = true	# equivalent to --silent
75	
76	[version_check]
77	# Check for new versions of the CLI on startup.
78	# Default: true
79	# enabled = false # equivalent to --no-version-check
80	
81	[analytics]
82	# Send anonymous product usage analytics to help improve SpecStory.
83	# Default: true
84	# enabled = false # equivalent to --no-usage-analytics
85	
86	[telemetry]
87	# OTLP gRPC collector endpoint (e.g., "localhost:4317" or "http://localhost:4317")
88	# endpoint = "localhost:4317"
89	
90	# Override the default service name (default: "specstory-cli")
91	# service_name = "my-service-name"
92	
93	# Include user prompt text in telemetry spans (default: true)
94	# prompts = false
95	
96	[resume]
97	# Default view mode for the 'specstory resume' picker: "dense" (more sessions) or
98	# "sparse" (more detail per session). The picker also remembers your last choice here.
99	# view_mode = "sparse"
100	#
101	# (managed automatically) the agent you last resumed into — used as the default target.
102	# last_agent = "claude"
103	
104	[providers]
105	# Agent execution commands by provider (used by specstory run)
106	# Pass custom flags (e.g. claude_cmd = "claude --allow-dangerously-skip-permissions")
107	# Use of these is equivalent to -c "custom command"
108	
109	# Claude Code command
110	# claude_cmd = "claude"
111	
112	# Codex CLI command
113	# codex_cmd = "codex"
114	
115	# Cursor CLI command
116	# cursor_cmd = "cursor-agent"
117	
118	# Droid CLI command
119	# droid_cmd = "droid"
120	
121	# Gemini CLI command
122	# gemini_cmd = "gemini"
123	
124	# DeepSeek TUI command
125	# deepseek_cmd = "deepseek"
126	`
127	
128	// Config represents the complete CLI configuration
129	type Config struct {
130		VersionCheck VersionCheckConfig `toml:"version_check"`
131		CloudSync    CloudSyncConfig    `toml:"cloud_sync"`
132		LocalSync    LocalSyncConfig    `toml:"local_sync"`
133		Logging      LoggingConfig      `toml:"logging"`
134		Analytics    AnalyticsConfig    `toml:"analytics"`
135		Telemetry    TelemetryConfig    `toml:"telemetry"`
136		Providers    ProvidersConfig    `toml:"providers"`
137		Resume       ResumeConfig       `toml:"resume"`
138	}
139	
140	// ResumeConfig holds preferences for the `specstory resume` picker. view_mode is a
141	// user setting; last_agent is written automatically as you resume.
142	type ResumeConfig struct {
143		// ViewMode is the picker layout: "dense" (more sessions) or "sparse" (more detail).
144		ViewMode string `toml:"view_mode"`
145		// LastAgent is the provider id of the agent you last resumed into (default target).
146		LastAgent string `toml:"last_agent"`
147	}
148	
149	// VersionCheckConfig holds version check settings
150	type VersionCheckConfig struct {
151		// Enabled controls whether to check for newer versions on startup
152		Enabled *bool `toml:"enabled"`
153	}
154	
155	// CloudSyncConfig holds cloud sync settings
156	type CloudSyncConfig struct {
157		// Enabled controls whether cloud sync is active
158		Enabled *bool `toml:"enabled"`
159	}
160	
161	// LocalSyncConfig holds local file sync settings
162	type LocalSyncConfig struct {
163		// Enabled controls whether local markdown files are written
164		// When false, only cloud sync is performed (equivalent to --only-cloud-sync)
165		Enabled *bool `toml:"enabled"`
166		// OutputDir is the custom output directory for markdown files
167		OutputDir string `toml:"output_dir"`
168		// LocalTimeZone enables local timezone for file name and content timestamps
169		LocalTimeZone *bool `toml:"local_time_zone"`
170	}
171	
172	// LoggingConfig holds logging settings
173	type LoggingConfig struct {
174		// DebugDir is the custom output directory for debug data
175		DebugDir string `toml:"debug_dir"`
176		// Console enables error/warn/info output to stdout
177		Console *bool `toml:"console"`
178		// Log enables writing error/warn/info output to .specstory/debug/debug.log
179		Log *bool `toml:"log"`
180		// Debug enables debug-level output (requires Console or Log)
181		Debug *bool `toml:"debug"`
182		// Silent suppresses all non-error output
183		Silent *bool `toml:"silent"`
184	}
185	
186	// AnalyticsConfig holds analytics settings
187	type AnalyticsConfig struct {
188		// Enabled controls whether usage analytics are sent
189		Enabled *bool `toml:"enabled"`
190	}
191	
192	// TelemetryConfig holds OpenTelemetry configuration.
193	// Telemetry is enabled when Endpoint is non-empty (no separate "enabled" flag).
194	type TelemetryConfig struct {
195		// Endpoint is the OTLP gRPC collector address (e.g., "localhost:4317" or "http://localhost:4317")
196		// Env var: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT
197		Endpoint string `toml:"endpoint"`
198	
199		// ServiceName overrides the default service name ("specstory-cli")
200		// Env var: OTEL_SERVICE_NAME
201		ServiceName string `toml:"service_name"`
202	
203		// Prompts controls whether user prompt text is included in telemetry spans.
204		// Defaults to true (prompts are included). Set to false to exclude prompt text
205		// from the specstory.exchange.prompt_text attribute for privacy.
206		Prompts *bool `toml:"prompts"`
207	}
208	
209	// ProvidersConfig holds custom agent execution commands by provider.
210	// These are used by `specstory run` as the equivalent of the -c flag,
211	// scoped to a specific provider.
212	type ProvidersConfig struct {
213		ClaudeCmd   string `toml:"claude_cmd"`
214		CodexCmd    string `toml:"codex_cmd"`
215		CursorCmd   string `toml:"cursor_cmd"`
216		DeepSeekCmd string `toml:"deepseek_cmd"`
217		DroidCmd    string `toml:"droid_cmd"`
218		GeminiCmd   string `toml:"gemini_cmd"`
219	}
220	
221	// CLIOverrides holds CLI flag values that override config file settings.
222	// These are applied after config files are loaded.
223	type CLIOverrides struct {
224		// General
225		OutputDir     string
226		LocalTimeZone bool
227	
228		// Version check
229		NoVersionCheck bool
230	
231		// Cloud sync
232		NoCloudSync   bool
233		OnlyCloudSync bool
234	
235		// Logging
236		DebugDir string
237		Console  bool
238		Log      bool
239		Debug    bool
240		Silent   bool
241	
242		// Analytics
243		NoAnalytics bool
244	
245		// Telemetry
246		TelemetryEndpoint    string
247		TelemetryServiceName string
248		NoTelemetryPrompts   bool
249	}
250	
251	// Load reads configuration from files and CLI flags.
252	// Priority: CLI flags > local project config > user-level config
253	// Note: For telemetry, OTEL_* env vars take highest priority per OTel conventions.
254	//
255	// Returns an error if a config file exists but cannot be parsed (TOML syntax error)
256	// or cannot be read (permission denied, I/O error). Missing config files are not
257	// treated as errors - they are simply skipped.
258	func Load(cliOverrides *CLIOverrides) (*Config, error) {
259		cfg := &Config{}
260	
261		// Load user-level config first (lowest priority)
262		userConfigPath := getUserConfigPath()
263		if userConfigPath != "" {
264			if err := loadTOMLFile(userConfigPath, cfg); err != nil {
265				if os.IsNotExist(err) {
266					slog.Debug("No user-level config file found", "path", userConfigPath)
267					ensureDefaultUserConfig(userConfigPath)
268				} else {
269					// Parse error or permission denied - return error to caller
270					return cfg, fmt.Errorf("failed to load user config %s: %w", userConfigPath, err)
271				}
272			} else {
273				slog.Debug("Loaded user-level config", "path", userConfigPath)
274			}
275		}
276	
277		// Load local project config (overwrites user-level)
278		localConfigPath := getLocalConfigPath()
279		if localConfigPath != "" {
280			if err := loadTOMLFile(localConfigPath, cfg); err != nil {
281				if os.IsNotExist(err) {
282					slog.Debug("No local project config file found", "path", localConfigPath)
283				} else {
284					// Parse error or permission denied - return error to caller
285					return cfg, fmt.Errorf("failed to load project config %s: %w", localConfigPath, err)
286				}
287			} else {
288				slog.Debug("Loaded local project config", "path", localConfigPath)
289			}
290		}
291	
292		// Apply CLI flag overrides (highest priority for most settings)
293		if cliOverrides != nil {
294			applyCLIOverrides(cfg, cliOverrides)
295		}
296	
297		// Apply OTel environment variables (highest priority for telemetry only)
298		applyTelemetryEnvOverrides(cfg)
299	
300		return cfg, nil
301	}
302	
303	// GetUserConfigPath returns the path to ~/.specstory/cli/config.toml
304	func GetUserConfigPath() string {
305		return getUserConfigPath()
306	}
307	
308	// GetLocalConfigPath returns the path to .specstory/cli/config.toml in the current directory
309	func GetLocalConfigPath() string {
310		return getLocalConfigPath()
311	}
312	
313	// getUserConfigPath returns the path to ~/.specstory/cli/config.toml
314	func getUserConfigPath() string {
315		home, err := os.UserHomeDir()
316		if err != nil {
317			slog.Debug("Could not determine home directory", "error", err)
318			return ""
319		}
320		return filepath.Join(home, SpecStoryDir, CLIDir, ConfigFileName)
321	}
322	
323	// getLocalConfigPath returns the path to .specstory/cli/config.toml in the current directory
324	func getLocalConfigPath() string {
325		cwd, err := os.Getwd()
326		if err != nil {
327			slog.Debug("Could not determine current directory", "error", err)
328			return ""
329		}
330		return filepath.Join(cwd, SpecStoryDir, CLIDir, ConfigFileName)
331	}
332	
333	// processTemplate processes the default config template for a given level
334	// ("user" or "project"). Template markers {u ...} and {p ...} delimit content
335	// specific to user-level or project-level config files respectively.
336	//
337	// For level "user": content inside {u ...} is kept (markers stripped),
338	// content inside {p ...} is removed entirely.
339	// For level "project": the opposite applies.
340	//
341	// Markers can span multiple lines. The opening marker ({u or {p) appears at
342	// the start of a line (after optional whitespace/comment prefix), and the
343	// closing } appears at the end of a line.
344	func processTemplate(template, level string) string {
345		var result strings.Builder
346		keepTag := "{u"
347		stripTag := "{p"
348		if level == "project" {
349			keepTag = "{p"
350			stripTag = "{u"
351		}
352	
353		insideKeep := false
354		insideStrip := false
355	
356		for line := range strings.SplitSeq(template, "\n") {
357			trimmed := strings.TrimSpace(line)
358			// Remove any leading "# " to inspect the marker
359			bare := strings.TrimPrefix(trimmed, "# ")
360	
361			switch {
362			case insideStrip:
363				// Check for closing brace — ends the stripped block
364				if strings.HasSuffix(bare, "}") {
365					insideStrip = false
366				}
367				// Drop the entire line regardless
368				continue
369	
370			case insideKeep:
371				// Check for closing brace — ends the kept block
372				if strings.HasSuffix(bare, "}") {
373					insideKeep = false
374					// Strip the trailing } from the line
375					idx := strings.LastIndex(line, "}")
376					line = line[:idx]
377					// Only emit if something remains after stripping
378					if strings.TrimSpace(line) == "" || strings.TrimSpace(line) == "#" {
379						continue
380					}
381				}
382				result.WriteString(line)
383				result.WriteString("\n")
384	
385			case strings.HasPrefix(bare, keepTag+" "):
386				insideKeep = true
387				// prefix is everything before the bare marker (e.g. "# ")
388				prefix := line[:strings.Index(line, bare)]
389				// Check if single-line block (closing } on same line)
390				if strings.HasSuffix(bare, "}") {
391					insideKeep = false
392					// Extract content between tag and closing brace
393					content := strings.TrimSpace(bare[len(keepTag)+1 : len(bare)-1])
394					reconstructed := prefix + content
395					if strings.TrimSpace(reconstructed) == "" || strings.TrimSpace(reconstructed) == "#" {
396						continue
397					}
398					result.WriteString(reconstructed)
399					result.WriteString("\n")
400				} else {
401					// Multi-line: strip the marker prefix, keep content after it
402					content := strings.TrimSpace(bare[len(keepTag)+1:])
403					reconstructed := prefix + content
404					if strings.TrimSpace(reconstructed) == "" || strings.TrimSpace(reconstructed) == "#" {
405						continue
406					}
407					result.WriteString(reconstructed)
408					result.WriteString("\n")
409				}
410	
411			case strings.HasPrefix(bare, stripTag+" "):
412				insideStrip = true
413				// Check if single-line block (closing } on same line)
414				if strings.HasSuffix(bare, "}") {
415					insideStrip = false
416				}
417				continue
418	
419			default:
420				result.WriteString(line)
421				result.WriteString("\n")
422			}
423		}
424	
425		// Remove trailing extra newline that accumulates from the split
426		out := result.String()
427		if strings.HasSuffix(out, "\n\n") && !strings.HasSuffix(template, "\n\n") {
428			out = out[:len(out)-1]
429		}
430		return out
431	}
432	
433	// ensureDefaultUserConfig creates a default user-level config file with all
434	// options commented out. This makes the config discoverable without changing
435	// any behavior. Failures are silently ignored — this is a convenience, not
436	// a requirement.
437	func ensureDefaultUserConfig(path string) {
438		dir := filepath.Dir(path)
439		if err := os.MkdirAll(dir, 0755); err != nil {
440			slog.Debug("Could not create config directory", "path", dir, "error", err)
441			return
442		}
443		processed := processTemplate(defaultConfigTemplate, "user")
444		if err := os.WriteFile(path, []byte(processed), 0644); err != nil {
445			slog.Debug("Could not write default config file", "path", path, "error", err)
446			return
447		}
448		slog.Debug("Created default user config file", "path", path)
449	}
450	
451	// EnsureDefaultProjectConfig creates a default project-level config file at
452	// .specstory/cli/config.toml if one doesn't already exist. All options are
453	// commented out so the file is self-documenting but inert.
454	//
455	// This should only be called from commands that imply active project work
456	// (run, sync, watch) to avoid scattering config files in arbitrary directories.
457	// Failures are silently ignored — this is a convenience, not a requirement.
458	func EnsureDefaultProjectConfig() {
459		path := getLocalConfigPath()
460		if path == "" {
461			return
462		}
463		// Don't overwrite an existing file
464		if _, err := os.Stat(path); err == nil {
465			return
466		}
467		dir := filepath.Dir(path)
468		if err := os.MkdirAll(dir, 0755); err != nil {
469			slog.Debug("Could not create config directory", "path", dir, "error", err)
470			return
471		}
472		processed := processTemplate(defaultConfigTemplate, "project")
473		if err := os.WriteFile(path, []byte(processed), 0644); err != nil {
474			slog.Debug("Could not write default config file", "path", path, "error", err)
475			return
476		}
477		slog.Debug("Created default project config file", "path", path)
478	}
479	
480	// loadTOMLFile reads a TOML file and decodes it into the config struct.
481	// Fields are merged (later calls overwrite earlier values).
482	func loadTOMLFile(path string, cfg *Config) error {
483		_, err := toml.DecodeFile(path, cfg)
484		return err
485	}
486	
487	// applyTelemetryEnvOverrides applies OTel environment variable overrides.
488	// Per OTel conventions, these take highest priority for telemetry settings.
489	func applyTelemetryEnvOverrides(cfg *Config) {
490		if endpoint := os.Getenv("OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT"); endpoint != "" {
491			cfg.Telemetry.Endpoint = endpoint
492		}
493	
494		if serviceName := os.Getenv("OTEL_SERVICE_NAME"); serviceName != "" {
495			cfg.Telemetry.ServiceName = serviceName
496		}
497	
498		// Note: OTEL_SDK_DISABLED is handled in IsTelemetryEnabled() rather than
499		// stored in the config struct, since there is no Enabled field — telemetry
500		// is enabled when an endpoint is configured.
501	}
502	
503	// ConfigValidationResult holds the result of validating a config file.
504	type ConfigValidationResult struct {
505		Path        string   // Path to the config file
506		Exists      bool     // Whether the file exists
507		ValidTOML   bool     // Whether the file is valid TOML
508		ParseError  string   // TOML parse error message, if any
509		UnknownKeys []string // Keys in the file that don't map to any known config field
510	}
511	
512	// ValidateConfigFile checks a config file for validity and schema conformance.
513	// Returns a result indicating whether the file exists, is valid TOML, and
514	// whether it contains any unknown sections or properties.
515	func ValidateConfigFile(path string) ConfigValidationResult {
516		result := ConfigValidationResult{Path: path}
517	
518		// Check if file exists
519		if _, err := os.Stat(path); os.IsNotExist(err) {
520			return result
521		}
522		result.Exists = true
523	
524		// Try to decode and check for unknown keys
525		var cfg Config
526		md, err := toml.DecodeFile(path, &cfg)
527		if err != nil {
528			result.ParseError = err.Error()
529			return result
530		}
531		result.ValidTOML = true
532	
533		// Check for keys in the TOML that didn't map to any struct field.
534		// Filter out child keys when the parent section is already unknown
535		// (e.g., if [bogus] is unknown, don't also report bogus.foo).
536		undecoded := md.Undecoded()
537		unknownSections := make(map[string]bool)
538		for _, key := range undecoded {
539			if len(key) == 1 {
540				unknownSections[key[0]] = true
541			}
542		}
543		for _, key := range undecoded {
544			// Skip child keys whose parent section is already flagged
545			if len(key) > 1 && unknownSections[key[0]] {
546				continue
547			}
548			result.UnknownKeys = append(result.UnknownKeys, key.String())
549		}
550	
551		return result
552	}
553	
554	// applyCLIOverrides applies CLI flag overrides to the config.
555	func applyCLIOverrides(cfg *Config, o *CLIOverrides) {
556		// Local sync
557		if o.OutputDir != "" {
558			cfg.LocalSync.OutputDir = o.OutputDir
559		}
560		if o.LocalTimeZone {
561			enabled := true
562			cfg.LocalSync.LocalTimeZone = &enabled
563		}
564		if o.DebugDir != "" {
565			cfg.Logging.DebugDir = o.DebugDir
566		}
567	
568		// Version check (--no-version-check sets enabled to false)
569		if o.NoVersionCheck {
570			disabled := false
571			cfg.VersionCheck.Enabled = &disabled
572		}
573	
574		// Cloud sync (--no-cloud-sync sets enabled to false)
575		if o.NoCloudSync {
576			disabled := false
577			cfg.CloudSync.Enabled = &disabled
578		}
579	
580		// Local sync (--only-cloud-sync sets enabled to false)
581		if o.OnlyCloudSync {
582			disabled := false
583			cfg.LocalSync.Enabled = &disabled
584		}
585	
586		// Logging flags only override if explicitly set (true)
587		if o.Console {
588			enabled := true
589			cfg.Logging.Console = &enabled
590		}
591		if o.Log {
592			enabled := true
593			cfg.Logging.Log = &enabled
594		}
595		if o.Debug {
596			enabled := true
597			cfg.Logging.Debug = &enabled
598		}
599		if o.Silent {
600			enabled := true
601			cfg.Logging.Silent = &enabled
602		}
603	
604		// Analytics (--no-usage-analytics sets enabled to false)
605		if o.NoAnalytics {
606			disabled := false
607			cfg.Analytics.Enabled = &disabled
608		}
609	
610		// Telemetry
611		if o.TelemetryEndpoint != "" {
612			cfg.Telemetry.Endpoint = o.TelemetryEndpoint
613		}
614		if o.TelemetryServiceName != "" {
615			cfg.Telemetry.ServiceName = o.TelemetryServiceName
616		}
617		if o.NoTelemetryPrompts {
618			disabled := false
619			cfg.Telemetry.Prompts = &disabled
620		}
621	}
622

671			return *c.Logging.Log

703	// IsTelemetryEnabled returns whether telemetry should be enabled.

706	func (c *Config) IsTelemetryEnabled() bool {
707		if disabled := os.Getenv("OTEL_SDK_DISABLED"); disabled == "true" || disabled == "1" {

758	
759	// SaveResumePrefs persists the resume view mode and last-resumed agent to the
760	// user-level config (~/.specstory/cli/config.toml). It upserts ONLY the [resume]
761	// section, preserving the rest of the file (comments and other sections), so the
762	// self-documenting template stays intact. Pass an empty value to leave that key out.
763	func SaveResumePrefs(viewMode, lastAgent string) error {
764		path := getUserConfigPath()
765		if path == "" {
766			return fmt.Errorf("could not determine user config path")
767		}
768		if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(path), 0o755); err != nil {
769			return fmt.Errorf("creating config directory: %w", err)
770		}
771	
772		data, err := os.ReadFile(path)
773		if err != nil {
774			if !os.IsNotExist(err) {
775				return fmt.Errorf("reading config: %w", err)
776			}
777			data = []byte(processTemplate(defaultConfigTemplate, "user"))
778		}
779	
780		updated := upsertResumeSection(string(data), viewMode, lastAgent)
781		if err := os.WriteFile(path, []byte(updated), 0o644); err != nil {
782			return fmt.Errorf("writing config: %w", err)
783		}
784		return nil
785	}
786	
787	// upsertResumeSection replaces the [resume] section of a TOML document with active
788	// view_mode/last_agent keys, preserving every other line. If no [resume] section
789	// exists it is appended. Only the [resume] block is rewritten — other sections and
790	// their comments are untouched.
791	func upsertResumeSection(content, viewMode, lastAgent string) string {
792		var block strings.Builder
793		block.WriteString("[resume]\n")
794		if viewMode != "" {
795			fmt.Fprintf(&block, "view_mode = %q\n", viewMode)
796		}
797		if lastAgent != "" {
798			fmt.Fprintf(&block, "last_agent = %q\n", lastAgent)
799		}
800	
801		lines := strings.Split(content, "\n")
802		start := -1
803		for i, ln := range lines {
804			if strings.TrimSpace(ln) == "[resume]" {
805				start = i
806				break
807			}
808		}
809	
810		// No existing [resume] section — append it after a blank-line separator.
811		if start == -1 {
812			out := strings.TrimRight(content, "\n")
813			if out != "" {
814				out += "\n\n"
815			}
816			return out + block.String()
817		}
818	
819		// Replace from [resume] up to (but not including) the next top-level section.
820		end := len(lines)
821		for j := start + 1; j < len(lines); j++ {
822			t := strings.TrimSpace(lines[j])
823			if strings.HasPrefix(t, "[") && strings.HasSuffix(t, "]") {
824				end = j
825				break
826			}
827		}
828	
829		var b strings.Builder
830		for i := 0; i < start; i++ {
831			b.WriteString(lines[i])
832			b.WriteString("\n")
833		}
834		b.WriteString(block.String())
835		for i := end; i < len(lines); i++ {
836			b.WriteString(lines[i])
837			if i < len(lines)-1 {
838				b.WriteString("\n")
839			}
840		}
841		return b.String()
842	}

850			return c.Providers.ClaudeCmd
```

```text
19	func reconstructSampleData() *schema.SessionData {
20		fm := "Created `hello.rs`:\n\n```rust\nfn main() { println!(\"Hello, World!\"); }\n```"
21		return &schema.SessionData{
22			SchemaVersion: "1.0",
23			Provider:      schema.ProviderInfo{ID: "cursor", Name: "Cursor CLI", Version: "x"},
24			SessionID:     "orig-cursor-123",
25			CreatedAt:     "2026-06-18T10:00:00.000Z",
26			WorkspaceRoot: "/tmp/proj",
27			Slug:          "hello world in rust",
28			Exchanges: []schema.Exchange{
29				{
30					ExchangeID: "orig-cursor-123:0",
31					Messages: []schema.Message{
32						{Role: schema.RoleUser, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "Create a hello world in Rust."}}},
33						{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "I'll create it."}}},
34						{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Tool: &schema.ToolInfo{Name: "write", Type: schema.ToolTypeWrite, FormattedMarkdown: strptr(fm)}},
35						{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "Done."}}},
36					},
37				},
38			},
39		}

42	// openStoreBytes writes the reconstructed store.db bytes to a temp file and reads
43	// back its blobs + meta head record.
44	func openStoreBytes(t *testing.T, content []byte) (map[string][]byte, map[string]interface{}) {
45		t.Helper()
46		path := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "store.db")
47		if err := os.WriteFile(path, content, 0o644); err != nil {
48			t.Fatalf("write store: %v", err)
49		}
50		db, err := sql.Open("sqlite", path+"?mode=ro")
51		if err != nil {
52			t.Fatalf("open store: %v", err)
53		}
54		defer func() { _ = db.Close() }()
55	
56		blobs := map[string][]byte{}
57		rows, err := db.Query("SELECT id, data FROM blobs")
58		if err != nil {
59			t.Fatalf("query blobs: %v", err)
60		}
61		for rows.Next() {
62			var id string
63			var data []byte
64			if err := rows.Scan(&id, &data); err != nil {
65				t.Fatalf("scan: %v", err)
66			}
67			blobs[id] = data
68		}
69		_ = rows.Close()
70	
71		var metaHex string
72		if err := db.QueryRow("SELECT value FROM meta WHERE key='0'").Scan(&metaHex); err != nil {
73			t.Fatalf("meta: %v", err)
74		}
75		metaBytes, err := hex.DecodeString(metaHex)
76		if err != nil {
77			t.Fatalf("meta hex: %v", err)
78		}
79		var meta map[string]interface{}
80		if err := json.Unmarshal(metaBytes, &meta); err != nil {
81			t.Fatalf("meta json: %v", err)
82		}
83		return blobs, meta
84	}
85	
86	// refsOf scans a protobuf node for 32-byte blob-id references in the given field.
87	func refsOf(node []byte, fieldNum int) []string {
88		tag := byte(fieldNum<<3 | 2)
89		var refs []string
90		for i := 0; i+34 <= len(node); i++ {
91			if node[i] == tag && node[i+1] == 0x20 {
92				refs = append(refs, hex.EncodeToString(node[i+2:i+34]))
93			}
94		}
95		return refs
96	}
97	
98	// TestReconstructSession_Structure validates the store.db has the content-addressed
99	// shape Cursor requires: sha256 ids, a head pointer, and a root whose field-1 lists
100	// the message blobs in order with a field-2 metadata node.
101	func TestReconstructSession_Structure(t *testing.T) {
102		data := reconstructSampleData()
103		expected := spi.FlattenSessionData(data, "") // user + 3 agent turns
104	
105		out, err := NewProvider().ReconstructSession(data, spi.ReconstructOptions{WorkspaceRoot: "/tmp/proj"})
106		if err != nil {
107			t.Fatalf("ReconstructSession: %v", err)
108		}
109		if out.SessionID == "" || filepath.Base(out.Filename) != "store.db" {
110			t.Fatalf("unexpected id/filename: %q / %q", out.SessionID, out.Filename)
111		}
112	
113		blobs, meta := openStoreBytes(t, out.Content)
114	
115		// Every blob is content-addressed by sha256.
116		for id, d := range blobs {
117			if sum := sha256.Sum256(d); hex.EncodeToString(sum[:]) != id {
118				t.Errorf("blob id %s != sha256(data)", id)
119			}
120		}
121	
122		head, _ := meta["latestRootBlobId"].(string)
123		root, ok := blobs[head]
124		if !ok {
125			t.Fatalf("head %q not present in blobs", head)
126		}
127		if meta["agentId"] != out.SessionID {
128			t.Errorf("meta agentId %v != session id %q", meta["agentId"], out.SessionID)
129		}
130	
131		// Root field 1 = message blobs in order; verify roles + text match the turns.
132		msgRefs := refsOf(root, 1)
133		if len(msgRefs) != len(expected) {
134			t.Fatalf("root references %d messages, want %d", len(msgRefs), len(expected))
135		}
136		for i, ref := range msgRefs {
137			var m struct {
138				Role    string `json:"role"`
139				Content []struct {
140					Text string `json:"text"`
141				} `json:"content"`
142			}
143			if err := json.Unmarshal(blobs[ref], &m); err != nil {
144				t.Fatalf("message %d not JSON: %v", i, err)
145			}
146			wantRole := "assistant"
147			if expected[i].Role == schema.RoleUser {
148				wantRole = "user"
149			}
150			if m.Role != wantRole {
151				t.Errorf("message %d role = %q, want %q", i, m.Role, wantRole)
152			}
153			if len(m.Content) == 0 || m.Content[0].Text != expected[i].Text {
154				t.Errorf("message %d text mismatch:\n got: %q\nwant: %q", i, m.Content, expected[i].Text)
155			}
156		}
157	
158		// Root field 2 = metadata node, which must resolve.
159		metaRefs := refsOf(root, 2)
160		if len(metaRefs) != 1 || blobs[metaRefs[0]] == nil {
161			t.Errorf("root should reference exactly one metadata node; got %v", metaRefs)
162		}
163	}
164	
165	func TestNativeSessionPath(t *testing.T) {
166		path, err := NewProvider().NativeSessionPath(t.TempDir(), filepath.Join("sess-id", "store.db"))
167		if err != nil {
168			t.Fatalf("NativeSessionPath: %v", err)
169		}
170		if !strings.Contains(path, filepath.Join(".cursor", "chats")) {
171			t.Errorf("path %q should be under .cursor/chats", path)
172		}
173		if !strings.HasSuffix(path, filepath.Join("sess-id", "store.db")) {
174			t.Errorf("path %q should end with <session-id>/store.db", path)
175		}
176	}
177	
178	func TestReconstructSession_Empty(t *testing.T) {
179		_, err := NewProvider().ReconstructSession(&schema.SessionData{SessionID: "x", WorkspaceRoot: "/tmp"}, spi.ReconstructOptions{})
180		if err == nil {
181			t.Fatal("expected error reconstructing a session with no content")
182		}
183	}
184
```

```text
43

47		}

49			t.Fatalf("unexpected id/filename: %q / %q", out.SessionID, out.Filename)
50		}
51	
52		var session dsSession

54			t.Fatalf("reconstructed content is not valid dsSession JSON: %v", err)
55		}

58		}

61		}

66		}

71		}

74				t.Errorf("turn %d mismatch:\n got: %+v\nwant: %+v", i, actual[i], expected[i])
75			}
76		}
77	}
```

```text
29						{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "I'll create hello.fs."}}},
30						{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeThinking, Text: "Forth uses dot-quote for strings."}}},
31						{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Tool: &schema.ToolInfo{Name: "write_file", Type: schema.ToolTypeWrite, FormattedMarkdown: strptr(fm)}},

39	// TestReconstructSession_RoundTrip reconstructs to native Gemini chat JSON, re-parses
40	// it through the forward path, and asserts the flattened transcript is preserved.
41	func TestReconstructSession_RoundTrip(t *testing.T) {
42		data := reconstructSampleData()
43		expected := spi.FlattenSessionData(data, "")
44	
45		out, err := NewProvider().ReconstructSession(data, spi.ReconstructOptions{WorkspaceRoot: "/tmp/proj"})
46		if err != nil {
47			t.Fatalf("ReconstructSession: %v", err)
48		}
49		if out.SessionID == "" {
50			t.Fatal("expected a fresh session ID")
51		}
52		if !strings.HasPrefix(out.Filename, "session-") || !strings.HasSuffix(out.Filename, ".json") {
53			t.Errorf("filename %q should look like session-<ts>-<id>.json", out.Filename)
54		}
55	
56		var session GeminiSession
57		if err := json.Unmarshal(out.Content, &session); err != nil {
58			t.Fatalf("reconstructed content is not valid GeminiSession JSON: %v", err)
59		}
60		if session.ID != out.SessionID {
61			t.Errorf("session sessionId = %q, want %q", session.ID, out.SessionID)
62		}
63	
64		regenerated, err := GenerateAgentSession(&session, "/tmp/proj")
65		if err != nil {
66			t.Fatalf("GenerateAgentSession: %v", err)
67		}
68	
69		actual := spi.FlattenSessionData(regenerated, "")
70		if len(actual) != len(expected) {
71			t.Fatalf("round-trip produced %d turns, want %d\n got: %+v\nwant: %+v", len(actual), len(expected), actual, expected)
72		}
73		for i := range expected {
74			if actual[i] != expected[i] {
75				t.Errorf("turn %d mismatch:\n got: %+v\nwant: %+v", i, actual[i], expected[i])
76			}
77		}
78	}
79	
80	// TestNativeSessionPath_CreatesProjectDir verifies that, for a project Gemini has
81	// not seen, NativeSessionPath creates the tmp project dir with a .project_root
82	// marker and returns a path under chats/.
83	func TestNativeSessionPath_CreatesProjectDir(t *testing.T) {
84		home := t.TempDir()
85		project := t.TempDir()
86	
87		originalHome := osUserHomeDir
88		osUserHomeDir = func() (string, error) { return home, nil }
89		t.Cleanup(func() { osUserHomeDir = originalHome })
90	
91		path, err := NewProvider().NativeSessionPath(project, "session-x.json")
92		if err != nil {
93			t.Fatalf("NativeSessionPath: %v", err)
94		}
95		if filepath.Base(path) != "session-x.json" {
96			t.Errorf("path %q should end with the filename", path)
97		}
98		if filepath.Base(filepath.Dir(path)) != "chats" {
99			t.Errorf("path %q should be under a chats/ dir", path)
100		}
101		// The .project_root marker should exist alongside chats/ and point at the project.
102		rootFile := filepath.Join(filepath.Dir(filepath.Dir(path)), ".project_root")
103		content, err := os.ReadFile(rootFile)
104		if err != nil {
105			t.Fatalf("expected .project_root marker: %v", err)
106		}
107		if !strings.Contains(string(content), filepath.Base(project)) {
108			t.Errorf(".project_root %q should reference the project path", string(content))
109		}
110	}
111
```

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 2026-06-29 15:27:58Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./pkg/analytics/events.go`

```text
1	package analytics
2	
3	import (
4		"os"
5	
6		"github.com/posthog/posthog-go"
7	)
8	
9	// Event name constants
10	const (
11		EventExtensionActivated     = "ext_activated"                // Tracks when `run` command is started
12		EventWatchActivated         = "ext_watch_activated"          // Tracks when `watch` command is started
13		EventResumeActivated        = "ext_resume_activated"         // Tracks when `resume` command is started
14		EventReindexCompleted       = "ext_reindex_completed"        // Tracks when `reindex` finishes building the restore index
15		EventProjectIdentityCreated = "ext_project_identity_created" // Tracks when new project identity is created
16		EventCheckInstallSuccess    = "ext_check_install_success"    // Tracks successful agent installation check
17		EventCheckInstallFailed     = "ext_check_install_failed"     // Tracks failed agent installation check
18		EventVersionCommand         = "ext_version_command"          // Tracks when users check the version
19		EventHelpCommand            = "ext_help_command"             // Tracks when users view help
20		EventAutosaveNew            = "ext_autosave_new"             // Tracks when a new markdown file is created during `run` or `--sync-markdown`
21		EventAutosaveSuccess        = "ext_autosave_success"         // Tracks when a markdown file is updated during `run`
22		EventAutosaveError          = "ext_autosave_error"           // Tracks when an error occurs generating or writing markdown during `run`
23		EventSyncMarkdownNew        = "ext_sync_markdown_new"        // Tracks when a new markdown file is created during `sync`
24		EventSyncMarkdownSuccess    = "ext_sync_markdown_success"    // Tracks when a markdown file is updated during `sync`
25		EventSyncMarkdownError      = "ext_sync_markdown_error"      // Tracks when an error occurs generating or writing markdown during `sync`
26		EventLoginAttempted         = "ext_login_attempted"          // Tracks when user starts the login flow
27		EventLoginCancelled         = "ext_login_cancelled"          // Tracks when user cancels the login flow
28		EventLoginSuccess           = "ext_login_success"            // Tracks successful login to SpecStory Cloud
29		EventLoginFailed            = "ext_login_failed"             // Tracks failed login attempts
30		EventLogout                 = "ext_logout"                   // Tracks user logout from SpecStory Cloud
31		EventCloudSyncComplete      = "ext_cloudsync_complete"       // Tracks cloud sync completion with statistics
32		EventSyncStatsComplete      = "ext_sync_stats_complete"      // Tracks when --only-stats sync completes
33		EventListSessions           = "ext_list_sessions"            // Tracks when users list sessions with the list command
34	)
35	
36	// Properties is a type alias for event properties to avoid exposing PostHog types
37	type Properties map[string]interface{}
38	
39	// TrackEvent sends a generic event to PostHog
40	func TrackEvent(eventName string, properties Properties) {
41		if !IsEnabled() {
42			return
43		}
44	
45		distinctID := GetDistinctID()
46	
47		// Convert to PostHog properties
48		phProperties := make(posthog.Properties)
49		for k, v := range properties {
50			phProperties[k] = v
51		}
52	
53		// Always include CLI command, device ID, and project path
54		phProperties["cli_command"] = GetCLICommand()
55		phProperties["$device_id"] = distinctID
56	
57		// Capture current working directory as project_path
58		if cwd, err := os.Getwd(); err == nil {
59			phProperties["project_path"] = cwd
60		}
61	
62		// Agent provider information
63		phProperties["agent_provider"] = GetAgentProviderName()
64	
65		// Editor information
66		phProperties["editor_name"] = "SpecStory CLI"
67		phProperties["editor_type"] = "CLI"
68		phProperties["extension_version"] = GetCLIVersion()
69	
70		// OS information (gathered once during initialization)
71		osArch, osName, osPlatform, osVersion := GetOSInfo()
72		phProperties["os_arch"] = osArch
73		phProperties["os_name"] = osName
74		phProperties["os_platform"] = osPlatform
75		phProperties["os_version"] = osVersion
76	
77		err := client.Enqueue(posthog.Capture{
78			DistinctId:       distinctID,
79			Event:            eventName,
80			Properties:       phProperties,
81			SendFeatureFlags: posthog.SendFeatureFlags(false),
82		})
83	
84		if err != nil {
85			// Silently fail - analytics should not break the app
86			return
87		}
88	}
89
```

```text
656	// ListAllAgentChatSessions enumerates every session in this provider's native store,
657	// regardless of project. See docs/SESSIONS-DB.md.
658	//
659	// Cursor stores sessions at ~/.cursor/chats/<projectHash>/<sessionID>/store.db, where
660	// projectHash = md5(canonical project path). Cursor records NO workspace path inside
661	// the store (the meta blob has only agent/model fields), and md5 is one-way — so
662	// OriginCwd cannot be filled from the store alone and is left empty here. The hash is
663	// embedded in NativePath, so `specstory reindex` recovers the cwd by reverse-matching
664	// that hash against the project paths the other providers surface.
665	func (p *Provider) ListAllAgentChatSessions() ([]spi.GlobalSessionRef, error) {
666		chatsDir, err := GetCursorChatsDir()
667		if err != nil {
668			return nil, err
669		}
670		hashEntries, err := os.ReadDir(chatsDir)
671		if err != nil {
672			if os.IsNotExist(err) {
673				return []spi.GlobalSessionRef{}, nil
674			}
675			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to read cursor chats directory: %w", err)
676		}
677	
678		var refs []spi.GlobalSessionRef
679		for _, hashEntry := range hashEntries {
680			if !hashEntry.IsDir() {
681				continue
682			}
683			hashDir := filepath.Join(chatsDir, hashEntry.Name())
684			sessionIDs, err := GetCursorSessionDirs(hashDir)
685			if err != nil {
686				continue
687			}
688			for _, sessionID := range sessionIDs {
689				if !HasStoreDB(hashDir, sessionID) {
690					continue
691				}
692				sessionPath := filepath.Join(hashDir, sessionID)
693				metadata, err := extractCursorSessionMetadata(sessionPath, sessionID)
694				if err != nil {
695					slog.Warn("reindex: failed to extract cursor session metadata", "sessionID", sessionID, "error", err)
696					continue
697				}
698				if metadata == nil {
699					continue // empty session
700				}
701				refs = append(refs, spi.GlobalSessionRef{
702					SessionID:  metadata.SessionID,
703					CreatedAt:  metadata.CreatedAt,
704					Slug:       metadata.Slug,
705					Name:       metadata.Name,
706					NativePath: filepath.Join(sessionPath, "store.db"),
707					OriginCwd:  "", // not stored by Cursor; recovered at reindex via the hash in NativePath
708				})
709			}
710		}
711		return refs, nil
712	}
713
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
420	// ListAllAgentChatSessions enumerates every session in this provider's native store,
421	// regardless of project. See docs/SESSIONS-DB.md.
422	//
423	// Gemini associates a tmp directory (~/.gemini/tmp/<hash>/) with a project via a
424	// .project_root marker file holding the project path, which is the originating cwd
425	// for every session in that directory.
426	func (p *Provider) ListAllAgentChatSessions() ([]spi.GlobalSessionRef, error) {
427		tmpDir, err := GetGeminiTmpDir()
428		if err != nil {
429			return nil, err
430		}
431		entries, err := os.ReadDir(tmpDir)
432		if err != nil {
433			if os.IsNotExist(err) {
434				return []spi.GlobalSessionRef{}, nil
435			}
436			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to read Gemini tmp directory: %w", err)
437		}
438	
439		var refs []spi.GlobalSessionRef
440		for _, entry := range entries {
441			if !entry.IsDir() {
442				continue
443			}
444			dir := filepath.Join(tmpDir, entry.Name())
445	
446			// The .project_root marker holds the project path — the originating cwd shared
447			// by every session in this tmp directory.
448			var cwd string
449			if content, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(dir, ".project_root")); err == nil {
450				cwd = strings.TrimSpace(string(content))
451			}
452	
453			sessions, err := FindSessions(dir)
454			if err != nil {
455				slog.Debug("reindex: failed to find gemini sessions", "dir", dir, "error", err)
456				continue
457			}
458			for _, session := range sessions {
459				metadata := extractGeminiSessionMetadata(session)
460				if metadata == nil {
461					continue // empty session
462				}
463				refs = append(refs, spi.GlobalSessionRef{
464					SessionID:  metadata.SessionID,
465					CreatedAt:  metadata.CreatedAt,
466					Slug:       metadata.Slug,
467					Name:       metadata.Name,
468					NativePath: session.FilePath,
469					OriginCwd:  cwd,
470				})
471			}
472		}
473		return refs, nil
474	}
```

```text
490	func isSyntheticMessage(content string) bool {
491		// warmup and the <TEXTBLOCK> title-generation wrapper can be embedded anywhere
492		// in the record, so match them with Contains.
493		if strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(content), "warmup") {
494			return true
495		}
496		if strings.Contains(content, "<TEXTBLOCK>") {
497			return true
498		}
499		// The slash-command scaffolding tags (shared with reconstruction via
500		// spi.SyntheticCommandTags) and the interrupt marker REPLACE the prompt, so they
501		// appear at the very START of the record. Match by prefix so a real prompt that
502		// merely mentions a tag mid-sentence is still treated as real. The interrupt
503		// marker's "…for tool use" variant is caught by the same prefix.
504		trimmed := strings.TrimSpace(content)
505		for _, marker := range spi.SyntheticCommandTags {
506			if strings.HasPrefix(trimmed, marker) {
507				return true
508			}
509		}
510		return strings.HasPrefix(trimmed, "[Request interrupted by user")
511	}
512	
513	// extractContentText extracts plain text from a message content field.
514	// The content field may be either a plain string or a JSON array of typed content
515	// blocks (e.g. [{type:"text",text:"..."},{type:"tool_use",...}]).
516	// When it's an array, all "text"-type blocks are joined with newlines.
517	// This handles the Claude Code JSONL format where IDE context tags (e.g.
518	// <ide_opened_file>) are injected as separate text blocks before the real question.
519	func extractContentText(content interface{}) string {
520		switch v := content.(type) {
521		case string:
522			return v
523		case []interface{}:
524			var parts []string
525			for _, block := range v {
526				if m, ok := block.(map[string]interface{}); ok {
527					if t, _ := m["type"].(string); t == "text" {
528						if text, ok := m["text"].(string); ok && text != "" {
529							parts = append(parts, text)
530						}
531					}
532				}
533			}
534			slog.Debug("extractContentText: extracted text from content block array",
535				"blockCount", len(v),
536				"textParts", len(parts))
537			return strings.Join(parts, "\n")
538		default:
539			return ""
540		}
541	}
542	
543	// findFirstUserMessage finds the first user message in a session for slug generation
544	// Returns empty string if no suitable user message is found
545	func findFirstUserMessage(session Session) string {
546		for _, record := range session.Records {
547			// Check if this is a user message
548			if recordType, ok := record.Data["type"].(string); !ok || recordType != "user" {
549				continue
550			}
551	
552			// Skip meta user messages
553			if isMeta, ok := record.Data["isMeta"].(bool); ok && isMeta {
554				continue
555			}
556	
557			// Extract content from message - content may be a string or a list of blocks
558			if message, ok := record.Data["message"].(map[string]interface{}); ok {
559				content := extractContentText(message["content"])
560				if content != "" {
561					// Skip synthetic messages (warmup, title generation prompts, etc.)
562					if isSyntheticMessage(content) {
563						continue
564					}
565					return content
566				}
567			}
568		}
569		return ""
570	}
571	
572	// FileSlugFromRootRecord generates the slug part of the filename from the session
573	// Returns the human-readable slug derived from the first user message, or empty string if none
574	func FileSlugFromRootRecord(session Session) string {
575		// Find the first user message and generate slug from it
576		firstUserMessage := findFirstUserMessage(session)
577		slog.Debug("FileSlugFromRootRecord: First user message",
578			"sessionId", session.SessionUuid,
579			"message", firstUserMessage)
580	
581		slug := spi.GenerateFilenameFromUserMessage(firstUserMessage)
582		slog.Debug("FileSlugFromRootRecord: Generated slug",
583			"sessionId", session.SessionUuid,
584			"slug", slug)
585	
586		return slug
587	}
```

```text
625					"file", filePath,
```
