```text
641	
642	// claudeSessionScan holds the minimal fields read from a session file in one pass:
643	// identity + first-message metadata, plus the originating cwd (Claude Code stamps a
644	// top-level "cwd" on every conversational record). foundRealMessage is false for
645	// warmup-only sessions (no real messages).
646	type claudeSessionScan struct {
647		sessionID        string
648		timestamp        string
649		firstUserMessage string
650		commandFallback  string // slash command title when there is no free-text prompt
651		cwd              string
652		foundRealMessage bool
653	}
654	
655	// scanClaudeSession reads minimal data from a session file: session id, first-real
656	// timestamp, first user message, and originating cwd. Shared by the project-scoped
657	// metadata path and the global enumeration (ListAllAgentChatSessions).
658	func scanClaudeSession(filePath string) (*claudeSessionScan, error) {
659		file, err := os.Open(filePath)
660		if err != nil {
661			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to open session file: %w", err)
662		}
663		defer func() {
664			_ = file.Close()
665		}()
666	
667		reader := bufio.NewReader(file)
668		scan := &claudeSessionScan{}
669	
670		// Read records until we find everything we need.
671		// Why: ReadString can return data AND io.EOF on the last line (no trailing newline),
672		// so we always process the line first, then check for EOF once at the bottom.
673		lineNum := 0
674		for {
675			line, readErr := reader.ReadString('\n')
676			if readErr != nil && readErr != io.EOF {
677				return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to read line: %w", readErr)
678			}
679	
680			lineNum++
681			line = strings.TrimSpace(line)
682	
683			if line != "" {
684				// Parse JSON record
685				var record map[string]interface{}
686				if jsonErr := json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &record); jsonErr != nil {
687					slog.Warn("Skipping malformed JSONL line",
688						"file", filepath.Base(filePath),
689						"line", lineNum,
690						"error", jsonErr)
691				} else {
692					// Extract session ID (from any record)
693					if scan.sessionID == "" {
694						if sid, ok := record["sessionId"].(string); ok {
695							scan.sessionID = sid
696						}
697					}
698	
699					// Capture the originating cwd (first record that carries one). This is
700					// the input to project-identity resolution for the restore index.
701					if scan.cwd == "" {
702						if cwd, ok := record["cwd"].(string); ok && cwd != "" {
703							scan.cwd = cwd
704						}
705					}
706	
707					// Only process non-sidechain, non-system records for message extraction
708					isSidechain, _ := record["isSidechain"].(bool)
709					recordType, hasType := record["type"].(string)
710					isSystemRecord := hasType && (recordType == "file-history-snapshot" || recordType == "file-change")
711	
712					if !isSidechain && !isSystemRecord {
713						scan.foundRealMessage = true
714						// created_at = the first real record that actually carries a timestamp.
715						// (The leading {mode,sessionId,type} record has none, so we can't gate
716						// timestamp capture on "first real record" — it would stay empty.)
717						if scan.timestamp == "" {
718							if ts, ok := record["timestamp"].(string); ok && ts != "" {
719								scan.timestamp = ts
720							}
721						}
722	
723						// Extract first user message for slug (if this is a user message)
724						// Content may be a string or a list of typed blocks (see extractContentText)
725						if scan.firstUserMessage == "" && hasType && recordType == "user" {
726							isMeta, _ := record["isMeta"].(bool)
727							if !isMeta {
728								if message, ok := record["message"].(map[string]interface{}); ok {
729									content := extractContentText(message["content"])
730									if content != "" {
731										if isSyntheticMessage(content) {
732											// A slash-command session may have no free-text prompt;
733											// remember the command name as a fallback title.
734											if scan.commandFallback == "" {
735												scan.commandFallback = extractCommandName(content)
736											}
737										} else {
738											scan.firstUserMessage = content
739										}
740									}
741								}
742							}
743						}
744					}
745				}
746			}
747	
748			// Single exit: found everything we need, or reached end of file
749			if (scan.sessionID != "" && scan.timestamp != "" && scan.firstUserMessage != "" && scan.cwd != "") || readErr == io.EOF {
750				break
751			}
752		}
753	
754		// Fall back to the slash command when there was no real user prompt (so a
755		// command-only session shows e.g. "/code-review" instead of its UUID).
756		if scan.firstUserMessage == "" {
757			scan.firstUserMessage = scan.commandFallback
758		}
759		return scan, nil
760	}
761	
762	// extractCommandName pulls the slash command from a Claude Code command record, e.g.
763	// "<command-name>/code-review</command-name>…" -> "/code-review". Falls back to the
764	// bare command-message name (prefixed with "/"). Returns "" when none is present.
765	func extractCommandName(content string) string {
766		if c := between(content, "<command-name>", "</command-name>"); c != "" {
767			return c
768		}
769		if c := between(content, "<command-message>", "</command-message>"); c != "" {
770			return "/" + c
771		}
772		return ""
773	}
774	
775	// between returns the text between the first open/close markers, trimmed, or "".
776	func between(s, openTag, closeTag string) string {
777		_, after, ok := strings.Cut(s, openTag)
778		if !ok {
779			return ""
780		}
781		inner, _, ok := strings.Cut(after, closeTag)
782		if !ok {
783			return ""
784		}
785		return strings.TrimSpace(inner)
786	}
787	
788	// extractSessionMetadata reads minimal data from a session file to extract metadata
789	// Returns nil if the session is warmup-only (no real messages)
790	func extractSessionMetadata(filePath string) (*spi.SessionMetadata, error) {
791		scan, err := scanClaudeSession(filePath)
792		if err != nil {
793			return nil, err
794		}
795	
796		// If no real message was found, this is a warmup-only session
797		if !scan.foundRealMessage {
798			return nil, nil
799		}
800	
801		return &spi.SessionMetadata{
802			SessionID: scan.sessionID,
803			CreatedAt: scan.timestamp,
804			Slug:      spi.GenerateFilenameFromUserMessage(scan.firstUserMessage),
805			Name:      spi.GenerateReadableName(scan.firstUserMessage),
806		}, nil
807	}
808	
809	// ListAllAgentChatSessions enumerates every Claude Code session across all projects. It is the
810	// no-progress form of ListAllAgentChatSessionsProgress.
811	func (p *Provider) ListAllAgentChatSessions() ([]spi.GlobalSessionRef, error) {
812		return p.ListAllAgentChatSessionsProgress(nil)
813	}
814	
815	// ListAllAgentChatSessionsProgress enumerates every Claude Code session across all projects by
816	// walking ~/.claude/projects/*/ for *.jsonl (the originating cwd comes from inside each session;
817	// the project directory name is a lossy, irreversible encoding of the path), reporting scan
818	// progress into r (nil-safe). Headers are scanned in parallel across CPUs; output order is
819	// irrelevant (reindex dedups and sorts later). Implements spi.ProgressEnumerator. See
820	// docs/SESSIONS-DB.md.
821	func (p *Provider) ListAllAgentChatSessionsProgress(r *spi.ScanReporter) ([]spi.GlobalSessionRef, error) {
822		projectsDir, err := GetClaudeCodeProjectsDir()
823		if err != nil {
824			// No projects directory yet → nothing to enumerate (not an error).
825			return []spi.GlobalSessionRef{}, nil
826		}
827	
828		// Phase 1: collect file paths (dirents only — no opens).
829		var paths []string
830		walkErr := filepath.WalkDir(projectsDir, func(path string, d os.DirEntry, err error) error {
831			if err != nil {
832				return nil // skip unreadable entries rather than abort the whole sweep
833			}
834			if d.IsDir() || !strings.HasSuffix(path, ".jsonl") {
835				return nil
836			}
837			paths = append(paths, path)
838			return nil
839		})
840		if walkErr != nil {
841			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to walk claude projects: %w", walkErr)
842		}
843	
844		// Phase 2: scan session headers in parallel — each file's read+parse is independent and
845		// CPU-bound, so this scales across cores.
846		workers := min(runtime.NumCPU(), 12)
847		if workers < 1 {
848			workers = 1
849		}
850		pathCh := make(chan string, workers)
851		var refs []spi.GlobalSessionRef
852		var mu sync.Mutex
853		var wg sync.WaitGroup
854		for i := 0; i < workers; i++ {
855			wg.Add(1)
856			go func() {
857				defer wg.Done()
858				for path := range pathCh {
859					scan, scanErr := scanClaudeSession(path)
860					if scanErr != nil {
861						slog.Warn("reindex: failed to scan claude session", "file", path, "error", scanErr)
862						continue
863					}
864					if !scan.foundRealMessage {
865						continue // warmup-only or sidechain-only transcript (not a session)
866					}
867					ref := spi.GlobalSessionRef{
868						SessionID:  scan.sessionID,
869						CreatedAt:  scan.timestamp,
870						Slug:       spi.GenerateFilenameFromUserMessage(scan.firstUserMessage),
871						Name:       spi.GenerateReadableName(scan.firstUserMessage),
872						NativePath: path,
873						OriginCwd:  scan.cwd,
874					}
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1068	// TestComputeProjectID is the executable spec for walk-up identity resolution:
1069	// the monorepo-subdir + two-clone scenario, plus worktree and remote-less cases.
1070	func TestComputeProjectID(t *testing.T) {
1071		// newRepo creates a temp dir with a .git/config carrying the given origin url
1072		// (empty url = a git repo with no remote).
1073		newRepo := func(t *testing.T, url string) string {
1074			t.Helper()
1075			root := t.TempDir()
1076			gitDir := filepath.Join(root, ".git")
1077			if err := os.MkdirAll(gitDir, 0o755); err != nil {
1078				t.Fatal(err)
1079			}
1080			cfg := "[core]\n\tbare = false\n"
1081			if url != "" {
1082				cfg += "[remote \"origin\"]\n\turl = " + url + "\n"
1083			}
1084			if err := os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(gitDir, "config"), []byte(cfg), 0o644); err != nil {
1085				t.Fatal(err)
1086			}
1087			return root
1088		}
1089		mkSub := func(t *testing.T, root, rel string) string {
1090			t.Helper()
1091			p := filepath.Join(root, rel)
1092			if err := os.MkdirAll(p, 0o755); err != nil {
1093				t.Fatal(err)
1094			}
1095			return p
1096		}
1097		compute := func(t *testing.T, dir string) string {
1098			t.Helper()
1099			id, _, err := ComputeProjectID(dir)
1100			if err != nil {
1101				t.Fatalf("ComputeProjectID(%q): %v", dir, err)
1102			}
1103			return id
1104		}

1214	// TestCanonicalizeWorkspacePath verifies the case-fold rule: paths are lowercased on
1215	// case-insensitive filesystems (macOS/Windows) and left byte-exact otherwise.
1216	func TestCanonicalizeWorkspacePath(t *testing.T) {
1217		orig := caseInsensitiveFilesystem
1218		defer func() { caseInsensitiveFilesystem = orig }()
1219	
1220		const mixed = "/Users/Sean/Source/Proj"
1221	
1222		caseInsensitiveFilesystem = true
1223		if got := canonicalizeWorkspacePath(mixed); got != "/users/sean/source/proj" {
1224			t.Errorf("case-insensitive: got %q, want lowercased", got)
1225		}
1226	
1227		caseInsensitiveFilesystem = false
1228		if got := canonicalizeWorkspacePath(mixed); got != mixed {
1229			t.Errorf("case-sensitive: got %q, want byte-exact %q", got, mixed)
1230		}
1231	}
1232	
1233	// TestWorkspaceIDCaseFolding is the regression test for the reported bug: on a
1234	// case-insensitive filesystem, the same physical directory named with different casing
1235	// (e.g. os.Getwd echoing a lowercase $PWD vs a recorded capital-cased cwd) must hash to
1236	// one workspace_id. On a case-sensitive filesystem the two are genuinely distinct.
1237	func TestWorkspaceIDCaseFolding(t *testing.T) {
1238		orig := caseInsensitiveFilesystem
1239		defer func() { caseInsensitiveFilesystem = orig }()
1240	
1241		upper := &ProjectIdentityManager{projectRoot: "/Users/Sean/Source/Proj"}
1242		lower := &ProjectIdentityManager{projectRoot: "/users/sean/source/proj"}
1243	
1244		caseInsensitiveFilesystem = true
1245		if upper.generateWorkspaceID() != lower.generateWorkspaceID() {
1246			t.Error("case-insensitive FS: differently-cased paths produced different workspace ids")
1247		}
1248	
1249		caseInsensitiveFilesystem = false
1250		if upper.generateWorkspaceID() == lower.generateWorkspaceID() {
1251			t.Error("case-sensitive FS: differently-cased paths should yield distinct workspace ids")
1252		}
1253	}
```

 `./pkg/providers/claudecode/path_utils.go`

```text
1	package claudecode
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"log/slog"
6		"os"
7		"path/filepath"
8		"regexp"
9		"strings"
10	)
11	
12	// projectDirNameRegex matches any character that is not alphanumeric or a dash.
13	// Claude Code replaces each such character with a dash to map a working directory
14	// to a project folder name under ~/.claude/projects.
15	var projectDirNameRegex = regexp.MustCompile(`[^a-zA-Z0-9-]`)
16	
17	// encodeProjectDirName converts a real (symlink-resolved) path into Claude Code's
18	// project directory name: non-alphanumeric/dash characters become dashes, with a
19	// guaranteed leading dash. Example: "/Users/sean/app" -> "-Users-sean-app".
20	func encodeProjectDirName(realPath string) string {
21		name := projectDirNameRegex.ReplaceAllString(realPath, "-")
22		if !strings.HasPrefix(name, "-") {
23			name = "-" + name
24		}
25		return name
26	}
27	
28	// resolveClaudeProjectDir returns ~/.claude/projects/<encoded> for the given
29	// project path, resolving symlinks, WITHOUT requiring the directory to exist.
30	// Used when writing a reconstructed session into the store (the caller creates
31	// the directory). Distinct from GetClaudeCodeProjectDir, which requires the
32	// projects directory to already exist.
33	func resolveClaudeProjectDir(projectPath string) (string, error) {
34		homeDir, err := os.UserHomeDir()
35		if err != nil {
36			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get user home directory: %v", err)
37		}
38	
39		cwd := projectPath
40		if cwd == "" {
41			cwd, err = os.Getwd()
42			if err != nil {
43				return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get current working directory: %v", err)
44			}
45		}
46	
47		// Resolve symlinks to match Claude Code's behavior; fall back to the raw path
48		// if resolution fails (e.g., a path component does not exist yet).
49		realPath, err := filepath.EvalSymlinks(cwd)
50		if err != nil {
51			realPath = cwd
52		}
53	
54		return filepath.Join(homeDir, ".claude", "projects", encodeProjectDirName(realPath)), nil
55	}
56	
57	// GetClaudeCodeProjectsDir returns the path to the Claude Code projects directory
58	func GetClaudeCodeProjectsDir() (string, error) {
59		// Get user's home directory
60		homeDir, err := os.UserHomeDir()
61		if err != nil {
62			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get user home directory: %v", err)
63		}
64	
65		// Construct the path to .claude/projects
66		projectsDir := filepath.Join(homeDir, ".claude", "projects")
67	
68		// Check if the directory exists
69		if _, err := os.Stat(projectsDir); err != nil {
70			if os.IsNotExist(err) {
71				return "", fmt.Errorf("claude projects directory not found at %s", projectsDir)
72			}
73			return "", fmt.Errorf("error checking Claude projects directory: %v", err)
74		}
75	
76		return projectsDir, nil
77	}
78	
79	// GetClaudeCodeProjectDir returns the Claude Code project directory for the given path.
80	// If no path is provided (empty string), it uses the current working directory.
81	// It resolves any symlinks in the path to match Claude Code's behavior, which uses the real path
82	// when creating project directories. Claude Code requires a specific naming convention to map
83	// working directories to project folders: each non-alphanumeric character is replaced with a dash
84	// to create a unique, filesystem-safe identifier
85	func GetClaudeCodeProjectDir(projectPath string) (string, error) {
86		// Use provided path or get current working directory
87		var cwd string
88		var err error
89		if projectPath == "" {
90			cwd, err = os.Getwd()
91			if err != nil {
92				return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get current working directory: %v", err)
93			}
94		} else {
95			cwd = projectPath
96		}
97	
98		// Resolve any symlinks in the path to match Claude Code's behavior
99		// Claude Code uses the real path when creating project directories, so
100		// if the user's working directory contains symlinks, we need to resolve
101		// them to find the correct project directory in ~/.claude/projects/
102		realPath, err := filepath.EvalSymlinks(cwd)
103		if err != nil {
104			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to resolve symlinks in working directory: %v", err)
105		}
106	
107		// Log if the path was changed by symlink resolution
108		if realPath != cwd {
109			slog.Debug("GetClaudeCodeProjectDir: Resolved symlinks in working directory",
110				"original", cwd,
111				"resolved", realPath)
112		}
113	
114		// Convert path to project directory format (matching Claude Code's behavior).
115		// Example: "/Users/sean/My Projects(1)/app" becomes "-Users-sean-My-Projects-1--app"
116		projectDirName := encodeProjectDirName(realPath)
117	
118		// Log the transformation for debugging path issues
119		slog.Debug("GetClaudeCodeProjectDir: Transformed working directory to project name",
120			"cwd", cwd,
121			"projectDirName", projectDirName)
122	
123		// Get Claude projects directory
124		projectsDir, err := GetClaudeCodeProjectsDir()
125		if err != nil {
126			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get Claude projects directory: %v", err)
127		}
128	
129		fullProjectPath := filepath.Join(projectsDir, projectDirName)
130		slog.Debug("GetClaudeCodeProjectDir: Constructed full project path",
131			"projectPath", fullProjectPath)
132	
133		return fullProjectPath, nil
134	}
135
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./pkg/providers/claudecode/reconstruct.go`

```text
1	package claudecode
2	
3	import (
4		"bytes"
5		"encoding/json"
6		"fmt"
7		"path/filepath"
8		"time"
9	
10		"github.com/google/uuid"
11	
12		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
13		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
14	)
15	
16	const (
17		// reconstructedClaudeModel is a placeholder model label for reconstructed
18		// assistant records. It is only a historical display value — on resume Claude
19		// Code uses its own configured model for the next turn.
20		reconstructedClaudeModel = "claude-opus-4-8"
21	
22		// reconstructedClaudeVersion mirrors the `version` field Claude Code writes on
23		// its records. A concrete value matching observed sessions maximizes the
24		// chance the loader accepts a synthesized file.
25		reconstructedClaudeVersion = "2.1.178"
26	)
27	
28	// ReconstructSession rebuilds a Claude Code native JSONL session from the neutral
29	// SessionData so `claude --resume <id>` can continue the conversation.
30	//
31	// The forward parser collapses Claude's parentUuid DAG and drops the API
32	// message.id and thinking signatures, so reconstruction synthesizes a fresh,
33	// linear parentUuid chain of plain user/assistant text records (tool calls and
34	// thinking are already flattened into agent text by FlattenSessionData). This is
35	// the structure validated by the Stage 1 spike. See docs/SESSION-PORTABILITY.md.
36	func (p *Provider) ReconstructSession(data *schema.SessionData, opts spi.ReconstructOptions) (*spi.ReconstructedSession, error) {
37		if data == nil {
38			return nil, fmt.Errorf("cannot reconstruct nil session data")
39		}
40	
41		// Prefer the explicit target workspace; fall back to the session's own root.
42		cwd := opts.WorkspaceRoot
43		if cwd == "" {
44			cwd = data.WorkspaceRoot
45		}
46	
47		turns := spi.FlattenSessionData(data, opts.MigrationNote)
48		if len(turns) == 0 {
49			return nil, fmt.Errorf("session has no content to reconstruct")
50		}
51	
52		newID := uuid.NewString()
53		base := time.Now().UTC()
54	
55		var buf bytes.Buffer
56		enc := json.NewEncoder(&buf)
57		// Preserve <, >, & in conversation/markdown text rather than \u-escaping them.
58		enc.SetEscapeHTML(false)
59	
60		var prevUUID string
61		for i, turn := range turns {
62			recUUID := uuid.NewString()
63			ts := base.Add(time.Duration(i) * time.Second).Format("2006-01-02T15:04:05.000Z")
64	
65			rec := map[string]interface{}{
66				"isSidechain": false,
67				"userType":    "external",
68				"cwd":         cwd,
69				"sessionId":   newID,
70				"version":     reconstructedClaudeVersion,
71				"gitBranch":   "",
72				"uuid":        recUUID,
73				"timestamp":   ts,
74			}
75	
76			// Linear chain: the first record roots the DAG (parentUuid null) and
77			// carries provenance back to the source session.
78			if prevUUID == "" {
79				rec["parentUuid"] = nil
80				rec["specstorySourceSessionId"] = data.SessionID
81			} else {
82				rec["parentUuid"] = prevUUID
83			}
84	
85			if turn.Role == schema.RoleUser {
86				rec["type"] = "user"
87				rec["message"] = map[string]interface{}{
88					"role":    "user",
89					"content": turn.Text,
90				}
91			} else {
92				rec["type"] = "assistant"
93				rec["message"] = map[string]interface{}{
94					"id":          "msg_" + recUUID,
95					"type":        "message",
96					"role":        "assistant",
97					"model":       reconstructedClaudeModel,
98					"content":     []map[string]interface{}{{"type": "text", "text": turn.Text}},
99					"stop_reason": "end_turn",
100					"usage":       map[string]interface{}{"input_tokens": 1, "output_tokens": 1},
101				}
102			}
103	
104			if err := enc.Encode(rec); err != nil {
105				return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to encode reconstructed record: %w", err)
106			}
107	
108			prevUUID = recUUID
109		}
110	
111		return &spi.ReconstructedSession{
112			SessionID: newID,
113			Filename:  newID + ".jsonl",
114			Content:   buf.Bytes(),
115		}, nil
116	}
117	
118	// NativeSessionPath returns where a reconstructed session file belongs in Claude
119	// Code's store: ~/.claude/projects/<cwd-encoded>/<filename>. The directory is not
120	// required to exist (the caller creates it).
121	func (p *Provider) NativeSessionPath(projectPath string, filename string) (string, error) {
122		dir, err := resolveClaudeProjectDir(projectPath)
123		if err != nil {
124			return "", err
125		}
126		return filepath.Join(dir, filename), nil
127	}
128
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
func isSyntheticMessage(content string) bool {
	// warmup and the <TEXTBLOCK> title-generation wrapper can be embedded anywhere
	// in the record, so match them with Contains.
	if strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(content), "warmup") {
		return true
	}
	if strings.Contains(content, "<TEXTBLOCK>") {
		return true
	}
	// The slash-command scaffolding tags (shared with reconstruction via
	// spi.SyntheticCommandTags) and the interrupt marker REPLACE the prompt, so they
	// appear at the very START of the record. Match by prefix so a real prompt that
	// merely mentions a tag mid-sentence is still treated as real. The interrupt
	// marker's "…for tool use" variant is caught by the same prefix.
	trimmed := strings.TrimSpace(content)
	for _, marker := range spi.SyntheticCommandTags {
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./main.go`

```text
433				debugRaw, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("debug-raw")
434				useUTC := !localTimeZone
435	
436				// Keep sessions.db current in real time alongside the markdown writes (nil/no-op
437				// if the index can't be opened — never block the running agent on it).
438				liveIndex := cmdpkg.NewLiveIndexer(cwd)
439				defer liveIndex.Close()
440	
441				// This callback pattern enables real-time processing of agent sessions
442				// without blocking the agent's execution. As the agent writes updates to its
443				// data files, the provider's watcher detects changes and invokes this callback,
444				// allowing immediate markdown generation and cloud sync. Errors are logged but
445				// don't stop execution because transient failures (e.g., network issues) shouldn't
446				// interrupt the user's coding session.
447				sessionCallback := func(session *spi.AgentChatSession) {
448					if session == nil {
449						return
450					}
451	
452					// Track the session ID for deep linking on exit
453					lastRunSessionID = session.SessionID
454	
455					// Process the session (write markdown and sync to cloud)
456					// Don't show output during interactive run mode
457					// This is autosave mode (true)
458					_, err := sessionpkg.ProcessSingleSession(context.Background(), session, config, sessionpkg.ProcessingOptions{
459						OnlyCloudSync:      onlyCloudSync,
460						IsAutosave:         true,
461						DebugRaw:           debugRaw,
462						UseUTC:             useUTC,
463						NoTelemetryPrompts: noTelemetryPrompts,
464					})
465					if err != nil {
466						// Log error but continue - don't fail the whole run
467						// In interactive mode, we prioritize keeping the agent running.
468						// Failed markdown writes or cloud syncs can be retried later via
469						// the sync command, so we just log and continue.
470						slog.Error("Failed to process session update",
471							"sessionId", session.SessionID,
472							"error", err)
473					}
474	
475					// Mirror the markdown write into the restore index in real time.
476					liveIndex.Record(providerID, session)
477	
478					// Push agent events to provenance engine for correlation
479					provenance.ProcessEvents(ctx, provenanceEngine, session)
480				}
481	
482				// Execute the agent and watch for updates
483				slog.Info("Starting agent execution and monitoring", "provider", provider.Name())
484				err = provider.ExecAgentAndWatch(cwd, customCmd, resumeSessionID, debugRaw, sessionCallback)
485	
486				if err != nil {
487					slog.Error("Agent execution failed", "provider", provider.Name(), "error", err)
488				}
489	
490				return err
491			},
492		}
```

```text
15	func strptr(s string) *string { return &s }

17	// reconstructSampleData returns a SessionData exercising user text, agent text,
18	// thinking, and a tool call (all of which flatten to user/agent text turns).
19	func reconstructSampleData() *schema.SessionData {
20		fm := "Wrote `hello.c`:\n\n```c\n#include <stdio.h>\nint main(void){ printf(\"Hello, World!\\n\"); }\n```"
21		return &schema.SessionData{
22			SchemaVersion: "1.0",
23			Provider:      schema.ProviderInfo{ID: "claude-code", Name: "Claude Code", Version: "x"},
24			SessionID:     "orig-claude-123",
25			CreatedAt:     "2026-06-17T10:00:00.000Z",
26			WorkspaceRoot: "/tmp/proj",
27			Exchanges: []schema.Exchange{
28				{
29					ExchangeID: "orig-claude-123:0",
30					Messages: []schema.Message{
31						{Role: schema.RoleUser, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "Create a Hello World in C."}}},
32						{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Model: "claude-x", Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "I'll create it."}}},
33						{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeThinking, Text: "Need a main function."}}},
34						{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Tool: &schema.ToolInfo{Name: "Write", Type: schema.ToolTypeWrite, FormattedMarkdown: strptr(fm)}},
35						{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "Done."}}},
36					},
37				},
38			},
39		}
40	}
41	
42	func parseClaudeJSONL(t *testing.T, content []byte) []JSONLRecord {
43		t.Helper()
44		var records []JSONLRecord
45		scanner := bufio.NewScanner(bytes.NewReader(content))
46		scanner.Buffer(make([]byte, 0, 64*1024), 16*1024*1024)
47		for scanner.Scan() {
48			line := strings.TrimSpace(scanner.Text())
49			if line == "" {
50				continue
51			}
52			var m map[string]interface{}
53			if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &m); err != nil {
54				t.Fatalf("invalid JSONL line %q: %v", line, err)
55			}
56			records = append(records, JSONLRecord{Data: m})
57		}
58		if err := scanner.Err(); err != nil {
59			t.Fatalf("scanning content: %v", err)
60		}
61		return records
62	}
63	
64	// TestReconstructSession_RoundTrip reconstructs a session to native Claude JSONL,
65	// re-parses it through the forward path, and asserts the flattened transcript is
66	// preserved.
67	func TestReconstructSession_RoundTrip(t *testing.T) {
68		data := reconstructSampleData()
69		expected := spi.FlattenSessionData(data, "")
70	
71		out, err := NewProvider().ReconstructSession(data, spi.ReconstructOptions{WorkspaceRoot: "/tmp/proj"})
72		if err != nil {
73			t.Fatalf("ReconstructSession: %v", err)
74		}
75		if out.SessionID == "" {
76			t.Fatal("expected a fresh session ID")
77		}
78		if !strings.HasSuffix(out.Filename, ".jsonl") {
79			t.Errorf("filename %q should end in .jsonl", out.Filename)
80		}
81	
82		records := parseClaudeJSONL(t, out.Content)
83		regenerated, err := GenerateAgentSession(Session{SessionUuid: out.SessionID, Records: records}, "/tmp/proj")
84		if err != nil {
85			t.Fatalf("GenerateAgentSession: %v", err)
86		}
87	
88		actual := spi.FlattenSessionData(regenerated, "")
89		if len(actual) != len(expected) {
90			t.Fatalf("round-trip produced %d turns, want %d\n got: %+v\nwant: %+v", len(actual), len(expected), actual, expected)
91		}
92		for i := range expected {
93			if actual[i] != expected[i] {
94				t.Errorf("turn %d mismatch:\n got: %+v\nwant: %+v", i, actual[i], expected[i])
95			}
96		}
97	}
98	
99	// TestReconstructSession_Chain verifies the records form a valid linear parentUuid
100	// chain rooted at null, all sharing the fresh session ID.
101	func TestReconstructSession_Chain(t *testing.T) {
102		out, err := NewProvider().ReconstructSession(reconstructSampleData(), spi.ReconstructOptions{WorkspaceRoot: "/tmp/proj"})
103		if err != nil {
104			t.Fatalf("ReconstructSession: %v", err)
105		}
106	
107		records := parseClaudeJSONL(t, out.Content)
108		if len(records) == 0 {
109			t.Fatal("no records produced")
110		}
111	
112		var prev string
113		for i, r := range records {
114			if sid, _ := r.Data["sessionId"].(string); sid != out.SessionID {
115				t.Errorf("record %d sessionId = %q, want %q", i, sid, out.SessionID)
116			}
117			parent := r.Data["parentUuid"]
118			if i == 0 {
119				if parent != nil {
120					t.Errorf("first record parentUuid = %v, want nil", parent)
121				}
122				if src, _ := r.Data["specstorySourceSessionId"].(string); src != "orig-claude-123" {
123					t.Errorf("first record provenance = %q, want orig-claude-123", src)
124				}
125			} else if ps, _ := parent.(string); ps != prev {
126				t.Errorf("record %d parentUuid = %v, want %q", i, parent, prev)
127			}
128			prev, _ = r.Data["uuid"].(string)
129		}
130	}
131	
132	func TestNativeSessionPath(t *testing.T) {
133		path, err := NewProvider().NativeSessionPath("/tmp/some-claude-proj", "abc.jsonl")
134		if err != nil {
135			t.Fatalf("NativeSessionPath: %v", err)
136		}
137		if !strings.Contains(path, filepath.Join(".claude", "projects")) {
138			t.Errorf("path %q should be under .claude/projects", path)
139		}
140		if !strings.HasSuffix(path, "abc.jsonl") {
141			t.Errorf("path %q should end with the filename", path)
142		}
143		// The project segment encodes the cwd (non-alphanumerics become dashes).
144		if !strings.Contains(path, "-tmp-some-claude-proj") {
145			t.Errorf("path %q should contain the encoded project dir", path)
146		}
147	}
148	
149	func TestReconstructSession_Empty(t *testing.T) {
150		_, err := NewProvider().ReconstructSession(&schema.SessionData{SessionID: "x", WorkspaceRoot: "/tmp"}, spi.ReconstructOptions{})
151		if err == nil {
152			t.Fatal("expected error reconstructing a session with no content")
153		}
154	}
```

```text
633		registry := factory.GetRegistry()

644			if err != nil {
645				if !printToStdout {

653		// Process each session ID

704						}
705						break // Found it, don't check other providers
```

```text
func FlattenSessionData(data *schema.SessionData, migrationNote string) []Turn {
	var turns []Turn

	if strings.TrimSpace(migrationNote) != "" {
		turns = append(turns, Turn{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Text: strings.TrimSpace(migrationNote)})
	}

	if data == nil {
		return turns
	}
```

```text
341	func (p *Provider) GetAgentChatSessionByPath(nativePath string, originCwd string, debugRaw bool) (*spi.AgentChatSession, error) {
342		meta, err := loadCodexSessionMeta(nativePath)
343		if err != nil {
344			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to load codex session meta: %w", err)
345		}
346		// debugRaw is only set when a caller wants provider-specific raw dumps + RawData; reindex
347		// (the only caller) never does. Honor it via the full path so that behavior is unchanged.
348		if debugRaw {
349			info := codexSessionInfo{
350				SessionID:   strings.TrimSpace(meta.Payload.ID),
351				SessionPath: nativePath,
352				Meta:        meta,
353			}
354			return processSessionToAgentChat(&info, originCwd, debugRaw)
355		}
356		// Index fast path: read records only (skip the whole-file rawData string reindex never
357		// reads) and skip slug derivation (reindex uses the slug from enumeration). The SessionData
358		// produced here is identical to the full path's.
359		records, err := readSessionRecords(nativePath)
360		if err != nil {
361			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to read session data: %w", err)
362		}
363		if len(records) == 0 {
364			return nil, nil
365		}
366		sessionData, err := GenerateAgentSession(records, originCwd)
367		if err != nil {
368			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to generate SessionData: %w", err)
369		}
370		return &spi.AgentChatSession{
371			SessionID:   strings.TrimSpace(meta.Payload.ID),
372			CreatedAt:   meta.Timestamp,
373			SessionData: sessionData,
374		}, nil
375	}
376	
377	// ExecAgentAndWatch executes the Codex CLI in interactive mode and monitors for session updates.
378	// The function blocks until the Codex CLI exits. During execution, it watches for JSONL file changes
379	// and invokes sessionCallback for each update, enabling real-time markdown generation and cloud sync.
380	func (p *Provider) ExecAgentAndWatch(projectPath string, customCommand string, resumeSessionID string, debugRaw bool, sessionCallback func(*spi.AgentChatSession)) error {
381		slog.Info("ExecAgentAndWatch: Starting Codex CLI execution and monitoring",
382			"projectPath", projectPath,
383			"customCommand", customCommand,
384			"resumeSessionID", resumeSessionID,
385			"debugRaw", debugRaw)
386	
387		// Log if resuming a specific session
388		if resumeSessionID != "" {
389			slog.Info("ExecAgentAndWatch: Will resume specific Codex session", "sessionID", resumeSessionID)
390		}
391	
392		// Set up the callback which enables real-time markdown generation and cloud sync during
393		// interactive sessions. As Codex CLI writes JSONL updates, the watcher detects changes
394		// and invokes this callback, allowing immediate processing without blocking the agent's
395		// execution. The defer ensures cleanup when the agent exits.
396		SetWatcherCallback(sessionCallback)
397		defer ClearWatcherCallback()
398	
399		// Set debug raw mode for the watcher
400		SetWatcherDebugRaw(debugRaw)
401	
402		// Start watching for Codex sessions in the background
403		slog.Info("Initializing Codex session monitoring...")
404	
405		if err := WatchForCodexSessions(projectPath, resumeSessionID); err != nil {
406			// Log the error but don't fail - watcher might work later
407			slog.Error("Failed to start Codex session watcher", "error", err)
408		}
409	
410		// Execute Codex CLI - this blocks until Codex exits
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
792	// preloadBulkSessionSizesIfNeeded optimizes bulk syncs by fetching all session sizes upfront.
793	// This avoids making individual HEAD requests for each session during sync operations.
794	//
795	// The function only performs the preload if:
796	//   - Cloud sync is enabled (noCloudSync flag is false)
797	//   - User is authenticated with SpecStory Cloud
798	//   - A valid projectID can be determined from the identity manager
799	//
800	// Parameters:
801	//   - identityManager: Provides project identity (git_id or workspace_id) for the current project
802	//
803	// The preloaded sizes are cached in the SyncManager and shared across all providers since they
804	// use the same projectID. If the bulk fetch fails or if projectID cannot be determined, individual
805	// sessions will gracefully fall back to HEAD requests during sync.
806	//
807	// This function is safe to call multiple times but should typically be called once before processing
808	// multiple sessions in batch sync operations.
809	func preloadBulkSessionSizesIfNeeded(identityManager *utils.ProjectIdentityManager) {
810		// Skip if cloud sync is disabled or user is not authenticated
811		if noCloudSync || !cloud.IsAuthenticated() {
812			return
813		}
814	
815		// Get projectID from identity manager - required for cloud sync
816		projectID, err := identityManager.GetProjectID()
817		if err != nil {
818			slog.Warn("Cannot preload session sizes: failed to get project ID", "error", err)
819			return
820		}
821	
822		// Preload bulk sizes once - shared across all providers since they use same projectID
823		if syncMgr := cloud.GetSyncManager(); syncMgr != nil {
824			syncMgr.PreloadSessionSizes(projectID)
825		} else {
826			slog.Warn("Cannot preload session sizes: sync manager is nil despite authentication")
827		}
828	}
829	
830	// syncProvider performs the actual sync for a single provider.
831	// The caller-provided statsCollector accumulates statistics across providers so
832	// they can be flushed to disk in a single I/O operation after all providers are done.
833	// Returns (sessionCount, error) for analytics tracking.
834	func syncProvider(provider spi.Provider, providerID string, config utils.OutputConfig, debugRaw bool, useUTC bool, statsCollector *sessionpkg.StatisticsCollector) (int, error) {
835		cwd, err := os.Getwd()
836		if err != nil {
837			slog.Error("Failed to get current working directory", "error", err)
838			return 0, err
839		}
```

```text
631	func readSessionRecords(sessionPath string) ([]map[string]interface{}, error) {
632		records, _, err := readCodexJSONL(sessionPath, false)
633		return records, err
634	}
635	
636	// readCodexJSONL reads a Codex session's JSONL records, and — when collectRaw is true — the raw
637	// JSONL content as one string. Callers that only need records pass collectRaw=false to skip the
638	// whole-file string builder.
639	func readCodexJSONL(sessionPath string, collectRaw bool) ([]map[string]interface{}, string, error) {
640		file, err := os.Open(sessionPath)
641		if err != nil {
642			return nil, "", fmt.Errorf("failed to open session file: %w", err)
643		}
644		defer func() {
645			_ = file.Close()
646		}()
647	
648		var records []map[string]interface{}
649		var rawBuilder strings.Builder
650	
651		// Use bufio.Reader instead of Scanner to handle arbitrarily large lines
652		// Scanner has a token size limit (even with custom buffer), but Reader does not
653		reader := bufio.NewReader(file)
654	
655		lineNumber := 0
656		for {
657			// Read line using ReadString which has no size limit
658			line, err := reader.ReadString('\n')
659			line = strings.TrimSuffix(line, "\n")
660	
661			// EOF is expected at end of file, other errors are genuine failures
662			if err != nil && err != io.EOF {
663				return nil, "", fmt.Errorf("error reading line %d: %w", lineNumber+1, err)
664			}
665	
666			// Determine if we're at end of file and if we have content to process
667			atEOF := err == io.EOF
668			hasContent := len(line) > 0
669	
670			// Increment line number for every line read (including empty lines) to match text editor line numbers
671			if hasContent || !atEOF {
672				lineNumber++
673			}
674	
675			// If no content, either skip empty line or exit at EOF
676			if !hasContent {
677				if atEOF {
678					break // Reached end of file with no content
679				}
680				continue // Empty line in middle of file, skip it
681			}
682	
683			// Sanity check to prevent OOM from pathological files
684			if len(line) > maxReasonableLineSize {
685				slog.Warn("line exceeds reasonable size limit",
686					"lineNumber", lineNumber,
687					"sizeMB", len(line)/MB,
688					"limitMB", maxReasonableLineSize/MB,
689					"file", filepath.Base(sessionPath))
690				return nil, "", fmt.Errorf("line %d exceeds reasonable size limit (%d MB): refusing to process potentially malformed file",
691					lineNumber, maxReasonableLineSize/MB)
692			}
693	
694			// Log when processing unusually large lines (helps debug performance issues)
695			if len(line) > 10*MB {
696				slog.Debug("processing large JSONL line",
697					"lineNumber", lineNumber,
698					"sizeMB", len(line)/MB,
699					"file", filepath.Base(sessionPath))
700			}
701	
702			// Trim whitespace and skip empty lines
703			line = strings.TrimSpace(line)
704			if line == "" {
705				if atEOF {
706					break
707				}
708				continue
709			}
710	
711			// Accumulate raw JSONL only when the caller asked for it (reindex does not).
712			if collectRaw {
713				rawBuilder.WriteString(line)
714				rawBuilder.WriteString("\n")
715			}
716	
717			// Parse JSON
718			var record map[string]interface{}
719			if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &record); err != nil {
720				// Log warning and skip corrupted lines rather than failing entire parse
721				slog.Warn("Skipping corrupted JSONL line",
722					"file", filepath.Base(sessionPath),
723					"line", lineNumber,
724					"error", err)
725				if atEOF {
726					break
727				}
728				continue
729			}
730	
731			records = append(records, record)
732	
733			// After processing record, check if we're done
734			if atEOF {
735				break
736			}
737		}
738	
739		return records, rawBuilder.String(), nil
740	}
741	
742	// loadCodexSessionMeta reads the first JSON line from a session file and parses the session metadata.
743	func loadCodexSessionMeta(sessionPath string) (*codexSessionMeta, error) {
744		file, err := os.Open(sessionPath)
745		if err != nil {
746			return nil, err
747		}
748		defer func() {
749			_ = file.Close()
750		}()
751	
752		scanner := bufio.NewScanner(file)
753		if !scanner.Scan() {
754			if scanErr := scanner.Err(); scanErr != nil {
755				return nil, scanErr
756			}
757			return nil, errors.New("codex session meta not found")
758		}
759	
760		line := strings.TrimSpace(scanner.Text())
761		if line == "" {
762			return nil, errors.New("codex session meta is empty")
763		}
764	
765		var meta codexSessionMeta
766		if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &meta); err != nil {
767			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to parse codex session meta: %w", err)
768		}
769	
770		if meta.Type != "session_meta" {
771			return nil, fmt.Errorf("unexpected codex session record type: %s", meta.Type)
772		}
773	
774		return &meta, nil
775	}
776
```

```text
834	func syncProvider(provider spi.Provider, providerID string, config utils.OutputConfig, debugRaw bool, useUTC bool, statsCollector *sessionpkg.StatisticsCollector) (int, error) {
835		cwd, err := os.Getwd()
836		if err != nil {
837			slog.Error("Failed to get current working directory", "error", err)
838			return 0, err
839		}
840	
841		// Keep sessions.db current for this project as we sync. We already hold each session's
842		// SessionData below, so indexing it is essentially free. Best-effort: a nil indexer (open
843		// failure) is a safe no-op and never affects the sync. Scoped to the synced project (cwd).
844		liveIndex := cmdpkg.NewLiveIndexer(cwd)
845		defer liveIndex.Close()
846	
847		// Create progress callback for parsing phase
848		// The callback updates the "Parsing..." line in place with [n/m] progress
849		var parseProgress spi.ProgressCallback
850		if !silent {
851			providerName := provider.Name()
852			parseProgress = func(current, total int) {
853				fmt.Printf("\rParsing %s sessions [%d/%d]", providerName, current, total)
854				_ = os.Stdout.Sync()
855			}
856		}
857	
858		// Get all sessions from the provider
859		sessions, err := provider.GetAgentChatSessions(cwd, debugRaw, parseProgress)
860		if err != nil {
861			return 0, fmt.Errorf("failed to get sessions: %w", err)
862		}
863	
864		sessionCount := len(sessions)
865	
866		if sessionCount == 0 && !silent {
867			// This comes after "Parsing..." message, on the same line
868			fmt.Printf(", no non-empty sessions found for %s\n", provider.Name())
869			fmt.Println()
870			return 0, nil
871		}
872	
873		if !silent && !onlyStats {
874			fmt.Printf("\nSyncing markdown files for %s", provider.Name())
875		}
876	
877		// Initialize statistics
878		stats := &SyncStats{
879			TotalSessions: sessionCount,
880		}
881	
882		historyPath := config.GetHistoryDir()
883		agentName := provider.Name()
884		ctx := context.Background()
885	
886		// Process each session
887		for i := range sessions {
888			session := &sessions[i]
889	
890			// Process session in a closure so defers scope to each iteration,
891			// ensuring spans are ended and metrics recorded even on early returns.
892			func() {
893				processingStart := time.Now()
894	
895				// Create a context with a deterministic trace ID from the session ID
896				sessionCtx := telemetry.ContextWithSessionTrace(ctx, session.SessionID)
897	
898				// Start an OTel span for this session processing
899				sessionCtx, span := telemetry.Tracer("specstory").Start(sessionCtx, "process_session")
900				defer span.End()
901	
902				// Compute session statistics
903				sessionStats := telemetry.ComputeSessionStats(agentName, session)
904				defer func() {
905					telemetry.RecordSessionMetrics(sessionCtx, sessionStats, time.Since(processingStart))
906				}()
907	
908				// Set session span attributes
909				telemetry.SetSessionSpanAttributes(span, sessionStats)
910	
911				// Create child spans for each exchange
912				if session.SessionData != nil && len(session.SessionData.Exchanges) > 0 {
913					telemetry.ProcessExchangeSpans(sessionCtx, sessionStats, session.SessionData.Exchanges, noTelemetryPrompts)
914				}
915	
916				sessionpkg.ValidateSessionData(session, debugRaw)
917				sessionpkg.WriteDebugSessionData(session, debugRaw)
918	
919				// Mirror this session into the restore index as we sync it (covers normal and
920				// only-stats modes — we have the SessionData either way).
921				liveIndex.Record(providerID, session)
922	
923				// Generate markdown from SessionData
924				markdownContent, err := sessionpkg.GenerateMarkdownFromAgentSession(session.SessionData, false, useUTC)
925				if err != nil {
926					slog.Error("Failed to generate markdown from SessionData",
927						"sessionId", session.SessionID,
928						"error", err)
929					// Track sync error
930					analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventSyncMarkdownError, analytics.Properties{
931						"session_id": session.SessionID,
932						"error":      err.Error(),
933					})
934					return
935				}
936	
937				// Compute statistics from the SessionData
938				sessionStatistics := sessionpkg.ComputeSessionStatistics(session.SessionData, markdownContent, providerID)
939				statsCollector.AddSessionStats(session.SessionID, sessionStatistics)
940	
941				// In only-stats mode, skip file writes and cloud sync
942				if onlyStats {
943					stats.SessionsSkipped++
944					slog.Info("Skipping local file write (only-stats mode)", "sessionId", session.SessionID)
945					return
946				}
947	
948				// Generate filename from timestamp and slug
949				fileFullPath := sessionpkg.BuildSessionFilePath(session, historyPath, useUTC)
950	
951				// Check if file already exists with same content
952				identicalContent := false
953				fileExists := false
```

```text
1045	func scanCodexSessionHeader(sessionPath string) (*codexSessionHeader, error) {
1046		file, err := os.Open(sessionPath)
1047		if err != nil {
1048			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to open session file: %w", err)
1049		}
1050		defer func() { _ = file.Close() }()
1051	
1052		reader := bufio.NewReader(file)
1053		h := &codexSessionHeader{}
1054		lineNum := 0
1055		for {
1056			line, readErr := reader.ReadString('\n')
1057			if readErr != nil && readErr != io.EOF {
1058				return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to read line: %w", readErr)
1059			}
1060			lineNum++
1061			trimmed := strings.TrimSpace(line)
1062	
1063			if trimmed != "" {
1064				if lineNum == 1 {
1065					// Line 1 is session_meta. Keep loadCodexSessionMeta's 64 KB first-line limit so
1066					// the set of indexed sessions is unchanged from the Scanner-based path.
1067					if len(trimmed) > bufio.MaxScanTokenSize {
1068						return nil, errors.New("codex session meta exceeds line limit")
1069					}
1070					var meta codexSessionMeta
1071					if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(trimmed), &meta); err != nil {
1072						return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to parse codex session meta: %w", err)
1073					}
1074					if meta.Type != "session_meta" {
1075						return nil, fmt.Errorf("unexpected codex session record type: %s", meta.Type)
1076					}
1077					h.sessionID = strings.TrimSpace(meta.Payload.ID)
1078					h.cwd = meta.Payload.CWD
1079					h.createdAt = meta.Timestamp
1080				} else if strings.Contains(trimmed, userMessageMarker) {
1081					// Cheap screen passed: only now pay for a full parse. The big context records
1082					// before the first user message lack the marker and are skipped without a parse.
1083					if msg := codexUserMessageText(trimmed); msg != "" {
1084						h.firstUserMessage = msg
1085						break
1086					}
1087				}
1088			}
1089	
1090			if readErr == io.EOF {
1091				break
1092			}
1093		}
1094	
1095		if h.sessionID == "" {
1096			return nil, errors.New("codex session meta not found")
1097		}
1098		if h.firstUserMessage == "" {
1099			return nil, nil // no user message → empty session, excluded from enumeration
1100		}
1101		return h, nil
1102	}
1103	
1104	// codexUserMessageText returns the message text when line is an event_msg user_message with a
1105	// non-empty message, else "". It is only called for lines that passed the userMessageMarker
1106	// screen, and applies the same structural checks extractCodexSessionMetadata used.
1107	func codexUserMessageText(line string) string {
1108		var record map[string]interface{}
1109		if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &record); err != nil {
1110			return ""
1111		}
1112		if recordType, _ := record["type"].(string); recordType != "event_msg" {
1113			return ""
1114		}
1115		payload, ok := record["payload"].(map[string]interface{})
1116		if !ok {
1117			return ""
1118		}
1119		if payloadType, _ := payload["type"].(string); payloadType != "user_message" {
1120			return ""
1121		}
1122		message, _ := payload["message"].(string)
1123		return message
1124	}
1125	
1126	// ListAllAgentChatSessions enumerates every Codex CLI session across all projects. It is the
1127	// no-progress form of ListAllAgentChatSessionsProgress.
1128	func (p *Provider) ListAllAgentChatSessions() ([]spi.GlobalSessionRef, error) {
1129		return p.ListAllAgentChatSessionsProgress(nil)
1130	}
1131	
1132	// ListAllAgentChatSessionsProgress enumerates every Codex CLI session across all projects by
1133	// walking ~/.codex/sessions for *.jsonl rollouts (Codex sessions are not project-keyed by
1134	// directory; the originating cwd lives in the session_meta record), reporting scan progress into r
1135	// (nil-safe). The per-file header read is the dominant cost of `specstory reindex` (parsing each
1136	// file's injected context), so headers are scanned in parallel across CPUs; output order is
1137	// irrelevant (reindex dedups and sorts later). Implements spi.ProgressEnumerator. See
1138	// docs/SESSIONS-DB.md.
1139	func (p *Provider) ListAllAgentChatSessionsProgress(r *spi.ScanReporter) ([]spi.GlobalSessionRef, error) {
1140		homeDir, err := osUserHomeDir()
1141		if err != nil {
1142			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to determine home directory: %w", err)
1143		}
1144		sessionsRoot := codexSessionsRoot(homeDir)
1145		if _, err := os.Stat(sessionsRoot); err != nil {
1146			// No sessions directory yet → nothing to enumerate (not an error).
1147			return []spi.GlobalSessionRef{}, nil
1148		}
1149	
1150		// Phase 1: collect file paths (dirents only — no opens).
1151		var paths []string
1152		walkErr := filepath.WalkDir(sessionsRoot, func(path string, d os.DirEntry, err error) error {
1153			if err != nil {
1154				return nil // skip unreadable entries rather than abort the sweep
1155			}
1156			if d.IsDir() || filepath.Ext(path) != ".jsonl" {
1157				return nil
1158			}
1159			paths = append(paths, path)
1160			return nil
1161		})
1162		if walkErr != nil {
1163			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to walk codex sessions: %w", walkErr)
1164		}
1165	
1166		// Phase 2: scan headers in parallel. Each file's single-pass read is CPU-bound (JSON) and the
1167		// files are independent, so this scales across cores.
1168		workers := min(runtime.NumCPU(), 12)
1169		if workers < 1 {
1170			workers = 1
1171		}
1172		pathCh := make(chan string, workers)
1173		refs := make([]spi.GlobalSessionRef, 0, len(paths))
1174		var mu sync.Mutex
1175		var wg sync.WaitGroup
1176		for i := 0; i < workers; i++ {
1177			wg.Add(1)
1178			go func() {
1179				defer wg.Done()
1180				for path := range pathCh {
1181					h, err := scanCodexSessionHeader(path)
1182					if err != nil {
1183						slog.Debug("reindex: failed to scan codex session header", "path", path, "error", err)
1184						continue
1185					}
1186					if h == nil {
1187						continue // empty session (no user message)
1188					}
1189					ref := spi.GlobalSessionRef{
1190						SessionID:  h.sessionID,
1191						CreatedAt:  h.createdAt,
1192						Slug:       spi.GenerateFilenameFromUserMessage(h.firstUserMessage),
1193						Name:       spi.GenerateReadableName(h.firstUserMessage),
1194						NativePath: path,
1195						OriginCwd:  h.cwd,
1196					}
1197					mu.Lock()
1198					refs = append(refs, ref)
1199					mu.Unlock()
1200					r.Add(1) // count sessions found, not files scanned (warmup/empty files yield none)
1201				}
1202			}()
1203		}
1204		for _, path := range paths {
1205			pathCh <- path
1206		}
1207		close(pathCh)
1208		wg.Wait()
1209		return refs, nil
1210	}
1211
```
</details></tool-use>

<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./pkg/providers/codexcli/reconstruct.go`

```text
1	package codexcli
2	
3	import (
4		"bytes"
5		"encoding/json"
6		"fmt"
7		"os"
8		"path/filepath"
9		"time"
10	
11		"github.com/google/uuid"
12	
13		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
14		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
15	)
16	
17	// reconstructedCodexCliVersion mirrors the `cli_version` Codex writes into
18	// session_meta. A concrete value matching observed sessions maximizes the chance
19	// the loader accepts a synthesized file.
20	const reconstructedCodexCliVersion = "0.139.0"
21	
22	// codexTimestamp formats a time the way Codex records do (RFC3339 millis, Z).
23	func codexTimestamp(t time.Time) string {
24		return t.Format("2006-01-02T15:04:05.000Z")
25	}
26	
27	// ReconstructSession rebuilds a Codex CLI native rollout from the neutral
28	// SessionData so `codex resume <id>` can continue the conversation.
29	//
30	// Codex resume replays the response_item model transcript, while the forward
31	// parser reads the event_msg UI stream — so reconstruction emits BOTH for each
32	// turn: a response_item message (what the agent replays) and a matching event_msg
33	// (what the UI and our own re-parse read). Tool calls and thinking are already
34	// flattened into agent text by FlattenSessionData.
35	//
36	// We do NOT synthesize an environment_context block: Codex injects its own fresh
37	// environment context on every resume, consistent with the cross-provider policy
38	// that the target supplies its own preamble. See docs/SESSION-PORTABILITY.md.
39	func (p *Provider) ReconstructSession(data *schema.SessionData, opts spi.ReconstructOptions) (*spi.ReconstructedSession, error) {
40		if data == nil {
41			return nil, fmt.Errorf("cannot reconstruct nil session data")
42		}
43	
44		cwd := opts.WorkspaceRoot
45		if cwd == "" {
46			cwd = data.WorkspaceRoot
47		}
48	
49		turns := spi.FlattenSessionData(data, opts.MigrationNote)
50		if len(turns) == 0 {
51			return nil, fmt.Errorf("session has no content to reconstruct")
52		}
53	
54		// Codex session IDs are UUIDv7. Fall back to v4 if v7 generation fails.
55		newID := uuid.NewString()
56		if v7, err := uuid.NewV7(); err == nil {
57			newID = v7.String()
58		}
59	
60		base := time.Now().UTC()
61	
62		var buf bytes.Buffer
63		enc := json.NewEncoder(&buf)
64		enc.SetEscapeHTML(false)
65	
66		encode := func(rec map[string]interface{}) error {
67			return enc.Encode(rec)
68		}
69	
70		// session_meta must be the first record; it also carries provenance.
71		metaTS := codexTimestamp(base)
72		if err := encode(map[string]interface{}{
73			"timestamp": metaTS,
74			"type":      "session_meta",
75			"payload": map[string]interface{}{
76				"id":                       newID,
77				"timestamp":                metaTS,
78				"cwd":                      cwd,
79				"originator":               "codex-tui",
80				"cli_version":              reconstructedCodexCliVersion,
81				"source":                   "cli",
82				"model_provider":           "openai",
83				"specstorySourceSessionId": data.SessionID,
84			},
85		}); err != nil {
86			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to encode session_meta: %w", err)
87		}
88	
89		for i, turn := range turns {
90			ts := codexTimestamp(base.Add(time.Duration(i+1) * time.Second))
91	
92			var role, contentType, eventType string
93			if turn.Role == schema.RoleUser {
94				role, contentType, eventType = "user", "input_text", "user_message"
95			} else {
96				role, contentType, eventType = "assistant", "output_text", "agent_message"
97			}
98	
99			// response_item: the model-facing transcript the agent replays on resume.
100			if err := encode(codexResponseItemMessage(ts, role, contentType, turn.Text)); err != nil {
101				return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to encode response_item: %w", err)
102			}
103			// event_msg: the UI/history stream the forward parser reads.
104			if err := encode(codexEventMessage(ts, eventType, turn.Text)); err != nil {
105				return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to encode event_msg: %w", err)
106			}
107		}
108	
109		filename := fmt.Sprintf("rollout-%s-%s.jsonl", base.Format("2006-01-02T15-04-05"), newID)
110	
111		return &spi.ReconstructedSession{
112			SessionID: newID,
113			Filename:  filename,
114			Content:   buf.Bytes(),
115		}, nil
116	}
117	
118	// NativeSessionPath returns where a reconstructed rollout belongs in Codex's
119	// store: ~/.codex/sessions/YYYY/MM/DD/<filename>, dated today. Codex sessions are
120	// not project-scoped by directory, so projectPath is unused. The directory is not
121	// required to exist (the caller creates it).
122	func (p *Provider) NativeSessionPath(projectPath string, filename string) (string, error) {
123		homeDir, err := os.UserHomeDir()
124		if err != nil {
125			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get user home directory: %w", err)
126		}
127		now := time.Now()
128		dir := filepath.Join(
129			codexSessionsRoot(homeDir),
130			fmt.Sprintf("%04d", now.Year()),
131			fmt.Sprintf("%02d", int(now.Month())),
132			fmt.Sprintf("%02d", now.Day()),
133		)
134		return filepath.Join(dir, filename), nil
135	}
136	
137	// codexResponseItemMessage builds a response_item "message" record.
138	func codexResponseItemMessage(ts, role, contentType, text string) map[string]interface{} {
139		return map[string]interface{}{
140			"timestamp": ts,
141			"type":      "response_item",
142			"payload": map[string]interface{}{
143				"type":    "message",
144				"role":    role,
145				"content": []map[string]interface{}{{"type": contentType, "text": text}},
146			},
147		}
148	}
149	
150	// codexEventMessage builds an event_msg record (user_message / agent_message).
151	func codexEventMessage(ts, eventType, message string) map[string]interface{} {
152		return map[string]interface{}{
153			"timestamp": ts,
154			"type":      "event_msg",
155			"payload": map[string]interface{}{
156				"type":    eventType,
157				"message": message,
158			},
159		}
160	}
161
```

```text
1	package codexcli
2

15	func strptr(s string) *string { return &s }
16	
17	// reconstructSampleData exercises user text, agent text, thinking, and a tool
18	// call carrying both a summary and formatted markdown.
19	func reconstructSampleData() *schema.SessionData {
20		summary := "Tool use: **exec_command** `ls`"
21		fm := "```\nhello.c\nhello\n```"
22		return &schema.SessionData{
23			SchemaVersion: "1.0",
24			Provider:      schema.ProviderInfo{ID: "codex-cli", Name: "Codex CLI", Version: "x"},
25			SessionID:     "orig-codex-123",
26			CreatedAt:     "2026-06-17T10:00:00.000Z",
27			WorkspaceRoot: "/tmp/proj",
28			Exchanges: []schema.Exchange{
29				{
30					ExchangeID: "orig-codex-123:0",
31					Messages: []schema.Message{
32						{Role: schema.RoleUser, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "Create a hello world in D."}}},
33						{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "Adding hello.d."}}},
34						{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeThinking, Text: "Check for a compiler."}}},
35						{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Tool: &schema.ToolInfo{Name: "exec_command", Type: schema.ToolTypeShell, Summary: strptr(summary), FormattedMarkdown: strptr(fm)}},
36						{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "Created hello.d."}}},
37					},
38				},
39			},
40		}
41	}
42	
43	func parseCodexJSONL(t *testing.T, content []byte) []map[string]interface{} {
44		t.Helper()
45		var records []map[string]interface{}
46		scanner := bufio.NewScanner(bytes.NewReader(content))
47		scanner.Buffer(make([]byte, 0, 64*1024), 16*1024*1024)
48		for scanner.Scan() {
49			line := strings.TrimSpace(scanner.Text())
50			if line == "" {
51				continue
52			}
53			var m map[string]interface{}
54			if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &m); err != nil {
55				t.Fatalf("invalid JSONL line %q: %v", line, err)
56			}
57			records = append(records, m)
58		}
59		if err := scanner.Err(); err != nil {
60			t.Fatalf("scanning content: %v", err)
61		}
62		return records
63	}
64	
65	// TestReconstructSession_RoundTrip reconstructs to native Codex rollout format,
66	// re-parses through the forward path, and asserts the flattened transcript is
67	// preserved.
68	func TestReconstructSession_RoundTrip(t *testing.T) {
69		data := reconstructSampleData()
70		expected := spi.FlattenSessionData(data, "")
71	
72		out, err := NewProvider().ReconstructSession(data, spi.ReconstructOptions{WorkspaceRoot: "/tmp/proj"})
73		if err != nil {
74			t.Fatalf("ReconstructSession: %v", err)
75		}
76		if out.SessionID == "" {
77			t.Fatal("expected a fresh session ID")
78		}
79		if !strings.HasPrefix(out.Filename, "rollout-") || !strings.HasSuffix(out.Filename, ".jsonl") {
80			t.Errorf("filename %q should look like rollout-<ts>-<id>.jsonl", out.Filename)
81		}
82	
83		records := parseCodexJSONL(t, out.Content)
84		regenerated, err := GenerateAgentSession(records, "/tmp/proj")
85		if err != nil {
86			t.Fatalf("GenerateAgentSession: %v", err)
87		}
88	
89		actual := spi.FlattenSessionData(regenerated, "")
90		if len(actual) != len(expected) {
91			t.Fatalf("round-trip produced %d turns, want %d\n got: %+v\nwant: %+v", len(actual), len(expected), actual, expected)
92		}
93		for i := range expected {
94			if actual[i] != expected[i] {
95				t.Errorf("turn %d mismatch:\n got: %+v\nwant: %+v", i, actual[i], expected[i])
96			}
97		}
98	}
99	
100	// TestReconstructSession_Structure checks the first record is session_meta with a
101	// matching id and provenance, that there is exactly one user_message event, and
102	// that no synthesized environment_context is emitted (Codex injects its own).
103	func TestReconstructSession_Structure(t *testing.T) {
104		out, err := NewProvider().ReconstructSession(reconstructSampleData(), spi.ReconstructOptions{WorkspaceRoot: "/tmp/proj"})
105		if err != nil {
106			t.Fatalf("ReconstructSession: %v", err)
107		}
108	
109		records := parseCodexJSONL(t, out.Content)
110		if len(records) == 0 {
111			t.Fatal("no records produced")
112		}
113	
114		meta := records[0]
115		if meta["type"] != "session_meta" {
116			t.Fatalf("first record type = %v, want session_meta", meta["type"])
117		}
118		payload, _ := meta["payload"].(map[string]interface{})
119		if payload["id"] != out.SessionID {
120			t.Errorf("session_meta id = %v, want %q", payload["id"], out.SessionID)
121		}
122		if payload["specstorySourceSessionId"] != "orig-codex-123" {
123			t.Errorf("provenance = %v, want orig-codex-123", payload["specstorySourceSessionId"])
124		}
125	
126		// Exactly one user_message event per user turn; no synthesized environment_context anywhere.
127		userEvents := 0
128		for _, r := range records {
129			if strings.Contains(toJSON(t, r), "environment_context") {
130				t.Error("reconstruction should not synthesize an environment_context block")
131			}
132			if r["type"] != "event_msg" {
133				continue
134			}
135			p, _ := r["payload"].(map[string]interface{})
136			if p["type"] == "user_message" {
137				userEvents++
138			}
139		}
140		if userEvents != 1 {
141			t.Errorf("got %d user_message events, want 1", userEvents)
142		}
143	}
144	
145	func toJSON(t *testing.T, v map[string]interface{}) string {
146		t.Helper()
147		b, err := json.Marshal(v)
148		if err != nil {
149			t.Fatalf("marshal: %v", err)
150		}
151		return string(b)
152	}
153	
154	func TestNativeSessionPath(t *testing.T) {
155		path, err := NewProvider().NativeSessionPath("/tmp/some-codex-proj", "rollout-x.jsonl")
156		if err != nil {
157			t.Fatalf("NativeSessionPath: %v", err)
158		}
159		if !strings.Contains(path, filepath.Join(".codex", "sessions")) {
160			t.Errorf("path %q should be under .codex/sessions", path)
161		}
162		if !strings.HasSuffix(path, "rollout-x.jsonl") {
163			t.Errorf("path %q should end with the filename", path)
164		}
165	}
166	
167	func TestReconstructSession_Empty(t *testing.T) {

169		if err == nil {
```
