✅ reindex.go:83-159 — runReindex orchestrates the entire flow with clear phase comments (lines 108, 141, 152).

**GRACEFUL SHUTDOWN & RESOURCE CLEANUP:**

✅ reindex.go:87 — signal.NotifyContext correctly traps Ctrl+C and SIGTERM for graceful shutdown.

✅ reindex.go:98 — Resource cleanup pattern `defer func() { _ = store.Close() }()` properly ignores close errors.

⚠️ reindex.go:96-102 — Inconsistent error wrapping: dbPath error returns bare (line 98), but store.Open error is wrapped with context (line 102). Normalize wrapping style.

**FINGERPRINTS & INCREMENTAL LOGIC:**

✅ reindex.go:108-119 — Fingerprints loaded when not forcing; absence gracefully falls back to re-index-all with warning.

✅ reindex.go:114-115 — Error logged at Warn level but not surfaced. Correct for best-effort fingerprint loading.

✅ reindex.go:118 — Empty fingerprints map on error means next check at line 268 will re-index everything (safe fallback).

**SESSION DISCOVERY & DEDUPLICATION:**

✅ reindex.go:121-124 — Three phases clearly separated: enumerate, dedup, select work.

✅ reindex.go:122-124 — All three results (order, best, foundPerAgent) used to populate display and select work.

⚠️ reindex.go:127 — "No agent sessions found" printed only to stderr. Should also log via slog.Warn for visibility in log files if stderr is redirected.

✅ reindex.go:130-139 — Display output distinguishes unchanged/indexed counts and shows progress summary before processing starts.

**WORKER POOL & CONCURRENCY:**

✅ reindex.go:139 — min(runtime.NumCPU(), 12) caps parallelism reasonably.

⚠️ reindex.go:139 — No comment explaining why 12 is the cap. Is this to prevent resource exhaustion? Empirically tuned? Should document the rationale.

✅ reindex.go:141 — Phase comment clearly marks transition to parallel parse + single-threaded write.

✅ reindex.go:142-145 — Progress reporter initialized and lifecycle (begin/end) properly managed.

**ANALYTICS & SUMMARY:**

⚠️ reindex.go:151-157 — analytics.TrackEvent only tracks `"sessions": len(work)` (incremental sessions indexed this run). Event name EventReindexCompleted suggests complete picture. Should also track `"sessions_total_found"` for full context, and `"unchanged_skipped"`.

✅ reindex.go:154-155 — ProjectCount and UnattributedCount queries reflect WHOLE index state (not just this run's work), which is correct for summary.

**ENUMERATION PHASE:**

✅ reindex.go:167-207 — enumerateAll correctly uses WaitGroup for concurrent provider enumeration.

✅ reindex.go:174-176 — Index-aligned slices (provs[i], perProvider[i]) correspond to ids[i]; nil provs[i] marks failed provider (line 221-228 check respects this).

✅ reindex.go:178-183 — visible parameter controls whether scanProgress renders live line (foreground: true, background warm: false).

✅ reindex.go:186-207 — Goroutine per provider with proper defer ewg.Done() pattern.

⚠️ reindex.go:186 — Loop variable `ewg` (enumeration wait group) abbreviated; consider `enumerationWg` for clarity.

⚠️ reindex.go:188-189 — Provider load errors silently skip provider with continue. Should log at Warn level so operator sees which providers failed to load (e.g., Codex binary missing).

✅ reindex.go:193-203 — Type assertion to ProgressEnumerator allows providers (Codex, Claude) to report progress; fallback to basic ListAllAgentChatSessions for others. Good pattern.

✅ reindex.go:206 — Enumeration errors logged at Warn with context (provider id).

✅ reindex.go:209 — scan.markDone safely called even if scan is nil (nil-safe at line 929).

**DEDUPLICATION LOGIC:**

✅ reindex.go:217-246 — dedupRefs correctly collapses by (agent, sessionID) key using FingerprintKey.

✅ reindex.go:231-234 — Sessions with empty SessionID (after TrimSpace) skipped with debug log. Correct guard against PK collisions.

✅ reindex.go:235 — statNative called once per ref during dedup; stat captured and reused (no re-stat during selectWork).

⚠️ reindex.go:236 — reindexItem constructed inline with size/mtime captured. Field names could clarify units: consider `fileSizeBytes` and `fileModTimeMs`.

✅ reindex.go:238-241 — When duplicate found, keeps item with highest mtime (freshest file). Clear and correct.

✅ reindex.go:242-245 — order maintains first-seen deterministic order; foundPerAgent counts distinct sessions per agent for "Found" line.

**INCREMENTAL FINGERPRINT FILTERING:**

✅ reindex.go:252-281 — selectWork applies fingerprint skip and optional project filter.

✅ reindex.go:256-265 — Project filter (used by background warm's current-project pass) correctly skips non-matching projects.

✅ reindex.go:262 — Fingerprint matching checks ALL three (Size, Mtime, Version). If ANY differs, re-indexes. Correct and defensive.

✅ reindex.go:268 — Version check ensures ANY logic change forces full re-index. This is crucial; if reindexVersion changes, all rows re-parsed regardless of mtime (prevents stale logic).

✅ reindex.go:270-275 — isNew logic: new sessions (no prior index row) skip FTS delete. Under --force, isNew stays false because fingerprints map is empty, so deletes are attempted to avoid duplicates. Comment correctly explains this edge case.

**WORKER POOL COORDINATION:**

✅ reindex.go:293-349 — processWork orchestrates worker pool + writer goroutine with clean separation:
   - workCh feeds work to workers
   - sessionsCh carries parsed results to writer
   - Writer runs in separate goroutine (line 316-319)

✅ reindex.go:300-305 — Early return if no work; worker count sanity check (min 1).

✅ reindex.go:307-311 — Work sorted by size descending (largest first). Comment clearly explains WHY: prevents end-of-run stragglers leaving cores idle. Excellent optimization.

✅ reindex.go:313-314 — sessionsCh buffered at 256 to absorb parsing variance without blocking workers.

✅ reindex.go:314 — writeErr captured by closure in writer goroutine (line 318).

✅ reindex.go:315 — writerDone signals when writer finishes (important for orderly shutdown).

✅ reindex.go:317 — defer close(writerDone) ensures writer always signals completion.

✅ reindex.go:321-333 — Worker goroutine pattern: iterate over workCh, build session, observe project, increment counter. Proper defer wwg.Done().

⚠️ reindex.go:322 — Loop variable `wwg` (worker wait group) abbreviated; consider `workerWg`.

✅ reindex.go:336-341 — Feed work with context cancellation check. If ctx.Done(), goto closing stops feeding work immediately.

⚠️ reindex.go:337-339 — goto closing pattern is acceptable but non-idiomatic Go. While correct for cleanup, a labeled error block or explicit error variable might be clearer. Acceptable as-is but document the pattern choice.

✅ reindex.go:344-348 — Orderly shutdown: close workCh, wait for workers, close sessionsCh, wait for writer. No goroutine leaks.

**BACKGROUND WARM INDEXING:**

✅ reindex.go:351-391 — warmIndexInBackground runs two silent passes with reusable enumeration result.

✅ reindex.go:358-363 — Opens store with nil-safety for db path and open errors (returns cleanly if index unavailable).

✅ reindex.go:360 — enumerateAll(registry, false) passes visible=false for silent enumeration.

✅ reindex.go:365 — Early return if context cancelled during enumeration.

✅ reindex.go:369 — min(runtime.NumCPU(), 4) uses smaller worker count for background work (user is interacting; be gentle).

⚠️ reindex.go:369 — No comment explaining why 4 is the background cap. Rationale should match line 139 cap of 12.

✅ reindex.go:373-388 — Pass 1 (current project): selects work, processes, checks context, calls onProjectWarmed callback to refresh TUI. Correct two-phase flow.

✅ reindex.go:386 — Critical: Fingerprints reloaded before Pass 2 so Pass 1 work registers as unchanged and is skipped. Prevents double-parsing.

**LIVE INDEXING:**

✅ reindex.go:405-420 — LiveIndexer struct holds store handle, cwd/projectID, and per-session lastSeen tracking.

✅ reindex.go:418 — mu sync.Mutex protects map access.

✅ reindex.go:425-442 — NewLiveIndexer nil-safely constructs, returning nil if index unavailable. Best-effort pattern avoids crashing the caller.

⚠️ reindex.go:430-432 — Errors logged at debug, but comment doesn't explicitly state why nil return is OK (best-effort principle mentioned in docstring above struct). Clear but could be more explicit in code.

✅ reindex.go:448-505 — Record method upserts live session row.

✅ reindex.go:449-450 — Nil checks on receiver, sess, SessionData. Safe on nil receiver.

✅ reindex.go:460-463 — UpdatedAt computed as CreatedAt or last message timestamp if available. Correct field derivation.

✅ reindex.go:465-466 — Lock/defer unlock pattern correct.

✅ reindex.go:469-470 — Skips upsert if UpdatedAt unchanged. Optimization to avoid FTS re-indexing on no-op updates. Comment could clarify that FTS index update is the expensive operation.

✅ reindex.go:472 — Only checks DB existence once per session (checked in lastSeen map). Efficient.

✅ reindex.go:477-482 — isNew detection: check DB only if session not yet written in this process. Prevents redundant lookups.

✅ reindex.go:485-500 — Session row built with all fields, including IsNew flag for store optimization.

⚠️ reindex.go:502 — Upsert error logged at debug and silently continues. Best-effort is correct, but should this increment a failure counter (e.g., analytics) so operators know live indexing is degraded?

✅ reindex.go:509-516 — Close method nil-safe, ignores close errors at debug level.

**SESSION BUILDING & PARSING:**

✅ reindex.go:518-562 — buildSession constructs index row, enriching metadata with body/turn counts if cwd available.

✅ reindex.go:523-537 — Metadata always populated from ref: projectID, agent, sessionID, createdAt, slug, name, native path, size, mtime.

✅ reindex.go:539-546 — If OriginCwd unknown (Cursor), returns metadata-only row. Graceful degradation documented.

✅ reindex.go:548-561 — Full parse attempt: if available, enriches UpdatedAt with last-message timestamp, body with flattened text, turn counts. On error, logs at debug and returns metadata-only.

✅ reindex.go:566-576 — parseFullSession tries PathSessionReader first (O(1) by path), falls back to by-id lookup. Comment correctly notes this avoids O(N²) Codex enumeration. Excellent optimization.

**BATCH WRITING:**

✅ reindex.go:578-597 — drainToStore batches upserts correctly.

✅ reindex.go:574 — Batch initialized with capacity reindexWriteBatch (200 sessions per transaction).

✅ reindex.go:575-587 — flush closure handles both mid-batch and final flushes. Resets slice after each flush (batch[:0]) for reuse.

✅ reindex.go:590-596 — Loop reads from channel, appends to batch, flushes when threshold hit, flushes remainder at end. Correct batching pattern.

**HELPER FUNCTIONS:**

✅ reindex.go:604-617 — flattenBody joins turns with double newlines. Uses spi.FlattenSessionData (which strips synthetic noise already). Clean.

✅ reindex.go:620-630 — countTurns iterates all messages, counts user vs. total. Simple and correct.

✅ reindex.go:633-643 — lastTimestamp finds final non-empty timestamp. Handles sessions with no timestamps (returns ""). Correct.

✅ reindex.go:646-652 — statNative calls os.Stat, returns size (bytes) and mtime (epoch ms). Returns zeros on error (safe fallback).

**PROJECT ID CACHE:**

✅ reindex.go:654-680 — projectIDCache memoizes ComputeProjectID by cwd.

✅ reindex.go:660-663 — resolve checks empty cwd, locks, checks cache, computes if missing, stores result, unlocks. Thread-safe pattern.

✅ reindex.go:665-673 — Cache hit/miss logic correct; error handling defaults to unknownProjectID.

**DISPLAY HELPERS:**

✅ reindex.go:684-693 — summarizeCounts renders "agent count · agent count" in registry order, omitting zeros. Used in both summary and live scan progress.

**PROGRESS RENDERING (REINDEX):**

✅ reindex.go:701-733 — reindexProgress renders per-agent progress bars.

✅ reindex.go:710-722 — done map holds per-agent atomic int64 pointers. Allows workers to increment without mutex contention.

✅ reindex.go:729-732 — Constructor captures tty mode once at startup.

✅ reindex.go:735-743 — begin spawns render loop; end waits for loop and draws final frame at 100%.

✅ reindex.go:745-749 — inc atomically increments agent counter.

✅ reindex.go:751-760 — observe records seen projects (skips unknownProjectID to avoid inflating live count).

✅ reindex.go:762-766 — projectCount locks, reads projects set size, unlocks.

✅ reindex.go:768-774 — totalDone sums all atomic counters without locking (safe; atomic.LoadInt64 reads current value).

✅ reindex.go:776-782 — grandTotal sums totals map (read-only, set at startup).

✅ reindex.go:784-800 — loop manages ticker, redraws at 100ms (TTY) or 1500ms (non-TTY). Defers close(doneCh).

✅ reindex.go:802-822 — draw method uses ANSI escape codes to redraw block in place (\033[%dA up, \033[2K clear). Tracks linesDrawn to position cursor correctly.

✅ reindex.go:824-847 — frameLines builds display: header with progress, per-agent bars with counts and checkmarks. Calculates column width for alignment.

✅ reindex.go:850-860 — progressBar renders fixed-width bar (14 chars), clamps filled to [0, w] (no overflow). Uses ▕, █, ░, ▏ characters.

**PROGRESS RENDERING (SCAN):**

✅ reindex.go:875 — scanSpinner defines 10 braille animation frames for visual feedback.

✅ reindex.go:878-910 — scanProgress renders one-line live "Scanning…" output while providers enumerate.

✅ reindex.go:902-917 — Constructor builds agent list and byID map for quick lookups.

✅ reindex.go:921-929 — reporterFor returns provider's ScanReporter (nil-safe: nil scanProgress yields nil).

✅ reindex.go:934-943 — markDone reconciles running count to final count. Only applies delta if nonzero (no-op for providers that report progress).

✅ reindex.go:945-956 — begin prints banner on non-TTY or draws live line on TTY immediately (before first tick).

✅ reindex.go:958-968 — end closes stopCh, waits for loop, erases live line on TTY so summary replaces it (good UX).

✅ reindex.go:970-991 — loop ticks at same intervals as reindexProgress, draws or prints counts.

✅ reindex.go:993-1007 — draw and line format ANSI cursor movement and spinner/counts output.

✅ reindex.go:1009-1023 — counts iterates agents, collects non-zero counts, returns formatted string (or "…" if none).

**SUMMARY OUTPUT:**

✅ reindex.go:1027-1041 — printReindexSummary displays final output with conditional formatting (shows unchanged count if >0, shows projects and unattributed sessions).

✅ reindex.go:307-311 — Work sorted by size descending (largest first). Comment clearly explains WHY: prevents end-of-run stragglers leaving cores idle. Excellent optimization.

✅ reindex.go:313-314 — sessionsCh buffered at 256 to absorb parsing variance without blocking workers.

✅ reindex.go:314 — writeErr captured by closure in writer goroutine (line 318).

✅ reindex.go:315 — writerDone signals when writer finishes (important for orderly shutdown).

✅ reindex.go:317 — defer close(writerDone) ensures writer always signals completion.

✅ reindex.go:321-333 — Worker goroutine pattern: iterate over workCh, build session, observe project, increment counter. Proper defer wwg.Done().

⚠️ reindex.go:322 — Loop variable `wwg` (worker wait group) abbreviated; consider `workerWg`.

✅ reindex.go:336-341 — Feed work with context cancellation check. If ctx.Done(), goto closing stops feeding work immediately.

⚠️ reindex.go:337-339 — goto closing pattern is acceptable but non-idiomatic Go. While correct for cleanup, a labeled error block or explicit error variable might be clearer. Acceptable as-is but document the pattern choice.

✅ reindex.go:344-348 — Orderly shutdown: close workCh, wait for workers, close sessionsCh, wait for writer. No goroutine leaks.

**BACKGROUND WARM INDEXING:**

✅ reindex.go:351-391 — warmIndexInBackground runs two silent passes with reusable enumeration result.

✅ reindex.go:358-363 — Opens store with nil-safety for db path and open errors (returns cleanly if index unavailable).

✅ reindex.go:360 — enumerateAll(registry, false) passes visible=false for silent enumeration.

✅ reindex.go:365 — Early return if context cancelled during enumeration.

✅ reindex.go:369 — min(runtime.NumCPU(), 4) uses smaller worker count for background work (user is interacting; be gentle).

⚠️ reindex.go:369 — No comment explaining why 4 is the background cap. Rationale should match line 139 cap of 12.

✅ reindex.go:373-388 — Pass 1 (current project): selects work, processes, checks context, calls onProjectWarmed callback to refresh TUI. Correct two-phase flow.

✅ reindex.go:386 — Critical: Fingerprints reloaded before Pass 2 so Pass 1 work registers as unchanged and is skipped. Prevents double-parsing.

**LIVE INDEXING:**

✅ reindex.go:405-420 — LiveIndexer struct holds store handle, cwd/projectID, and per-session lastSeen tracking.

✅ reindex.go:418 — mu sync.Mutex protects map access.

✅ reindex.go:425-442 — NewLiveIndexer nil-safely constructs, returning nil if index unavailable. Best-effort pattern avoids crashing the caller.

⚠️ reindex.go:430-432 — Errors logged at debug, but comment doesn't explicitly state why nil return is OK (best-effort principle mentioned in docstring above struct). Clear but could be more explicit in code.

✅ reindex.go:448-505 — Record method upserts live session row.

✅ reindex.go:449-450 — Nil checks on receiver, sess, SessionData. Safe on nil receiver.

✅ reindex.go:460-463 — UpdatedAt computed as CreatedAt or last message timestamp if available. Correct field derivation.

✅ reindex.go:465-466 — Lock/defer unlock pattern correct.

✅ reindex.go:469-470 — Skips upsert if UpdatedAt unchanged. Optimization to avoid FTS re-indexing on no-op updates. Comment could clarify that FTS index update is the expensive operation.

✅ reindex.go:472 — Only checks DB existence once per session (checked in lastSeen map). Efficient.

✅ reindex.go:477-482 — isNew detection: check DB only if session not yet written in this process. Prevents redundant lookups.

✅ reindex.go:485-500 — Session row built with all fields, including IsNew flag for store optimization.

⚠️ reindex.go:502 — Upsert error logged at debug and silently continues. Best-effort is correct, but should this increment a failure counter (e.g., analytics) so operators know live indexing is degraded?

✅ reindex.go:509-516 — Close method nil-safe, ignores close errors at debug level.

**SESSION BUILDING &amp; PARSING:**

✅ reindex.go:518-562 — buildSession constructs index row, enriching metadata with body/turn counts if cwd available.

✅ reindex.go:523-537 — Metadata always populated from ref: projectID, agent, sessionID, createdAt, slug, name, native path, size, mtime.

✅ reindex.go:539-546 — If OriginCwd unknown (Cursor), returns metadata-only row. Graceful degradation documented.

✅ reindex.go:548-561 — Full parse attempt: if available, enriches UpdatedAt with last-message timestamp, body with flattened text, turn counts. On error, logs at debug and returns metadata-only.

✅ reindex.go:566-576 — parseFullSession tries PathSessionReader first (O(1) by path), falls back to by-id lookup. Comment correctly notes this avoids O(N²) Codex enumeration. Excellent optimization.

**BATCH WRITING:**

✅ reindex.go:578-597 — drainToStore batches upserts correctly.

✅ reindex.go:574 — Batch initialized with capacity reindexWriteBatch (200 sessions per transaction).

✅ reindex.go:575-587 — flush closure handles both mid-batch and final flushes. Resets slice after each flush (batch[:0]) for reuse.

✅ reindex.go:590-596 — Loop reads from channel, appends to batch, flushes when threshold hit, flushes remainder at end. Correct batching pattern.

**HELPER FUNCTIONS:**

✅ reindex.go:604-617 — flattenBody joins turns with double newlines. Uses spi.FlattenSessionData (which strips synthetic noise already). Clean.

✅ reindex.go:620-630 — countTurns iterates all messages, counts user vs. total. Simple and correct.

✅ reindex.go:633-643 — lastTimestamp finds final non-empty timestamp. Handles sessions with no timestamps (returns ""). Correct.

✅ reindex.go:646-652 — statNative calls os.Stat, returns size (bytes) and mtime (epoch ms). Returns zeros on error (safe fallback).

**PROJECT ID CACHE:**

✅ reindex.go:654-680 — projectIDCache memoizes ComputeProjectID by cwd.

✅ reindex.go:660-663 — resolve checks empty cwd, locks, checks cache, computes if missing, stores result, unlocks. Thread-safe pattern.

✅ reindex.go:665-673 — Cache hit/miss logic correct; error handling defaults to unknownProjectID.

**DISPLAY HELPERS:**

✅ reindex.go:684-693 — summarizeCounts renders "agent count · agent count" in registry order, omitting zeros. Used in both summary and live scan progress.

**PROGRESS RENDERING (REINDEX):**

✅ reindex.go:701-733 — reindexProgress renders per-agent progress bars.

✅ reindex.go:710-722 — done map holds per-agent atomic int64 pointers. Allows workers to increment without mutex contention.

✅ reindex.go:729-732 — Constructor captures tty mode once at startup.

✅ reindex.go:735-743 — begin spawns render loop; end waits for loop and draws final frame at 100%.

✅ reindex.go:745-749 — inc atomically increments agent counter.

✅ reindex.go:751-760 — observe records seen projects (skips unknownProjectID to avoid inflating live count).

✅ reindex.go:762-766 — projectCount locks, reads projects set size, unlocks.

✅ reindex.go:768-774 — totalDone sums all atomic counters without locking (safe; atomic.LoadInt64 reads current value).

✅ reindex.go:776-782 — grandTotal sums totals map (read-only, set at startup).

✅ reindex.go:784-800 — loop manages ticker, redraws at 100ms (TTY) or 1500ms (non-TTY). Defers close(doneCh).

✅ reindex.go:802-822 — draw method uses ANSI escape codes to redraw block in place (\033[%dA up, \033[2K clear). Tracks linesDrawn to position cursor correctly.

✅ reindex.go:824-847 — frameLines builds display: header with progress, per-agent bars with counts and checkmarks. Calculates column width for alignment.

✅ reindex.go:850-860 — progressBar renders fixed-width bar (14 chars), clamps filled to [0, w] (no overflow). Uses ▕, █, ░, ▏ characters.

**PROGRESS RENDERING (SCAN):**

✅ reindex.go:875 — scanSpinner defines 10 braille animation frames for visual feedback.

✅ reindex.go:878-910 — scanProgress renders one-line live "Scanning…" output while providers enumerate.

✅ reindex.go:902-917 — Constructor builds agent list and byID map for quick lookups.

✅ reindex.go:921-929 — reporterFor returns provider's ScanReporter (nil-safe: nil scanProgress yields nil).

✅ reindex.go:934-943 — markDone reconciles running count to final count. Only applies delta if nonzero (no-op for providers that report progress).

✅ reindex.go:945-956 — begin prints banner on non-TTY or draws live line on TTY immediately (before first tick).

✅ reindex.go:958-968 — end closes stopCh, waits for loop, erases live line on TTY so summary replaces it (good UX).

✅ reindex.go:970-991 — loop ticks at same intervals as reindexProgress, draws or prints counts.

✅ reindex.go:993-1007 — draw and line format ANSI cursor movement and spinner/counts output.

✅ reindex.go:1009-1023 — counts iterates agents, collects non-zero counts, returns formatted string (or "…" if none).

**SUMMARY OUTPUT:**

✅ reindex.go:1027-1041 — printReindexSummary displays final output with conditional formatting (shows unchanged count if &gt;0, shows projects and unattributed sessions).

✅ reindex.go:1044-1049 — prettyPath abbreviates home directory to ~ for display (user-friendly paths).</result>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-06-29 15:33:32Z)**_

All 8 review slices are in. Here is the consolidated, sequentially-numbered line-by-line review of PR #244. Because the PR is ~9,700 lines, I've kept every **concern/question/bug** as its own numbered item, and consolidated runs of routine "this is correct" approvals into single numbered observations (noted as such) so the document stays navigable.

---

# PR #244 — Cross-project and cross-agent session portability

## Architecture summary

The PR adds a **reconstruct framework** (`pkg/spi`) that flattens any provider's `SessionData` into plain turns, plus per-provider `ReconstructSession` implementations that rebuild a *native* session file for a target agent. A new **SQLite session index** (`pkg/sessionindex`) with FTS5 powers cross-project/cross-agent search. New commands `resume`, `reindex`, `search` and a 2,010-line Bubble Tea TUI tie it together, with `LiveIndexer` keeping the index warm from `run`/`watch`/`sync`. Project identity is reworked to be stable across machines (git-root walk-up + case-folding).

---

## `pkg/spi/global.go`

1. ✅ **global.go:5-15** — `GlobalSessionRef` documented as metadata-only; explains why project is *discovered* not supplied. Strong "why" comment.
2. ✅ **global.go:25-41** — New `PathSessionReader` interface; doc explains the O(N²)→O(N) motivation for path-keyed reads. Good.
3. ✅ **global.go:43-64** — `ScanReporter` uses `atomic.Int64` and nil-safe `Add`/`Found` (guard `if r != nil`). Correct for concurrent, optional reporting.
4. ✅ **global.go:67-72** — `ProgressEnumerator` optional interface, well-documented as opt-in per-agent progress.

## `pkg/spi/provider.go`

5. ✅ **provider.go:99-124** — Three new interface methods (`ReconstructSession`, `NativeSessionPath`, `ListAllAgentChatSessions`) documented clearly: pure transform vs. path resolver vs. global enumerator. Good contracts.

## `pkg/spi/reconstruct.go`

6. ✅ **reconstruct.go:10-27** — `ErrReconstructionUnsupported` + `ReconstructOptions` (`WorkspaceRoot`, `MigrationNote`) with "why" comments.
7. ✅ **reconstruct.go:46-96** — `FlattenSessionData`: locked-design rules, returns `nil` (not empty) when no data, migration note trimmed and prepended. Guard-clause ordering (role → text → synthetic filter → append) is sound.
8. ✅ **reconstruct.go:98-141** — `SyntheticCommandTags` marked single-source-of-truth (shared with `claudecode.isSyntheticMessage`); `isSyntheticTurnText` correctly uses `Contains` (flattened turns concatenate parts) vs. claudecode's prefix match — and the difference is documented.
9. ✅ **reconstruct.go:143-195** — `messageTurnText`/`toolTurnText` use `strings.Builder`, are nil-safe on `tool.Summary`, and fall back to `"Tool use: " + Name`. Idiomatic.

## `pkg/spi/reconstruct_test.go`

10. ✅ **reconstruct_test.go:11-173** — Eight focused tests covering pass-through, thinking-folding, tool fallback, all six synthetic tags, interrupt markers, nil-vs-empty return, blank-turn skipping, tool-only sessions. Non-tautological; would catch real regressions. `strptr` helper is idiomatic.
11. ❓ **reconstruct_test.go (gap)** — No `ScanReporter` concurrency test and no negative-`n` `Add` test. Low priority (atomic wrapper), worth a note only.

---

## `pkg/sessionindex/store.go`

12. ✅ **store.go:22-29, 110, 172-187** — Connection-pool sizing rationale documented; DSN uses `filepath.ToSlash`; composite index `(project_id, updated_at, created_at)` matches the `ListByProject` hot path; FTS5 with `UNINDEXED` join keys is the right pattern. (Consolidated approval.)
13. ✅ **store.go:196-233, 245-297** — Idempotent migrations (`ALTER … IF NOT EXISTS`, `DROP INDEX IF EXISTS`); `upsertOne` inserts the FTS row first to capture rowid (avoids O(n) FTS rescans); `deleteOldFTSRow` handles legacy NULL `fts_rowid`. Strong design.
14. ✅ **store.go:317-393** — `prepareUpsert` recovers via `closer()` on prep failure; `UpsertBatch` uses the committed-flag + deferred-rollback pattern. Textbook transaction handling.
15. ✅ **store.go (SQL safety)** — All queries parameterized; the only string-built column lists come from constant `sessionColumns`. No injection surface. (Consolidated.)
16. ⚠️ **store.go:195** — Schema-exec error returned bare; inconsistent with the `%w`-wrapping elsewhere (e.g. line 129). Wrap as `fmt.Errorf("execute schema: %w", err)`.
17. ⚠️ **store.go:140** — Comment says "scanSession" but the function is `scanSessions` (typo).
18. ⚠️ **store.go:545** — `LIMIT 500` is unexplained. Add a comment on why 500 (UI responsiveness? memory bound?).
19. ⚠️ **store.go:588** — `snippet(…, char(2), char(3), '…', 12)` uses magic numbers. Document the markers/length or extract named constants.
20. ⚠️ **store.go:482-484** — `ProjectSummary` only sets `ProjectName` when empty, assuming all rows of a `project_id` share a name. Add a comment stating that assumption.
21. ❓ **store.go:481** — `AgentCounts[agent] += n` relies on the `GROUP BY project_id, agent` shape to avoid double-counting; a one-line comment would lock that contract in.
22. ❌ **store.go (observability)** — No `slog` on error paths (schema setup, open timeout, commit failure) and no `analytics.TrackEvent`. Reindex/search failures here are silent. Add at least `slog.Error` on the commit/open failures.

## `pkg/sessionindex/store_test.go`

23. ✅ **store_test.go:99-159** — `TestUpsertIsIdempotent` and `TestUpsertHandlesLegacyNullRowid` are exemplary: they exercise stale-FTS cleanup and the NULL-rowid migration fallback — exactly the bugs the design risks. High value.
24. ✅ **store_test.go:207-281** — `TestListProjectsRollup` and `TestSearchAndSnippets` validate per-agent counts, recency sort, STX/ETX snippet markup, project exclusion, and wildcard queries. Deterministic, `t.TempDir`-isolated, not testing the driver.
25. ⚠️ **store_test.go:41-73** — `TestUpsertAndListByProject` never asserts the `updated_at DESC, created_at DESC` ordering — the main reason for the composite index. Add an order assertion.
26. ⚠️ **store_test.go:75-97** — FTS test only uses bare terms; add a wildcard/prefix case (`oau*`) to lock in prefix-query behavior.

---

## `pkg/cmd/reindex.go`

(Largest new file, ~1,043 lines. Routine approvals consolidated; concerns itemized.)

27. ✅ **reindex.go:27-47** — `reindexVersion` carries a changelog explaining each bump; version mismatch forces a full re-index. Good versioning discipline.
28. ✅ **reindex.go:83-159** — `runReindex` is cleanly phased (enumerate → dedup → select → process → summarize) with `signal.NotifyContext` for graceful Ctrl-C/SIGTERM and deferred `store.Close`.
29. ✅ **reindex.go:167-246** — `enumerateAll` runs one goroutine per provider with index-aligned slices (nil `provs[i]` marks failure); `dedupRefs` collapses by `(agent, sessionID)` keeping the freshest by mtime; empty `SessionID` rows skipped. Sound concurrency.
30. ✅ **reindex.go:252-281** — `selectWork` skips unchanged sessions only when Size+Mtime+Version all match; `isNew` lets the store skip FTS deletes for genuinely new rows. Correct and defensive.
31. ✅ **reindex.go:293-349** — `processWork` worker-pool + single writer goroutine, work sorted largest-first to avoid end-of-run stragglers, buffered `sessionsCh`, orderly shutdown (close workCh → wait workers → close sessionsCh → wait writer). No leaks.
32. ✅ **reindex.go:351-391** — `warmIndexInBackground` does a current-project pass then reloads fingerprints before the all-projects pass so pass-1 work registers as unchanged. Uses gentler worker cap. Good.
33. ✅ **reindex.go:405-516** — `LiveIndexer` is nil-safe on construct/Record/Close, mutex-guards `lastSeen`, and skips upsert when `UpdatedAt` is unchanged (avoids needless FTS churn).
34. ✅ **reindex.go:518-597** — `buildSession` degrades to metadata-only when `OriginCwd` is unknown (Cursor); `parseFullSession` prefers `PathSessionReader` then by-id (avoids O(N²) Codex re-enumeration); `drainToStore` batches 200/transaction. Strong.
35. ✅ **reindex.go:701-1049** — `reindexProgress`/`scanProgress` use atomic per-agent counters and ANSI in-place redraw with TTY/non-TTY cadence; `prettyPath` abbreviates `$HOME`→`~`. (Consolidated approval of ~40 render bullets.)
36. ⚠️ **reindex.go:96-102** — Inconsistent error wrapping: `dbPath` returns bare while `store.Open` is wrapped. Normalize.
37. ⚠️ **reindex.go:127** — "No agent sessions found" only goes to stderr; also `slog.Warn` so it survives stderr redirection.
38. ⚠️ **reindex.go:139 & 369** — Worker caps (12 foreground, 4 background) have no rationale comment. Document why.
39. ⚠️ **reindex.go:33** — `reindexWriteBatch = 200` unexplained (transaction size/latency tradeoff). Add a comment.
40. ⚠️ **reindex.go:186, 322** — Abbreviated waitgroup names `ewg`/`wwg` → `enumerationWg`/`workerWg`.
41. ⚠️ **reindex.go:188-189** — Provider load errors are silently `continue`d. Log at Warn so a missing agent binary is visible.
42. ⚠️ **reindex.go:79-80, 236** — `reindexItem` fields `size`/`mtime` would read clearer as `fileSizeBytes`/`fileModTimeMs`.
43. ⚠️ **reindex.go:337-339** — `goto closing` for cancellation is correct but non-idiomatic; consider a labeled block or sentinel, or comment the choice.
44. ⚠️ **reindex.go:151-157** — `EventReindexCompleted` only tracks `len(work)` (this run's incremental count). Also emit total-found and unchanged-skipped for a complete picture.
45. ⚠️ **reindex.go:502** — Live-index upsert errors are debug-logged and dropped; consider a failure counter so a degraded live index is observable.

## `pkg/cmd/reindex_test.go`

46. ✅ **reindex_test.go:76-101** — `TestProgressBar` (table-driven: 0%, 100%, 50%, zero-total no-divide-by-zero, >100% clamp) and `TestSummarizeCounts` (zero omission, registry order) are good, focused tests.
47. 🐛 **reindex_test.go (coverage gap)** — Only 6 tests for ~1,043 lines; they cover trivial helpers but **none of the core orchestration**: `dedupRefs` keeping freshest-by-mtime, `selectWork` fingerprint-skip, `processWork` coordination/cancellation, `LiveIndexer.Record` concurrency/`isNew`, `buildSession` metadata fallback. This is the most important test gap in the PR (see test section below).
48. ⚠️ **reindex_test.go:66-74** — `TestProjectIDCacheMemoizes` pre-seeds the cache and only checks read-back; it doesn't exercise the real resolution path.

## `pkg/cmd/resume.go`

49. ✅ **resume.go:31-49** — `fprintf`/`fprintln` best-effort UI helpers (terminal write errors not actionable); `resumePlan` documents the `fromCwd` cross-project edge case. Excellent.
50. ✅ **resume.go:204-209** — `analytics.TrackEvent(EventResumeActivated, …)` includes from/to providers and a `cross_agent` flag. Exactly the right instrumentation.
51. ✅ **resume.go:268-325** — `prepareResumeTarget`: clean same-agent guard, then cross-agent reconstruct path with the `fromCwd`→`cwd` fallback (the core cross-project fix), and a friendly message on `ErrReconstructionUnsupported`.
52. ✅ **resume.go:340-432** — `writeReconstructedSession` is production-grade: same-dir temp file → chmod 0o644 → `Sync` → atomic rename → best-effort dir fsync; `waitForSessionFileVisible`/`sessionFileReadable` poll readability (read one line) to defeat weak-coherence/network FS lag. "Why" comments throughout.
53. ⚠️ **resume.go:113-117** — `os.Getwd()` failure handling likely duplicates `run`/`watch`; consider a shared helper if identical.
54. ⚠️ **resume.go:195-197** — `EnsureProjectIdentity` logs-but-ignores its error, whereas the adjacent output-config errors are returned. Inconsistent; add a one-line "why it's safe to ignore" comment.
55. ⚠️ **resume.go:290** — `GetAgentChatSession(..., false)` — add a comment that raw loading isn't needed (only `SessionData` is required for reconstruction).
56. 🐛 **resume_test.go:212** — `markdownSize` assigned but unused; discard with `_` or assert on it.

## `pkg/cmd/resume_test.go`

57. ✅ **resume_test.go:16-145** — Table-driven `TestFTSQuery`/`TestQueryReady`/`TestShortID` plus filesystem tests (`TestWriteReconstructedSession`, `TestSessionFileReadable`, `TestWaitForSessionFileVisible`) cover real weak-coherence edge cases (empty file = not yet propagated). High value.
58. ✅ **resume_test.go:239-294** — `TestPrepareResumeTargetLoadsSourceFromOriginCwd` and `…FallsBackToCurrentCwd` are precise regression tests for the cross-project fix; `fakeProvider` captures `gotLoadPath` to prove source loads from origin cwd. Excellent.
59. ✅ **resume_test.go:299-382** — `TestLiveIndexerRecord` covers insert, immediate FTS searchability, dedup, in-place update, and nil-indexer safety.
60. ⚠️ **resume_test.go:147-189** — TUI-mode tests poke unexported struct fields (`m.beginResume`, `rm.mode`, `rm.chosen`) — testing implementation detail rather than behavior. Not tautological, but fragile; consider a thin test helper.

## `pkg/cmd/search.go`

61. ✅ **search.go:21-49** — Clear command; empty-index check gives actionable next steps. Good UX.
62. ✅ **search.go:78-117** — Starts in all-projects FTS, warms the index in the background and cancels on exit, and sets `fromCwd: rm.result.session.OriginCwd` (the cross-project fix) with an explanatory comment.
63. ⚠️ **search.go:32-48** — Index-open + home-project computation duplicates `resume.go:128-136`. Extract a shared helper.
64. ⚠️ **search.go:92-95** — Unchecked type assertion `final.(sessionTUI)` can panic if `p.Run()` returns another type. Use the comma-ok form or comment why it's guaranteed.
65. 🐛 **search.go:118** — `resumeLaunchOpts` only sets `useUTC: true`; all flags users can pass to `resume` (`--output-dir`, `--debug-raw`, cloud-sync toggles, provenance, telemetry) are silently dropped. Either thread them through or document why `search`→resume uses fixed defaults. **Most impactful functional inconsistency in this slice.**

## `pkg/cmd/watch.go`

66. ✅ **watch.go:191-232** — Wires `LiveIndexer` into watch's `sessionCallback` (nil = no-op), mirroring `resume`/`sync`. Correct and well-commented.

---

## `pkg/cmd/session_tui.go` (2,010 lines, no test file)

67. ✅ **session_tui.go:35-130, 200-284** — `tuiMode` enum + `Update` dispatch are well-organized; debounce uses a seq counter and cancels the prior in-flight query. Idiomatic Bubble Tea, no blocking in `Update`.
68. ✅ **session_tui.go:694-786** — `ftsQuery`/`fieldTokens`/`segmentTokens`/`queryReady` mirror the FTS unicode61 tokenizer and enforce `minQueryLen=2`. Well-commented business logic.
69. ✅ **session_tui.go:1076-1125** — `relativeTime`, `renderGlamour` (graceful raw-markdown fallback), `headerRow` are clean.
70. 🐛 **session_tui.go:504-529, 1451-1476, 1742-1767** — **Three near-identical cursor-move + scroll-clamp pairs** (`moveCursor`/`moveProjCursor`/`moveGlobalCursor` and their `clamp*Scroll`). Extract one generic `move(delta, len, *cursor, *top)`. Top DRY issue in the TUI.
71. ⚠️ **session_tui.go:890-920, 1851-1875** — `sessionRow` and `globalRow` duplicate row-rendering logic (global adds a project column). Extract a shared renderer with a `includeProjColumn bool`.
72. ⚠️ **session_tui.go:626, 345-348, 1131-1139** — Several silent error swallows: `applyFilter` ignores `store.Search` errors, `drillInto` returns silently on `ListByProject` failure, `sessionMarkdown` silently falls back when re-parse from `OriginCwd` fails (degraded mode invisible to user). Add at least `slog.Debug`/`Warn`.
73. ⚠️ **session_tui.go:1268** — `final.(sessionTUI)` unchecked assertion (same risk as search.go:92) — can panic.
74. ⚠️ **session_tui.go:1194, 1215-1218** — `startIndexWarm`'s `p.Send` failure unhandled; agent-load loop silently `continue`s on error (no per-agent visibility).
75. ⚠️ **session_tui.go:1045** — `agentTag` hardcodes `%-8s`; agent IDs >8 chars break alignment. Validate ID width at load time.
76. ⚠️ **session_tui.go:2006-2008** — Snippet loads use `context.Background()` rather than the search context; stale snippet work can run (the seq check guards correctness but wastes work).
77. ⚠️ **session_tui.go:924-967** — `renderSnippet` control-char protocol (`\x02`/`\x03` highlight markers from `store.Snippets`) is undocumented. Add a comment.
78. 🐛 **session_tui.go (file size/testability)** — 2,010 lines in one file is at the maintainability seam; logically splittable into model/list/browser/search/async/helpers. More importantly, ~8 pure functions (`ftsQuery`, `queryReady`, `fieldTokens`, `relativeTime`, `truncate`, `dateBucket`, `renderSnippet`, `sessionTitle`) — ~300 lines of testable logic — have **zero tests**. (See test section.)

---

## Providers — Claude Code

79. ✅ **jsonl_parser.go:541-572** — `buildDAGs` rewritten from O(N²) to O(N) via a precomputed `childrenByParent` adjacency map, with a capacity hint and a comment quantifying the win (25k records, 50s+ in `sync`). Excellent.
80. ✅ **jsonl_parser_test.go:834-892** — Performance regression test raised to 30k records with a 3s ceiling (~100× margin) and a comment explaining why N must stay large to trip a quadratic bug. Well-calibrated tripwire.
81. ✅ **path_utils.go:15-55, 116** — Extracts `encodeProjectDirName` (kills regex duplication) and adds `resolveClaudeProjectDir` (write-time, dir need not exist) as the counterpart to the read-time `GetClaudeCodeProjectDir`. Clean DRY refactor with a symlink-resolution fallback.
82. ✅ **provider.go:490-510, 762-786** — `isSyntheticMessage` extended for slash-command scaffolding using **start-of-record prefix** matching (avoids false positives mid-prompt), referencing shared `spi.SyntheticCommandTags`; `extractCommandName`/`between` helpers are clear.
83. ✅ **provider.go:642-876** — `scanClaudeSession` single-pass header reader (captures `cwd` for the restore index, falls back to command name for slash-only session titles) + parallel Phase-1-walk/Phase-2-scan worker pool capped at `min(NumCPU,12)`. Sound.
84. ⚠️ **provider.go:490-511** — The expanded `isSyntheticMessage` would benefit from a one-line summary comment listing what it filters (warmup, title-gen, slash noise, interrupts).
85. ✅ **reconstruct.go:36-116** — Builds Claude Code JSONL with `SetEscapeHTML(false)` (preserves `<…>` tags/code), correct role/content/usage schema, provenance `specstorySourceSessionId`.
86. ⚠️ **reconstruct.go:36-116** — No `slog`/analytics on reconstruction (true for every provider's reconstruct — see #100). Add `slog.Info("session reconstructed", sourceID, newID)`.
87. ✅ **reconstruct_test.go:67-154** — Round-trip test (data → reconstruct → re-parse → flatten → compare), parent-chain test, path-encoding test, empty-session error test. Exemplary, non-tautological.

## Providers — Codex

88. ✅ **codexcli/provider.go:341-375, 631-715** — `GetAgentChatSessionByPath` and `collectRaw=false` path skip multi-MB raw-JSONL string building during reindex. Real memory win.
89. ✅ **codexcli/provider.go:1045-1210** — `scanCodexSessionHeader` (respects 64KB scan-token limit, screens for `userMessageMarker` before JSON parse) + same Phase-1/Phase-2 worker pool. Consistent with Claude.
90. ⚠️ **codexcli/provider.go:347-354** — Minor `SessionID`-extraction duplication between fast and full paths; acceptable but noted.
91. ✅ **codexcli/reconstruct.go:40-134** — UUIDv7 with v4 fallback; emits `session_meta` (with provenance) then per-turn dual `response_item` + `event_msg` records; date-based `~/.codex/sessions/YYYY/MM/DD` path. Matches observed Codex format.
92. ✅ **codexcli/reconstruct_test.go:68-165** — Round-trip + structure test (asserts exactly one `user_message` event per turn, no synthesized `environment_context`) + path test. Strong.

## Providers — Cursor / DeepSeek / Droid / Gemini

93. ✅ **cursorcli/reconstruct.go:21-156** — Rebuilds the content-addressed Merkle-DAG `store.db` (sha256 blob IDs, protobuf index nodes). Well-documented given the format's complexity.
94. ✅ **droidcli/reconstruct.go:59-94** — `session_start` carries provenance; linear `parentId` chaining with incrementing timestamps is correct.
95. ✅ **geminicli/reconstruct.go:126-158** — `prepareGeminiProjectDir` handles basename collisions with a hash-based fallback dir + `.project_root` marker. Correct, if complex.
96. 🐛 **Cross-provider DRY (cursor/deepseek/droid/gemini reconstruct.go)** — All four repeat the same nil-data check + `FlattenSessionData` + "session has no content to reconstruct" guard (~15 lines each). Extract `spi.ValidateSessionDataForReconstruction`.
97. 🐛 **Cross-provider DRY (cwd resolution)** — `cwd := opts.WorkspaceRoot; if cwd=="" { cwd = data.WorkspaceRoot }` appears identically in deepseek/droid/gemini. Extract `spi.ResolveWorkspaceRoot(opts, data)`.
98. 🐛 **Cross-provider DRY (`ListAllAgentChatSessions`)** — deepseek:326-355, droid:588-617, gemini:426-474 share a ~60-line enumerate→parse→skip-on-error→append template. Extract a shared helper/base in `spi`.
99. ⚠️ **Inconsistent enumeration log level** — cursorcli/provider.go:695 uses `slog.Warn` for skip-bad-file; the other three use `slog.Debug`. Normalize to `Debug` (skipping a malformed file isn't a system warning).
100. ❓ **All four reconstruct.go (+ claude/codex)** — No `slog`/`analytics.TrackEvent` on reconstruction success/failure. This is a critical recovery operation; add structured logging and a tracked event across all providers.
101. ⚠️ **droidcli/reconstruct.go:64** — `os.Getenv("USER")` for the owner field can be empty (CI/containers). Default to `"specstory"`.
102. ⚠️ **geminicli/reconstruct.go:104** — Uses `osGetwd()` alias (mock seam) with no comment; the other providers call `os.Getwd()` directly. Add a comment or rename to signal test-mockability.
103. ⚠️ **geminicli/reconstruct.go:145-146** — `MkdirAll` error message omits the path. Include `%q` target.
104. ⚠️ **cursorcli/reconstruct.go:47** — `hex.DecodeString(idHex)` discards the error; safe because input is always `blobID` output, but add a "safe: input is always valid hex" comment.
105. ⚠️ **cursorcli/reconstruct_test.go** — Unlike the other three, Cursor has **no round-trip test** — only a structural one. Can't confirm the rebuilt `store.db` is parseable. Add a round-trip test.
106. ⚠️ **All four test files** — `strptr` and `reconstructSampleData` helpers are copy-pasted; move to a shared test util. Negative coverage is limited to the empty-session case (no invalid path, huge content, or control-char tests).
107. ⚠️ **geminicli/reconstruct_test.go:87-89** — Globally mocks `osUserHomeDir` without parallel isolation; document "must not run in parallel" or scope it.

---

## `pkg/utils/project_identity.go`

108. ✅ **project_identity.go:68, 503-554** — `resolveIdentity` walks to git root before deriving IDs (fixes monorepo subdir fragmentation); `ComputeProjectID` documents the 3-tier precedence (git_id → persisted workspace_id → fresh workspace_id) so reindex/resume/cloud all agree.
109. ✅ **project_identity.go:187-208** — `canonicalizeWorkspacePath` case-folds on macOS/Windows, byte-exact on Linux, via a testable package var. Solves the real `os.Getwd()` casing-drift bug.
110. ✅ **project_identity.go:418-496** — `readOriginURL` regex extracted to a package var; `findGitRoot` + `resolveGitConfigPath` correctly follow `.git`-file → `gitdir` → `commondir` for worktrees. Thorough.
111. ⚠️ **project_identity.go:68** — `resolveIdentity`'s fourth return (`root`) is ignored at this call site though documented as "the resolved root." Either use it or trim the tuple.

## `pkg/utils/project_identity_test.go`

112. ✅ **project_identity_test.go:1070-1253** — `TestComputeProjectID` is an executable spec: monorepo collapse, SSH+HTTPS forms hashing equal, worktree resolution, remote-less path IDs, no-git stability, persisted-ID precedence, empty-cwd error. `TestCanonicalizeWorkspacePath`/`TestWorkspaceIDCaseFolding` validate both FS modes via the mockable var. DRY helpers, independent subtests. Excellent.

## `pkg/config/config.go` + test

113. ✅ **config.go:96-147, 746-785** — `[resume]` template + `ResumeConfig` + getters (`GetResumeViewMode` defaults "dense"); `SaveResumePrefs` wraps every error with context and special-cases missing file.
114. ✅ **config.go:791-842** — `upsertResumeSection` replaces only the `[resume]` block, preserving all other sections/comments, with correct newline handling.
115. ✅ **config_test.go:1283-1330** — Append-when-absent and replace-preserving-others tests validate via real `toml.Decode` round-trips (catches structural bugs string-matching would miss). Good.

## `pkg/analytics/events.go` + `main.go`

116. ✅ **events.go:13-14** — `EventResumeActivated`/`EventReindexCompleted` follow the `ext_*` convention; grep confirms both are actually emitted (resume.go / reindex.go).
117. ✅ **main.go:436-845** — `LiveIndexer` wired into `run`, `syncSpecificSessions`, and `syncProvider`, each nil-safe with deferred Close and "why" comments (indexing is "essentially free" since `SessionData` is already in hand). `foundAgentID` correctly captures the resolving provider for the Record call.
118. ✅ **main.go:1456-1470** — `resume`/`reindex`/`search` commands registered; all three constructors verified to exist.

---

## The 2–5 most important improvements

1. **Eliminate the cross-provider reconstruct duplication (#96–98).** The four newest providers repeat nil/flatten/empty validation, cwd resolution, and the `ListAllAgentChatSessions` enumeration template. Hoist `spi.ValidateSessionDataForReconstruction`, `spi.ResolveWorkspaceRoot`, and a shared enumeration helper. This is the single highest-leverage cleanup — it shrinks each provider, removes drift risk, and makes future providers trivial.

2. **Close the reindex orchestration test gap (#47).** The riskiest, most concurrent code in the PR (dedup-by-mtime, fingerprint skip, worker/writer coordination, context cancellation, `LiveIndexer` concurrency) has no tests, while trivial helpers do. Add table-driven tests on a temp dir of fixture session files (see test section).

3. **Fix the dropped-flags inconsistency in `search`→resume (#65).** `search.go:118` hardcodes `useUTC` and silently drops every other resume flag. Either thread the flags through or document the contract; today a user's `--output-dir`/provenance/cloud settings vanish when resuming from search.

4. **Add observability to reconstruction and the index store (#22, #100).** Reconstruction (all providers) and `store.go` error paths emit nothing. For a portability/recovery feature, add `slog` on success/failure and an `analytics.TrackEvent` on reconstruction so degraded behavior is visible in the field.

5. **De-duplicate the TUI cursor/scroll/row code and harden the two unchecked type assertions (#70, #71, #64, #73).** Three identical move/clamp pairs and two `final.(sessionTUI)` panics-in-waiting are the clearest correctness+maintainability wins in the UI layer; extracting the helpers also unblocks unit testing.

---

## Where new/updated tests are genuinely warranted

The PR's *existing* tests are mostly excellent — the reconstruct round-trips (#87, #92), store idempotency/legacy-rowid (#23), and `ComputeProjectID` spec (#112) are exactly the right kind: non-trivial, non-tautological, deterministic, testing our logic not the driver. The gaps worth filling (complex logic only, not coverage-chasing):

- **`reindex.go` orchestration (highest priority).** Build a temp directory of fixture session files with controlled mtimes/sizes and assert:
  - `dedupRefs` keeps the freshest file when `(agent, sessionID)` collide.
  - `selectWork` skips a session whose Size+Mtime+Version all match a fingerprint, and re-includes it when `reindexVersion` changes.
  - `LiveIndexer.Record` `isNew` detection and concurrent calls (it has a mutex that is never exercised under `-race`).
  These are combinatorial and bug-prone — precisely what merits tests.

- **TUI pure functions (#78).** Extract and table-test `ftsQuery` (bare/quoted/unclosed-quote/special chars), `queryReady` (1 vs 2 chars), `relativeTime` (each bucket + year boundary), `dateBucket`, and `renderSnippet` (control-char markers + width overflow). ~300 lines of logic with clean inputs/outputs and no UI dependency. Note: `ftsQuery`/`queryReady`/`shortID` *are* already tested via `resume_test.go` — confirm there's no duplicate copy in the TUI that can drift.

- **`store.go` ordering & wildcard (#25, #26).** Add the `updated_at DESC, created_at DESC` order assertion and a prefix-wildcard FTS case — both guard real behavior the index promises.

- **Cursor round-trip (#105).** The one provider without a round-trip test; structural-only leaves the data contract unverified.

What I would **not** add: more `ScanReporter`/atomic-wrapper tests (#11), config getter tautologies beyond what exists, or any test that just re-asserts stdlib/toml/sqlite behavior.

Overall this is a large but well-engineered PR — the durability/atomic-write work, the O(N²)→O(N) fix, the project-identity walk-up, and the reconstruct round-trip tests are standout. The actionable items are concentrated in DRY consolidation across the four newest providers, reindex test coverage, the `search`→resume flag gap, and observability.

---

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./pkg/spi/reconstruct.go`

```text
1	package spi
2	
3	import (
4		"errors"
5		"strings"
6	
7		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
8	)
9	
10	// ErrReconstructionUnsupported is returned by a provider that carries the
11	// ReconstructSession responsibility but does not yet have a native serializer.
12	// Reconstruction is on the Provider interface so every provider implements the
13	// method, but only some providers (initially Claude Code and Codex CLI) produce
14	// real native output.
15	var ErrReconstructionUnsupported = errors.New("session reconstruction not supported by this provider")
16	
17	// ReconstructOptions controls how a session is reconstructed into native form.
18	type ReconstructOptions struct {
19		// WorkspaceRoot is the target working directory the reconstructed session
20		// should belong to. Native paths/IDs are derived from it. When empty, the
21		// provider falls back to the SessionData's own WorkspaceRoot.
22		WorkspaceRoot string
23	
24		// MigrationNote, when set, is prepended as a leading agent turn so the agent
25		// understands the prior turns were imported from another session. Optional.
26		MigrationNote string
27	}
28	
29	// ReconstructedSession is the native output of reconstruction. It is data only;
30	// the caller decides where (and whether) to write it.
31	type ReconstructedSession struct {
32		SessionID string // freshly minted, native-format session ID
33		Filename  string // suggested native filename (base name, not a full path)
34		Content   []byte // native session file bytes
35	}
36	
37	// Turn is a single role-tagged text turn in the flattened transcript. The
38	// reconstruction model collapses everything an agent said, thought, or did into
39	// plain user/agent text turns (see docs/SESSION-PORTABILITY.md), which each
40	// provider then serializes into its native format.
41	type Turn struct {
42		Role string // schema.RoleUser or schema.RoleAgent
43		Text string
44	}
45	
46	// FlattenSessionData reduces a SessionData into an ordered list of plain
47	// user/agent text turns. This is the single place the cross-provider flattening
48	// policy lives, so every provider's serializer consumes the same transcript.
49	//
50	// Rules (per the locked design):
51	//   - user/agent text content -> a turn of that role
52	//   - thinking content -> folded into the agent turn's text (not dropped)
53	//   - tool calls -> folded into the agent turn's text via the pre-rendered
54	//     Summary / FormattedMarkdown captured on the forward pass
55	//   - model, usage, and path hints -> dropped
56	//
57	// migrationNote, when non-empty, becomes a leading agent turn.
58	func FlattenSessionData(data *schema.SessionData, migrationNote string) []Turn {
59		var turns []Turn
60	
61		if strings.TrimSpace(migrationNote) != "" {
62			turns = append(turns, Turn{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Text: strings.TrimSpace(migrationNote)})
63		}
64	
65		if data == nil {
66			return turns
67		}
68	
69		for i := range data.Exchanges {
70			for j := range data.Exchanges[i].Messages {
71				msg := &data.Exchanges[i].Messages[j]
72	
73				// Only user and agent roles produce turns; anything else is skipped.
74				if msg.Role != schema.RoleUser && msg.Role != schema.RoleAgent {
75					continue
76				}
77	
78				text := messageTurnText(msg)
79				if strings.TrimSpace(text) == "" {
80					continue
81				}
82	
83				// Drop synthetic, non-conversational turns (slash-command invocations,
84				// local-command output, etc.) so they are not replayed into the resumed
85				// session. This is intentionally reconstruction-only — archival markdown
86				// stays faithful to the source session.
87				if isSyntheticTurnText(text) {
88					continue
89				}
90	
91				turns = append(turns, Turn{Role: msg.Role, Text: text})
92			}
93		}
94	
95		return turns
96	}
97	
98	// SyntheticCommandTags are the wrapper tags coding agents inject for
99	// non-conversational slash-command records: the command invocation, its piped
100	// local stdout/stderr, and the local-command caveat. They are conversation
101	// scaffolding, never a real user prompt. This is the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for
102	// the tag set, shared by reconstruction's turn filter here (matched anywhere in a
103	// flattened turn) and Claude Code's title-selection filter
104	// (claudecode.isSyntheticMessage, matched as a leading tag), so the two cannot
105	// drift apart.
106	var SyntheticCommandTags = []string{
107		"<command-name>",
108		"<command-message>",
109		"<command-args>",
110		"<local-command-stdout>",
111		"<local-command-stderr>",
112		"<local-command-caveat>",
113	}
114	
115	// extraSyntheticTurnMarkers are synthetic markers beyond the shared command tags
116	// that must also never be replayed into a resumed session: Claude Code's
117	// <TEXTBLOCK> title-generation wrapper and its interrupt marker (which also has a
118	// "…for tool use" variant, caught by the prefix).
119	var extraSyntheticTurnMarkers = []string{
120		"<TEXTBLOCK>",
121		"[Request interrupted by user",
122	}
123	
124	// isSyntheticTurnText reports whether a flattened turn is a synthetic,
125	// non-conversational artifact that should not be replayed into a resumed session.
126	// It matches by Contains because a flattened turn concatenates content parts, so a
127	// marker can appear anywhere in the text. Markdown generation does NOT use this
128	// filter and stays faithful to source.
129	func isSyntheticTurnText(text string) bool {
130		for _, marker := range SyntheticCommandTags {
131			if strings.Contains(text, marker) {
132				return true
133			}
134		}
135		for _, marker := range extraSyntheticTurnMarkers {
136			if strings.Contains(text, marker) {
137				return true
138			}
139		}
140		return false
141	}
142	
143	// messageTurnText flattens a single message into its turn text: all text/thinking
144	// content parts joined, followed by the tool's pre-rendered markdown when present.
145	func messageTurnText(msg *schema.Message) string {
146		var parts []string
147	
148		for _, part := range msg.Content {
149			// Both "text" and "thinking" parts carry their content in Text; thinking
150			// is intentionally treated as ordinary agent text.
151			if strings.TrimSpace(part.Text) != "" {
152				parts = append(parts, part.Text)
153			}
154		}
155	
156		text := strings.Join(parts, "\n\n")
157	
158		if msg.Tool != nil {
159			toolText := toolTurnText(msg.Tool)
160			if toolText != "" {
161				if text != "" {
162					text += "\n\n" + toolText
163				} else {
164					text = toolText
165				}
166			}
167		}
168	
169		return text
170	}
171	
172	// toolTurnText renders a tool call as agent text, reusing the markdown the
173	// forward pass already produced (Summary + FormattedMarkdown). Falls back to a
174	// minimal description when neither is available.
175	func toolTurnText(tool *schema.ToolInfo) string {
176		var b strings.Builder
177	
178		if tool.Summary != nil && strings.TrimSpace(*tool.Summary) != "" {
179			b.WriteString(strings.TrimSpace(*tool.Summary))
180		}
181	
182		if tool.FormattedMarkdown != nil && strings.TrimSpace(*tool.FormattedMarkdown) != "" {
183			if b.Len() > 0 {
184				b.WriteString("\n\n")
185			}
186			b.WriteString(strings.TrimRight(*tool.FormattedMarkdown, "\n"))
187		}
188	
189		if b.Len() == 0 {
190			// Nothing pre-rendered; emit a minimal, non-empty description so the tool
191			// activity is at least acknowledged in the transcript.
192			b.WriteString("Tool use: " + tool.Name)
193		}
194	
195		return b.String()
196	}
197
```
</details></tool-use>

 `./pkg/providers/deepseektui/reconstruct.go`

```text
1	package deepseektui
2	
3	import (
4		"bytes"
5		"encoding/json"
6		"fmt"
7		"path/filepath"
8		"strings"
9		"time"
10	
11		"github.com/google/uuid"
12	
13		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
14		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
15	)
16	
17	// deepseekTimestamp formats a time the way DeepSeek records do (RFC3339 micros, Z).
18	func deepseekTimestamp(t time.Time) string {
19		return t.Format("2006-01-02T15:04:05.000000Z")
20	}
21	
22	// ReconstructSession rebuilds a DeepSeek TUI native session from the neutral
23	// SessionData so `deepseek --resume <id>` can continue the conversation.
24	//
25	// DeepSeek stores a session as a single JSON document
26	// `{schema_version, system_prompt, metadata, messages}` where each message is
27	// `{role, content:[{type:"text",text}]}`. Tool calls and thinking are already
28	// flattened into agent text by FlattenSessionData, so reconstruction emits only
29	// `user`/`assistant` text messages. The session is associated with a project via
30	// metadata.workspace. See docs/SESSION-PORTABILITY.md.
31	func (p *Provider) ReconstructSession(data *schema.SessionData, opts spi.ReconstructOptions) (*spi.ReconstructedSession, error) {
32		if data == nil {
33			return nil, fmt.Errorf("cannot reconstruct nil session data")
34		}
35	
36		cwd := opts.WorkspaceRoot
37		if cwd == "" {
38			cwd = data.WorkspaceRoot
39		}
40	
41		turns := spi.FlattenSessionData(data, opts.MigrationNote)
42		if len(turns) == 0 {
43			return nil, fmt.Errorf("session has no content to reconstruct")
44		}
45	
46		newID := uuid.NewString()
47		now := deepseekTimestamp(time.Now().UTC())
48	
49		title := strings.TrimSpace(data.Slug)
50		if title == "" {
51			title = "Resumed session"
52		}
53	
54		messages := make([]map[string]interface{}, 0, len(turns))
55		for _, turn := range turns {
56			role := "assistant"
57			if turn.Role == schema.RoleUser {
58				role = "user"
59			}
60			messages = append(messages, map[string]interface{}{
61				"role":    role,
62				"content": []map[string]interface{}{{"type": "text", "text": turn.Text}},
63			})
64		}
65	
66		session := map[string]interface{}{
67			"schema_version": 1,
68			"system_prompt":  "",
69			"metadata": map[string]interface{}{
70				"id":            newID,
71				"title":         title,
72				"created_at":    now,
73				"updated_at":    now,
74				"message_count": len(messages),
75				"total_tokens":  0,
76				"model":         "",
77				"workspace":     cwd,
78				"mode":          "agent",
79			},
80			"messages": messages,
81		}
82	
83		var buf bytes.Buffer
84		enc := json.NewEncoder(&buf)
85		enc.SetEscapeHTML(false)
86		enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
87		if err := enc.Encode(session); err != nil {
88			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to encode deepseek session: %w", err)
89		}
90	
91		return &spi.ReconstructedSession{
92			SessionID: newID,
93			Filename:  newID + ".json",
94			Content:   buf.Bytes(),
95		}, nil
96	}
97	
98	// NativeSessionPath returns where a reconstructed session belongs in DeepSeek's
99	// store: ~/.deepseek/sessions/<filename>. DeepSeek sessions are not project-scoped
100	// by directory (the project is recorded in metadata.workspace), so projectPath is
101	// unused. The directory is not required to exist (the caller creates it).
102	func (p *Provider) NativeSessionPath(projectPath string, filename string) (string, error) {
103		dir, err := resolveSessionsDir()
104		if err != nil {
105			return "", err
106		}
107		return filepath.Join(dir, filename), nil
108	}
109
```
</details></tool-use>

<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./pkg/providers/droidcli/reconstruct.go`

```text
1	package droidcli
2	
3	import (
4		"bytes"
5		"encoding/json"
6		"fmt"
7		"os"
8		"path/filepath"
9		"strings"
10		"time"
11	
12		"github.com/google/uuid"
13	
14		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
15		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
16	)
17	
18	// droidTimestamp formats a time the way Factory Droid records do (RFC3339 millis, Z).
19	func droidTimestamp(t time.Time) string {
20		return t.Format("2006-01-02T15:04:05.000Z")
21	}
22	
23	// ReconstructSession rebuilds a Factory Droid native JSONL session from the neutral
24	// SessionData so `droid --resume <id>` can continue the conversation.
25	//
26	// Droid stores a session as a `session_start` header followed by `parentId`-chained
27	// `message` records, each `{message:{role, content:[{type:"text",text}]}}`. Tool
28	// calls and thinking are already flattened into agent text by FlattenSessionData,
29	// so reconstruction emits only `user`/`assistant` text messages. The store is
30	// project-scoped (see NativeSessionPath). See docs/SESSION-PORTABILITY.md.
31	func (p *Provider) ReconstructSession(data *schema.SessionData, opts spi.ReconstructOptions) (*spi.ReconstructedSession, error) {
32		if data == nil {
33			return nil, fmt.Errorf("cannot reconstruct nil session data")
34		}
35	
36		cwd := opts.WorkspaceRoot
37		if cwd == "" {
38			cwd = data.WorkspaceRoot
39		}
40	
41		turns := spi.FlattenSessionData(data, opts.MigrationNote)
42		if len(turns) == 0 {
43			return nil, fmt.Errorf("session has no content to reconstruct")
44		}
45	
46		newID := uuid.NewString()
47		base := time.Now().UTC()
48	
49		title := strings.TrimSpace(data.Slug)
50		if title == "" {
51			title = "Resumed session"
52		}
53	
54		var buf bytes.Buffer
55		enc := json.NewEncoder(&buf)
56		enc.SetEscapeHTML(false)
57	
58		// session_start header (also carries provenance).
59		if err := enc.Encode(map[string]interface{}{
60			"type":                      "session_start",
61			"id":                        newID,
62			"title":                     title,
63			"sessionTitle":              title,
64			"owner":                     os.Getenv("USER"),
65			"version":                   2,
66			"cwd":                       cwd,
67			"isSessionTitleManuallySet": false,
68			"sessionTitleAutoStage":     "first_message",
69			"specstorySourceSessionId":  data.SessionID,
70		}); err != nil {
71			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to encode session_start: %w", err)
72		}
73	
74		var prevID string
75		for i, turn := range turns {
76			msgID := uuid.NewString()
77			role := "assistant"
78			if turn.Role == schema.RoleUser {
79				role = "user"
80			}
81	
82			rec := map[string]interface{}{
83				"type":      "message",
84				"id":        msgID,
85				"timestamp": droidTimestamp(base.Add(time.Duration(i) * time.Second)),
86				"message": map[string]interface{}{
87					"role":    role,
88					"content": []map[string]interface{}{{"type": "text", "text": turn.Text}},
89				},
90			}
91			// Linear chain: the first message roots the thread (no parentId).
92			if prevID != "" {
93				rec["parentId"] = prevID
94			}
95	
96			if err := enc.Encode(rec); err != nil {
97				return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to encode message: %w", err)
98			}
99			prevID = msgID
100		}
101	
102		return &spi.ReconstructedSession{
103			SessionID: newID,
104			Filename:  newID + ".jsonl",
105			Content:   buf.Bytes(),
106		}, nil
107	}
108	
109	// NativeSessionPath returns where a reconstructed session belongs in Droid's store:
110	// ~/.factory/sessions/<cwd-encoded>/<filename>. The directory is not required to
111	// exist (the caller creates it).
112	func (p *Provider) NativeSessionPath(projectPath string, filename string) (string, error) {
113		if projectPath == "" {
114			var err error
115			projectPath, err = os.Getwd()
116			if err != nil {
117				return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get current working directory: %w", err)
118			}
119		}
120	
121		dir, err := resolveProjectSessionDir(projectPath)
122		if err != nil {
123			return "", err
124		}
125		if dir == "" {
126			return "", fmt.Errorf("droidcli: cannot resolve session directory for project path")
127		}
128		return filepath.Join(dir, filename), nil
129	}
130
```
</details></tool-use>

 `./pkg/providers/geminicli/reconstruct.go`

```text
1	package geminicli
2	
3	import (
4		"bytes"
5		"encoding/json"
6		"fmt"
7		"os"
8		"path/filepath"
9		"time"
10	
11		"github.com/google/uuid"
12	
13		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
14		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
15	)
16	
17	// geminiTimestamp formats a time the way Gemini chat records do (RFC3339 millis, Z).
18	func geminiTimestamp(t time.Time) string {
19		return t.Format("2006-01-02T15:04:05.000Z")
20	}
21	
22	// ReconstructSession rebuilds a Gemini CLI native chat session from the neutral
23	// SessionData so `gemini --resume <id>` can continue the conversation.
24	//
25	// Gemini stores a session as a single JSON document: a top-level object with
26	// `sessionId`/`projectHash`/`startTime`/`lastUpdated`/`kind` and a `messages`
27	// array. Each message is `{id, timestamp, type, content}` where a user message's
28	// content is `[{text}]` and a gemini message's content is a plain string. Tool
29	// calls and thinking are already flattened into agent text by FlattenSessionData,
30	// so reconstruction emits only `user`/`gemini` text messages. See
31	// docs/SESSION-PORTABILITY.md.
32	func (p *Provider) ReconstructSession(data *schema.SessionData, opts spi.ReconstructOptions) (*spi.ReconstructedSession, error) {
33		if data == nil {
34			return nil, fmt.Errorf("cannot reconstruct nil session data")
35		}
36	
37		cwd := opts.WorkspaceRoot
38		if cwd == "" {
39			cwd = data.WorkspaceRoot
40		}
41	
42		turns := spi.FlattenSessionData(data, opts.MigrationNote)
43		if len(turns) == 0 {
44			return nil, fmt.Errorf("session has no content to reconstruct")
45		}
46	
47		newID := uuid.NewString()
48		base := time.Now().UTC()
49	
50		// Best-effort project hash; Gemini stores it but resume keys off the project dir.
51		projectHash, _ := HashProjectPath(cwd)
52	
53		messages := make([]map[string]interface{}, 0, len(turns))
54		for i, turn := range turns {
55			msg := map[string]interface{}{
56				"id":        uuid.NewString(),
57				"timestamp": geminiTimestamp(base.Add(time.Duration(i) * time.Second)),
58			}
59			if turn.Role == schema.RoleUser {
60				msg["type"] = "user"
61				msg["content"] = []map[string]interface{}{{"text": turn.Text}}
62			} else {
63				msg["type"] = "gemini"
64				msg["content"] = turn.Text
65			}
66			messages = append(messages, msg)
67		}
68	
69		session := map[string]interface{}{
70			"sessionId":   newID,
71			"projectHash": projectHash,
72			"startTime":   geminiTimestamp(base),
73			"lastUpdated": geminiTimestamp(base.Add(time.Duration(len(turns)) * time.Second)),
74			"kind":        "main",
75			"messages":    messages,
76		}
77	
78		var buf bytes.Buffer
79		enc := json.NewEncoder(&buf)
80		enc.SetEscapeHTML(false)
81		enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
82		if err := enc.Encode(session); err != nil {
83			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to encode gemini session: %w", err)
84		}
85	
86		// Filename: session-<YYYY-MM-DDTHH-MM>-<first 8 of id>.json (Gemini convention).
87		filename := fmt.Sprintf("session-%s-%s.json", base.Format("2006-01-02T15-04"), newID[:8])
88	
89		return &spi.ReconstructedSession{
90			SessionID: newID,
91			Filename:  filename,
92			Content:   buf.Bytes(),
93		}, nil
94	}
95	
96	// NativeSessionPath returns where a reconstructed chat belongs in Gemini's store
97	// and, unlike the Claude/Codex resolvers, prepares the project directory: Gemini
98	// associates a tmp dir with a project via a `.project_root` marker, so for a
99	// project Gemini has never seen we create `~/.gemini/tmp/<name>/` with that marker
100	// and a `chats/` subdir. Returns `<projectDir>/chats/<filename>`.
101	func (p *Provider) NativeSessionPath(projectPath string, filename string) (string, error) {
102		if projectPath == "" {
103			var err error
104			projectPath, err = osGetwd()
105			if err != nil {
106				return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get current working directory: %w", err)
107			}
108		}
109	
110		// Reuse the project dir Gemini already associates with this project, if any.
111		dir, err := ResolveGeminiProjectDir(projectPath)
112		if err != nil {
113			dir, err = prepareGeminiProjectDir(projectPath)
114			if err != nil {
115				return "", err
116			}
117		}
118	
119		return filepath.Join(dir, "chats", filename), nil
120	}
121	
122	// prepareGeminiProjectDir creates a Gemini project directory for a project that
123	// Gemini has not seen yet: ~/.gemini/tmp/<basename>/ (falling back to the hash dir
124	// if that basename is taken by a different project), with a `.project_root` marker
125	// and a `chats/` subdirectory.
126	func prepareGeminiProjectDir(projectPath string) (string, error) {
127		tmpDir, err := GetGeminiTmpDir()
128		if err != nil {
129			return "", err
130		}
131		if err := os.MkdirAll(tmpDir, 0o755); err != nil {
132			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to create gemini tmp dir: %w", err)
133		}
134	
135		canonical := canonicalizeProjectPath(projectPath)
136		dir := filepath.Join(tmpDir, filepath.Base(projectPath))
137	
138		// If the basename dir exists but belongs to a different project, key off the hash instead.
139		if info, statErr := osStat(dir); statErr == nil && info.IsDir() && !matchesProjectRoot(dir, canonical) {
140			if dir, err = GetGeminiProjectDir(projectPath); err != nil {
141				return "", err
142			}
143		}
144	
145		if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(dir, "chats"), 0o755); err != nil {
146			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to create gemini chats dir: %w", err)
147		}
148	
149		// Write the project-root marker so Gemini maps this dir to the project on resume.
150		rootFile := filepath.Join(dir, ".project_root")
151		if _, statErr := osStat(rootFile); os.IsNotExist(statErr) {
152			if err := os.WriteFile(rootFile, []byte(canonical), 0o644); err != nil {
153				return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to write gemini .project_root: %w", err)
154			}
155		}
156	
157		return dir, nil
158	}
159
```

```text
=== deepseektui/provider.go ListAllAgentChatSessions ===
func (p *Provider) ListAllAgentChatSessions() ([]spi.GlobalSessionRef, error) {
	files, err := listSessionFiles()
	if err != nil {
		if os.IsNotExist(err) {
			return []spi.GlobalSessionRef{}, nil
		}
		return nil, err
	}

	var refs []spi.GlobalSessionRef
	for _, file := range files {
		metadata, err := extractSessionMetadata(file.Path)
		if err != nil {
			slog.Debug("reindex: failed to extract deepseek session metadata", "path", file.Path, "error", err)
			continue
		}
		if metadata == nil {
			continue // empty session
		}
		refs = append(refs, spi.GlobalSessionRef{
			SessionID:  metadata.SessionID,
			CreatedAt:  metadata.CreatedAt,
			Slug:       metadata.Slug,
			Name:       metadata.Name,
			NativePath: file.Path,
			OriginCwd:  extractWorkspaceFast(file.Path),
		})
	}
	return refs, nil
}
=== droidcli/provider.go ListAllAgentChatSessions ===
func (p *Provider) ListAllAgentChatSessions() ([]spi.GlobalSessionRef, error) {
	files, err := listSessionFiles()
	if err != nil {
		if os.IsNotExist(err) {
			return []spi.GlobalSessionRef{}, nil
		}
		return nil, err
	}

	var refs []spi.GlobalSessionRef
	for _, file := range files {
		session, err := parseFactorySession(file.Path)
		if err != nil {
			slog.Debug("reindex: failed to parse droid session", "path", file.Path, "error", err)
			continue
		}
		if session == nil || len(session.Blocks) == 0 {
			continue // empty session
		}
		refs = append(refs, spi.GlobalSessionRef{
			SessionID:  session.ID,
			CreatedAt:  session.CreatedAt,
			Slug:       session.Slug,
			Name:       generateDroidSessionName(session),
			NativePath: file.Path,
			OriginCwd:  strings.TrimSpace(session.WorkspaceRoot),
		})
	}
	return refs, nil
}
=== geminicli/provider.go ListAllAgentChatSessions ===
func (p *Provider) ListAllAgentChatSessions() ([]spi.GlobalSessionRef, error) {
	tmpDir, err := GetGeminiTmpDir()
	if err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}
	entries, err := os.ReadDir(tmpDir)
	if err != nil {
		if os.IsNotExist(err) {
			return []spi.GlobalSessionRef{}, nil
		}
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to read Gemini tmp directory: %w", err)
	}

	var refs []spi.GlobalSessionRef
	for _, entry := range entries {
		if !entry.IsDir() {
			continue
		}
		dir := filepath.Join(tmpDir, entry.Name())

		// The .project_root marker holds the project path — the originating cwd shared
		// by every session in this tmp directory.
		var cwd string
		if content, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(dir, ".project_root")); err == nil {
			cwd = strings.TrimSpace(string(content))
		}

		sessions, err := FindSessions(dir)
		if err != nil {
			slog.Debug("reindex: failed to find gemini sessions", "dir", dir, "error", err)
			continue
		}
		for _, session := range sessions {
			metadata := extractGeminiSessionMetadata(session)
			if metadata == nil {
				continue // empty session
			}
			refs = append(refs, spi.GlobalSessionRef{
				SessionID:  metadata.SessionID,
				CreatedAt:  metadata.CreatedAt,
				Slug:       metadata.Slug,
				Name:       metadata.Name,
				NativePath: session.FilePath,
				OriginCwd:  cwd,
			})
		}
	}
	return refs, nil
}
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-06-29 15:48:26Z)**_

I now have the full picture. Here's what the duplication actually looks like and the decisions I need from you before editing.

**Piece 1 — reconstruct validation (all 6 providers, identical):**
```go
if data == nil { return nil, fmt.Errorf("cannot reconstruct nil session data") }
turns := spi.FlattenSessionData(data, opts.MigrationNote)
if len(turns) == 0 { return nil, fmt.Errorf("session has no content to reconstruct") }
```

**Piece 2 — workspace-root fallback (5 of 6; cursor doesn't use cwd, identical):**
```go
cwd := opts.WorkspaceRoot
if cwd == "" { cwd = data.WorkspaceRoot }
```

**Piece 3 — `ListAllAgentChatSessions` enumeration (in provider.go, not reconstruct.go):** DeepSeek and Droid are near-identical (list files → parse → skip-on-error → build ref), but **Gemini diverges substantially** (two-level dir walk + `.project_root` marker + nested loop), and Claude/Codex use a different *parallel worker-pool* variant. So this one is a partial, lower-confidence consolidation.

Pieces 1 & 2 are an unambiguous win — exactly the "reconstruct duplication" MIP #1 names, all in `pkg/spi/reconstruct.go` (existing file, no new files). Piece 3 is riskier and more divergent. Two decisions:

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-06-29 15:50:13Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="generic" data-tool-name="AskUserQuestion"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **AskUserQuestion**</summary>
**How much of the duplication should this change cover?**

Options:
- **Reconstruct helpers only** - Hoist pieces 1 & 2 into pkg/spi and update all 6 providers' ReconstructSession. Clean, low-risk, fully covered by the existing round-trip tests. Defer the enumeration template (piece 3) as a separate task.
- **Helpers + DeepSeek/Droid enumeration** - Also extract a shared enumeration helper for the two providers that match cleanly (DeepSeek, Droid), leaving Gemini and the Claude/Codex parallel variant alone. More surface, callback-based abstraction.

**What shape for the two reconstruct helpers?**

Options:
- **Two separate helpers** - PrepareTurns does the nil+flatten+empty check; ResolveWorkspaceRoot does the cwd fallback. Composable — cursor calls only PrepareTurns since it has no cwd. Matches single-responsibility.
- **One combined helper** - PrepareReconstruct returns (turns, cwd, error) in one call. Fewest lines per provider, but cursor ignores the returned cwd.
