<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./pkg/providers/claudecode/reconstruct.go`

```text
1	package claudecode
2	
3	import (
4		"bytes"
5		"encoding/json"
6		"fmt"
7		"path/filepath"
8		"time"
9	
10		"github.com/google/uuid"
11	
12		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
13		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
14	)
15	
16	const (
17		// reconstructedClaudeModel is a placeholder model label for reconstructed
18		// assistant records. It is only a historical display value — on resume Claude
19		// Code uses its own configured model for the next turn.
20		reconstructedClaudeModel = "claude-opus-4-8"
21	
22		// reconstructedClaudeVersion mirrors the `version` field Claude Code writes on
23		// its records. A concrete value matching observed sessions maximizes the
24		// chance the loader accepts a synthesized file.
25		reconstructedClaudeVersion = "2.1.178"
26	)
27	
28	// ReconstructSession rebuilds a Claude Code native JSONL session from the neutral
29	// SessionData so `claude --resume <id>` can continue the conversation.
30	//
31	// The forward parser collapses Claude's parentUuid DAG and drops the API
32	// message.id and thinking signatures, so reconstruction synthesizes a fresh,
33	// linear parentUuid chain of plain user/assistant text records (tool calls and
34	// thinking are already flattened into agent text by FlattenSessionData). This is
35	// the structure validated by the Stage 1 spike. See docs/SESSION-PORTABILITY.md.
36	func (p *Provider) ReconstructSession(data *schema.SessionData, opts spi.ReconstructOptions) (*spi.ReconstructedSession, error) {
37		if data == nil {
38			return nil, fmt.Errorf("cannot reconstruct nil session data")
39		}
40	
41		// Prefer the explicit target workspace; fall back to the session's own root.
42		cwd := opts.WorkspaceRoot
43		if cwd == "" {
44			cwd = data.WorkspaceRoot
45		}
46	
47		turns := spi.FlattenSessionData(data, opts.MigrationNote)
48		if len(turns) == 0 {
49			return nil, fmt.Errorf("session has no content to reconstruct")
50		}
51	
52		newID := uuid.NewString()
53		base := time.Now().UTC()
54	
55		var buf bytes.Buffer
56		enc := json.NewEncoder(&buf)
57		// Preserve <, >, & in conversation/markdown text rather than \u-escaping them.
58		enc.SetEscapeHTML(false)
59	
60		var prevUUID string
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	package codexcli
2	
3	import (
4		"bytes"
5		"encoding/json"
6		"fmt"
7		"os"
8		"path/filepath"
9		"time"

11		"github.com/google/uuid"

13		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
14		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
15	)
16	
17	// reconstructedCodexCliVersion mirrors the `cli_version` Codex writes into
18	// session_meta. A concrete value matching observed sessions maximizes the chance
19	// the loader accepts a synthesized file.
20	const reconstructedCodexCliVersion = "0.139.0"
21	
22	// codexTimestamp formats a time the way Codex records do (RFC3339 millis, Z).
23	func codexTimestamp(t time.Time) string {
24		return t.Format("2006-01-02T15:04:05.000Z")
25	}
26	
27	// ReconstructSession rebuilds a Codex CLI native rollout from the neutral
28	// SessionData so `codex resume <id>` can continue the conversation.
29	//
30	// Codex resume replays the response_item model transcript, while the forward
31	// parser reads the event_msg UI stream — so reconstruction emits BOTH for each
32	// turn: a response_item message (what the agent replays) and a matching event_msg
33	// (what the UI and our own re-parse read). Tool calls and thinking are already
34	// flattened into agent text by FlattenSessionData.
35	//
36	// We do NOT synthesize an environment_context block: Codex injects its own fresh
37	// environment context on every resume, consistent with the cross-provider policy
38	// that the target supplies its own preamble. See docs/SESSION-PORTABILITY.md.
39	func (p *Provider) ReconstructSession(data *schema.SessionData, opts spi.ReconstructOptions) (*spi.ReconstructedSession, error) {
40		if data == nil {
41			return nil, fmt.Errorf("cannot reconstruct nil session data")
42		}
43	
44		cwd := opts.WorkspaceRoot
45		if cwd == "" {
46			cwd = data.WorkspaceRoot
47		}
48	
49		turns := spi.FlattenSessionData(data, opts.MigrationNote)
50		if len(turns) == 0 {
51			return nil, fmt.Errorf("session has no content to reconstruct")
52		}
53	
54		// Codex session IDs are UUIDv7. Fall back to v4 if v7 generation fails.
55		newID := uuid.NewString()
56		if v7, err := uuid.NewV7(); err == nil {
57			newID = v7.String()
58		}
59	
60		base := time.Now().UTC()
```

```text
100		var lastAssistantText string
101		asstSeq := 0
102		for _, turn := range turns {
103			var raw []byte
104			var err error
105			if turn.Role == schema.RoleUser {
106				raw, err = marshalCompact(cursorUserMsg{Role: "user", Content: []cursorTextPart{{Type: "text", Text: turn.Text}}})
107			} else {
108				asstSeq++
109				raw, err = marshalCompact(cursorAsstMsg{ID: fmt.Sprintf("%d", asstSeq), Role: "assistant", Content: []cursorTextPart{{Type: "text", Text: turn.Text}}})
110				lastAssistantText = turn.Text
111			}
112			if err != nil {
113				return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to encode message: %w", err)
114			}
115			msgIDs = append(msgIDs, put(raw))
116		}
117	
118		// 2. Field-2 metadata subtree: title leaf, empty placeholder, preview node.
119		titleID := put(append(pbField(1, []byte(name)), pbField(2, []byte(uuid.NewString()))...))
120		emptyID := put(pbField(3, nil))                                     // {f3:""}
121		previewID := put(pbField(1, pbField(1, []byte(lastAssistantText)))) // {f1:{f1:text}}
122		metaNodeID := put(concat(pbRef(1, titleID), pbRef(2, emptyID), pbRef(2, previewID)))
123	
124		// 3. Root node: field 1 = ordered message refs, field 2 = metadata node.
125		var root []byte
126		for _, mid := range msgIDs {
127			root = append(root, pbRef(1, mid)...)
128		}
129		root = append(root, pbRef(2, metaNodeID)...)
130		rootID := put(root)
131	
132		// 4. meta['0'] head record (hex-encoded JSON).
133		metaJSON, err := marshalCompact(map[string]interface{}{
134			"agentId":          newID,
135			"latestRootBlobId": rootID,
136			"name":             name,
137			"mode":             "default",
138			"createdAt":        time.Now().UTC().UnixMilli(),
139		})
140		if err != nil {
141			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to encode meta: %w", err)
142		}
143	
144		// 5. Materialize the SQLite store.db and return its bytes. SQLite needs a file
145		// handle, so we build in a temp file (created and removed within this call).
146		content, err := buildStoreDB(blobs, hex.EncodeToString(metaJSON))
147		if err != nil {
148			return nil, err
149		}
150	
151		return &spi.ReconstructedSession{
152			SessionID: newID,
153			Filename:  filepath.Join(newID, "store.db"),
154			Content:   content,
155		}, nil
156	}
```

```text
48		// Preserve <, >, & in conversation/markdown text rather than \u-escaping them.
49		enc.SetEscapeHTML(false)
50	
51		var prevUUID string
52		for i, turn := range turns {
53			recUUID := uuid.NewString()
54			ts := base.Add(time.Duration(i) * time.Second).Format("2006-01-02T15:04:05.000Z")
55	
56			rec := map[string]interface{}{
57				"isSidechain": false,
58				"userType":    "external",
59				"cwd":         cwd,
60				"sessionId":   newID,
61				"version":     reconstructedClaudeVersion,
62				"gitBranch":   "",
63				"uuid":        recUUID,
64				"timestamp":   ts,
65			}
66	
67			// Linear chain: the first record roots the DAG (parentUuid null) and
68			// carries provenance back to the source session.
69			if prevUUID == "" {
70				rec["parentUuid"] = nil
71				rec["specstorySourceSessionId"] = data.SessionID
72			} else {
73				rec["parentUuid"] = prevUUID
74			}
75	
76			if turn.Role == schema.RoleUser {
77				rec["type"] = "user"
78				rec["message"] = map[string]interface{}{
79					"role":    "user",
80					"content": turn.Text,
81				}
82			} else {
83				rec["type"] = "assistant"
84				rec["message"] = map[string]interface{}{
85					"id":          "msg_" + recUUID,
86					"type":        "message",
87					"role":        "assistant",
88					"model":       reconstructedClaudeModel,
89					"content":     []map[string]interface{}{{"type": "text", "text": turn.Text}},
90					"stop_reason": "end_turn",
91					"usage":       map[string]interface{}{"input_tokens": 1, "output_tokens": 1},
92				}
93			}
94	
95			if err := enc.Encode(rec); err != nil {
96				return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to encode reconstructed record: %w", err)
97			}
98	
99			prevUUID = recUUID
100		}
101	
102		return &spi.ReconstructedSession{
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-06-29 15:53:58Z)**_

Done. MIP #1 is addressed. Here's a summary.

## What changed

**New shared helpers in `pkg/spi/reconstruct.go`** (existing file — no new files):

- `PrepareTurns(data, opts) ([]Turn, error)` — centralizes the two guards every `ReconstructSession` repeated: reject nil `SessionData`, flatten, reject content-free sessions. Error messages now live in exactly one place and can't drift.
- `ResolveWorkspaceRoot(opts, data) string` — centralizes the explicit-target → session-root → empty precedence, with a defensive nil-data guard.

**All six providers updated** to call them, replacing ~12 (or ~6 for cursor) lines of inline boilerplate each:

| Provider | Before | After |
|---|---|---|
| claude, codex, deepseek, droid, gemini | nil check + cwd fallback + flatten + empty check | `PrepareTurns` + `ResolveWorkspaceRoot` |
| cursor (no workspace) | nil check + flatten + empty check | `PrepareTurns` only |

**Tests added** to `pkg/spi/reconstruct_test.go` — two table-driven tests locking the now-centralized logic:
- `TestPrepareTurns`: nil data, no content, migration-note-as-content, real content.
- `TestResolveWorkspaceRoot`: target-wins, session-root fallback, empty, nil-safe.

## Verification

- `go build ./...` + binary build: clean
- `go test ./...`: all pass, no failures/panics
- `golangci-lint run` (whole project): **0 issues**

## Notes

- I went with the **two separate helpers** shape (my recommendation) since cursor genuinely needs only `PrepareTurns`. Say the word if you'd rather the combined `PrepareReconstruct` returning `(turns, cwd, err)`.
- I deliberately preserved the existing **nil-data-rejected-outright** behavior: `PrepareTurns` checks `data == nil` before flattening, so a nil session with only a migration note still errors exactly as before (rather than silently producing a one-turn session).
- **Piece 3** (the `ListAllAgentChatSessions` enumeration template) was left untouched per your call — it's the more divergent, riskier consolidation and belongs in its own change if you want it later.
- The `interface{} → any` and `strptr → new()` diagnostics that surfaced are pre-existing style hints in those files, not introduced by this change.

---

```text
74	type reindexItem struct {
75		agent string
76		prov  spi.Provider
77		ref   spi.GlobalSessionRef
78		size  int64
79		mtime int64
80		isNew bool // no existing index row → writer can skip the standalone-FTS delete
81	}
82	
83	func runReindex(force bool) error {
84		start := time.Now()
85	
86		// Graceful Ctrl+C: stop feeding work, flush what's written, exit cleanly.
87		ctx, cancel := signal.NotifyContext(context.Background(), os.Interrupt, syscall.SIGTERM)
88		defer cancel()
89	
90		dbPath, err := sessionindex.DefaultPath()
91		if err != nil {
92			return err
93		}
94		store, err := sessionindex.Open(dbPath)
95		if err != nil {
96			return fmt.Errorf("opening restore index: %w", err)
97		}
98		defer func() { _ = store.Close() }()
99	
100		registry := factory.GetRegistry()
101	
102		// ---- Phase 1: enumerate every provider concurrently, then dedup ----
103		ids, provs, perProvider := enumerateAll(registry, true)
104	
105		// Existing fingerprints, so unchanged sessions can be skipped (the incremental path).
106		fingerprints := map[string]sessionindex.Fingerprint{}
107		if !force {
108			if fps, err := store.Fingerprints(); err != nil {
109				slog.Warn("reindex: could not load fingerprints, re-indexing all", "error", err)
110			} else {
111				fingerprints = fps
112			}
113		}
114	
115		best, order, foundPerAgent := dedupRefs(ids, provs, perProvider)
116		found := len(order)
117		cache := &projectIDCache{m: map[string]projectIDName{}}
118		work, totals, unchanged := selectWork(order, best, fingerprints, "", force, cache)
119	
120		if found == 0 {
121			fprintln(os.Stderr, "No agent sessions found to index.")
122			return nil
123		}
124		fprintf(os.Stderr, "✓   Found %d sessions  ·  %s\n", found, summarizeCounts(ids, foundPerAgent))
125		if len(work) == 0 {
126			fprintf(os.Stderr, "✓   All %d sessions already up to date.  (%.1fs)\n", found, time.Since(start).Seconds())
127			return nil
128		}
129		if unchanged > 0 {
130			fprintf(os.Stderr, "    %d unchanged · indexing %d…\n\n", unchanged, len(work))
131		} else {
132			fprintln(os.Stderr, "")
133		}
134	
135		// ---- Phase 2+3: parse (worker pool) → write (single goroutine), with live progress ----
136		prog := newReindexProgress(ids, totals)
137		prog.begin()
138		indexedAt := start.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)
139		writeErr := processWork(ctx, store, work, cache, indexedAt, min(runtime.NumCPU(), 12), prog)
140		prog.end()
141	
142		if writeErr != nil {
143			return fmt.Errorf("writing restore index: %w", writeErr)
144		}
145	
146		// ---- Summary (counts reflect the WHOLE index, not just this run's work) ----
147		elapsed := time.Since(start)
148		projects, _ := store.ProjectCount(unknownProjectID)
149		unattributed, _ := store.UnattributedCount(unknownProjectID)
150		printReindexSummary(ids, foundPerAgent, dbPath, elapsed, prog.totalDone(), unchanged, projects, unattributed)
151		analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventReindexCompleted, analytics.Properties{
152			"sessions":     len(work),
153			"unchanged":    unchanged,
154			"projects":     projects,
155			"unattributed": unattributed,
156			"duration_ms":  elapsed.Milliseconds(),
157		})
158		return nil
159	}
160	
161	// ---- reindex engine (shared by the foreground command and the background warm) ----
162	
163	// enumerateAll concurrently lists every installed provider's sessions. The returned slices
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./pkg/cmd/reindex.go`

```text
217	func dedupRefs(ids []string, provs []spi.Provider, perProvider [][]spi.GlobalSessionRef) (best map[string]reindexItem, order []string, foundPerAgent map[string]int) {
218		best = map[string]reindexItem{}
219		foundPerAgent = map[string]int{}
220		for i, id := range ids {
221			if provs[i] == nil {
222				continue
223			}
224			for _, ref := range perProvider[i] {
225				if strings.TrimSpace(ref.SessionID) == "" {
226					slog.Debug("reindex: skipping session with no id", "agent", id, "path", ref.NativePath)
227					continue
228				}
229				size, mtime := statNative(ref.NativePath)
230				item := reindexItem{agent: id, prov: provs[i], ref: ref, size: size, mtime: mtime}
231				key := sessionindex.FingerprintKey(id, ref.SessionID)
232				if cur, ok := best[key]; ok {
233					if mtime > cur.mtime {
234						best[key] = item
235					}
236					continue
237				}
238				best[key] = item
239				order = append(order, key)
240			}
241		}
242		for _, key := range order {
243			foundPerAgent[best[key].agent]++
244		}
245		return best, order, foundPerAgent
246	}
247	
248	// selectWork applies the incremental fingerprint skip (and an optional project filter) to the
249	// deduped set. projectFilter "" means all projects; otherwise only sessions whose originating
250	// cwd resolves to that project id are kept (used by the background warm's current-project
251	// pass). totals is the per-agent to-do count (drives the foreground progress bars).
252	func selectWork(order []string, best map[string]reindexItem, fingerprints map[string]sessionindex.Fingerprint, projectFilter string, force bool, cache *projectIDCache) (work []reindexItem, totals map[string]int, unchanged int) {
253		totals = map[string]int{}
254		for _, key := range order {
255			item := best[key]
256			if projectFilter != "" {
257				if pid, _ := cache.resolve(item.ref.OriginCwd); pid != projectFilter {
258					continue
259				}
260			}
261			fp, ok := fingerprints[key]
262			if ok && fp.Size == item.size && fp.Mtime == item.mtime && fp.Version == reindexVersion {
263				unchanged++
264				continue // already indexed and unchanged
265			}
266			// A session absent from the fingerprint set has no sessions row, hence no sessions_fts
267			// row either (the pair is written atomically) — so the writer can skip the O(rows)
268			// standalone-FTS delete. Under --force the fingerprint map is deliberately empty, so
269			// absence proves nothing; keep the delete to avoid duplicate FTS rows.
270			item.isNew = !force && !ok
271			work = append(work, item)
272			totals[item.agent]++
273		}
274		return work, totals, unchanged
275	}
276	
277	// reindexReporter receives per-session progress from processWork. The foreground command
278	// passes *reindexProgress (live bars); the background warm passes nopReporter (silent).
279	type reindexReporter interface {
280		observe(projectID string)
281		inc(agent string)
282	}
283	
284	// nopReporter is the silent reporter used by the background warm.
285	type nopReporter struct{}
286	
287	func (nopReporter) observe(string) {}
288	func (nopReporter) inc(string)     {}
289	
290	// processWork parses each work item (worker pool) and writes the results to the store via a
291	// single writer goroutine, reporting progress through rep. It stops feeding work when ctx is
292	// cancelled; the writer flushes what it already received. Returns the writer's error, if any.
293	func processWork(ctx context.Context, store *sessionindex.Store, work []reindexItem, cache *projectIDCache, indexedAt string, workers int, rep reindexReporter) error {
294		if len(work) == 0 {
295			return nil
296		}
297		if workers < 1 {
298			workers = 1
299		}
300	
301		// Schedule the largest sessions first. A few huge Codex files (hundreds of MB) would
302		// otherwise be end-of-run stragglers that leave most worker cores idle; starting them
303		// first overlaps them with the long tail of small sessions. Order does not affect the
304		// resulting index.
305		sort.SliceStable(work, func(i, j int) bool { return work[i].size > work[j].size })
306	
307		sessionsCh := make(chan sessionindex.Session, 256)
308		var writeErr error
309		writerDone := make(chan struct{})
310		go func() {
311			defer close(writerDone)
312			writeErr = drainToStore(store, sessionsCh)
313		}()
314	
315		workCh := make(chan reindexItem)
316		var wwg sync.WaitGroup
317		for w := 0; w < workers; w++ {
318			wwg.Add(1)
319			go func() {
320				defer wwg.Done()
321				for item := range workCh {
322					sess := buildSession(item, cache, indexedAt)
323					rep.observe(sess.ProjectID)
324					sessionsCh <- sess
325					rep.inc(item.agent)
326				}
327			}()
328		}
329	
330		for _, item := range work {
331			select {
332			case <-ctx.Done():
333				goto closing
334			case workCh <- item:
335			}
336		}
337	closing:
338		close(workCh)
339		wwg.Wait()
340		close(sessionsCh)
341		<-writerDone
342		return writeErr
343	}
344	
345	// warmIndexInBackground keeps ~/.specstory/sessions.db warm as a side effect of resume/search.
346	// It runs two SILENT incremental passes over its own writer handle: the current project first
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./pkg/cmd/reindex.go`

```text
400	// Everything is best-effort: real-time indexing must never disrupt the agent or the markdown
401	// write, so construction failures yield a nil *LiveIndexer (whose methods are no-ops) and
402	// per-update errors are logged at debug. A live row carries no native-file fingerprint
403	// (Size/Mtime/NativePath are unknown without enumerating), so the next reindex re-verifies and
404	// rewrites it with a stable fingerprint — the live row is correct and queryable in the interim.
405	type LiveIndexer struct {
406		store       *sessionindex.Store
407		cwd         string
408		projectID   string
409		projectName string
410		indexedAt   string
411	
412		mu       sync.Mutex
413		lastSeen map[string]string // session id -> last indexed UpdatedAt, to skip no-op upserts
414	}
415	
416	// NewLiveIndexer opens the index for writing and resolves the project for cwd. It returns nil
417	// (not an error) when the index can't be opened, so the caller keeps running with real-time
418	// indexing simply disabled. The caller owns Close.
419	func NewLiveIndexer(cwd string) *LiveIndexer {
420		dbPath, err := sessionindex.DefaultPath()
421		if err != nil {
422			slog.Debug("live index: no db path; real-time indexing disabled", "error", err)
423			return nil
424		}
425		store, err := sessionindex.Open(dbPath)
426		if err != nil {
427			slog.Debug("live index: cannot open index; real-time indexing disabled", "error", err)
428			return nil
429		}
430		projectID, projectName, err := utils.ComputeProjectID(cwd)
431		if err != nil || projectID == "" {
432			projectID, projectName = unknownProjectID, ""
433		}
434		return &LiveIndexer{
435			store:       store,
436			cwd:         cwd,
437			projectID:   projectID,
438			projectName: projectName,
439			indexedAt:   time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
440			lastSeen:    map[string]string{},
441		}
442	}
443	
444	// Record upserts the live session into the index. agentID is the provider's registry id (e.g.
445	// "claude"). It skips the write when the session's last activity hasn't advanced since the
446	// previous record (the watcher can fire repeatedly without new content). Safe on a nil
447	// receiver, and serialized so concurrent provider watchers don't contend on the single writer.
448	func (li *LiveIndexer) Record(agentID string, sess *spi.AgentChatSession) {
449		if li == nil || sess == nil || sess.SessionData == nil {
450			return
451		}
452		data := sess.SessionData
453	
454		updatedAt := sess.CreatedAt
455		if ts := lastTimestamp(data); ts != "" {
456			updatedAt = ts
457		}
458	
459		li.mu.Lock()
460		defer li.mu.Unlock()
461	
462		// Nothing advanced since the last write → skip the (FTS-heavy) upsert.
463		if updatedAt != "" && li.lastSeen[sess.SessionID] == updatedAt {
464			return
465		}
466	
467		// Skip the whole-table FTS delete (session_id/agent are UNINDEXED, so it scans the entire
468		// FTS) when this session has no existing row to replace. Only the first write of a session
469		// in this process can be new, and even then only if it isn't already in the index from a
470		// prior run — so we check by primary key just once, before lastSeen records the write.
471		isNew := false
472		if _, written := li.lastSeen[sess.SessionID]; !written {
473			if exists, err := li.store.Exists(agentID, sess.SessionID); err == nil {
474				isNew = !exists
475			}
476		}
477	
478		userTurns, totalTurns := countTurns(data)
479		row := sessionindex.Session{
480			ProjectID:    li.projectID,
481			ProjectName:  li.projectName,
482			Agent:        agentID,
483			SessionID:    sess.SessionID,
484			CreatedAt:    sess.CreatedAt,
485			UpdatedAt:    updatedAt,
486			UserTurns:    userTurns,
487			TotalTurns:   totalTurns,
488			Slug:         sess.Slug,
489			OriginCwd:    li.cwd,
490			IndexVersion: reindexVersion,
491			IndexedAt:    li.indexedAt,
492			Body:         flattenBody(data),
493			IsNew:        isNew,
494		}
495		if err := li.store.Upsert(row); err != nil {
496			slog.Debug("live index: upsert failed", "sessionId", sess.SessionID, "error", err)
497			return
498		}
499		li.lastSeen[sess.SessionID] = updatedAt
500	}
501	
502	// Close releases the writer handle. Safe on a nil receiver.
503	func (li *LiveIndexer) Close() {
504		if li == nil {
505			return
506		}
507		if err := li.store.Close(); err != nil {
508			slog.Debug("live index: close failed", "error", err)
509		}
510	}
511	
512	// buildSession turns one enumeration ref into a fully-built index row. Every session
513	// yields a row from its metadata; the body + turn counts + last-activity time are
514	// ENRICHED via a full parse only when the originating cwd lets us re-locate the
515	// session (so Cursor, whose cwd is unknown for now, lands metadata-only). Nothing is
516	// dropped.
517	func buildSession(item reindexItem, cache *projectIDCache, indexedAt string) sessionindex.Session {
518		ref := item.ref
519		projectID, projectName := cache.resolve(ref.OriginCwd)
520	
521		sess := sessionindex.Session{
522			ProjectID:    projectID,
523			ProjectName:  projectName,
524			Agent:        item.agent,
525			SessionID:    ref.SessionID,
526			CreatedAt:    ref.CreatedAt,
527			UpdatedAt:    ref.CreatedAt, // default; overwritten by last-turn timestamp below
528			Slug:         ref.Slug,
529			Name:         ref.Name,
530			NativePath:   ref.NativePath,
531			OriginCwd:    ref.OriginCwd,
532			Size:         item.size, // captured during the freshness check (no re-stat)
533			Mtime:        item.mtime,
534			IndexVersion: reindexVersion,
535			IndexedAt:    indexedAt,
536			IsNew:        item.isNew,
537		}
538	
539		if ref.OriginCwd == "" {
540			return sess // cannot re-locate without a cwd → metadata-only
541		}
542		full, err := parseFullSession(item.prov, ref)
543		if err != nil || full == nil || full.SessionData == nil {
544			slog.Debug("reindex: full parse unavailable, indexing metadata only",
545				"agent", item.agent, "session", ref.SessionID, "error", err)
546			return sess
547		}
548	
549		data := full.SessionData
550		sess.Body = flattenBody(data)
551		sess.UserTurns, sess.TotalTurns = countTurns(data)
552		if ts := lastTimestamp(data); ts != "" {
553			sess.UpdatedAt = ts
554		}
555		return sess
556	}
557	
558	// parseFullSession reads a session's full data for indexing. It prefers a provider's
559	// path-keyed parse (spi.PathSessionReader) using the ref's already-known NativePath, which
560	// avoids the provider's by-id discovery search — for Codex that search walks the entire
561	// ~/.codex/sessions tree, so this is the difference between an O(N) and an O(N²) reindex.
562	// Providers without the capability (or refs lacking a NativePath) fall back to the by-id
563	// lookup. Parsing the known NativePath is also more consistent: the row's freshness
564	// fingerprint is stat'd from NativePath, so the body now comes from that same file.
565	func parseFullSession(prov spi.Provider, ref spi.GlobalSessionRef) (*spi.AgentChatSession, error) {
566		if pr, ok := prov.(spi.PathSessionReader); ok && ref.NativePath != "" {
567			return pr.GetAgentChatSessionByPath(ref.NativePath, ref.OriginCwd, false)
568		}
569		return prov.GetAgentChatSession(ref.OriginCwd, ref.SessionID, false)
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
23				{Messages: []schema.Message{userMsg("2026-06-18T10:05:00Z", "now ship it"), agentMsg("2026-06-18T10:06:00Z", "done")}},
24			},

28	func TestCountTurns(t *testing.T) {

30		if user != 2 {
31			t.Errorf("user turns = %d; want 2", user)

36	}

38	func TestLastTimestamp(t *testing.T) {

40			t.Errorf("lastTimestamp = %q; want the final message ts", got)
41		}

44		if got := lastTimestamp(noTS); got != "" {

46		}
47	}

54			}

77		cases := []struct {
78			done, total int64
79			wantFull    int // number of █ expected
80		}{
81			{0, 10, 0},
82			{10, 10, 14},
83			{5, 10, 7},
84			{1, 0, 0},    // zero total → no divide-by-zero, empty bar
85			{20, 10, 14}, // clamped, never overflows
86		}
87		for _, tc := range cases {
88			got := progressBar(tc.done, tc.total)
89			if full := strings.Count(got, "█"); full != tc.wantFull {
90				t.Errorf("progressBar(%d,%d) had %d full cells; want %d (%q)", tc.done, tc.total, full, tc.wantFull, got)
91			}
92		}
93	}
94	
95	func TestSummarizeCounts(t *testing.T) {
96		ids := []string{"claude", "codex", "cursor"}
97		got := summarizeCounts(ids, map[string]int{"claude": 683, "codex": 0, "cursor": 49})
98		if got != "claude 683 · cursor 49" {
99			t.Errorf("summarizeCounts = %q; want zeros omitted, registry order", got)
100		}
101	}
```

```text
524			m.top = m.cursor - h + 1
525		}
```

```text
888	// sessionRow renders one list row. When snippet is non-empty (search active) the row
889	// shows the highlighted match context instead of the session title.
890	func (m sessionTUI) sessionRow(s sessionindex.Session, selected bool, snippet string) string {
891		cursor := "  "
892		if selected {
893			cursor = styCursor.Render("▸ ")
894		}
895		agent := m.agentTag(s.Agent)
896		when := fmt.Sprintf("%-4s", relativeTime(s.UpdatedAt))
897	
898		if m.viewMode == "sparse" {
899			turns := styDim.Render(fmt.Sprintf("%d prompts", s.UserTurns))
900			label := renderName(sessionTitle(s), selected, m.lineWidth()-24)
901			if snippet != "" {
902				label = renderSnippet(snippet, m.lineWidth()-26)
903			}
904			head := cursor + agent + "  " + label + "   " + turns
905			sub := "    " + styFaint.Render(fmt.Sprintf("%s ago · %s", relativeTime(s.UpdatedAt), shortID(s.SessionID)))
906			return head + "\n" + sub
907		}
908		turns := styDim.Render(fmt.Sprintf("%4d", s.UserTurns))
909		// A year-stamped date ("Dec 31 '25") is wider than the 4-col slot; shrink the label by the
910		// overflow so the right-hand turns column can't get pushed off the line and wrap.
911		extra := len(when) - 4
912		if extra < 0 {
913			extra = 0
914		}
915		label := renderName(sessionTitle(s), selected, m.lineWidth()-22-extra)
916		if snippet != "" {
917			label = renderSnippet(snippet, m.lineWidth()-24-extra)
918		}
919		return cursor + agent + " " + styDim.Render(when) + "  " + label + "  " + turns
920	}
921	
922	// renderSnippet renders a FTS snippet (matched terms wrapped in the control-char marks)
923	// with the matches highlighted, collapsing whitespace and clipping to maxWidth columns.
```

```text
99	func openWith(path string, maxConns int) (*Store, error) {
100		if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(path), 0o755); err != nil {
101			return nil, fmt.Errorf("creating database directory: %w", err)
102		}
103	
104		dsn := fmt.Sprintf("file:%s?_pragma=busy_timeout=15000&_pragma=journal_mode(WAL)", filepath.ToSlash(path))
105		db, err := sql.Open("sqlite", dsn)
106		if err != nil {
107			return nil, fmt.Errorf("opening sessions.db: %w", err)
108		}
109	
110		db.SetMaxOpenConns(maxConns)
111		db.SetMaxIdleConns(maxConns)
112	
113		for _, p := range []string{
114			"PRAGMA synchronous=NORMAL",
115			"PRAGMA cache_size = -64000",
116			"PRAGMA temp_store = MEMORY",
117			"PRAGMA mmap_size = 268435456",
118			"PRAGMA page_size = 8192",
119		} {
120			if _, err := db.Exec(p); err != nil {
121				_ = db.Close()
122				return nil, fmt.Errorf("setting pragma %q: %w", p, err)
123			}
124		}
125	
126		s := &Store{db: db}
127		if err := s.ensureSchema(); err != nil {
128			_ = db.Close()
129			return nil, fmt.Errorf("ensuring schema: %w", err)
130		}
131		return s, nil
132	}
133	
134	// Close closes the database connection.
135	func (s *Store) Close() error {
136		return s.db.Close()
137	}
138	
139	// sessionColumns is the canonical sessions column list, shared by every SELECT so the
140	// scan order in scanSession stays in lockstep with it.
141	const sessionColumns = `project_id, project_name, agent, session_id, created_at, updated_at,
142		user_turns, total_turns, slug, name, native_path, origin_cwd, size, mtime, index_version, indexed_at`
143	
144	// sessionInsertColumns is sessionColumns plus fts_rowid, the internal link to the session's
145	// FTS row. fts_rowid is write-only — set on insert, read only by SessionBody's join — so it is
146	// deliberately kept out of sessionColumns and the Session struct rather than threaded through
147	// every read path.
148	const sessionInsertColumns = sessionColumns + `, fts_rowid`
149	
150	func (s *Store) ensureSchema() error {
151		schema := `
152		CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sessions (
153			project_id   TEXT NOT NULL,
154			project_name TEXT,
155			agent        TEXT NOT NULL,
156			session_id   TEXT NOT NULL,
157			created_at   TEXT,
158			updated_at   TEXT,
159			user_turns   INTEGER,
160			total_turns  INTEGER,
161			slug         TEXT,
162			name         TEXT,
163			native_path  TEXT,
164			origin_cwd   TEXT,
165			size          INTEGER,
166			mtime         INTEGER,
167			index_version INTEGER,
168			indexed_at    TEXT,
169			fts_rowid     INTEGER,
170			PRIMARY KEY (agent, session_id)
171		);
172		-- Composite over the project filter PLUS the picker's sort order. ListByProject
173		-- (the resume picker's hot path) filters by project_id and orders by
174		-- updated_at DESC, created_at DESC; this index serves both, so SQLite walks it
175		-- backward instead of filtering then sorting in a temp b-tree. project_id is the
176		-- left prefix, so ProjectCount/UnattributedCount still use it. Replaces the old
177		-- single-column idx_sessions_project (dropped in the migration below).
178		CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_sessions_project_recent ON sessions(project_id, updated_at, created_at);
179	
180		-- Standalone FTS5 index over the conversation body + name. session_id/agent ride
181		-- along UNINDEXED as join keys back to the sessions row. See docs/SESSIONS-DB.md.
182		CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sessions_fts USING fts5(
183			session_id UNINDEXED,
184			agent UNINDEXED,
185			name,
186			body
187		);
188		`
189		if _, err := s.db.Exec(schema); err != nil {
190			return err
191		}
192		// Migration for indexes created before index_version existed (the column is part of
193		// the freshness fingerprint). Ignore the error when it already exists.
194		_, _ = s.db.Exec(`ALTER TABLE sessions ADD COLUMN index_version INTEGER DEFAULT 0`)
195		// fts_rowid links a session row to its sessions_fts row, so the body read and the
196		// delete-before-insert are O(1) rowid lookups instead of whole-FTS scans (session_id/agent
197		// are UNINDEXED). NULL on rows written before this column existed; a reindex (reindexVersion
198		// bump) repopulates it. Ignore the error when the column already exists.
199		_, _ = s.db.Exec(`ALTER TABLE sessions ADD COLUMN fts_rowid INTEGER`)
200		// Drop the old single-column project index now superseded by the composite
201		// idx_sessions_project_recent (project_id is its left prefix). Idempotent; a no-op
202		// on fresh databases that never had it.
203		_, _ = s.db.Exec(`DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_sessions_project`)
204		return nil
205	}
206	
207	// Fingerprints returns the freshness fingerprint of every indexed session, keyed by
208	// fingerprintKey(agent, session_id). reindex loads this once and skips any session
209	// whose native file is unchanged (same size + mtime) and was indexed by the current
210	// logic version.
211	func (s *Store) Fingerprints() (map[string]Fingerprint, error) {
212		rows, err := s.db.Query(`SELECT agent, session_id, size, mtime, index_version FROM sessions`)
213		if err != nil {
```

 `./pkg/cmd/resume.go`

```text
266	// Same-agent resume reuses the existing session; cross-agent reconstructs the
267	// source SessionData into the target's native store and returns the fresh ID.
268	func prepareResumeTarget(plan *resumePlan, cwd string, out io.Writer) (string, error) {
269		// Same agent: native resume on the existing session, no transform.
270		if plan.fromID == plan.toID {
271			fprintf(out, "\nResuming %s session %s in place...\n", plan.from.Name(), shortID(plan.sessionID))
272			return plan.sessionID, nil
273		}
274	
275		// Cross-agent: load source SessionData, reconstruct, write into target store.
276		// The source must be read from the directory it was originally launched in, not the
277		// user's current cwd — a session picked from another project (all-projects browser)
278		// lives elsewhere, and providers like Codex locate it by its recorded cwd. The
279		// reconstructed session is still written and launched under the current cwd below.
280		fromCwd := plan.fromCwd
281		if fromCwd == "" {
282			fromCwd = cwd
283		}
284		fromSession, err := plan.from.GetAgentChatSession(fromCwd, plan.sessionID, false)
285		if err != nil {
286			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to load source session: %w", err)
287		}
288		if fromSession == nil || fromSession.SessionData == nil {
289			return "", fmt.Errorf("source session %s has no data to reconstruct", plan.sessionID)
290		}
291	
292		note := fmt.Sprintf("Resumed from a %s session via SpecStory.", plan.from.Name())
293		rec, err := plan.to.ReconstructSession(fromSession.SessionData, spi.ReconstructOptions{
294			WorkspaceRoot: cwd,
295			MigrationNote: note,
296		})
297		if err != nil {
298			if errors.Is(err, spi.ErrReconstructionUnsupported) {
299				return "", utils.ValidationError{Message: fmt.Sprintf(
300					"%s can't yet be a cross-agent resume target. Choose Claude Code or Codex CLI (or resume in %s itself).",
301					plan.to.Name(), plan.from.Name())}
302			}
303			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to reconstruct session for %s: %w", plan.to.Name(), err)
304		}
305	
306		path, err := plan.to.NativeSessionPath(cwd, rec.Filename)
307		if err != nil {
308			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to resolve target session path: %w", err)
309		}
310		if err := writeReconstructedSession(path, rec.Content); err != nil {
311			return "", err
312		}
313		// The agent runs in a separate process and resolves the session by reading this file the
314		// instant it starts. On a filesystem without strong close-to-open coherence the freshly
315		// written file can lag, so confirm it is actually readable before handing the agent a
316		// --resume it would otherwise reject as "session not found".
317		if err := waitForSessionFileVisible(path, sessionFileVisibleTimeout); err != nil {
318			return "", fmt.Errorf("reconstructed %s session is not ready to resume: %w", plan.to.Name(), err)
319		}
320	
321		slog.Info("resume: wrote reconstructed session", "path", path, "newID", rec.SessionID)
322		fprintf(out, "\nReconstructed %s session into %s as %s.\n", plan.from.Name(), plan.to.Name(), shortID(rec.SessionID))
323		return rec.SessionID, nil
324	}
325

327	func shortID(id string) string {
```

```text
1	package analytics

3	import (
4		"os"

8	
9	// Event name constants
10	const (
11		EventExtensionActivated     = "ext_activated"                // Tracks when `run` command is started
12		EventWatchActivated         = "ext_watch_activated"          // Tracks when `watch` command is started
13		EventResumeActivated        = "ext_resume_activated"         // Tracks when `resume` command is started
14		EventReindexCompleted       = "ext_reindex_completed"        // Tracks when `reindex` finishes building the restore index
15		EventProjectIdentityCreated = "ext_project_identity_created" // Tracks when new project identity is created
16		EventCheckInstallSuccess    = "ext_check_install_success"    // Tracks successful agent installation check
17		EventCheckInstallFailed     = "ext_check_install_failed"     // Tracks failed agent installation check
18		EventVersionCommand         = "ext_version_command"          // Tracks when users check the version
19		EventHelpCommand            = "ext_help_command"             // Tracks when users view help
20		EventAutosaveNew            = "ext_autosave_new"             // Tracks when a new markdown file is created during `run` or `--sync-markdown`
21		EventAutosaveSuccess        = "ext_autosave_success"         // Tracks when a markdown file is updated during `run`
22		EventAutosaveError          = "ext_autosave_error"           // Tracks when an error occurs generating or writing markdown during `run`
23		EventSyncMarkdownNew        = "ext_sync_markdown_new"        // Tracks when a new markdown file is created during `sync`
24		EventSyncMarkdownSuccess    = "ext_sync_markdown_success"    // Tracks when a markdown file is updated during `sync`
25		EventSyncMarkdownError      = "ext_sync_markdown_error"      // Tracks when an error occurs generating or writing markdown during `sync`
26		EventLoginAttempted         = "ext_login_attempted"          // Tracks when user starts the login flow
27		EventLoginCancelled         = "ext_login_cancelled"          // Tracks when user cancels the login flow
28		EventLoginSuccess           = "ext_login_success"            // Tracks successful login to SpecStory Cloud
29		EventLoginFailed            = "ext_login_failed"             // Tracks failed login attempts
30		EventLogout                 = "ext_logout"                   // Tracks user logout from SpecStory Cloud
31		EventCloudSyncComplete      = "ext_cloudsync_complete"       // Tracks cloud sync completion with statistics
32		EventSyncStatsComplete      = "ext_sync_stats_complete"      // Tracks when --only-stats sync completes
33		EventListSessions           = "ext_list_sessions"            // Tracks when users list sessions with the list command
34	)
35	
36	// Properties is a type alias for event properties to avoid exposing PostHog types
37	type Properties map[string]interface{}
38	
39	// TrackEvent sends a generic event to PostHog
40	func TrackEvent(eventName string, properties Properties) {
41		if !IsEnabled() {
42			return
43		}
44	
45		distinctID := GetDistinctID()
46	
47		// Convert to PostHog properties
48		phProperties := make(posthog.Properties)
49		for k, v := range properties {
50			phProperties[k] = v
51		}
52	
53		// Always include CLI command, device ID, and project path
54		phProperties["cli_command"] = GetCLICommand()
55		phProperties["$device_id"] = distinctID
56	
57		// Capture current working directory as project_path
58		if cwd, err := os.Getwd(); err == nil {
59			phProperties["project_path"] = cwd
60		}
61	
62		// Agent provider information
63		phProperties["agent_provider"] = GetAgentProviderName()
64	
65		// Editor information
66		phProperties["editor_name"] = "SpecStory CLI"
67		phProperties["editor_type"] = "CLI"
68		phProperties["extension_version"] = GetCLIVersion()
69	
70		// OS information (gathered once during initialization)
71		osArch, osName, osPlatform, osVersion := GetOSInfo()
72		phProperties["os_arch"] = osArch
73		phProperties["os_name"] = osName
74		phProperties["os_platform"] = osPlatform
75		phProperties["os_version"] = osVersion
76	
77		err := client.Enqueue(posthog.Capture{
78			DistinctId:       distinctID,
79			Event:            eventName,
80			Properties:       phProperties,
81			SendFeatureFlags: posthog.SendFeatureFlags(false),
82		})
83	
84		if err != nil {
85			// Silently fail - analytics should not break the app
86			return
87		}
88	}
89
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-06-29 15:56:45Z)**_

I now have full context. Two findings worth noting before I ask:

- **Reconstruction is already partially observable**: `prepareResumeTarget` (resume.go:321) already `slog.Info`s on success and the `resume` command already fires `EventResumeActivated`. Reconstruction has a **single call site**, so the right place to add the rest is there — *not* in all six provider methods (which would re-introduce the duplication MIP #1 just removed).
- **store.go errors are already surfaced** to callers who log them (reindex wraps "writing restore index", `NewLiveIndexer` logs open failures). So internal store logging would mostly double-log; the concrete non-redundant fix there is the bare schema-error wrap (#16).

The move/clamp dedup (#70) and the two `final.(sessionTUI)` assertions (#64/#73) are unambiguous — I'll just do those. Two genuine design forks need your call:

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-06-29 16:01:44Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="generic" data-tool-name="AskUserQuestion"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **AskUserQuestion**</summary>
**For reconstruction observability (MIP #4), how much telemetry do you want?**

Options:
- **New event + logging** - Add a tracked EventResumeReconstructed (from_agent, to_agent, outcome: success/unsupported/error) at the single call site, plus slog on start/failure. Gives visibility into cross-agent portability usage and failure rates.
- **Logging only** - Add slog.Info(start)/slog.Warn(failure) at the call site, no new analytics event. Less telemetry surface, nothing new sent to PostHog.

**For the TUI row duplication (MIP #5 / #71), how aggressive should the dedup be?**

Options:
- **Extract sub-helpers** - Pull out the genuinely-duplicated bits (cursor prefix + snippet-or-title label selection) into small helpers; keep sessionRow and globalRow as separate functions since their column layouts (turns vs project) really differ. Safer, stays readable.
- **Full merge** - Collapse both into one parameterized row function with includeProject/includeTurns flags and conditional width math. Maximum DRY but more conditional branching in one function.


**Answer:** New event + logging" selected preview:
analytics: + EventResumeReconstructed
  props: {from_agent, to_agent, outcome}
resume.go prepareResumeTarget:
  slog.Info(start) / slog.Warn(fail) / event, Extract sub-helpers" selected preview:
+ rowCursor(selected)
+ rowLabel(s, selected, snippet, titleW, snipW)
sessionRow / globalRow: keep, call helpers
(no risky behavioral merge)
</details></tool-use>

```text
299	func TestLiveIndexerRecord(t *testing.T) {
300		store, err := sessionindex.Open(filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "sessions.db"))
301		if err != nil {
302			t.Fatalf("open index: %v", err)
303		}
304		defer func() { _ = store.Close() }()
305	
306		li := &LiveIndexer{
307			store:       store,
308			cwd:         "/work/proj",
309			projectID:   "proj-1",
310			projectName: "proj",
311			indexedAt:   "2026-06-25T00:00:00Z",
312			lastSeen:    map[string]string{},
313		}
314	
315		msg := func(role, ts, text string) schema.Message {
316			return schema.Message{
317				Role:      role,
318				Timestamp: ts,
319				Content:   []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: text}},
320			}
321		}
322		sess := func(msgs ...schema.Message) *spi.AgentChatSession {
323			return &spi.AgentChatSession{
324				SessionID: "sess-1",
325				CreatedAt: "2026-06-25T00:00:00Z",
326				Slug:      "hello-world",
327				SessionData: &schema.SessionData{
328					Provider:  schema.ProviderInfo{Name: "Claude Code"},
329					Exchanges: []schema.Exchange{{Messages: msgs}},
330				},
331			}
332		}
333		user1 := msg(schema.RoleUser, "2026-06-25T00:01:00Z", "index this please")
334		agent1 := msg(schema.RoleAgent, "2026-06-25T00:02:00Z", "done indexing")
335	
336		// First record: one user + one agent message → one fully-derived row.
337		li.Record("claude", sess(user1, agent1))
338		rows, err := store.ListByProject("proj-1")
339		if err != nil {
340			t.Fatalf("list: %v", err)
341		}
342		if len(rows) != 1 {
343			t.Fatalf("want 1 row, got %d", len(rows))
344		}
345		r := rows[0]
346		if r.Agent != "claude" || r.SessionID != "sess-1" || r.OriginCwd != "/work/proj" {
347			t.Errorf("identity wrong: agent=%q session=%q cwd=%q", r.Agent, r.SessionID, r.OriginCwd)
348		}
349		if r.UserTurns != 1 || r.TotalTurns != 2 {
350			t.Errorf("turns = %d/%d, want 1/2", r.UserTurns, r.TotalTurns)
351		}
352		if r.UpdatedAt != "2026-06-25T00:02:00Z" {
353			t.Errorf("updatedAt = %q, want last message timestamp", r.UpdatedAt)
354		}
355	
356		// The conversation body is full-text searchable immediately.
357		if hits, _ := store.Search(ftsQuery("indexing"), "proj-1"); len(hits) != 1 {
358			t.Errorf("search for body word returned %d hits, want 1", len(hits))
359		}
360	
361		// Dedup guard: re-recording with no new activity is a no-op.
362		li.Record("claude", sess(user1, agent1))
363		if rows, _ := store.ListByProject("proj-1"); len(rows) != 1 {
364			t.Errorf("dedup: want 1 row, got %d", len(rows))
365		}
366	
367		// New activity (a later message) updates the same row in place.
368		li.Record("claude", sess(user1, agent1, msg(schema.RoleUser, "2026-06-25T00:03:00Z", "more")))
369		rows, _ = store.ListByProject("proj-1")
370		if len(rows) != 1 {
371			t.Fatalf("want 1 row after update, got %d", len(rows))
372		}
373		if rows[0].UserTurns != 2 || rows[0].TotalTurns != 3 || rows[0].UpdatedAt != "2026-06-25T00:03:00Z" {
374			t.Errorf("after update: turns=%d/%d updatedAt=%q, want 2/3 and advanced timestamp",
375				rows[0].UserTurns, rows[0].TotalTurns, rows[0].UpdatedAt)
376		}
377	
378		// A nil indexer (index failed to open) is safe to use.
379		var nilLI *LiveIndexer
380		nilLI.Record("claude", sess(user1))
381		nilLI.Close()
382	}
383
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-06-29 16:07:40Z)**_

All three MIPs are done and verified. Here's the summary.

## MIP #2 — Reindex orchestration tests

Added four tests to `pkg/cmd/reindex_test.go` covering the previously-untested core logic (reusing the existing `fakeProvider`, all `t.TempDir`-isolated):

- **`TestDedupRefs`** — duplicate `(agent, sessionID)` collapses to the **freshest native file by mtime** (real temp files via `os.Chtimes`), blank session IDs dropped, a nil/failed provider skipped, `foundPerAgent` counts distinct sessions.
- **`TestSelectWork`** — table-driven over the incremental skip: unchanged (size+mtime+version match → skipped), changed mtime, **version bump forces reindex**, new-session `isNew=true`, and `--force` keeps `isNew=false`.
- **`TestSelectWorkProjectFilter`** — the background-warm current-project pass keeps only matching projects (pre-seeded cache, no real path resolution).
- **`TestLiveIndexerRecordConcurrent`** — 24 goroutines recording distinct sessions; **passes under `-race`**, guarding the `lastSeen` mutex that was never exercised.

## MIP #4 — Observability (you chose: new event + logging)

- New `analytics.EventResumeReconstructed` constant.
- Instrumented the **single** reconstruction call site (`prepareResumeTarget` in resume.go) — *not* the six provider methods, to avoid re-introducing the duplication MIP #1 removed: `slog.Info` on start, `slog.Warn` on failure, and the outcome event fired at all three terminal branches (`success` / `unsupported` / `error`) with `from_agent`/`to_agent`.
- `store.go`: fixed the bare schema-exec error to wrap with `%w` (#16) and corrected the `scanSession`→`scanSessions` doc typo (#17). I deliberately did **not** add internal store logging — those errors are already surfaced and logged by callers, so it would just double-log.

## MIP #5 — TUI dedup + assertion hardening (you chose: extract sub-helpers)

- **Cursor/scroll dedup**: extracted `moveCursorWithin`/`clampScrollWithin` (pointer-based); the three method pairs (`moveCursor`/`moveProjCursor`/`moveGlobalCursor` + clamps) are now thin wrappers — ~60 lines → ~12, call sites unchanged.
- **Row dedup**: extracted `rowCursor` and `rowLabel` (the genuinely-duplicated cursor gutter + snippet-or-title selection, 4 copies → 1), keeping `sessionRow`/`globalRow` separate since their column layouts really differ.
- **Type assertions**: both `final.(sessionTUI)` (session_tui.go + search.go) now use comma-ok and return a clear `unexpected model type %T` error instead of panicking.

## Verification

- `go build ./...` + binary: clean
- Full `go test ./...` (and `-race` on the new tests): all pass, no failures
- `golangci-lint run` (whole project): **0 issues**

The `interface{}→any`, `minmax`, `rangeint`, and `maps.Copy` diagnostics that surfaced are pre-existing gopls modernization hints in those files (the project's lint config doesn't enforce them — hence 0 issues), not introduced here.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-06-29 16:17:37Z)**_

Here's the full picture. The session-impacting flags on `run` (ignoring `--command` and `--resume` which are launch-mechanics) are **11**, and `watch` — the true "launch-and-watch a session" analog — carries all 11. Here's how the three commands compare today:

| Flag | `run`/`watch` | `resume` today | `search` today | Impacts resumed session? |
|---|:---:|:---:|:---:|---|
| `--output-dir` | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | Yes — where markdown is written |
| `--debug-dir` | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | Yes — debug data location |
| `--local-time-zone` | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | Yes — filename/content timestamps |
| `--no-cloud-sync` | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | Yes — disables upload |
| `--no-telemetry-prompts` | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | Yes — telemetry redaction |
| `--telemetry-endpoint` | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | Yes — OTLP target |
| `--telemetry-service-name` | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | Yes — telemetry service id |
| `--only-cloud-sync` | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | **Yes** — skip local saves, upload only |
| `--cloud-url` | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | **Yes** — cloud API base |
| `--debug-raw` | ✅ (hidden) | ✅ | ❌ | **Yes** — raw debug dumps |
| `--provenance` | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | **Yes** — AI provenance tracking during the session |

So, to answer directly: **your 7 are all correct and necessary — but the list is incomplete.** Two things fall out:

1. **`search` is missing all 11** (it currently hardcodes only `useUTC` internally) — that's the real #65 bug.
2. **`resume` itself is already missing two**: `--telemetry-endpoint` and `--telemetry-service-name` (it has `--no-telemetry-prompts` but not the endpoint/service-name that configure where telemetry actually goes). So this isn't purely a `search` fix — `resume`'s own set needs completing too.

The four your list omitted — `--only-cloud-sync`, `--cloud-url`, `--debug-raw`, `--provenance` — all genuinely affect the resumed session (they change where output goes, whether local files are written, and whether provenance is tracked while the agent runs).

Note the **global** persistent flags (`--debug`, `--log`, `--console`, `--silent`, `--no-usage-analytics`, `--no-version-check`, `--cloud-token`) are defined on the root command, so `resume`/`search` already inherit them — no need to re-add those.

My recommendation: **make `resume` and `search` both carry the exact same session-impacting set as `watch` (all 11)** — `watch` is the natural template since it also launches-and-watches, and matching it keeps the three consistent rather than inventing a third subset.

Let me confirm the one genuine judgment call before I build it:

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-06-29 16:20:46Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="generic" data-tool-name="AskUserQuestion"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **AskUserQuestion**</summary>
**Which flag set should resume + search expose for the resumed session?**

Options:
- **All 11 (match watch)** - resume + search both get the full session-impacting set watch already has: your 7 plus --only-cloud-sync, --cloud-url, --debug-raw, --provenance. Also adds the two telemetry flags resume is currently missing. Most consistent.
- **Just your 7** - Only the 7 you listed. Simpler surface, but leaves out cloud-url/only-cloud-sync/debug-raw/provenance (which do affect the session) and resume/search end up with a different set than watch/run.


**Answer:** All 11 (match watch)" selected preview:
output-dir, debug-dir, local-time-zone,
no-cloud-sync, only-cloud-sync, cloud-url,
debug-raw, provenance,
telemetry-endpoint, telemetry-service-name,
no-telemetry-prompts
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./pkg/cmd/resume.go`

```text
56	
57	// CreateResumeCommand builds the `specstory resume` command. It opens the interactive
58	// picker (a Bubble Tea TUI over the sessions.db index — see docs/RESUME-TUI.md): browse
59	// the current project's sessions across all agents, pick one, choose a target agent, then
60	// reconstruct (cross-agent) or native-resume (same agent) and launch with auto-save.
61	func CreateResumeCommand(cloudURL *string, localTimeZone bool, debugDir string) *cobra.Command {
62		longDesc := `Resume a past coding-agent session — in the same agent, or a different one.
63	
64	'resume' opens an interactive picker of the sessions in the current project across all
65	agents. Pick one, choose which installed agent to continue it in, and go. Resuming into a
66	different agent reconstructs the conversation into that agent's native format first.
67	
68	Pass an agent to set the resume target up front, e.g. 'specstory resume codex' — the picker
69	then skips the target-selection step and resumes straight into that agent. The agent must be
70	a known, installed provider, or the command errors.`
71	
72		resumeCmd := &cobra.Command{
73			Use:   "resume [agent]",
74			Short: "Resume a past session, optionally in a different agent",
75			Long:  longDesc,
76			Args:  cobra.MaximumNArgs(1),
77			RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
78				config.EnsureDefaultProjectConfig()
79				slog.Info("Running in resume mode")
80	
81				registry := factory.GetRegistry()
82	
83				// Optional positional arg pre-selects the target agent (e.g. `resume codex`).
84				// An unknown agent name should fail the run rather than be silently ignored, so
85				// validate it against the registry up front. When set and valid, the picker skips
86				// the target-selection step entirely (see selectResumeViaTUI / beginResume); whether
87				// the named agent is actually installed is checked there, where the installed set
88				// is known.
89				presetTarget := ""
90				if len(args) == 1 {
91					presetTarget = strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(args[0]))
92					if _, err := registry.Get(presetTarget); err != nil {
93						return utils.ValidationError{Message: fmt.Sprintf(
94							"unknown agent %q. Valid agents: %s", presetTarget, registry.GetProviderList())}
95					}
96				}
97	
98				// Read flags (subset mirroring run/watch).
99				debugRaw, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("debug-raw")
100				useLocalTimezone, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("local-time-zone")
101				useUTC := !useLocalTimezone
102				outputDir, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("output-dir")
103				flagDebugDir, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("debug-dir")
104				noCloudSync, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-cloud-sync")
105				onlyCloudSync, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("only-cloud-sync")
106				provenanceEnabled, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("provenance")
107				noTelemetryPrompts, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-telemetry-prompts")
108	
109				if flagDebugDir != "" {
110					spi.SetDebugBaseDir(flagDebugDir)
111				}
112	
113				cwd, err := os.Getwd()
114				if err != nil {
115					slog.Error("Failed to get current working directory", "error", err)
116					return err
117				}
118	
119				// Open (building if needed) the session index and run the interactive picker.
120				// builtFresh = the index was just rebuilt in the foreground, so the picker skips
121				// the background index warm (it would be redundant).
122				store, builtFresh, err := openOrBuildResumeIndex()
123				if err != nil {
124					return err
125				}
126				defer func() { _ = store.Close() }()
127	
128				projectID, projectName, idErr := utils.ComputeProjectID(cwd)
129				if idErr != nil || projectID == "" {
130					projectID, projectName = unknownProjectID, filepath.Base(cwd)
131				}
132	
133				plan, err := selectResumeViaTUI(registry, store, projectID, projectName, presetTarget, builtFresh)
134				if err != nil {
135					return err
136				}
137				if plan == nil {
138					// User quit or nothing to resume; the picker already explained why.
139					return nil
140				}
141	
142				return launchResume(plan, cwd, resumeLaunchOpts{
143					outputDir:          outputDir,
144					flagDebugDir:       flagDebugDir,
145					debugRaw:           debugRaw,
146					useUTC:             useUTC,
147					noCloudSync:        noCloudSync,
148					onlyCloudSync:      onlyCloudSync,
149					provenanceEnabled:  provenanceEnabled,
150					noTelemetryPrompts: noTelemetryPrompts,
151				})
152			},
153		}
154	
155		resumeCmd.Flags().String("output-dir", "", "custom output directory for markdown files (default: ./.specstory/history)")
156		resumeCmd.Flags().String("debug-dir", debugDir, "custom output directory for debug data (default: ./.specstory/debug)")
157		resumeCmd.Flags().Bool("only-cloud-sync", false, "skip local markdown file saves, only upload to cloud (requires authentication)")
158		resumeCmd.Flags().Bool("no-cloud-sync", false, "disable cloud sync functionality")
159		resumeCmd.Flags().Bool("debug-raw", false, "debug mode to output pretty-printed raw data files")
160		_ = resumeCmd.Flags().MarkHidden("debug-raw")
161		resumeCmd.Flags().Bool("local-time-zone", localTimeZone, "use local timezone for file name and content timestamps (when not present: UTC)")
162		resumeCmd.Flags().Bool("provenance", false, "enable AI provenance tracking (correlate file changes to agent activity)")
163		_ = resumeCmd.Flags().MarkHidden("provenance")
164		resumeCmd.Flags().StringVar(cloudURL, "cloud-url", "", "override the default cloud API base URL")
165		_ = resumeCmd.Flags().MarkHidden("cloud-url")
166		resumeCmd.Flags().Bool("no-telemetry-prompts", false, "exclude prompt text from telemetry spans, if telemetry is enabled")
167	
168		return resumeCmd
169	}
170	
171	// resumeLaunchOpts carries the resume launch configuration shared by `resume` and
172	// `search` (whose `r` action resumes a found session through the same path).
173	type resumeLaunchOpts struct {
174		outputDir          string
175		flagDebugDir       string
176		debugRaw           bool
177		useUTC             bool
178		noCloudSync        bool
179		onlyCloudSync      bool
180		provenanceEnabled  bool
181		noTelemetryPrompts bool
182	}
183	
184	// launchResume reconstructs (cross-agent) or natively resumes the planned session and
185	// runs the agent with auto-save + provenance — the shared tail of `resume` and `search`.
186	func launchResume(plan *resumePlan, cwd string, o resumeLaunchOpts) error {
187		// Setup output configuration and project identity (needed for auto-save + cloud).
188		outConfig, err := utils.SetupOutputConfig(o.outputDir, o.flagDebugDir)
189		if err != nil {
190			return err
191		}
192		if err := utils.EnsureHistoryDirectoryExists(outConfig); err != nil {
193			return err
194		}
195		if _, err := utils.NewProjectIdentityManager(cwd).EnsureProjectIdentity(); err != nil {
196			slog.Error("Failed to ensure project identity", "error", err)
197		}
198	
199		CheckAndWarnAuthentication(o.noCloudSync)
200		if o.onlyCloudSync && !cloud.IsAuthenticated() {
201			return utils.ValidationError{Message: "--only-cloud-sync requires authentication. Please run 'specstory login' first"}
202		}
203	
204		analytics.SetAgentProviders([]string{plan.to.Name()})
205		analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventResumeActivated, analytics.Properties{
206			"from_provider": plan.fromID,
207			"to_provider":   plan.toID,
208			"cross_agent":   plan.fromID != plan.toID,
209		})
210	
211		resumeSessionID, err := prepareResumeTarget(plan, cwd, os.Stdout)
212		if err != nil {
213			return err
214		}
215	
216		// Context for graceful cancellation (Ctrl+C).
217		ctx, cancel := signal.NotifyContext(context.Background(), os.Interrupt, syscall.SIGTERM)
218		defer cancel()
219	
220		// Provenance infrastructure (mirrors run/watch).
221		provenanceEngine, provenanceCleanup, err := provenance.StartEngine(o.provenanceEnabled)
222		if err != nil {
223			return err
224		}
225		defer provenanceCleanup()
226		fsCleanup, err := provenance.StartFSWatcher(ctx, provenanceEngine, cwd)
227		if err != nil {
228			return err
229		}
230		defer fsCleanup()
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-06-29 16:22:32Z)**_

Now I fully understand the wiring. The pattern is clear:

- **`--telemetry-endpoint` / `--telemetry-service-name`** are consumed **process-wide** by main.go's `os.Args` pre-scan → `telemetry.Init` (main.go:1351-1386, 1555). `watch` registers them as **inert local flags** (watch.go:337-338) purely so cobra accepts them; the global scan does the real work. So `resume`/`search` just need to *register* them — no per-command threading.
- **`--cloud-url`** rides the shared `cloudURL *string` pointer applied in `PersistentPreRunE`.
- The other 8 (output-dir, debug-dir, local-time-zone, no/only-cloud-sync, debug-raw, provenance, no-telemetry-prompts) are consumed **per-session** via `resumeLaunchOpts` → `ProcessSingleSession`/`ExecAgentAndWatch`.

So `resume` only needs the two telemetry registrations added; `search` needs the whole set wired. Let me read `search.go` in full and the command registration in main.go.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-06-29 16:22:33Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./pkg/cmd/search.go`

```text
1	package cmd
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"os"
6		"path/filepath"
7		"strings"
8	
9		tea "charm.land/bubbletea/v2"
10		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
11	
12		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/config"
13		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/factory"
14		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/utils"
15	)
16	
17	// CreateSearchCommand builds `specstory search [query…]`. It is the same interactive UI as
18	// `specstory resume` (see sessionTUI), entered straight into the all-projects full-text
19	// search with the input focused: type to search, `space` previews a match (glamour-rendered),
20	// and `r` resumes it through the same launch path as `resume`. See docs/SESSION-SEARCH.md.
21	func CreateSearchCommand() *cobra.Command {
22		return &cobra.Command{
23			Use:   "search [query…]",
24			Short: "Search and read your past coding-agent sessions",
25			Long: `Full-text search across every session SpecStory has indexed, then read the match.
26	
27	'search' opens an interactive search of your session history. Type to search, press space to
28	preview a session (rendered for the terminal), and press 'r' to resume it in an agent. Anything
29	after the command pre-seeds the query, e.g. 'specstory search max cpu'.`,
30			Args: cobra.ArbitraryArgs,
31			RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
32				registry := factory.GetRegistry()
33				initialQuery := strings.TrimSpace(strings.Join(args, " "))
34	
35				cwd, err := os.Getwd()
36				if err != nil {
37					return err
38				}
39	
40				store, builtFresh, err := openOrBuildResumeIndex()
41				if err != nil {
42					return err
43				}
44				defer func() { _ = store.Close() }()
45	
46				if total, _ := store.Count(); total == 0 {
47					fprintln(os.Stderr, "\nNo agent sessions indexed yet. Run an agent, then try again (or `specstory reindex`).")
48					return nil
49				}
50	
51				homeID, homeName, idErr := utils.ComputeProjectID(cwd)
52				if idErr != nil || homeID == "" {
53					homeID, homeName = unknownProjectID, filepath.Base(cwd)
54				}
55				homeSessions, _ := store.ListByProject(homeID)
56	
57				agents := map[string]agentMeta{}
58				var installed []agentChoice
59				for _, id := range registry.ListIDs() {
60					prov, err := registry.Get(id)
61					if err != nil {
62						continue
63					}
64					agents[id] = agentMeta{name: prov.Name(), accent: colorForAgent(id)}
65					if prov.Check("").Success {
66						installed = append(installed, agentChoice{id: id, provider: prov})
67					}
68				}
69	
70				viewMode, lastAgent := "dense", ""
71				if cfg, _ := config.Load(nil); cfg != nil {
72					viewMode = cfg.GetResumeViewMode()
73					lastAgent = cfg.GetResumeLastAgent()
74				}
75	
76				// `search` is the same TUI as `resume`, entered straight into the all-projects
77				// FTS with the input focused. See newSessionTUI / sessionTUIOpts.
78				model := newSessionTUI(store, registry, homeID, homeName, homeSessions, agents, installed, sessionTUIOpts{
79					title:         "SpecStory Search",
80					lastAgent:     lastAgent,
81					viewMode:      viewMode,
82					initialQuery:  initialQuery,
83					startInSearch: true,
84				})
85				p := tea.NewProgram(model)
86				cancelWarm := startIndexWarm(p, homeID, builtFresh)
87				final, err := p.Run()
88				cancelWarm() // stop warming the moment search exits (never block on it)
89				if err != nil {
90					return fmt.Errorf("search failed: %w", err)
91				}
92				rm, ok := final.(sessionTUI)
93				if !ok {
94					return fmt.Errorf("search returned unexpected model type %T", final)
95				}
96				if rm.result.cancelled || rm.result.session == nil {
97					return nil // cancelled: nothing to launch
98				}
99	
100				// The user asked to resume a found session — launch via the shared path.
101				fromProv, err := registry.Get(rm.result.session.Agent)
102				if err != nil {
103					return fmt.Errorf("unknown source agent %q: %w", rm.result.session.Agent, err)
104				}
105				toProv, err := registry.Get(rm.result.targetID)
106				if err != nil {
107					return fmt.Errorf("unknown target agent %q: %w", rm.result.targetID, err)
108				}
109				_ = config.SaveResumePrefs(viewMode, rm.result.targetID)
110	
111				plan := &resumePlan{
112					from:      fromProv,
113					fromID:    rm.result.session.Agent,
114					sessionID: rm.result.session.SessionID,
115					// A search hit can be from another project; resume must load the source from
116					// its original cwd, not the current directory.
117					fromCwd: rm.result.session.OriginCwd,
118					to:      toProv,
119					toID:    rm.result.targetID,
120				}
121				return launchResume(plan, cwd, resumeLaunchOpts{useUTC: true})
122			},
123		}
124	}
125
```

```bash
done
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-06-29 16:25:12Z)**_

MIP #3 is done and verified. Summary:

## MIP #3 — `search`/`resume` flag parity

**You confirmed: all 11 session-impacting flags, matching `watch`.** And `--debug-raw` stays hidden.

### What I found
- `search` had **none** of the flags (hardcoded `useUTC` only) — the actual #65 bug.
- `resume` was itself missing **two**: `--telemetry-endpoint` and `--telemetry-service-name`.

### What changed
- **Extracted two shared helpers in `resume.go`** so `resume` and `search` stay in lockstep instead of duplicating ~25 lines:
  - `registerResumeLaunchFlags(cmd, cloudURL, localTimeZone, debugDir)` — registers all 11, marks `debug-raw`/`provenance`/`cloud-url` hidden.
  - `readResumeLaunchOpts(cmd)` — reads the 8 per-session flags into `resumeLaunchOpts` and applies the `--debug-dir` override (mirrors run/watch).
- **`resume.go`**: replaced its inline flag block and registration with the helpers — net effect, it gains the two telemetry flags it was missing.
- **`search.go`**: changed signature to `CreateSearchCommand(cloudURL *string, localTimeZone bool, debugDir string)`, registers the full set, reads `launchOpts` early (so `--debug-dir` takes effect before the index/TUI open), and passes it through `launchResume` instead of `{useUTC: true}`.
- **`main.go`**: updated the `CreateSearchCommand(&cloudURL, localTimeZone, debugDir)` call.

### How each flag is consumed (and why this is correct, not dead flags)
- **8 per-session** (output-dir, debug-dir, local-time-zone, no/only-cloud-sync, debug-raw, provenance, no-telemetry-prompts) → threaded via `resumeLaunchOpts` into `ProcessSingleSession`/`ExecAgentAndWatch`.
- **`cloud-url`** → the shared `cloudURL *string` pointer applied in root `PersistentPreRunE`.
- **`telemetry-endpoint`/`telemetry-service-name`** → consumed process-wide by main.go's `os.Args` startup scan → `telemetry.Init`; registered as inert local flags only so cobra accepts them (exactly how `watch` does it).

### Verification
- `resume --help` and `search --help` now show **identical** flag surfaces; the 3 hidden flags are accepted but unlisted (`--debug-raw` confirmed hidden-but-functional).
- All 5 hidden/telemetry flags accepted by cobra on both commands (no "unknown flag").
- `go build`: clean · `go test ./...`: all pass · `golangci-lint run`: **0 issues**.

That completes all five MIPs from the review (#1 reconstruct dedup, #2 reindex tests, #3 flag parity, #4 observability, #5 TUI dedup/hardening).
